I admit to still using liquid Stevia for coffee, and I believe Dr. K's stance is, it's ok....for now. Meaning, liquid (i.e. pure, not the powdered kind which has binders and other things in it) has not been shown to have adverse effects....yet.

I had a Diet soft drink about 2 months ago after not having any for over a year, and it tasted terrible. I was somewhere where it's all they had (or beer), and I was really thirsty. Next time, I'll bring my own water.

### Albert83BCN — 2013-06-23T11:15:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/artificial-sweetners.7429/post-88725>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=88585)
>
> If you're going to have a sweetner - what nature provides is better than what man makes. The question is why do you need something sweet? I did that earlier this month - went down and picked a huge bag of blackberries/mulberries growing wild .... waiting right now for the next round to turn ripe and waiting for the wild raspberries in the front yard to ripen.
>
> I occasionally have local honey - but only in summer. same goes for maple syrup. this isn't something I'd do daily. I really don't like sweet any more. I've kicked the habit. there are days I've found that fruit is too sweet for me.

My approach has been very like Shijin13's, if I want a sweet taste, I resort to either a few drops of honey, raisins or mashed dates, all things that I considere pretty naturaly provided but nature. But in the same fashion she reports, I no longer feel that need to have something sweet. In fact I do it more when I experiment and think that some fast carb form might be needed for something like post workout recovery, etc.

But I used to crave sweets a lot in the past and looked also to every alternative I could. Turns out that even if the alternatives might be healthier or not so detrimental, this means you still might haven't heal the hypothalamus and probably are not leptin sensitive enough already, or whataver. Being able to subsist on protein and fat taste being satiated I found it out to be the most important clue that I might be regaining my 'ancestral self'.

Just some thoughs! Of course it depends on what you pursue, I could concede that life must be also joyful and having some sweet every now and then could be OK, we got a favouring for sweet tastes for something, just I'd stick to the most natural ones.

### LRichard12 — 2013-06-24T12:57:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/artificial-sweetners.7429/post-88798>

I appreciate all of the comments concerning alt sweeteners. I do understand the idea behind getting away from ones sweet tooth and not teasing oneself with sweet tasting things (except natural sweetness).

In the morning I have a mix of cocoa powder (unsweetened), Chorella, cocoa nibs, unsweetened rhubarb, unsweetened descanted coconut, coconut oil, and codliver oil. The only 'sweetner' I put into it is FOS; only for the gut benifits. I appreciate the bitter taste and don't really need to sweeten it.

The only reason I am asking about Erythritol is because we have a girl on our team (I'm a missionary serving oversees) from Austria who loves to bake. After politely turning down some baked goods informing her that I am avoiding sugar, she made me a sugar free cake (with refined white flour). So I bought some organic coconut flour, coconut oil, and Erythritol and gave it to her to experiment with low carb baking. The result has been some amazing brownies, coconut cake, etc. She makes it all without eggs or any other additives apart from coconut, coconut oil, coconut milk, unsweetened cocoa powder, and Erythritol. Amazing stuff!

So what do you think? Am I hurting myself by eating an Organic coconut brownie sweetened with pure Erythritol (no additives)?

Until you finish the Leptin Reset - the answer is YES, you're stalling your progress. every time you eat something like that, you actually start back at zero. its also quite possible your weight gain might be related to these cheats vs eating strict protein and fat, w/only limited green veggies for carbs.

FWIW it took me 3 months to kick sugar to the curb. I did use liquid stevia. eventually I got to the point the stevia is too sweet ( I actually dislike the fact that stevia is in my BHRT sublingual Pg drops and I'm contemplating just going with the straight meds minus the stevia). my biggest sweet deliverer was my coffee. now I put D-ribose in my coffee and other than 85-95% dark chocolate - that's the only sweet I eat. I bought local organic strawberries and I.DON'T.WANT.THEM. which is so bizzar...

### Renfrew — 2013-07-06T23:45:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/artificial-sweetners.7429/post-89621>

**Erythritoil is benign**

Erythritol is save.
I have been using it for 2 years now and has been in use in Japan and Germany for some years. As far as I can tell, there are no side effects, my blood sugar has not been affected.

[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Erythritol](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Erythritol)

I don't see why it can't be used (in moderate quantities) or why it should hinder any leptin reset.

### Anna — 2013-07-15T12:22:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/artificial-sweetners.7429/post-90134>

Stevia is 100% pure from the plant - important rule of thumb one must always remember, eat a food from it's closest nature source as possible. Perhaps also you might like dates - a fab idea is to blend up around 200gms of dates with about a tablespoon of coconut oil, roll into balls and refridgerate  But everyone's comments here are correct, that as Leptin becomes active, our tastebuds change so our old desires disappear, no will power ever needed.

### Hope — 2013-06-21T17:46:41+0300

[http://www.cnn.com/2013/06/21/showbiz/jackson-death-trial/index.html?hpt=hp\_t1](http://www.cnn.com/2013/06/21/showbiz/jackson-death-trial/index.html?hpt=hp_t1)

### Jack Kruse — 2013-06-21T18:19:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/michael-jackson-60-days-without-rem-sleep.7430/post-88588>

I wrote this a yr ago........no surprise to me.[http://www.jackkruse.com/brain-gut-9-what-really-killed-michael-jackson/](http://www.jackkruse.com/brain-gut-9-what-really-killed-michael-jackson/)

### Hope — 2013-06-21T19:22:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/michael-jackson-60-days-without-rem-sleep.7430/post-88593>

Yes, it's one of your first blogs I ever read. I especially love these paragraphs:

You might see the way Michael Jackson died in a different light now. Was he a creative genius and talented to the max? Sure he was. But why did his career peak in 1984 and fall off a cliff? Thriller was when he was at his apex of ability. Ironically it was also when the human brain fully matures and myelinates using ketosis biochemistry in the brain. Yes, safe starch folks, the brain myelinates via ketosis not using the Krebs cycle. Once again, Dr. Cunnane’s data comes back to teach you something paleo has not. **He spent his entire young life in a studio surrounded by man made EMF’s that altered his blood brain barrier and increased his brain’s sensitivity to glucose that also easily changed his gut flora. His primordial environment on this planet was a sea of manmade EMF’s from electrified music and performances.** In 25 years he went from supremely talented to total destruction. 25 years. Now think about when we electrified the planet and began to use EMF and think about when neolithic disease began. Yes, their is a fly in the ointment. EMF’s destroy your gut flora quickly.

I believe this is when Mr. Jackson’s altered pathologic gut flora caught up to his brain and destroyed it over the last 25 years of his life. As result of this mismatch, he found he could not sleep at all, and it he had to rely on powerful anesthetic drugs to put him down…….so powerful they killed him. Was it irresponsible medicine that killed him? Sure that played a role, but the question no seems to asks what was the pre existing state that got Mr. Jackson to that point? How did he get to a point where he needed a drug like Diprivan and Ketamine to sleep? I think this blog opens that rabbit hole for you to think about now. Think about his life, the stressors, his family dynamic, the vitiligo, the veganism, the bizarre behavior and his strange choices from childhood to adulthood. Where they a pop star’s eccentric coincidences or **modern epigenetics of a bad gut flora being simplified to slowly destroy his brain and his ability to sleep?**

Read my last cite and see if things are not adding up. In my opinion, he was the poster boy of what a bad gut could do to wonderful brain. It is the giant circle of evolutionary life in my opinion. His rise was meteoric and his de-evolution was just as dramatic. No one sees the evolutionary biology in his story, but I do. It is a brain gut story to be sure and why it make this series now.

~ thanks once again for this great post. I so appreciate the explanation and direct ties to him....it really does make sense, what happened to him, and it's not because he was some crazy, weird person...like some people describe him which I hate - they just don't get it and are eager to judge. I bet Elvis Presley had something VERY similar nearly 30 years before Michael...and even Whitney Houston and many others...ugh.

# Thread: Migraines and Magnesium
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/migraines-and-magnesium.7432/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hope — 2013-06-21T21:43:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/migraines-and-magnesium.7432/post-88613>

Thanks to the Hormone 102, I think I have figured out why, when I used to get migraines (and thankfully I now rarely do) monthly, coinciding with the start of a cycle, my then-local MD's infusions of magnesium worked (and drugs like Advil or Imitrex did nothing) to relieve the pain....along with drinking lots of water and the fact that the IV itself came in a lot of water. I wish that MD would have tested my hormones, DHEA, IL-6...back then. But oh well. It all makes so much sense now.....

I really hope some day, all MDs will be required to train in the kinds of things Dr Kruse is teaching us all here. Although I guess the Pharma companies won't allow it because they would lose so much business. Sigh....

Here is the quote:

These mechanisms are mediated by the hormone DHEA which is one of the major antioxident in the brain and correlates directly with effective sleep by lowering IL-6 levels. Higher IL-6 levels are seen in people with altered adrenal stress index testing and those with altered melatonin cycles. When melatonin cycles are altered it creates an imbalance in Magnesium-calcium optimization inside the cells of the brain. The result is **dehydration** and a change in the density of water in our CSF. This is very similar to what we see in pseudotumor cerebri. **It also may help you understand what causes a hangover headache and why re-hydration seems to help.** When the Mag-Ca balance is off it tells the astute clinician that melatonin and serotonin balance is also off in the brain gut axis. All the quantum physiology in this post has a common tie to the neural immune and endocrine systems and many of the diseases that still perplex modern healthcare.

### Shijin13 — 2013-06-21T22:21:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/migraines-and-magnesium.7432/post-88619>

I've found the same thing Hope... chocolate cravings are an indication for me that I'm low on Mg. I tanked up at the beach last week... and I felt great... but 1 day after returning home, I wasn't getting enough Mag to keep me from getting a migraine... then throw in the artificial light & the stress I came back too that is why I had 3 migraines this week.

I'm much more relaxed today... and just rolling with it...drinking lots of water, got some afternoon sun (not as much as I'd like) I'll be spending the evening outside having dinner and celebrating the Solstice... no complaints when you let nature nurture you....

> I'm much more relaxed today... and just rolling with it...drinking lots of water, got some afternoon sun (not as much as I'd like) I'll be spending the evening outside having dinner and celebrating the Solstice... no complaints when you let nature nurture you....

Hey Gretchen - Thanks for the tip about chocolate cravings and low magnesium!
I've been addicted to 'mocha's' lately (quite literally) - I just can't seem to get enough of them even though it is a migraine risk with the chocolate AND caffeine.
But I did not know that the craving could indicate low magnesium.
I know it is really hard to get magnesium levels up. I know that CT in the ocean can really help but it is not always practical.

I have lost so many work days due to migraines the last few years. I'm lucky to be in a union that protects my job but still - you want to be worth something to your employer.

I have a frustrating dilemma: My headaches are caused almost exclusively from the stimulant medication that I take for - so called - "ADHD". For me it works as a great antidepressant.
So I have a choice - migraines or depression.
Anyway, thanks for the tip - I'm going to make magnesium a priority.

### Penny — 2015-04-20T23:26:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/migraines-and-magnesium.7432/post-161842>

I would make the magnesium "magnesium malate" and get rid of aluminum at the same time... to this repertoire I would also add sublingual hydroxy b12, molybdenum @1000 mg/day in 3 divided doses and sublingual pantathene - in a high EMF environment, calcium is effluxing thus letting in any glutamates you might be eating - this includes meat that is cooked - the high EMF turns into blue light which acts like a carb, which uses 28 molecules of mag to break down one molecule of sugar (or carb or blue light which is what a carb turns into) - meat also uses up zinc/activated b6 (P5P) thus less is left to make BH4 which is required for neurotransmitters - not only that, but whatever crappy gut flora you have is generating lots of ammonia which then uses up BH4 to process the ammonia - so meat+bugs=less BH4=less dopamine/serotonin -

The molybdenum helps process the ammonia also - I'm also a huge fan of Life Extension's Super K formula -

My sister in-law can stop a migraine in 2 minutes using magnesium and b12, for what it's worth...

So, lower the toxic load of the bugs by reducing your EMF which feeds the bugs - take probiotics to balance your gut flora - I would make the protein count by making it all seafood - naturally I would use lots of coconut oil and bone broths -

Getting rid of the bugs is a huge redox-sucker IMHO - and zinc lozengers are fantastic for you if you're not eating/digesting lots of oysters...

Penny, I just wanted to thank you sincerely for this post. My wife has been struggling with debilitating migraines the last 10 years and I have been trying my best to figure out what was happening to her, to no avail, until I tried your suggestions!

Our life went from borderline hell (8month breasfeeding child, no sleep, migraines... ugh), to a happy place in a few days. THANK YOU.

The migraines are now way less frequent/powerful and we seem to be able to control them with heavy doses of magnesium malata and magnesium bicarbonate + some of the supplements you mentioned -

p5p (1 a day)
25mg zinc gluconate (1 a day)
2-4pills 1500mg mag malate per day
magnesuim bicarboate mixed in throughout the day,.
cod liver oil, 2Ts day
home made sourkraut all week
lowering extra glutamate foods (soy sauce, dried shitake, fish sauce,etc)
CT soon.

I've also started implementing Kruse's ideas as heavily as is doable. We currently have a 8month son, my wife is breastfeeding, and we live in central Los Angeles. 5G/wifi signals are everywhere.

**I'm wondering if you had a minute to recommend any other steps she could take?**

I am ordering hydroxy b12, pantethine and molybdenum now.

Little background:
My wife has struggled with poor health the last 10 years, been stuck in a blue light room designing in front of a computer. She developed psoriasis,
debilitating migraines (she would take exedrin for years). She HATES cold water and has never been able to participate in sports due to scoliosis. She was also stuffed with grains and dairy as a kid and developed ear infections and was pumped full of antibiotics throughout her childhood. Joy.

Now she eats a home made paleo diet (4+) years - evolving to epi-paleo the last 2months, and her health is improving heavily. But the migraines have persisted, so here I am, looking for the next step beyond the food... I've switched all the lightbulbs to red incadencent, installed Iris on the computers and we got our Uvex glasses. Oysters were found fresh at Lax-C downtown LA... And we're considering moving closer to the ocean if we can afford it.

My wife is off Irish decent, red hair, fair skin with frekles. Her redox is without a doubt, probably quite poor... but improving.

Thanks in advance for any help,

Gabriel + Amanda
LA,

Migraines are typically triggered by constriction of blood vessels to the brain.
Please stop all subscenes which have this effect including: cheese, all molds (including the air you breathe)
I'm sure your wife is beautiful. I see you've made good food improvements.

# Thread: FAA may relax rules for using devices below 10K ft
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/faa-may-relax-rules-for-using-devices-below-10k-ft.7435/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 8

### Hope — 2013-06-21T22:01:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/faa-may-relax-rules-for-using-devices-below-10k-ft.7435/post-88616>

[http://www.techhive.com/article/204...oking-to-relax-in-flight-electronics-use.html](http://www.techhive.com/article/2042669/the-faas-finally-looking-to-relax-in-flight-electronics-use.html)

They say:

The basic argument against using your smartphone during landing and takeoff is that all those devices whizzing and whirring in the cabin could interfere with the airplaneâ€™s navigation systems. But that rationale was shown to be false in a late 2011 article by Nick Bilton at The New York Times . Bilton visited EMT Labs, an independent testing facility, to see what kind of interference our gadgets were putting out that could help bring down a plane. The result? Nada.

....yeah, but they should also check the impact all that stuff has on the really important players..us!! Going backwards......going....gone....

### Shijin13 — 2013-06-21T22:26:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/faa-may-relax-rules-for-using-devices-below-10k-ft.7435/post-88620>

Hope - so many people use these devices anyway during take off... once the flight attendant sits down - look around people pull them out and start playing on their iphone/ipad/kindlefire etc.... its been happening for the past 10 years...

the horse is out of the barn, its a bit late to close the barn doors - so to speak. The question - how do you mitigate your risk when you fly? the answer may be choosing not to fly... or moving to where you want to spend the majority of your time... etc... or you may push the mitochondrial rx hard to make recovery from the situation easier..

### Hope — 2013-06-21T22:32:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/faa-may-relax-rules-for-using-devices-below-10k-ft.7435/post-88621>

Yeah, I know, i hate it all....what can you do. I really fly to Europe bc it's the best way. Would LOVE to live there......but no such luck for now. Otherwise, not much need to fly longer trips...

I just think it's so silly...it's all about the flight, not the people....

### Shijin13 — 2013-06-21T22:35:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/faa-may-relax-rules-for-using-devices-below-10k-ft.7435/post-88623>

### Hope — 2013-06-21T23:27:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/faa-may-relax-rules-for-using-devices-below-10k-ft.7435/post-88625>

Ha, that would be a dream....though I don't know if there are any epipaleo friendly cruises?? Not sure I could bring all my food and keep it in a tiny cabin. LOL. But I have thought about it. It is, after all, how it all used to be....

### lioness7 — 2013-06-22T00:20:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/faa-may-relax-rules-for-using-devices-below-10k-ft.7435/post-88627>

Not quite sure what their fuss is all about. I travelled to Europe recently and read in one of those on board magazines that most flights will have wifi soon but you have to pay for it. So, my question is .....does it really have that much of an impact on the planes's navigations system?

A bit off topic...Being a Canadian that has travelled to the States many times, less frequently lately as at our airport, there is a separate U.S. check.......and man, do they ever make you feel like a criminal. I wonder often how good the border patrolling is or if it is just intimidation. Upon arriving home once, I discovered five .22 bullets in my CARRY ON, (DH used it to go hunting a while before) and this was after an x ray and hand search..no one noticed going there or coming back. I almost fainted thinking of the potential repercussions. Yet at the same time, we both were called back by border patrol when some genius noticed that my passport last name had a space and his did not....haha, worry about the space whilst I walk on board with bullets you did not detect despite your searches......makes me laugh hilariously!!!!!!! I doubt wifi interference is an issue. They just had to figure out a system from which they can profit like they have recently charging extra for isle and window seats. But then again, I know zero about airplane navigation. Just wondering......

### Hope — 2013-06-22T01:33:34+0300

As to the border patrol...hm.....that is so weird. I have had beets taken away, sliced and in a glass, fermented. I was upset for sure. And you 'got through' with that. Perhaps they thought it was something else? Who knows.

### caroline — 2013-06-22T03:18:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/faa-may-relax-rules-for-using-devices-below-10k-ft.7435/post-88636>

# Thread: Thinking differently

### nonchalant — 2013-06-22T18:19:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thinking-differently.7440/post-88666>

Thanks, Jack, for giving us the background to start understanding our health. I was just reading this LEF article about retinal tears, and how it is important to get to an eye doctor asap.

> "Every eye has a clear gel, called the vitreous, that fills and gives shape to the back part of the eyeball. As people hit their 50s and 60s, the vitreous becomes less like Jell-O and more like sticky fluid, Bhisitkul said. Sometimes, the changing fluid moves and sticks to the eye, dragging pieces of the retina with it.
>
> When the sticky fluid tugs on the eye tissue, the retina fires off a flurry of electrical signals to the brain. Those signals are interpreted as light, causing the flashes. The floaters are bits of debris and red blood cells that slip into the liquefying vitreous when the retina peels off the back of the eye. If the gel contracts or folds, people describe seeing curtains or the appearance of objects."
>
>
> "It's not considered a hereditary condition, and there's no genetic test that can predict who is at risk for retinal tears. Unlike some other age-related eye diseases, vitamins and supplements won't stop the vitreous from changing with age.
>
> As Khurana said: "Awareness is the main thing." "

[http://www.lef.org/news/LefDailyNews.htm?NewsID=19512](http://www.lef.org/news/LefDailyNews.htm?NewsID=19512)

Made me want to scream aloud "DEHYDRATION!!"

It's all so complex, but it's all so simple. The biology/physics is so complex, with our multiple-redundant systems, and the possible diseases are bewildering, but our needs are quite simple.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-05-26T22:57:46+0300

You could listen to the Q & A. **THIS IS WHY MEMBERSHIP MATTERS:** There must be a way to reverse every disease since living things are not designed to by nature to be filled with illness as its default state. It is the job of doctors to solve the riddle and when they fail you it is your job to take up where they left off. My job is to support that continuing journey for the curious among you. But you have to have skin in that game. There is a difference between interest and committment. Come join me at my website for a journey of a lifetime of discovery.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-05-26T23:03:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thinking-differently.7440/post-214791>

You have no defects in your cells..........your environment contains the defects. Your choices are linked to the environment. Your choices are linked to the amount of dopamine you create in your eye and brain from the environment you're in right now. Your mitochondria makes that water you all seem to misunderstand.........this is where the dehydration comes from. If you cannot see or sense what is bothering you it is a sign to a "mitochondriac" that your dopamine levels might be low. I have many blogs on that topic for free. Reality 12 is the latest. We are designed to work in the environment we are best attuned too. That environment is now rare in the modern world because of human decisions. This means once you learn how your mitochondria are tuned you'll know where to go to reverse your issues. Are you committed to change yet? Anyone can dabble in change, but once you've made that commitment, it's very hard to stop you. A committed person never gives up, despite all the obstacles they face, they keep walking towards their destiny not allowing even the slightest discomfort to distract them. That's the difference between interest and commitment. When you're interested in doing something, you do it only when circumstance permits, but when you're committed to something, you accept no excuses, only results. Commitment is the reason why some people succeed and others wonder if they could ever. Personal life commitments are centered around three F's. Be Fixed. Be Focused. Be Faithful. Charisma may get you in the front door, but it's the commitment that closes the back door! The first person we have to commit to, is ourselves. A committed mind never falters under a load. Commitment is the reason you might be living your life a little different in order to carry out your vision. People say "how do you find the time?" Time is always there; there was 24 hours in the day for DaVinci just like there is for us all. You don't find the time, you find the commitment to get things done. Today, begin.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-05-26T23:04:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thinking-differently.7440/post-214792>

Evolution focuses in on those who have skin in the game and so do I. Guess where I learned that lesson? Mother Nature. Many dabble in this forum because they are mearly interested. If you are going to dabble and not want direction you best understand the limitations to your approach. The trick is to identify and remember your beliefs or assumptions whenever a new idea is building upon the new data. Your “scientific house of beliefs” should be plumb with the foundations things are built upon. My members get schooled in that all the time when we speak. Ask one of them. I don't let my own family off the hook.

It is a wise thought every now and then to put a question mark on the beliefs we have long taken for granted. We should always question the things we most believe in.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-05-26T23:05:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thinking-differently.7440/post-214793>

Poor thinking is a function of low dopamine. If you are headed in the wrong direction what good is your speed of arrival? We are the CEO of our life and we must act like it to set the course to the destination we want. **The lack of direction, not lack of time, is mankind's modern problem.** What is called progress by culture today is now harmful in ways we have yet to perceive. That is why frequency of light matters to a "mitochondriac."

### Jack Kruse — 2017-05-27T00:48:04+0300

The truth is hidden in plain sight.........and our desires blind us to it. Those desires are built in your dopamine bank account.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-05-27T00:52:57+0300

The world is changing but we are all on the transition team………It is the mark of an educated mind to be able to entertain a thought without accepting the terms of the thought. (As a an absolute truth)

### JanSz — 2017-05-27T01:24:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thinking-differently.7440/post-214813>

Thank you for saying this.
I hope people will stop drinking water by the gallons.
That likely hurts kidneys and other stuff.

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=214791)
>
> You have no defects in your cells..........your environment contains the defects. Your choices are linked to the environment. Your choices are linked to the amount of dopamine you create in your eye and brain from the environment you're in right now. Your mitochondria makes that water you all seem to misunderstand.........**this is where the dehydration comes from.** If you cannot see or sense what is bothering you it is a sign to a "mitochondriac" that your dopamine levels might be low. I have many blogs on that topic for free. Reality 12 is the latest. We are designed to work in the environment we are best attuned too. That environment is now rare in the modern world because of human decisions. This means once you learn how your mitochondria are tuned you'll know where to go to reverse your issues. Are you committed to change yet? Anyone can dabble in change, but once you've made that commitment, it's very hard to stop you. A committed person never gives up, despite all the obstacles they face, they keep walking towards their destiny not allowing even the slightest discomfort to distract them. That's the difference between interest and commitment. When you're interested in doing something, you do it only when circumstance permits, but when you're committed to something, you accept no excuses, only results. Commitment is the reason why some people succeed and others wonder if they could ever. Personal life commitments are centered around three F's. Be Fixed. Be Focused. Be Faithful. Charisma may get you in the front door, but it's the commitment that closes the back door! The first person we have to commit to, is ourselves. A committed mind never falters under a load. Commitment is the reason you might be living your life a little different in order to carry out your vision. People say "how do you find the time?" Time is always there; there was 24 hours in the day for DaVinci just like there is for us all. You don't find the time, you find the commitment to get things done. Today, begin.

# Thread: How much is balance a part of achieving optimal fitness?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-is-balance-a-part-of-achieving-optimal-fitness.7441/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dextery — 2013-06-22T23:59:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-is-balance-a-part-of-achieving-optimal-fitness.7441/post-88682>

**How much is balance a part of achieving optimal athletic abilities?**

[http://www.mymadmethods.com/articles/mobility-articles/1269-balance-is-power](http://www.mymadmethods.com/articles/mobility-articles/1269-balance-is-power)

For the recreational athelete as well as elite athletes...we have some on this forum...this balance training seems to accelerate the abilities of everyone in only 8 weeks. He says runners run faster!! Now who would not want that?

It seems like it is more like brain training rather than muscle training. It seems elite athletes emphasize muscle training to excel but what would happen if brain exercise training were incorporated into the mix?

Something more for Barry, Ben, Meghank, BigPapaChakra777 & newcomer erixsparhawk to consider in pushing the brain & body to the max in endurance competition.

Be sure to scroll down to the video demonstration.

Anyone ready for a trip to Park City, Utah?

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-06-23T01:03:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-is-balance-a-part-of-achieving-optimal-fitness.7441/post-88685>

I'd take a trip down there with some forum goers if anyone was willing (after I save a bit of money). Anywhere, actually, lol. It's interesting you posted this, I believe Tim Ferriss just posted this exact article on facebook, I just skimmed it but didn't read it. I definitely want to incorporate more things like this into my fitness/athletic regiment, I really only do BBS inspired resistance training with some tweaks and foundation training for flexibility and possible height increasing effects. Thanks for posting this, now I have to actually go
and read through it. If you haven't already, look into Z-Health, it's another aspect I'd like to engage myself in, it's more about training the neruomuscular pathways for proper movement and utilization of all muscle fibers and it has promising results.

### colt — 2013-06-23T02:27:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-is-balance-a-part-of-achieving-optimal-fitness.7441/post-88690>

### Dextery — 2013-06-23T03:00:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-is-balance-a-part-of-achieving-optimal-fitness.7441/post-88695>

The developer of the tightrope system said

"After 6 to 8 weeks of training, the athlete will see great improvements in their sport. By then, we feel the balance system is activated and becomes like riding a bike, they just need to do it once a week after that."

Scroll down to see the video demonstration.

### colt — 2013-06-23T03:09:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-is-balance-a-part-of-achieving-optimal-fitness.7441/post-88697>

\*\*\*I am not convinced that innate abilities other than healthy and normal
nervous and musculoskeletal systems play a major role in the ability to
balance. The ability to balance and carry out any demanding motor skills
depend heavily on learning via optimal practice and mental attitude towards
the task. Some of the experienced gymnasts whom we tested were exceptional
in being able to assume balance rapidly, even with very narrow foot spacing
and with visual cues removed. Those who were not gymnasts but who had done
a great deal of ball balancing were far inferior to gymnasts and martial
artists in these tests. Some sedentary students were also superior to ball
balancers, but in their case their ability not to become anxious, muscularly
tense and apprehensive about the tests seemed to play some role in their
motor abilities in these tests.

There is no simple answer to your question about ability to rapidly manage
stabilisation, since each individual seems to rely on different strategies
and since one's childhood and youthful experiences play a significant role in
imparting balancing capabilities. Some of the most interesting research in
sports science is now taking place in motor control, but not too much of the
complex information emerging from some of the fascinating work there is
filtering down to the general world of sports training. Consequently, far
too many personally held beliefs and commercialised balancing systems and
schemes continue to dominate the world of fitness and rehabilitation.

So far, the research which is available seems to show that the balancing and
stabilisation abilities are highly sport specific, so that wobble boards and
physio balls do not appear to offer the competitive athlete any noticeable
advantage over balancing ability developed within the sport itself or in
other sports involving similar types of stabilisation. Maybe others have
come across some well-researched proof (and not anecdotal comments) that
balls and boards do offer some training advantages beyond basic early
rehabilitation. If so, please share it with us.

Dr Mel C Siff
Denver, USA

From a different site

Adequate has come to mean maximal to me over the years. If you first look at the response side of the equation,more than at the stimulus side,the only way to have a maximal response there is to have a maximal stimulus here. Maximal response means maximal recovery from the stimulus too. As soon as the stimulus becomes sub max in any of its parameters,then it emerges the need to fill the gap with timing of the next stim,recovery means,tempo,and similar.
Volume then can only be dictated by the ability of the system to engage in and dis-engage from maximal input/output situations.

Velocity!!

### colt — 2013-06-23T03:23:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-is-balance-a-part-of-achieving-optimal-fitness.7441/post-88699>

Want to improve on a marathon or tri? Get faster!!! Speed reserve

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-06-23T04:09:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-is-balance-a-part-of-achieving-optimal-fitness.7441/post-88705>

Mel C Siff is incredible, people should check out his books (Super Training, I believe is the title). That being said, you have to look at everything. People should look at Z-Health, I think the nervous system is just as important as the muscles themselves. In fact, look up Elliott Hulse or Strength Camp on youtube - he is a professional strongman and basically a philosopher - he does tons of work on his nervous system, grounding, bio-energetic releases, etc. and after incorporating his alternative approach, he is now stronger at a lighter body weight and he used to do steroids.... basically he got drastically stronger. Really I'm just saying that this style of balance training may be a great \*addition\* to other forms of training - especially for what I do - Jiu-Jitsu, balance and coordination along with patience and fundamental skill trump any strength, endurance, or maximal power/speed output. There's some powerlifter dude who started training with us shortly before I had to cancel my membership for a couple months (until this upcoming month) and he consistently went against our Master who probably weighed 30-40lbs less and doesn't lift weights or run - all he does is practice the sport, stretch, and do some almost yogic style activities and he dominates everyone in the gym (even has an undefeated amateur MMA record). I'd rather be coordinated, flexible, balanced, and have the reflexes of some crazy ninja cat than be super strong/fast, and this is coming from someone who still kind of has a sick addiction to building muscle/body building.

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-06-23T04:11:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-is-balance-a-part-of-achieving-optimal-fitness.7441/post-88706>

And I think this style of 'Balance Training' may be different. It looks like the vibration plate of balance balls and what not, lo;l

### Dextery — 2013-06-23T04:18:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-is-balance-a-part-of-achieving-optimal-fitness.7441/post-88707>

BigPapa...I like your integrative approach...thinking outside the box from what everyone else is pursuing. Be strong but train like a cat.

### colt — 2013-06-23T04:21:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-is-balance-a-part-of-achieving-optimal-fitness.7441/post-88708>

nfrared light penetrates below the surface of skin to speed up healing time. Red light has the ability to **stimulate the production of ATP**, an important source of energy in body tissues. This allows ATP to promote rapid action by the bodyâ€™s defenses. ATP helps to increase blood circulation, which reduces swelling and inflammation. Increased blood circulation can benefit those with high blood pressure because good circulation allows the heart to do less work. Red light activated ATP stimulates white blood cells that work to repair damaged tissues, and increases collagen production that builds elasticity in the skin and can aid in wound healing. ATP also activates endorphins that provide soothing relief from chronic and acute pain.

Because RED light therapy triggers the bodyâ€™s own defenses, it actually treats the sources of pain and doesnâ€™t just mask the symptoms. RED light therapy provides relief from pain and discomfort without the side effects of pharmaceutical drugs or surgery.

Would I recommend it? Yes

The benefits I have noticed are difficult to describe since I am doing other protocols also. .... One easy to associate benefit is doing red light after lifting weights results in my muscles feeling more relaxed.

Phototherapy is important on this website, and I consider red light one of my sources of phototherapy. ... I always feel better after relaxing for 20 minutes in the RL bed.

Dr. Kruse has stated that red light helps decalcify the pineal gland .... Just got my first salivary melatonin test results back .... I clearly need to continue decalcifying my pineal.

I believe some sun spots on my face are slowly diminishing and my facial skin appears to be softening.

In summary, I consider red light therapy one part of my ongoing mission for optimal health.

# Thread: How to begin for everyone:
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-begin-for-everyone.7445/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2013-06-23T15:36:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-begin-for-everyone.7445/post-88735>

Beginning always gives hope for the future. It is the appropriate setting for momentum to start. Since I have transformed my own thinking I have come to realize "the beginning" of things in life always must happen today. Renewing myself in health came about because I became willing to be a "beginner" every single morning. I now believe as long as I am breathing, in my eyes, I am just beginning. We are socialized to believe that in life we should ultimately pursue the conclusions of things and not the beginnings. I now think differently. Although no one can go back and make a brand new start, anyone can start from now in their present and make a brand new ending. They can create any new reality they see fit. I realized in my transformation that I had to reject this "social Occam's razor', and seek the start of everything first to reclaim my self awareness. To me, the end is in the beginning of all things and lies far ahead of what is to come. Today, begin to raze the old using curiosity and begin to raise the new reality in your life.

### Don — 2013-06-23T16:05:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-begin-for-everyone.7445/post-88739>

Love it… "It is what it is. Where do we go from here?"

### Jack Kruse — 2013-06-23T16:29:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-begin-for-everyone.7445/post-88743>

It is what it is and it aint what it aint..........

### caroline — 2013-06-24T01:21:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-begin-for-everyone.7445/post-88769>

I am beginning to realise that I have never had fences or boundaries - at least for myself. I grew up virtually on my own without resources - so - if I wanted something or needed something or wanted to be somewhere ....I had to think outside the box to accomplish whatever it was. The human spirit is an amazing thing .... altho I was constantly ridiculed and abused I never really took it personally ......... I just simply knew intuitively that it wasn't who I was ......everyday is a new beginning....full of wonderment and all kinds of possibilities.

# Thread: Thyroid Function,  Dr. David Brownstein,  & Iodine Deficiency......Video

### Dextery — 2013-06-23T21:45:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-function-dr-david-brownstein-iodine-deficiency-video.7448/post-88757>

I subscribe by email to Newsmax and a couple of days ago, I got a solicitation to watch a Dr Brownstein video regarding thyroid function and how everything works. Signs & symptoms of thyroid disfunction. You might see a few symptoms in yourself that you may have not been aware of.

[http://w3.newsmax.com/newsletters/brownstein/thyroid\_video/video3\_fb1.cfm?promo\_code=1328D-1](http://w3.newsmax.com/newsletters/brownstein/thyroid_video/video3_fb1.cfm?promo_code=1328D-1)

There is a section on how proper thyroid function is dependent on Iodine. This video has good information but just know that it is a solicitation to purchase a year's subscription to his own monthly newsletter, Natural Way to Health, plus another subscription to another newsletter, Health Radar, put out by Newsmax that runs for 3 months and if you do not want to continue that portion, cancel it before the end of three months.

With the Newsletter subscription you get his book, _Overcoming Thyroid Disorders_, you get his book, Iodine, _Why You Need It, Why You Don't._ You also get a DVD video entitled Iodine Deficiency and another video that I believe is a longer version of the first video you get. Either $54 or $59 digital or mailed to you monthly.

The solicitation video is worth the 1/2 hour even if you do not subscribe.

Combine these books and videos with the book written by Lynne Farrow _The Iodine Crisis_ [http://lynnefarrow.net/](http://lynnefarrow.net/) and with Dr. Kruse's book _Epi-paleo Rx: The Prescription for Disease Reversal and Optimal Health_ [http://amzn.to/11XaA9t](http://amzn.to/11XaA9t) you will know more about your health than does your primary care physician.

I generally don't post an advertisement solicitation, but I thought the 30 minute video might be useful for some on the forum.

# Thread: Study Links EMF to Unborn Baby Being Obese Later In Life
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/study-links-emf-to-unborn-baby-being-obese-later-in-life.7449/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kall1 — 2013-06-24T01:42:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/study-links-emf-to-unborn-baby-being-obese-later-in-life.7449/post-88770>

A Prospective Study of In-utero Exposure to Magnetic Fields and the Risk of Childhood Obesity

De-Kun Li, Jeannette R. Ferber, Roxana Odouli & Charles P. Quesenberry Jr
Published 27 July 2012

We conducted a prospective study to examine whether in-utero exposure to magnetic fields (MFs) increases the risk of childhood obesity. Participating women carried a meter measuring MF levels during pregnancy and 733 of their children were followed up to 13 years to collect clinically recorded information on growth patterns with 33 weight measurements per child on average.

[http://www.nature.com/srep/2012/120727/srep00540/full/srep00540.html](http://www.nature.com/srep/2012/120727/srep00540/full/srep00540.html)

# Thread: Artificial Light Kills Fish - A Study Reveals
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/artificial-light-kills-fish-a-study-reveals.7450/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kall1 — 2013-06-24T04:26:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/artificial-light-kills-fish-a-study-reveals.7450/post-88777>

Interesting if you have a chance to travel to Alaska & see a salmon hatchery. The salmon eggs kept in large trays are stored in rooms kept completely dark - in fact artificial light is not allow. The only light allowed is a small amount from a hand held flash light. When hatcheries first started using incubation methods to hatch salmon eggs - the rooms were lit with fluorescent lights. Only after figuring out the lights were the cause of fish being killed did they switch to dark rooms. Makes you wonder what artificial light does to humans?!?.....

**Scientific Study**

[http://www.tandfonline.com/doi/abs/...51:SEOALO\]2.0.CO;2?journalCode=utaf20#preview](http://www.tandfonline.com/doi/abs/10.1577/1548-8659%281957%2987%5B151%3ASEOALO%5D2.0.CO%3B2?journalCode=utaf20#preview)

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/documentary-about-the-god-particle-amazing.7452/post-88794>

If you have a chance check out the documentary "**Particle Fever**".

Imagine being able to watch as Edison turned on the first light bulb, or as Franklin received his first jolt of electricity.

For the first time, a film gives audiences a front row seat to our generation's most significant and inspiring scientific breakthrough as it happens. Particle Fever follows six brilliant scientists during the launch of the Large Hadron Collider, marking the start-up of the biggest and most expensive experiment in the history of the planet, pushing the edge of human innovation.

As they seek to unravel the mysteries of the universe, 10,000 scientists from over 100 countries joined forces in pursuit of a single goal: to recreate conditions that existed just moments after the Big Bang and find the Higgs boson, potentially explaining the origin of all matter.

### LRichard12 — 2013-06-24T13:11:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bromocriptine.7454/post-88799>

Anybody have thoughts on the use of bromocriptine (as recommended by Lyle McDonald) for helping in the Leptin reset (at least with symptoms). I am experiencing extreme debilitating hunger, rebound weight gain, low metabolic rate, low libido, and low total T3 after serious weight loss (100lbs) on a low calorie Atkins style diet. I am doing the Leptin rx (faithfully following the protocol), and have effectively gained from 150 to 170 (now 20lbs)!!!!! Which I'm told is normal. I've upped my fats (in the form of coconut oil mainly), eating a ketogenic form of paleo. I'm a bit frustrated with the weight gain but understand it as my body's reaction to starving myself for a year. I am intrigued reading Lyle McDonald and his explanation of cyclical dieting to avoid the very symptoms that I am now experiencing.

Don't get me wrong I'm not jumping off of the Leptin Rx bandwagon and jumping onto Lyle McDonald's. I am just curious as to your opinions on his approach, especially as it relates to Bromocriptine.

### Shijin13 — 2013-06-24T15:22:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bromocriptine.7454/post-88800>

Jack recommends Cycloset (band name form of bromacriptine) to help you reset your circadian cycles, and support increased dopamine and cortisol upon waking. However this isn't something everyone should be taking. you need to work with a doctor, and see what your ASI looks like. Additionally, before starting cycloset, you should have been focusing on strict light/seasonal cycle management.

Finally, in order to gain the correct effects from cycloset you must use the Brand name - to get the proper dosing of .8mg (which you increase upto 4.8mg based upon FBG, nausea and aversion to eating breakfast that is not HIGH FAT) the generic - bromacriptine does not come in .8mg and does not address resetting circadian rhythms

Richard - give the LR time to work, and apply all the circadian cycle management as well.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bromocriptine.7454/post-88819>

Richard

pay attention to Light cycles - if its dark out - you shouldn't be using artificial light - if you do use a red light.

you should be waking with sun rise, and going to bed after the sunset.

seasonal circadian cycles - refers to eating foods that are "in Season" for your local region. eg. in VA local strawberries are now in season and you'll find them at the farmers market - I only buy berries locally - I don't buy california/florida berries - and I only buy them when they're producing locally. Same goes for raspberries - wild raspberries aren't ripe yet, so I don't buy them. I can pick wild blackberries/mulberries - so if I can find local I'll buy them. you won't find these fruits beyond August in VA - so I don't buy them after Aug and before the beginning of June, b/c they're not growing/producing at that point

make sense?

# Thread: Better sense of smell
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-sense-of-smell.7456/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nonchalant — 2013-06-24T17:16:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-sense-of-smell.7456/post-88807>

I've been noticing some improvement in my sense of smell. Food is becoming more vivid, and as a result, I'm becoming even more picky about what I eat. I never realized that coffee, as well as tea, had such flowery undertones. It's not exactly subtle. And I've been noticing the smell of my natural gas cookstove. It smells almost as strongly as a propane stove. I've been avoiding the unlit fumes lately. And if I come home in the evening, I can still smell the bacon I cooked that morning.

Perhaps I should try wine again, maybe I can appreciate it more now.
Or maybe I shouldn't!

### Shijin13 — 2013-06-24T18:15:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-sense-of-smell.7456/post-88818>

Interesting... In all this time I've never lost my sense of smell. my sniffer is very keen. almost overly sensitive. I've always been able to tell if some one has issues by their smell - I'm also extremely sensitive to perfume/cologn. I hate it and it usually overwhelms me to the point it causes a migraine. its quite interesting...

and yes, good coffee and tea have wonderful notes you can smell before tasting, and smell as you taste. same with good wine. even coconut oil and ghee have notes as well...

### Adria — 2013-07-02T16:44:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-sense-of-smell.7456/post-89377>

Pros and cons of this I know oh too well! Smelling everything and everyone, good and bad. Just like our dogs says my DH.  Especially can't stand the body smells of certain coworkers. And their lunches, prepacked microwave meals smell like chemicals. Holding my breath sharing the elevator.

What a different life we lead...

Your blood lipids look great. Hopefully others will be along to talk about your APO A and APO B ration and what all that means.

You appear to be hypothyroid. with RT3 of 14.3ng/dl and T3 83 ng/dl Your ratio is 5.8. 83/14.5 This ratio needs to be 10 or above

stopthethyroidmadness site is one of the best for thyroid problems. Your T3 is barely above the ref range. To get to a ratio of 10, you need to increase T3 to 143 to be at 10 or some combination of lowering rt3 and raising t3.

I am no expert and neither is my primary care physician. It has been this group and the members that have shed some light for me on thyroid function.

[http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/reverse-t3/](http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/reverse-t3/)
[http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/rt3-ratio/](http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/rt3-ratio/)

### Shijin13 — 2013-06-24T23:03:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/got-some-of-my-labs-can-someone-help-me-understand.7462/post-88838>

LDL, HDL, & Trigs look great! & it looks like you've got the big fluffly LDL not the small LDL that causes artery blockage based upon your sdLDL-C # and %.

HsCRP is good at <0.3

Looking at your thyroid - welcome to the world of hypothryoidism...

your TSH is high - indicating your pitutary is telling your thyroid you need T4 -> T3

What T4 you make is actually converting to RT3 (T4-> RT3)

RT3 is binding to receptor sites blocking any T3 that is made from being activated.

So add this story above with your Cholesterol - means you LDL isn't properly being converted to Pregnenolone, for 2 reasons - Lack of Vitamin A (read QB10 again) and T3.

LDL + VITAMIN A +T3 = Pregnenelone

See chart below

you're APOE 3/4 which is neutral - read more about APOE here: [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apolipoprotein\_E](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apolipoprotein_E)

you really need to tighten the cicadian cycles - as a former shift worker its hard work getting everything back to where its supposed to be...

You have a high EPA

eat selenium and iodine, see what that does.

if
if
your cortisolAM is high
you may use 1-2 grains of natural thyroid hormone

\---------------

pregnenolone
progesterone

SHBG
cortisol (7:30AM), (11:30AM), (3PM)
DHEA
DHEAs
Hgb

plus work on Circadian Rhythm (sleep at night, active at dat time)

### Shijin13 — 2013-06-25T00:32:01+0300

John you do have elevated quantities of your sdLDL-C - however when you look at the % its below range. all this means is that its floating around longer in your blood stream and your HDL isn't picking it up as quickly as it should, and its not being converted to pregnenelone. Remember LDL + VITAMIN A + T3 = pregnenelone.

Pregnenelone -> to all other hormones, with the end being Vitamin D3 (25 hydroxide)

HDL is your LDL recycler & you've got a lot of it. but b/c of pregnenelone steal - and your body's demand for more LDL to make the hormones you're not making b/c of pregnenelone steal, your HDL can't keep up w/what you're body's making to support making hormones - so the excess is just sitting around longer, and your HDL isn't vacuuming it up as fast as it should - you basically need a new filter/bag (think your liver) . throw in shift work, and your VITAMIN A clock inside your brain doesn't know what's going on... which makes it worse....

make sense?

### nonchalant — 2013-06-25T03:02:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/got-some-of-my-labs-can-someone-help-me-understand.7462/post-88864>

JanSz, thanks for the tip about selenium. I didn't realize selenium was important for the thyroid. It seems to be needed everywhere. I'll increase my brazil nut eating, and buy more selenium tablets.

It's one thing to be told to consume selenium (iodine cofactor), and it's another to be told why. I just researched it and see that supplementing with iodine but not selenium can lead to a "heavy thyroid" which sounds something like what I've been dealing with, as my iodine use increases. Guess I can't leave out the selenium, while I've been paring down all the supplements.

I had been matching my selenium intake to my seafood intake, but now I need to match it to my Lugol's intake as well. And perhaps I need to reread Brownstein's book.

### RickD — 2013-06-25T03:29:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/got-some-of-my-labs-can-someone-help-me-understand.7462/post-88867>

Fire,

With your Thyroid check your temperature 3x during the day and average. I suggest that because I have a TSH at 4 but good T3 and T4 numbers and my average daily temperature is right at 98.6F. With those other values my ND suggested I ignore the TSH.

Next your Testosterone is on the low end. That should be addressed before it falls further. Check Total and Free T along with E2(Estradiol) and SHBG on the sex hormone side and get an ASI on the adrenal side to determine a course of action.

2/day-->Super Selenium Complex, 200 mcg 100 capsules (lef.org)
good read up selenium/iodine(selenium 400mg/day max---->
[http://www.greenwillowtree.com/Page.bok?file=selenium.iodine.html](http://www.greenwillowtree.com/Page.bok?file=selenium.iodine.html)
[http://www.greenwillowtree.com/Page.bok?file=selenium.iodine.html](http://www.greenwillowtree.com/Page.bok?file=selenium.iodine.html)
"

> **\[COLOR=""#FF0000""\]Selenium\[/COLOR\]** is also essential for the production of estrogen sulfotranserfase which is the enzyme which breaks down estrogen.
> A deficiency of selenium can thus lead to **\[COLOR=""#FF0000""\]excessive amounts of estrogen\[/COLOR\]**,
> which may depress thyroid function, and also upset the **\[COLOR=""#FF0000""\]progesterone-estrogen balance\[/COLOR\]**.

"

1 dropperfull/day=48mg/day
Lugol's Iodine Family Pack -- 6 (2 fl. oz.) bottles
[http://www.amazon.com/Lugols-Iodine-Family-Pack-bottles/dp/B001RRP3FW](http://www.amazon.com/Lugols-Iodine-Family-Pack-bottles/dp/B001RRP3FW)
recalculate dose if using other iodine/iodide source.

### Albert83BCN — 2013-06-25T08:15:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/got-some-of-my-labs-can-someone-help-me-understand.7462/post-88881>

> [Dextery said:](/goto/post?id=88873)
>
> You want your morning temperature to be between 97.6 & 98.2 at morning temperature taken before getting up. Lower than 97.6 equals hypothyroid. [http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SzCryUF050U](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SzCryUF050U) Dr Tennant. this is 3/5. Watch the entire series from one to five if you would like. Good info.

This is perfect timing for me to throw some questions, please let me chime in

How would you intereprete the following scenario? When I woke up I have low body temp usually near 96.8F, but right after having the BAB it can go quickly into the normal 97.8-98.1 range. Then on some other times during the day it might stay normal but by the time I go to bed it can be as low as 96.2! I eat three times per day trying to respect circadian rythms, I feel the nighly low temp is more affected when I eat with plenty of time before going to bed (so worse if I eat at 7:30 than let's say 8, 8:30 or 9PM) and the more VLC I'm, despite being in a normo-caloric HFLC diet AFAIK. Epi-paleo template.

My numbers on the last blood check where like this:

Triiodothyronine ( T3 ) 1.14 ng/mL \[ 0.80 - 2.00 \]
Free Triiodothyronine ( FT3 ) 2.27 pg/mL \[ 2.60 - 4.40 \]
Thyroxine ( T4 ) 8.07 μg/dL \[ 5.10 - 14.10 \]
Thyroxine Free ( FT4 ) 1.31 ng/dL \[ 0.70 - 1.70 \]

TSH (thyrotropin) 3.28 μUI/ml \[ 0.25 - 4.50 \] -> 10 months ago this was 3.91, think I'm doing better.

So the only one that seems off would be the final T3, I don't have reverse T3 but I have to undestand it must be high. I really think that this is all due to high cortisol and VLC, now that I've cut back my training schedule and paid a lot of attention to the light cycles I feel like I'm doing better but that's also easy to feel now that we are in summer, I guess if we were still in winter I'd feel so cold like back then as my body temps has not rised a little bit in the last 3-4 months, even when I'm trying CT and being more mindful, working out less and having plenty of sunlight...

I bet if I were to have higher carb intake I could easily get through this but I'd like to find a workaround so I can stay LC on a regular basis, from what Jack has make clear in the blog series it must be possible for sure but I cannot seem to fully reap the benefits from it, even when I upped seafood a lot and even have some brazil nuts for selenium, drinking mineral water low in fluoride and chlorides... etc etc.

# Thread: Good morning from the end of the world (almost)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/good-morning-from-the-end-of-the-world-almost.7468/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lahelada — 2013-06-25T16:06:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/good-morning-from-the-end-of-the-world-almost.7468/post-88891>

Well, good morning from Argentina. I am glad I have found this forum. Even though I have not been able to read a lot here yet, I already know that I am going to like it here.

So how did I end up here. Physically through google. The contemplation on CT though was started by a musing on our boat as to why I can stand cold temps in just a long sleeved T shirt when others get out the cold weather gear. The conversation made me realise that I have always loved cold temperatures. Even when I was a child much to the chagrin of my grandmother who always swaddled me in layers, something I deeply appreciated as it was easy to secretly take off and put back on just before going home. Same grandmother though who made me sleep under a big eiderdown in an unheated room. It only happened once but one morning I had ice roses on the inside of my window.

To the present: I put myself to researching the subject, thinking that I might eke out a slight acceleration to my pitifully slow weight loss. Apart from all the reading I was reminded of some family history, where a family member cured himself from the left side paralysis caused by a stroke. The doctors had told him that it was for life. He cured himself by doing Kneipp treatments with no additional meds,only cold water immersion treatments in mountain river water. Sebastian Kneipp was ac a priest who had recognised the healing powers of cold water back in the 1800s.Lore has it that he cured himself from TB. The English sources relegate this man to quaint history but his work is still recognised today in Germany. There his cures are accepted as a valid treatment. Medical insurances pay for them and you can guess that they are very picky as indeed they are in other parts of the world.

So what have I been doing. In the beginning nothing much. I took the dog to the park for 10 minutes at a time in summer clothes. The looks ....
When we went sailing with temps around 15 C or 60F I only wore 2 long sleeved T shirts with wind cheater at the ready, if I felt the cold which yesterday I did. But all in all I loved being slightly cold. It is invigorating to me and actually puts me in a good mood. I prefer dry cold though. As I am already very cold adapted without even trying I have the hope that my beige/ brown fat still exists and is only dormant as I am living in a majority hot climate with fat layers to insulate me on top of that "mismatch". These cold air baths have been my routine since June 19, so very recent indeed.

Yesterday I went a step further. I came home from shopping with heavy bags and carrying anything produces me a pain in the neck/shoulder region. It has gotten better with the aid of magnesium, but has not gone away. So when I got in I had my usual shower and thought now is as good a time as any and if the pain goes so much the better. I had fully expected to do a dance when the cold water hit me, but no. It was slightly unpleasant, but yes again I loved it after a while. I was still chicken though and only stayed in for three minutes.The neck pain has not gone away, but it has dulled it to an acceptable level. I don't know what temperature it was but the water comes from an outside tank with pipes exposed to the outside so if it was ambient temp I would think around 65. I will buy a thermometer in the near future to test it.

My stats are 49 years old, currently weigh 190 lbs or 86.5 kilos. I started at 224 back in November with non ketogenic Atkins ( my invention ) and changed to the normal Atkins around the middle of March. Since then I only lost 10 lbs .
I am happy with ketogenic. I think I am quite close to paleo, but need to do more reading to know all that is involved.

So here I am.

### nonchalant — 2013-06-25T17:31:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/good-morning-from-the-end-of-the-world-almost.7468/post-88897>

Hi Lahelada, glad to have you with us! Sounds like you have a head start on CT.
Do you eat much seafood?

### Lahelada — 2013-06-25T18:13:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/good-morning-from-the-end-of-the-world-almost.7468/post-88899>

Thank you for the kind welcome !

Nonchalant : Seafood err, makes me run a mile in the opposite direction. Fish I can tolerate well disguised in a strong flavoured sauce or before low carb with loads and loads of batter. Oily fish like sardines or salmon no chance. I can even spot the slightly fishy smell in avocadoes and ground linseed . So completely anti fishoil which is not a good thing I believe in terms of what we would like to achieve.

### nonchalant — 2013-06-25T19:16:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/good-morning-from-the-end-of-the-world-almost.7468/post-88901>

Hmm. Maybe you could try eating more fish (strongly disguised) and after a few weeks you may start changing your mind? I used to eat seafood once or twice a month, but now I love it. It really is important, for the DHA and iodine and selenium, zinc, etc.

### Lahelada — 2013-06-26T00:57:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/good-morning-from-the-end-of-the-world-almost.7468/post-88923>

Thank you for the ideas non chalant. I really do not have much hope in that regard. I can see myself increasing my fish intake, but seafood makes me ill just from the smell alone I am thinking nuts and seeds like chia could help with the minerals you mention but I am new to this ,so don't know what are the implications of that. I have a lot of reading to do here.

### caroline — 2013-06-26T02:56:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/good-morning-from-the-end-of-the-world-almost.7468/post-88930>

Welcome .... and cheers from down under!

### Lahelada — 2013-06-26T16:18:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/good-morning-from-the-end-of-the-world-almost.7468/post-88976>

Thank you for the welcome Caroline. Looks like we are hemisperial neighbours.

# Thread: Water help.

### MonteD — 2013-06-25T19:32:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-help.7470/post-88903>

Do any of you here who are drinking ultra purified water (reverse osmosis/distiller) bother with remineralizing your water?

I know Dr. k has said that distiller was not optimal. Was this because of the lack of minerals and thus lessened conductivity?

I've read that RO water only removes 10% of fluoride ions. So is spring water better? I've also read the naturally occurring fluoride in some spring water is no well absorbed and definitely not the same as that which is added to municipal water supplies... Is this true?

### nonchalant — 2013-06-25T22:20:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-help.7470/post-88911>

Hi Monte. Yes, I put sea salt into my water, because I drink a LOT of water, and I'll get strong salt cravings otherwise. I get spring water straight out of the ground. I think it does have some naturally-occurring fluoride in it, but I think natural fluoride is better than the industrial waste fluoride they add to our water supply here.

I am running low on the spring water, because of a change in plans last weekend. I've started drinking some RO water from the store. I used to like it, but now I find it sorely lacking. Can't wait to get more real water!

### Jonathin — 2013-06-25T23:31:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-help.7470/post-88916>

> [montediaz@live.com said:](/goto/post?id=88903)
>
> Do any of you here who are drinking ultra purified water (reverse osmosis/distiller) bother with remineralizing your water?
>
> I know Dr. k has said that distiller was not optimal. Was this because of the lack of minerals and thus lessened conductivity?
>
> I've read that RO water only removes 10% of fluoride ions. So is spring water better? I've also read the naturally occurring fluoride in some spring water is no well absorbed and definitely not the same as that which is added to municipal water supplies... Is this true?

Typically RO membranes removes about 95% of most minerals including fluoride in water. Check the specs from membrane manufacturers.

Personally speaking, I do add salt to morning water (at least a qt.) and add small amount (1/4 tsp.) of low sodium ocean water extract to most of the water I drink. Some of the magnesium oils or flakes could be added in very small amounts instead possibly.

I have also been tracking the conductivity of my urine with a calibrated meter in an attempt to gauge how much water to drink in my high and dry location.

### Grizz — 2013-06-26T00:51:33+0300

Our family drinks distilled water and we do not add minerals. We get all the minerals we need from FOOD. The salads we eat every day provides all the minerals we need. IMO, it is truly a waste of money to add minerals to distilled water.

### JanSz — 2013-06-26T02:43:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-help.7470/post-88929>

On this board, when discussing water, we should break this discussion into two topics.
#1, is that water clean and suitable to drink
#2, when water is clean and acceptable for drinking by general population,
we want to know about that water's other qualities.
Is it structured or is it mostly bulk.

These days when I think of water I give less weight to mineral or other contents,
and more weight to question about its structured or its bulk water qualities.

When I have seen claims that spring or mineral waters have more structured content because they were recently exposed to Schumann resonances while under ground,
that was easier to buy.

When I hear that RO water is also good, I am confused.
If that RO water was made from bulk water, recently exposed to man made EMF's,
it should be worth less or should be avoided.
Fact that it is chemical free belongs to different discussion.

\-------------------------------

While at it, it may be a worthwhile to resurrect discussion about juicing.
This time I am thinking of juices from green vegetables, possibly watermelon as a source of good water.

Note,
there must be a tremendous difference between
freshly squeezed juce and
store bought "100% juice" reconstituted from powder and tap water.

...

..

### JanSz — 2013-06-26T19:46:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-help.7470/post-88994>

> [CindyB said:](/goto/post?id=88983)
>
> The ultimate in vicinal or structured water has got to be properly prepared bone broth or agar agar jelly. Agar agar can turn water into a gel with a 1% by weight amount of agar agar powder. 99% by weight water held "solid" by 1% by weight of polyelectrolyte polymer chains of anhydro-galactose and galactose. The water aligns itself along the length of the galactose chains so well that the solution becomes a gel when cooled. Bone broth prepared with cartilaginous bits of animal joints, tendons, hooves and feet will turn into a gel you can pick up when cooled. **By definition, a gel\* is structured water.** The largest quantity of proteins drawn off from the animal bits are: glycine, proline, hydroxyproline, glutamic acid and alanine. Their protein chemical structure is such that the water dipoles will align with the acid end (COO-) of the protein molecule. Gylcine's small size allows it to wiggle around as the fluid cools to enable alignment of structured micelles until they merge into full volume gels.
> Seaweeds other than the gelidium genus will create gels, too, but gelidium is the most prolific producer of the agar agar component.
>
> Agar agar can be bought in the Asian foods section of your local grocery store. Bones can be had easily. There are many great recipes for making bone broth but all have a small amount of vinegar added to aid in extracting the gelling components. Don't add too much vinegar or the broth won't gel.
>
> \* actually, other fluids can become gels with the right gelling agent... for our human consumption purposes, we'll just speak of water as the gelling fluid.

> **By definition, a gel\* is structured water.**

I do not know, but if above statement is true, if true, one of the biggest hurdles is over.

And so far it looks like RO water would be the best choice for home made gels.

Our daily life would be much simpler when we would get good definition of what is what.
After that, there is likely no real need for Schumann generators and other ways to structure our waters.

Structured water is not bianary. It is dynamic and takes on multiple forms.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-07-03T01:46:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-help.7470/post-89412>

> [CindyB said:](/goto/post?id=89395)
>
> From Gerald Pollack's "The Fourth Phase of Water" pg. 67
>
> "In a different vein, the planar model helps us to reconcile an issue that chemists have not resolved: why gels retain so much water. Gels hold their water. Remember that common gels don't leak, even when their fractional water content exceeds 99.9 percent of their total mass \[Fig. 1.1\]. Now we can venture an explanation of that phenomenon. The gel matrix comprises numerous hydrophilic strands. The strands' surfaces convert bulk water into EZ \[Exclusion Zone\] water. The EZ planes stick to those nucleating strands and also to one another; hence, your gelatin dessert remains hydrated. The EZ water doesn't dribble out."
>
> For ingesting fully structured water, "eat" gels. As previously stated, you can make gels out of real food (e.g. bone broth and seaweed extracts) for very cheap. The acids in your stomach will dissociate them into their micelles (individual strands of small amino acid or galactose polymer chains which hold their water molecules tight in a bunch of honey-comb layers.) Those hydrated micelles will carry the water across the intestinal lining. Thus you are ingesting structured water.
>
> Of course, you want to use water that does not have, for example, non-iodine halide contaminants or other hormone-mimics to make your gel.
>
> RO vs "distilled" vs spring vs ground vs rain vs tap vs whatever method of obtaining water is a matter of what contaminates are retained in the water you ingest. Outside of a semiconductor manufacturing facility or restrictively controlled research facility, you cannot maintain the purity of water. When you process water into a holding tank, it picks up the contaminates in the tank and lines. When you pour it in your glass, it picks up the contaminates retained from washing and "rinsing" your glass. As it passes your lips, it picks up contaminates from your lips. The polyelectrolyte surfaces of every cell in your body from lip skin to intestinal lining will structure the water that contacts the surface almost immediately and structure it outward from the surface in less than a minute. (ibid, pg 73).
>
> Did you know that ultra-pure water does not conduct electricity? Yep. In semiconductor manufacturing, water purity is monitored by measuring electrical resistance. 18.2 megohm-cm water is the goal.
>
> I wonder if the big value tradition observes with "Jewish penicillin" (chicken broth) is it's ability to re-hydrate an ill person with the vicinal (structured) water carried on the small amino acids extracted from the chicken carcass.
>
> If you're trying to ingest structured water, gels will allow it. The water processing methods are all about what type and how much non-H2O components are included in your "water".

EZ zone water is a where the quantum dance happens.......at these zones in nanotubes this is where protonicity and superconduction and the zero entropy system begin.

### MonteD — 2013-07-03T02:09:41+0300
