### RickD — 2013-07-05T02:34:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-help.7470/post-89509>

> [CindyB said:](/goto/post?id=89395)
>
> From Gerald Pollack's "The Fourth Phase of Water" pg. 67
>
> "In a different vein, the planar model helps us to reconcile an issue that chemists have not resolved: why gels retain so much water. Gels hold their water. Remember that common gels don't leak, even when their fractional water content exceeds 99.9 percent of their total mass \[Fig. 1.1\]. Now we can venture an explanation of that phenomenon. The gel matrix comprises numerous hydrophilic strands. The strands' surfaces convert bulk water into EZ \[Exclusion Zone\] water. The EZ planes stick to those nucleating strands and also to one another; hence, your gelatin dessert remains hydrated. The EZ water doesn't dribble out."
>
> For ingesting fully structured water, "eat" gels. As previously stated, you can make gels out of real food (e.g. bone broth and seaweed extracts) for very cheap. The acids in your stomach will dissociate them into their micelles (individual strands of small amino acid or galactose polymer chains which hold their water molecules tight in a bunch of honey-comb layers.) Those hydrated micelles will carry the water across the intestinal lining. Thus you are ingesting structured water.
>
> Of course, you want to use water that does not have, for example, non-iodine halide contaminants or other hormone-mimics to make your gel.
>
> RO vs "distilled" vs spring vs ground vs rain vs tap vs whatever method of obtaining water is a matter of what contaminates are retained in the water you ingest. Outside of a semiconductor manufacturing facility or restrictively controlled research facility, you cannot maintain the purity of water. When you process water into a holding tank, it picks up the contaminates in the tank and lines. When you pour it in your glass, it picks up the contaminates retained from washing and "rinsing" your glass. As it passes your lips, it picks up contaminates from your lips. The polyelectrolyte surfaces of every cell in your body from lip skin to intestinal lining will structure the water that contacts the surface almost immediately and structure it outward from the surface in less than a minute. (ibid, pg 73).
>
> Did you know that ultra-pure water does not conduct electricity? Yep. In semiconductor manufacturing, water purity is monitored by measuring electrical resistance. 18.2 megohm-cm water is the goal.
>
> I wonder if the big value tradition observes with "Jewish penicillin" (chicken broth) is it's ability to re-hydrate an ill person with the vicinal (structured) water carried on the small amino acids extracted from the chicken carcass.
>
> If you're trying to ingest structured water, gels will allow it. The water processing methods are all about what type and how much non-H2O components are included in your "water".

Cindy this comment intrigued me so I bought some Knox gelatin and made a quart of it last night leaving it in the refrigerator overnight. Today after a hot bike ride I cooled down eating my bowl of water with a spoon. Not sure if it'll help refill my nanotubes, but it was very refreshing.

### Grizz — 2013-07-22T21:06:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-help.7470/post-90582>

**Reverse Osmosis Filters have SERIOUS problems !**

> Reverse osmosis water treatments are guzzlers when it comes to water. Household reverse osmosis units use a **lot** of water because they have low back pressure. As a result, they recover only 5 to 15 percent of the water entering the system. **The remainder is discharged as waste water.** Because waste water carries with it the rejected contaminants, methods to recover this water are not practical for household systems. Wastewater is typically connected to the house drains **and will add to the load on the household septic system**. An RO unit delivering 5 gallons of treated water per day may discharge anywhere between 20 and 90 gallons of waste water per day. So as you can see, unless your home needs an RO system you are spending a lot for water going into the sewer or flooding your septic field.
> [http://www.septic-problems-fixed.co...s-a-lot-of-waste-any-ideas-on-how-to-stop-it/](http://www.septic-problems-fixed.com/have-a-watts-reverse-osmosis-and-there-is-a-lot-of-waste-any-ideas-on-how-to-stop-it/)

An extra 90 gallons a day sent into a septic leach field can cause serious problems. If you have to pay for water usage, be prepared for a big jump in your water bill.

This is why we distill our water.

Grizz

### Jonathin — 2013-07-22T22:54:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-help.7470/post-90590>

The usual home RO system fills a small pressure tank. Due to the back pressure caused by the pressure tank the unit becomes less efficient than necessary. Without back pressure RO membranes make 1 gal for every 3-4 gal of waste water. This may sound like a waste but so is using electricity to boil water to make pure water. My domestic water costs me less than .015 per gal from the water company. That is less than .10 to make a gallon of RO water with no exposure to high current emf and magnetic fields. If you want to get the most efficiency from your kitchen RO leave the little faucet open and collect water into containers (like gallon jars) as it is produced. Shut off the water supply (probably under the counter) to the unit when you have stockpiled enough water. Your filters will last longer, too.

### cinnamon — 2013-07-31T22:07:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-help.7470/post-91282>

> [MeghanK said:](/goto/post?id=91277)
>
> @cinnamon... how did you make green tea gelatin?
>
> @ ALL...do you think gelatin "snacks" are just as beneficial as bone broth? how much bone broth do you drink a day? How many gelatins would = a good amount per day to get in your diet?

Hi MeghanK,

The green tea gelatin is really easy. You can buy envelopes of unflavoured gelatin at the grocery store (mine is Knox brand, but there might be others). Each envelope can be mixed with 2 cups of liquid to make a gelatin.

-   Step 1 is sprinkle the crystals in the package over a quarter cup of water.
-   Step 2 is to add a quarter cup of boiling water and to stir well until the crystals are dissolved.
-   Step 3 is to add a cup and a half of whatever liquid you wish and chill for two or three hours.

I used spring water for Steps 1 and 2. For Step 3, I used green tea made with spring water (described below). Mine was cold and had steeped a long time - I think that might be better than adding hot tea.

To make green tea, I put spring water in the kettle and heat to almost boiling (green tea is bitter if the water is too hot). Add about half a cup of the near-boiling water to the teapot with the green tea bag and dip the bag in and out for about 45 to 60 seconds or so. Pour out this water as it has most of the caffeine. Fill the teapot from the kettle and let steep a second time for a long time. I leave the bag in and drink the tea over the next day or two - the first cup is usually hot, but the rest are room temp.

I can't answer your other question about gelatin vs bone broth and how much might be good. I don't know. I have only just started experimenting with the gelatin as a way to add structure to water.

RO is not structured......but it becomes structured below 1.4 nm in carbon nanotubes........and I have not talked about the phase transitions of water in tight spaces. There are always things to know..........
\==============================

> [brookway17 said:](/goto/post?id=210106)
>
> Thanks for getting my head straight on this. I am in the process of setting up a reverse osmosis System and was concerned about the destructuring that occurs. It is interesting that nature invented carbon nanotubes and not man.
> Planning to upgrade to silver soon , still trying to get up to speed

destructuring
Putting water thru reverse osmosis membrane does not cause change phase, EZ water into liquid water.
It just filters it. Making it devoid of bad (and good) additions.

steam
liquid
ice
EZ water

It is good idea to keep remembering that liquid water,
is able
to change phase ONLY when it is constrained to a space of less than 1.4nm

that
applies to any (liquid) water, RO, spring water, tap water

On a ice ring, between ice and ice scatting blade,
if I am not mistaken, there is EZ water effect, three phases are at work
ice-water-EZ

To constrain only at minimum, one surface is required.
No carbon nanotube is required to create EZ water, but nanotube makes the space more defined.
IIRC, flow (without obvious ΔP ) can be observed in nanotubes
blood flow in very small veins, smaller than red blood cell, is not due to pressure from heart but rather similar to EZ water flow thru nanotubes
\================================
any change of phase happens with a cost
latent heat
The quantity of heat absorbed or released by a substance undergoing a change of state, such as ice changing to water or water to steam, at constant temperature and pressure. Also called _heat of transformation_.
\================================

**I have not seen discussion about latent heat in regard of EZ water**

\=================

# Thread: Carbon DiOxide - Why It Matters & How It Effects Your Health
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carbon-dioxide-why-it-matters-how-it-effects-your-health.7478/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kall1 — 2013-06-26T05:49:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carbon-dioxide-why-it-matters-how-it-effects-your-health.7478/post-88954>

All too often when your Doctor pulls a Chem Panel they over look the CO2 levels if it's within normal limits. Just to dig into the science behind CO2 is rather amazing.

Having worked in the medical field for sometime now I've seen first hand the primary cause of death in a hospital. What? over ventilating someone in the ICU. The patients O2 sats are down so geee let's give them pure O2 via an ET tube at 10 L/m on a ventilator that pumps the air for them. Which is the wrong thing to do. Russians use pure CO2 for patients with low O2 sats. A friend of mine who's a Doctor in Germany will put a plastic bag over a patients head to raise their CO2 levels for 20 -30 mins, instant boost in overall health.

In fact Submarines pump CO2 into the environment at 20 x's the normal levels. But CO2 is the cause of green house gasses that lead to global warming right? Oh? Then how come Green Houses & nurseries pump high levels of CO2 into their crops? ummmm....... shoots the whole global warming out the window:

[http://www.infowars.com/co2-myth-busted-why-we-need-more-carbon-dioxide-to-grow-food-and-forests/](http://www.infowars.com/co2-myth-busted-why-we-need-more-carbon-dioxide-to-grow-food-and-forests/)

All living creatures know how to regulate their own CO2 levels - frogs burrow into the mud to keep levels higher in times of stress. Mice know to burrow into a tunnel to build up their levels to maintain health. Queen Bees live 47 x's longer than other bees - why? Her levels are 5 x's higher than outside the hive. It's estimated our current CO2 on Earth are hundreds of times LOWER than it was during Cave Man era. Another reason why we see so much disease than we did 20 years ago. Less CO2 = disease, low metabolism and so on.

CO2 affects hormones - why? Amines.... Makes measuring hormone levels nearly impossible. What's what? Not sure what that means?

Do you know where and how "TONIC" water got it's name? It'll floor you how this was started as a medical treatment. Simple Sparkling Water given to Pt's with TSA or Strokes - are not suffering the horrible downsides you would normally see. that was discovered in the later 1800's! Doctors are keeping it from patients.

Most patients post op in Germany are given doses of CO2 - brain health is greatly improved & less infections and more.......

What's missing in 100% of US HBOT centers? CO2 ! In Europe they inject CO2 into HBOT tubes - huge difference in the cures seen from this simple treatment.

I use a plastic bag over my head when I'm CT'ing to really boost the healing effects of both

Here's Dr Peat, PhD to explain it better than anyone I know.

CO2 & Your Health:

\[video=youtube;AZkGxrntmTE\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AZkGxrntmTE&feature=player\_embedded\[/video\]

Peat says chronic saturation of cells by CO2 or bicarbonate increases the number of mitochondria, increases the ratio of NADH to NAD, puts the tissues into a more chemically reduced environment, increases bone density, forces tissues to rehydrate, and increases thyroid and liver function. ### Shijin13 — 2013-06-26T17:16:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-help-dhea-or-melatonin.7480/post-88982>

> [Trey625 said:](/goto/post?id=88981)
>
> What are the most important labs to be done? Hes going to docs next week and i want to send him with a list of labs to show his doctor otherwise he will prob just draw a CBC and BMP

at minimum
Vitamin D (25 Hydroxy)
HsCRP
DHEA-S

If you can get the following (Check with Insurance to find out what labs they'll cover)
Berkley, NMR or VAP LIpid panel
Fasting blood glucose Test # 001818, CPT Code 82947
CBC Test # 005009, CPT Code 85025
Comp Metabolic Panel Test # 322000, CPT code 80053
Thyroid:
TSH, Test # 004259, CPT Code 84443
Free T4, Test # 001974, CPT code 84439
Free T3, Test # 010389, CTP Code 84481
RT3, Test # 070104, CPT 84482
Total T4 (Dzugan requested) Test # 001149 CPT Code 84436
Total T3 (Dzugan requested) Test # 002188, CPT code 84480
Progestrone, Test # 004312, CPT Code 84144
Estradiol, Sensitive, Test # 140244, CPT Code 82670
Total Estrogens Test# 004549, CPT Code 82672
SHGB Test #082016 CPT Code: 84270
Testosterone Free Direct, & serum with Total Testosterone: Test # 140103, CPT Codes 84402, 84403
Prolactin Test # 004465, CPT code 84146
cortisol Test # 004051, CPT Code 82533
DHEA-S Test # 004020, CPT Code 82627
IGF-1 Test #010636, CPT Code 84305
RBC magnesium Test # 080283, CPT Code 83735
Vitamin D25 Hyrdoxy Test # 081950, CPT code 82306
Serotonin Test# 120204, CPT Code 84260 (requires frozen specimens)
Pregnenolone Test# 140707, CPT Code 84140 (requires Frozen Specimens)

# Thread: My first time with ice on chest and cold water - Yikes
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-first-time-with-ice-on-chest-and-cold-water-yikes.7485/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### fireforlunch — 2013-06-26T23:24:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-first-time-with-ice-on-chest-and-cold-water-yikes.7485/post-89008>

I worked myself up to 60 minutes with about 25 lbs of ice on my stomach and chest.

According to JK's protocol you can add water to the tub once you get to that point.

Well today was the first time that I did it with water from the coldest setting out of the faucet with the ice on my chest.

O good Lord was it so much harder than just the ice on the chest.

I quit after 25 minutes.

The worst part is that the water wasn't even that cold - 78 degrees.

### nonchalant — 2013-06-27T00:53:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-first-time-with-ice-on-chest-and-cold-water-yikes.7485/post-89018>

Fire, the first time I went into the cold tub, it was in the 70's, and I stayed in 10 minutes. Or maybe it was 7 minutes.
Anyway, the tub is way different from ice packs, or face dunking. That said, it's the only way I go now. Last winter I stayed in cold rooms, so I was shivering on and off all day, but that had nowhere near the impact that CT in a tub has. Nothing else affects my hormones like the cold tub does.

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-27T15:07:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-first-time-with-ice-on-chest-and-cold-water-yikes.7485/post-89050>

> Anyway, the tub is way different from ice packs, or face dunking. That said, it's the only way I go now. Last winter I stayed in cold rooms, so I was shivering on and off all day, but that had nowhere near the impact that CT in a tub has. Nothing else affects my hormones like the cold tub does.

### nonchalant — 2013-06-27T15:40:06+0300

> Thanks for posting.
>
> Do u put ice on your chest and stomach as well as JK suggests while in the water?

Never!!!
Oops, don't tell Jack...

I have iced my back many times, and feet and even my tummy a few times, but never my chest, and not while in the tub.
However, I do get in a tub that is just above freezing... so yeah, perhaps you could say I ice my chest. ;-)
Actually, it is my upper back that objects most strenuously to the cold. I use all the ice I buy to bring down the water temp.

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-27T15:58:41+0300

### nonchalant — 2013-06-27T16:22:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-first-time-with-ice-on-chest-and-cold-water-yikes.7485/post-89057>

I would guess ... about a year? But I stayed at 50-55F for a long time. I took a lot of convincing to try lower temps. Now when I get out of the tub it's always below 50F. And I stay in for at least an hour.

I would have to look at old posts to make sure, but I think it took me a month or two to get down into the 50's. I would get accustomed to a particular temp, then go down a couple degrees, and have to get used to that new temp. Ok, I'll admit it, I was a wimp!

# Thread: Help me Understand "Hot" Thermogensis vs. Cold being better....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-understand-hot-thermogensis-vs-cold-being-better.7486/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MeghanK — 2013-06-27T03:21:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-understand-hot-thermogensis-vs-cold-being-better.7486/post-89024>

This makes sense logically... why is it wrong?

Whatâ€™s the bodyâ€™s defense against icy cold temperatures? Body fat â€“ polyunsaturated body fat at that. At the higher latitudes you see more and more polyunsaturated fat, and higher body fat percentages working its way into the local fauna. Even the amount of body fat in a fish is pretty closely tied to the water temperature. Thatâ€™s why you see omega 3 polyunsaturated fat accumulating in coldwater fish, as well as seals, whales, walruses, and other blubbery cold weather dwellers.

And high levels of polyunsaturated fat lead to a lower metabolic rate. So jumping into the cold lake will cause a huge caloric burn, but do it over and over and the body mounts its own self defense. It reduces metabolism by lowering body temperature. What are the effect of temperature on health? From the same post:

Cold temperatures in winter are not conducive to better health and a higher metabolic rate either - or at least not greater leanness. Winter is fattening and exacerbates most health conditions, whereas the hot temperatures of summer or the tropics tend to favor greater leanness and lessen most health conditions. Most on thyroid meds have to up their dosage in the winter time just to keep hypothyroid symptoms at bay.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-06-27T03:59:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-understand-hot-thermogensis-vs-cold-being-better.7486/post-89028>

you need to really read up.......no wild animals are fatter in winter.......just humans. Epic fail post really.

### Dextery — 2013-06-27T04:31:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-understand-hot-thermogensis-vs-cold-being-better.7486/post-89032>

Cold Thermogenesis series 1 through 13. Index of blogs: [http://www.jackkruse.com/blog-index/](http://www.jackkruse.com/blog-index/)

### freesia — 2013-06-27T04:32:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-understand-hot-thermogensis-vs-cold-being-better.7486/post-89033>

> [MeghanK said:](/goto/post?id=89024)
>
> Cold temperatures in winter are not conducive to better health and a higher metabolic rate either - or at least not greater leanness.

Curious. Is this 'wisdom' based on someone's n-1 experience? Did the person who wrote this get regular cold exposure over a full Winter and measure certain health parameters?
If so, yep sounds like they should avoid CT.
If not, they should grow some really then speak from experience..

### HoneyChild — 2013-06-27T13:32:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-understand-hot-thermogensis-vs-cold-being-better.7486/post-89046>

I remember on TV they said that the ptarmigan is fatter in the winter than in the summer which I thought was odd. But based on further reading I see that this bird actually puts on fat at in summer in preparation for winter. Over the winter months the body fat is gradually used up.

"It is the vernal increase in day length that triggers the weight gain, beginning in August (at the end of the period of continuous light) and peaking in November (at the onset of continuous dark), upon which body mass declines gradually through the winter until March "

[http://www.plosone.org/article/info:doi/10.1371/journal.pone.0015490](http://www.plosone.org/article/info:doi/10.1371/journal.pone.0015490)

# Thread: Let's Do the Time Warp Again
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-do-the-time-warp-again.7488/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TheKid — 2013-06-27T09:25:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-do-the-time-warp-again.7488/post-89045>

Hello all. I've been on forum hiatus working some hacks and focusing on getting well. But I had something interesting to report and wanted to share.

Let me preface this by saying that with Jack I have often found it valuable to listen and then observe. That is to say, I like to take in what he's putting out there, and then see where is manifests in my life or somewhere else. Then I go back and try to piece it all together.

So for those who know me, you know I live in the Middle East and have been darn sick for a good while. Jack has always emphasized that where you live matters a ton, and I know that living where I do is either causing or complicating matters for me. But sometimes it's hard to see the impact of where you live. Symptoms creep up on you, things are hard to pin down.

However in my case, because my place of residence is land of extremes, and maybe because i have been so sick, I have noticed something quite amazing when leaving for a period of time.

Case in point: I recently flew to Germany. Within a day of arriving, I felt like I was literally caught in some quantum time warp. And I mean that quite literally.

You see all of the sudden time began to stretch, to dilate. I am not exaggerating when I say that time seemed to double.

I noticed this, because my brain was still cognitively on Middle East time; and my routine remained the same: I would wake up, use the bathroom, catch up on email and news, dress, go to breakfast, etc. Cognitively, I was aware of how long it takes me to do all that back home. And so in Germany, I would do all that and look at my watch expecting a certain amount of time to have passed. Yet when I looked, very little time would have actually elapsed.

This kept happening over and over and over again. Things that would take an hour back home would take 30 minutes or less in Germany. It was as if suddenly I had all the time in the world.

Now I know the skeptical mind will say that this can all be attributed to me being on vacation and under less stress. I've give that a lot of thought and I don't believe that to be the case.

First of all, i wasn't in vacation. Secondly, I was sick as dog an under a lot of stress (actually the sickness did not improve much at all while there. Only the time shift happened) And lastly, I know what it's like to decompress on vacation. This was nothing like that. The change was just too stark and immediate. And this is not the first time that this has happened. In fact, the last time I was in Germany, something similar took place. And when I returned home, I started feeling sick almost immediately upon entering the airport.

Now the funny thing is that I have never noticed this going back to the USA. Maybe the time zone jump back to the US throws things off. Maybe I was healthier he last time I was back in the USA. I don't know.

All I know is that Germany is like a time warp. And the effect was so profound that I just couldn't ignore that it was happening. The clocks simply refused to run fast. Score one for quantum biology.

### Shijin13 — 2013-06-27T13:47:29+0300

I felt that when I first moved to Guam. and every time I returned to to island life. even when I moved to Okinawa - and was under lots of stress... and travel. Idaho does the same thing for me, I can deal with stress there w/out issue...

I know what you mean Jason...

sounds like part of your brain was noticing the shift in the electromagnetic field from where your live - to when you're in Germany.... cool N=1.... the question is - can you find that same place for you - but in a location that helps your health too....

As I get my head wrapped around the relationships of time, mass, and energy it is all starting to make some sense now.

### Inger — 2013-06-28T08:30:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-do-the-time-warp-again.7488/post-89120>

Very interesting observations indeed.
I do all I can to avoid those places and situation that "runs too fast". It feels like I loose life then. I just do not like it at all. I so much like the slow pace of time. Wow. I sometimes think.. huh I have so much life! So much beautiful life.
Germany is a great country to live in for sure. Still, when I fly from Germany to Scandinavia.. I feel the same you describe above. Time goes even slower. It goes even slower up here. Maybe it goes slower the closer you get to the North Pole? I wonder. Or it has to do with population density and urban areas. The more wilderness the more time you have.

### Trey625 — 2013-06-29T04:41:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-do-the-time-warp-again.7488/post-89165>

I feel the same way..i spend my days couped up in an inner city hospital surrounded by noise,stress,high EMF exposure,Type A people,etc...im reaching a point in my life where it goes against all im becoming and i just wanna run as fast as I can to the country...i want slow, quiet,simplicity!! Recently i curled up and watched old episodes of Andy Griffith and Leave it to Beaver and had such a peaceful feeling inside...made me wish we could turn back time to a simpler lifestyle...when people had more time for family, friends, and moms got to stay home with their babies...in the pursuit of the American dream,i think we lost alot along the way...

### Jack Kruse — 2013-06-29T04:43:09+0300

### Inger — 2013-06-29T08:27:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-do-the-time-warp-again.7488/post-89186>

> [Trey625 said:](/goto/post?id=89165)
>
> I feel the same way..i spend my days couped up in an inner city hospital surrounded by noise,stress,high EMF exposure,Type A people,etc...im reaching a point in my life where it goes against all im becoming and i just wanna run as fast as I can to the country...i want slow, quiet,simplicity!! Recently i curled up and watched old episodes of Andy Griffith and Leave it to Beaver and had such a peaceful feeling inside...made me wish we could turn back time to a simpler lifestyle...when people had more time for family, friends, and moms got to stay home with their babies...in the pursuit of the American dream,i think we lost alot along the way...

That is why I live like I do, even if some things are challenging. The nature and peace is so important to me that I am willing to live in my parents house for a while.... which is not always easy. But I have realized all those issues I can easily overcome when I have the right mindset. And wow, that is liberating! Feels like I get wings, really! Because here I still have the beauty of the nature.. and I suck it in and it gives me so much power! Ok I am maybe not rich in money or stuff or possessions... but that is fun and challenging too. Makes one eat way more wild foods for free.. and they are always better than the cultivated anyways. Life is magic.
I have realized, I NEED peace. I NEED the nature. There is no way around... it feels like they are my birth right.. my life force somehow. Without it I feel not whole. I need it to regenerate. To stay healthy. To stay happy. And I do not care one bit if someone thinks my life is weird..lol. Because I like it.

I need no action, no movies, nothing man made "fun" stuff. I just thought about it the other day.. I have not watched a movie in maybe 2 years! Huh!? I have totally lost interest in such things. They are now only boring to me. I do not even listen to music or radio anymore! If, very seldom. and then I sing too lol. Look, to me it feels those "modern days" have so much challenges and people are suffer so many issues, all around you.. it is so much "noise" and that is enough. The time I am alone I need just, silence. To hear the birds.. the waterfall.. the wind in trees. Those sounds are so healing. It is like they whisper to me, what I have to do, what is important. It is like magic.. like the answers are all around, I just need the silence to get the answers

### Inger — 2013-06-29T08:37:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-do-the-time-warp-again.7488/post-89187>

We was at the sea yesterday with my bro and he's girlfriend and their baby. He is 11 months and still gets almost only mothers milk. I am so proud of her. She stays home with him.. and she plays with him so lovely all the time. he is such a sweet boy! Ok, they eat a not so great diet but they do try to eat a bit healthier. But only the fact that she loves to stay home with him, living a simple life with not much money, tells me she has her priorities right. She has a beautiful heart. It is absolutely beautiful to see her and their son. Her doctor have told her she MUST give the kid other foods when he is 6 months old.. but he did not want and just refused to eat. I told her he will be perfectly fine with only mothers milk at least until he is one yo. She listened to me and now this little guy is the most perfect little being. He's body is just perfect, so smooth and beautiful, not too fat or too thin, just very beautiful. And always good moods.

I am kind of thinking.. we women have so much power. If we only knew. We are able to change the world, really. If we could only 100% enjoy what we truly are. And not fall pray to the pressure and dogmas of our environment.

### prAna303 — 2013-06-29T09:53:29+0300

Thanks to that environment we are suffering from inputs that works with our computer (brain). Today with presentation of media on digital platforms we loose detail and we cannot see it as a result (think about Jacks blogpost the other week).

When we put us in the nature we get bombed with inputs, thru skin, thru eyes, thru ears and every little cell starts to resonate. This is why many feel so good on vacations, see this very much in Sweden today. The boring non information world of 2013 modern tech getÂ´s stupid to us, we cannot get the great amount of stress to our system. When we have minimal information to do the computing the brain will go crazy in doing calculations from nothing.

Again, when we start to attack our system with REAL inputs something getÂ´s to us. We starts to be ALIVE again.

This is how i think we should see training also, in the elements where time and space ends. When your resonate with these elements you start to live motion and stop simulating it.

### caroline — 2013-06-29T10:19:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-do-the-time-warp-again.7488/post-89194>

As usual Inger and Peter ..... you blow me away with your insights and mostly how you are able to articulate them ....... I always feel left at the starting gate.....

### Inger — 2013-06-29T10:28:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-do-the-time-warp-again.7488/post-89195>

Peter I totally get what you are saying! So right. That must be why movies etc. feel so boring. Because Nature is never boring, there is so much variation.. so much excitement.. so many surprises.. oh man!

### prAna303 — 2013-06-29T10:37:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-do-the-time-warp-again.7488/post-89196>

Thanks... A simple test if you would like to do it, either in the brain or in the real world.

Think about music.

If we would like to listen to a recording, not live music. We used to grab a physical plate which were covered in paper with art and text. Slowly put in on a turntable, clean it from dust, lift the needle and put in on the record. You would turn up the volume to a nice level, place yourself in a nice chair with perhaps a nice glass of wine. Slowly close your eyes and take in the room and the space that were recorded. The output of the speakers would make a copy of the room where to tunes were recorded. You start to move to that place and enjoy the complete recording without switching tunes.

Today... Think about the way we get music and how we listen to it. Does it seem real?

In my world analog will always win against digital which always try to compress reality. This reflects movies, music, art and life.

This is why we want "old fashioned" today because we lack the sensing of real things. It would not be this fun if we would go back to wars and stuff like that, instead make your choices today.

We live in a time where it really is up to YOU how you take on this world.

### sjoshua — 2013-07-19T00:33:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-do-the-time-warp-again.7488/post-90348>

>
> Today... Think about the way we get music and how we listen to it. Does it seem real?
>
> In my world analog will always win against digital which always try to compress reality. This reflects movies, music, art and life.

Wow, that's really interesting that you worded it this way. Very well put. Considering everything "natural", if that's the right word, succumbs to quantum physics and is therefore analog (since until something is observed it can be in a broad range of states).

Whereas techology, computers, etc rely heavily on DIGITAL processes (1/0), since they need definite inputs to make definite outputs. This is creating an entire world that is abnormal.

# Thread: Source Field - What The Bleep We Don't Know & Should
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/source-field-what-the-bleep-we-dont-know-should.7492/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kall1 — 2013-06-28T06:03:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/source-field-what-the-bleep-we-dont-know-should.7492/post-89113>

.

This video presentation may step on some toes - where did we come from? How did everything evolve? What is life? Plus - soooo much more....
The following video is an hour long segment of a 16 hour presentation over a 3 day period. Mind blowing research that no one has discussed before.

Then if you really want to blow your socks of on the wonders of water look at the work of Prof. Ignacio Ochoa Pacheco. I'm still in awe.

If that's not mind blowing enough - how about the work of Dr Peter Gariaev. He could transform DNA from say a frog and turn into a lizard using nothing more than a laser.

Did you know your Pineal Gland is actually Retinal Tissue as that find in the eye.... so yes your pineal gland can "see". Photo cascade transduction.
There are crystals in the gland that communicate with the rest of the world. You'll learn about even more about the Pineal Gland. The presentation will discuss the work of Dr Strassman, MD - his work dubbed the Spirit Molecule.

# Thread: Ever decreasing circles...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1206
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lahelada — 2013-06-28T16:29:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-89134>

I named this journal ever decreasing circles as this is what it feels like to delve into the vast knowledge and experience shared here. When I started reading I thought that this forum must be my chemistry professors revenge as I had old him quite clearly that chmistry was something I was doing for school not for life. Yup,adolescents know everything. But things are starting to make sense so that is the ever decreasing part-

I was brought here by a fluke. I realised while sailing that being exposed to cold makes me feel good and started to make use of it by walking around outside in summer clothes. Just recently I have started to include cold showers,two so far to be exact and of very short duration 5-7 ninutes . Of course I had not prepared properly as I started before reading here, so no drinking of icewater or bitter melon. I don't think we have the extract here, but I will scour the chinese stores for the vegetable.

Well, my last shower was yesterday and at night I decided to drink red wine, two glasses. Big mistake. I am still in ketosis but feel as if I have been run over by a truck. There is a chance that it is a flu like thing that is going round, but I do suspect the wine as I also could not sleep very well. Am I more cold adapted than I think.

The biggest hurdle I can see for me is getting enough Omega 3. I cannot do fish except for very mild fish. So I am planning to look for plant sources and supplement with capsules. Chia seeds and avocado spring to mind. I hope that will be good enough.

### Lahelada — 2013-06-29T16:58:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-89202>

Good morning guys ! Thank you for the welcomes and tips.

_fitness@home_ Definitely not a coincidence. I borrowed my Bertrand Russell quote from a blog post by Dr K as it actually convinced me to come on here. I am not keen on scientists, doctors people who think they have nothing more to learn. Thank you for the welcome.

_Caroline_, I always loved learning from other people, other cultures, so great that there are many of us here. I can do white fish, cod, haddock, calamar, also swordfish but abstain from the latter because of their worm infestation. Strange story about the swordfish actually. I learned about it in a splurge dinner at the Inn of the Seventh Ray in California. Of all the delicious food they offered I wanted the fish ! (???) I had always had this aversion to fish since I was a child,so the sudden need was incomprehensible to me. I can also do the little sprats. If I can find a way of battering them locarb they are great as they do not even need to touch the sides .

I have been following a ketogenic diet since March and am well into ketosis, but have not lost much (10lbs in 3 months), hence the search for answers. That search brought up more and interesting questions though. What I learned so far is that the pain between my shoulder blades may have nothing to do with muscle/bone wear and tear. Candida? Cortisol excess? Or something else. I have a recurring inflammation in my ankle,too. I need to find out though why after 3 months of ketogenic diet it is still flaring up though. What is the missing link?

_Inger_, I will abstain from the supplements for now. I did a reconnaissance mission and apart from the price I doubted the quality. I have chia at home so will use up what I have. I crush them so I always know if they are rancid. If they smell of fish they are off which also makes me run a mile. It is actually a serious aversion, not a "just not liking the taste " thing. I once tried to please my neighbour and eat her Xmas salt cod (bacalao) and the overpowering fishy taste made me throw up. No,not at her house. As I had only eaten a spoon I pretended to have forgotten something urgent at home, excused myself in sign language as the offending fish was still sitting in my esophagus and ran home to release the fish into its natural habitat once again . I think she stretched the expensive salt cod with tuna and that this brought on my reaction. So you see it is a long way for me to like fish. I will try though. BTW I saw your before and after pics . Amazing ! You look great.

New things in my life:
\-Supplementing with curcumin (clean all) and paprika(niacin)
\-finally sleeping in a dark room
\-magnesium/D3 supplement for the last 4 weeks
\-cold showers in addition to air cooling ( sailing walking the dog in summer clothes)
\-read up on natto as K2 supplement is not available here, but natto is.
\-doing the 50 g protein at breakfast routine to ascertain leptin resistance.I had toyed with big breakfast several times, but abandoned it for bulletproof coffee as the common idea was that insulin resistant people do not do well with eating in the morning.Reading here I threw that idea overboard.

Today I am battling with some sort of bug going around. Everyone has fever but I don't. What I do have is a slight cough and a dull pain from the waist down. Not feeling like doing anything much except write lengthy journal entries

### caroline — 2013-06-30T04:29:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-89219>

I think that you have a great start with the fish that you can eat! Baby steps ..... do what you can for now and see where it takes you. Do you eat shrimp?
There is a great recipe for seafood chowder/stew in the recipe section - if you can't find it let me know. You can just combine all the above mentioned seafood that you can eat - and chow down!
Don't stress - this is a pretty steep learning curve! It is all about your n=1.......

### Lahelada — 2013-06-30T17:29:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-89243>

Good morning people. First of all thank you for stopping in and encouraging me, Caroline. I am not stressing over fish etc but need to forma battle plan. I will do a no obligation flypast of the WM fish counter today and we shall see. Anything I won't eat the dog will be ecstatic about.

Yesterday was a strange day. I went to bed at 9.30pm with aches and pains in my lower body.I also felt a chill to my bones which is unusual for me. At night around 2am when I woke I was a furnace but no fever at either times.

For you that bedtime might even seem late, but here not even the kids go to bed this early. 9.30pm is dinner time. We eat earlier at around 8pm though. Eating at grandparent's hour it is called here.

Well, I woke at 2am with very uncomfortable pain, so I finally succumbed to the idea of taking an aspirine which allowed me to sleep until 8. Sunrise here, so I started the day with an "express" grounding followed by my BIA which I managed to get in within 35 minutes of waking. That part seemed so easy but is not actually . New routines I guess take a while. It is too large for me now. It might have to do with feeling down because of the strange chest bug.

I did a half CT cold shower during the day yesterday. I am not sure if that was the best idea but it did make me feel better temporarily.

Today the pain is gone. More cough and a bit of nasal congestion though. But it does not stop play which the pains yesterday did.

### caroline — 2013-07-01T03:23:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-89269>

Could it be detox?

And - what about having a seafood dinner at a restaurant you trust? Ask for exactly what you want - develope a relationship with your favorite restaurant...... Hopefully a small family owned place.. They will love your candor and bend over backwards to serve you whatever you need and want! Food always taste better when someone else prepares it!!

### Lahelada — 2013-07-01T14:45:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-89291>

Good morning Caroline and everyone. Funnily enough I was thinking the same thing yesterday but could not come to a definite conclusion. It is probably a bit of both as I do have the sniffles and cough, which is the same as my partner has. He never did have the body aches though, so it might be that it is a combination of both. Something is happening though as since starting the BIA 3 days agoI have lost 2.5 lbs. Now that is my **weightloss** per month previously or near enough. I am putting it down to my liberal use of curcumin as well. I get two good doses a day. Eye measure,maybe a teaspoon in each maincourse. I am convinced that the detox has started. So 188 lbs today !

**Appetite** is nonexistent. I have to force myself to eat even after a small dinner like yesterday ( 200 g green veg,sauce and 2 ozs beef)

**Sleep** is strange. Yesterday I did not go to bed until one in the morning and was already up at 7. I must make arrangements today to make the living room less bright. I do feel refreshed though.

Fish: Caroline, I came to a similar conclusion yesterday about the fish and seafood. The seafood restaurants unless you go to the coast are not very good here. The general opinion is that if ....(insert deity of choice) had wanted us to eat fish why did he invent cows ? That is mostly only broken at Easter time when people eat fish for one day, just in case they were wrong the rest of the year .  I exaggerate to make a point. However,there are good fishmongers around ( yes,someone must be eating it I presume) and I know one that does not smell, so their produce must be good. I did a survey yesterday of what is available and the omega 3 content. If I can find it I will give the swordfish a go. Let the Curcumin handle the worms if there should be any. Another thing is hake,not so high in omega3s, but easily disguisable. The other thing is squid or calamar. Both swordfish and squid are at the high end of omega 3 according to nutritiondata, so if I only get an ounce down it is a win.

### Lahelada — 2013-07-02T14:54:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-89370>

Good morning. A slightly better day today but then again it is early. Thank you so much for looking up these recipes for me. The Brazilian one looks almost doable. ### GlenPDQ — 2013-07-02T16:29:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-89375>

I suspect than a little of WM fish is twice frozen if the package says "product of China". Alot of it gets frozen once onboard the harvest ship, hauled to China where it gets thawed out and processed, then frozen again for shipment to other countries.

### JanSz — 2013-07-02T17:43:52+0300

> I suspect than a little of WM fish is twice frozen if the package says "product of China". Alot of it gets frozen once onboard the harvest ship, hauled to China where it gets thawed out and processed, then frozen again for shipment to other countries.

[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Factory\_ship](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Factory_ship)
A factory ship, also known as a fish processing vessel, is a large ocean-going vessel with extensive on-board facilities for processing and freezing caught fish.

Contemporary factory ships are automated and enlarged versions of the earlier whalers and their use for fishing has grown dramatically. Some factory ships also function as mother ships.

\================================
[http://www.futureworld.org/PublicZone/MindBullets/MindBulletsDetails.aspx?MindBulletID=324](http://www.futureworld.org/PublicZone/MindBullets/MindBulletsDetails.aspx?MindBulletID=324)

CHINA OFFSHORES ITS FACTORIES
**Not off their shores - off yours!**
Dateline: 7 February 2010
In an amazing New World manufacturing coup, **China has delivered 170 factory ships just outside the territorial waters of the United States.** These are not fish factories, but fully functional floating industrial enterprises, with their own power, workforce quarters, clinics, broadband communications and regular supplies of raw materials.
Now you can order any Chinese commodity you need, and rest assured that it will be manufactured overnight, just 220 miles offshore, **and delivered the very next morning.**

Good morning. Feeling better,feeling worse. In the mornings I feel good,in the afternoon I have a headache.It could be from lack of food, too, as I am doing the BAB, but by 5 I have a headache. Today as I got up early I shall eat at 4 and see what happens.

### caroline — 2013-07-05T12:38:12+0300

### Lahelada — 2013-07-05T16:10:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-89528>

Good morning,everybody,good morning Caroline. Yes, indeed only two meals. I understand that I can have 3, but I have lived ketogenically for 3 month prior to coming here, so the appetite is not all that great. I don't have cravings either . The only thing I had to get used to is the longer interval between breakfast and lunch/dinner which in conjunction with the cold, detox was not very nice. Yesterday I was feeling better all round and was not even hungry when I got in at 5 . I ate though so as not to disturb the new rythm.

I had detoxed from carbs already and I started eating more organically before coming here , but had not done a general "bodycleanse" which I am doing now with Curcumin, magnesium and epsom salts. I break that "fast" (it does not feel like one) at around 5 by having a snack while I prepare the evening meal. I am happy with that routine as then I do not have to worry about carrying food with me during the day. From what I read that is ok according to the guidelines?

I had previously experimented with something from Josef Stocker that did the same, but only allowed fat until lunch which could be a full meal and then dinner. That idea was based on using the bodyÂ´s own fat burning rythm which apparently goes on until midday if not interrupted by additional food intake, ie other food groups than fat.This one did not work for me . BAB and then lunch dinner at 5 is excellent in many ways. Thank you Dr K for pointing me in that direction. Left up to me it would have been my least favourite option.

Other than that I did the "iodine drop test" yesterday,the one where you put a patch on your skin to see if it absorbs rapidly. It absorbed within 4 hours. I read up on the test and the consensus is that it is not conclusive as evaporation, breakdown of iodine into iodide also occurs on the skin surface, decolouring the skin naturally, but it is something to think about. My partner is a Chinese medicine practitioner and we agreed to figure that part out together,if there is something to figure out.

There is now almost a routine to my madness with regards to cold showers and CT. I am almost all day at the point of shivering through judicious use of clothes layers. Additionally around 7pm for the past two days I have done an ice skin stimulation followed by a cold shower. The ice before the shower feels really nice and because of it I could extend the cold shower after it. For some reason coldish flowing water affects me more than running a chunk of ice directly on my skin. Yesterday I did an Epsom salt foot bath in the morning and panicked a little as it was a hot one. I figured that all the anecdotal evidence says it works with hot, so that I should do it,too. Well, it does not seem to have done me any harm and I come to the reason why I think this next.

The bombshell news this morning. I am down to 84.4 kilos or 186 lbs. Since starting this in a hap hazard way and only firming up on a routine 2 days ago I have lost 2.5 kilos in a fortnight which is an enormous improvement on 10 kilos in 4 months. Keeping an eye though on not losing too much all at once, so as not to overload my body with toxins every day.Fine balance.

### Lahelada — 2013-07-05T21:46:36+0300

### Lahelada — 2013-07-06T15:22:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-89593>

Good morning all, good morning Caroline. Jejeje, now there is an aspect of that ice massage I had not thought about.
However, the reality is less evocative. Basically what I do is make a good chunk of ice and melt it running it all over my skin. It should be a big chunk as the idea is to simulate immersion and with a small cube you cannot sweep over your body in long strokes and get a lasting effect. I don't know why but large chunks of ice feel colder than an ice cube. I do that sitting in the shower and when I have done my whole body I turn on the cold shower. When I finish, after about 10 minutes at the moment I repeat the ice procedure and let the water evaporate instead of towelling off. That is why I asked about taking hot showers still. The evaporation on the skin does not leave me with an idea of cleanliness. It is imaginary no doubt but then so many things are.

My partnerÂ´s take on this is very traditional, very Zen. Energy in, energy out. Discipline. However, he can now see the problems we are faced with being surrounded by Frankenfoods designed to make us overeat. He is coming round and now taking an active role. One of the things we are going to do today is a glandular stimulation by means of auriculotherapy. What he thinks about the whole picture I do not yet know, but I can see things working in him

So yesterday I went out to the Chinese quarter and bought fish. Dusky grouper! I fried it only as I needed to see how fishy it was and to be honest I can't understand anymore what the fuss was about. I think it was my GrandmaÂ´s fish. On Monday you still knew that it had been Friday three days ago, Friday being the traditional fish day. The fish yesterday did not smell fishy as it was super fresh, no smell even while cooking and the taste was so neutral I was not sure if I was eating the cauliflower or the fish at points. Only thing wrong was the bones. I will select better next time. Oh and it is possibly dangerous to buy fish. I was elbowed out of the way by a tiny Chinese lady. The dangers the dangers....
I had a look at the shellfish,too . Great photo opportunity some of the beasties but on a plate? I still reserve judgment on that.

\[/SIZE\]

### caroline — 2013-07-11T01:36:26+0300

You will get there ......doesn't happen overnite! I am just realizing that after a year or more - lots of little things have happened. They just kind of sneak up on you! I have just realized that I have a rib cage and ribs ....Who knew??

### Lahelada — 2013-07-11T17:59:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-89887>

Well 85.5 again today.

I have tried something new to change my sleep rhythm and whoa did it work. I took a 3mg melatonin and I went to bed at 11.45pm and woke up at 11am. I am aware that this is not the recommended thing , but my idea is to get a headstart for a week or so and then drop it, sort of like a weeks vacation for the brain and body. It is not meant as a permanent supplementation.

Other than that the other thing that happened to me regular as clockwork all this week is that at 5.30 I wake up with a hot feeling in my belly as if I had drunk 2 Mc D coffees at once in the space of 20 seconds. Water does not diminish it, my skin is cold , the rest of me is cold to shivering point. It lasts 20 mins and then goes. My last meal was 4 hours before bedtime as prescribed. ..
I have been thinking that I need more water during the day. LetÂ´s see if it comes back tonight.

Pleasant news: Cocoa is chockfull of PQQ which is a supplement I can't get here. So yesterday I made an After 8 special basically water cocoa with mint tea and cream. Should have put it in a dark green cup, too as it was that good.

Congrats Caroline on the sighting of your ribcage. That is a really nice discovery to make. I am getting an inkling of it, too, the ribcage at the front that is .No news about the ribs themselves yet, jeje.

### caroline — 2013-07-12T11:58:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-89938>

so - you just make mint tea and add cocoa and cream? what other tea would work I wonder?

It sounds like you got a great sleep! did you have to get up for the bathroom?

### Lahelada — 2013-07-12T16:48:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-89953>

Good morning everybody. Today 85.4 kilos. I weigh every day to catch tendencies etc to see what is happening with changes I make . Well at the moment nothing much haha.

Yes Caroline,that is basically what I do. After Eight tea as in the mint chocolate wafer. I would imagine it works with any other tea like English breakfast. The tea only serves to give the cocoa a deeper richer flavour when you make it with water. Using mint tea is just a way of getting in a different natural flavour. I also make Earl Grey with coconut milk. I make my own coconut milk from desiccated coconut and that has residual sweetness and coconut flavour. Combines really well with coffee or Earl Grey.
Thinking about it green tea and cocoa would make the best PQQ drink, but have not tried it mainly as I don't have the right kind of tea yet. I am going to the chinese quarter today to buy fish and I will see if I can find a nice exotic green tea that will make me feel exotic, so that I won't mind giving up my coffee .  I jest. I am a big coffee lover though, but the more I read about cortisol I am wondering, if I am auto medicating low cortisol in the morning.

Yes, the sleep indeed felt good. I usually only sleep for 6 hours. My idea was to extend that, which worked the night before. I took another yesterday and it did not do the same for me. I woke up at 4.30 this morning ,only slightly hot.Then I fell asleep again at 6 until 9. So it was a 4.5 and then 3 hour sleep.I feel good though. I wonder if I was right about the water intake and that it had to do with the feeling of heat in my stomach. Yesterday I drank a lot. I do not actually get up to go to the bathroomthough. If I can't fall asleep again and stay awake then yes I do need to go after a while, but it is not the reason for waking up. I am wondering if these are the famous hot flushes. After all I am old enough to have lived when APPLE and BLACKBERRY were just fruit....

So,pollock a la hungara today. Basically a fried fish with tomato sauce and green pepper strips. The tomato sauce is homemade and cooked for hours the way Nonna used to make,well as she would have made if I were Italian to nix the oxalic acid content present in night shade plants.Cauliflower will be the side.

### Lahelada — 2013-07-12T23:55:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-90001>

Brain malfunction alert... I am looking forward to the fish I just bought.. Remember where we came in as far as fish eating is concerned .

Well, on todays menu is pollock, the safe option if the other variety does not appeal and pseudo semifascist perch. I invented that name from its pseudopercis semifasciata latin name. The general name it goes by is white salmon ( nothing to do with that family so again living up to the pseudo) or Argentine sandperch. I might rethink my preparation method.

### Lahelada — 2013-07-13T16:01:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-90040>

Good morning,

well the fish was a success. It will not appear too much on the menu though as it is also an overfished fish. As the offer in the store changes according to the catch of the day which may be seasonal or price related I never get to investigate the fish beforehand. Yesterday I watched the Wholefoods video and found out that Dr KruseÂ´s favourite fish Chilean seabass is quite common here and not too expensive ,only to find that this time they did not have any . Fishy goings on.

Sleeping is an issue . Even with melatonin I wake up between 4 and 6 pm for no apparent reason. I put a question in the beginners area.

### caroline — 2013-07-14T05:22:52+0300

sorry that you aren't sleeping well .... have you tried C/T in the evening? I can't remember...

### Lahelada — 2013-07-14T16:53:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-90098>

Good morning,good morning Caroline. Yes, I tried a little CT in the evening, but recently I have felt so cold at night that it did not appeal. I am actually shivering in my armchair fully dressed. Possibly the cumulative effect of living in a T shirt in all weathers.
However, maybe I have found a cheap surprising solution. Ear plugs. I always thought that around here was pretty quiet and that nothing disturbed my sleep. But then I made the supposition, that any strange noise that did occur in the middle of the night would be magnified in the silence and possibly add to a feeling of stress even while sleeping. So last night with ear plugs and melatonin I slept 9 hours almost on the trot. I woke up once , I would guess arround 6 and just went right back to sleep. So far so good. Two more days of melatonin I think and then ear plugs only.
I am looking into finding DHEA but things here are strange.It exists as a medication but only in conjunction with Procaine and B12. The B12 is welcome but the procaine I am thinking instinctively no. No idea why,I have no facts to bacl that up with yet. There are offers on the local ebay but for stuff like that I prefer a brick and mortar store. So I will need to investigate more.

### Lahelada — 2013-07-15T16:35:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-90146>

Just for a change 85.3 kilos , a bit less no biggie.
Good morning! Yesterday was the first time I slept with the door closed to get max darkness. 8.5 hours sleep! A roaring success for me, but the house was freezing, well for standards here. I checked the interior temp and it was 17 degrees. Great !! Partner not so happy,he hates the cold. That was the reason the door is open as the bedroom has no heating and the apartment gets heated by one heater in the lounge. So the only thing that is missing in the lifestyle change is going to bed with the chickens. And there was me thinking eating fish was the biggest hurdle.
Oh another little change this morning. They stopped importing my favourite coffee so I took the opportunity to switch to green tea in the morning. The good news is that I don't suffer from the morning lethargy anymore, have not done so since going lo carb. Back then you could not talk to me before my coffee mix.

### Lahelada — 2013-07-16T04:15:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-90206>

Hiya Caroline,thanks for looking in on me. The heater is a gas heater , small 3000 cals per hour no electric minus the initial spark.
It is not that I pride myself at going to bed late. The problem is that life here is late. In summer we have our last customers around 10pm.We get home at 11pm. Today was going to be an earlyish night and the landlord came over stayed for half an hour and the whole thing went out of the window. I will come up with a solution that is better and in winter I might even be able to do the recommended thing but for summer I do not hold out much hope to make it before midnight. I read up on sleep pattern and physical cell regeneration happening between 10 pm and 2 pm so I understand the importance of it.
Blue blockers I have not found here yet so my darkest sunglasses will have to do for now . Lots of things that you get hold of reasonably easily are not common here. Struggling with the dhea at the moment and am probably going to give up on it until a friend can bring me some over in a couple of months.
Signing off now,way over my allotted time.Nite nite.

### Lahelada — 2013-07-16T18:58:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-90237>

Good morning Caroline. You made me laugh with the party animal comment. We are such "old folks" here in terms of our lifestyle . No partying, but a really standard lifestyle of work sleep and our one hobby sailing. An average Argentine sleeps around 6 hours , we manage 8 or 9 albeit in my case in two parts often. Social life happens after 11 until the wee hours of the morning, not only club life also family life. Yes, heat is the problem as A/C is not as common as in the US for example although it is gaining traction. But we are also talkng about 100s of years of Mediterranean culture. Madrid and Barcelona are the same. We socialise with other sailors while sailing so we do not follow the norm but our life also happens later than say in Berlin or London.

Thank you for the links for ordering online. Import restrictions make it very hard if not impossible to order. Stressful ! However I will pay a visit to the lab that says that has it and enquire how they import it, where it comes from. Supposedly they have an open door policy.

I will work out my way of doing things it will just take more time. I tried to find the specs of the blublockers yesterday and will approach an optician next to see if they have the lenses. If they make them here all well and good.

I guess there is no point writing here further as I do not represent the all or nothing lifestyle Dr K envisages but my own search and trial and error and I do not wish to detract from his message in this diary. I am grateful to Dr K for the information he has made available and have adopted some of the principles and lifestyle, but the total lifestyle change is simply not for me, whatever that means and will entail.

Thank you Caroline for all your support and help. It has helped and will help even when I am not following the Epi Paleo way as prescribed .

### caroline — 2013-07-17T01:58:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-90252>

I really hope you do keep writing in your journal ....I so enjoy reading it. Our journal is our own personal journey to wellness and, certainly, like no one else's.

I do think you misunderstand a little ....it is not all or nothing for most of us here. We just plug away and do what we can. Dr. Kruse has always said that - just do what you are able to. I know, for me, lately I suck at C/T. I intended to go in the ocean everyday and I do walk in the surf most days ....but it has been hard to get full body immersion. I have turned into a big baby!

This whole, entire journey is about finding our own n=1 ....It is hugely different for all of us.....and that is precisely what you are doing. You have been making changes and then see how it affects you and then maybe try something else.

All of us go about it differently - depending on our problems and resources and how broken we are.

I truly hope I haven't made it seem like an "all or nothing journey". It is far from that. I think most here find that they work on something and then realize something else is sliding.

I have really appreciated learning a little about you and your lifestyle......

### caroline — 2013-07-17T03:28:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-90262>

What you are describing is not unusual in the least - especially for those of us outside the U.S. everyone always rolls their eyes - especially my family! All my old doctors are positive adrenal fatigue doesn't exist and where am I getting this stuff from anyway. You have to think outside the box - actually - way outside the box. Sometimes to get a Doc to listen - you need to manufacture a family health history so they get why you want certain labs. That is why Dr. K Is shining his lite on all of this ....we have to understand.....no one cares about our health as much as we do. We have to keep reading and trying to understand and figure it out. From the very beginning Dr. K. Didn't dumb stuff down - he said to keep reading until you understand - no matter how many times it takes!

It is too bad that you don't listen to the webinars - Dr. K. Is very easy to listen to and understand. Have you listened to the last podcast? It ties a lot of things together in a way that us non geeks can somewhat get!

If you follow Shijin's story - she has been working on her health for 15 years. Gretchen has made it her mission to understand her body and what works for her....and it often is completely different to what works for someone else ( sorry Gretchen for talking about you!)

Please read Inger's guest blog about her journey and also her journal - she has little in the way of resources (I am in the same boat) but she has amazing intuition about what is right for her and her body. Inger has had very difficult circumstances in her life - but is thriving beautifully.

You are doing really well - you are considering everything we are throwing at you ....and doing what you can. Your life style is very different there - it is going to take time to see what works for you.

I think what must be hard for new people on the forum is .....all of this. Jack has been giving all of this to us somewhat slowly over the last couple of years- sort of building on a foundation. There was a big melt down awhile ago when Jack released the EMF series. Some didn't understand why he didn't do it sooner and he said - we weren't ready ....we had to absorb everything that came before. But you new guys kinda get it all thrown at you - especially by us who are trying to help ......so sorry for that.....

I will look up that link for the last podcast for you.

And what fitness said is so very true. - you never know when someone is going to post something that connects some dots for someone else......we are all supporting each other....

Stepping off my soap box now ....

### Linz — 2013-07-17T12:02:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-90271>

Lahelada,

I don't have a journal here but I have loved reading about your approach to Dr K's advice. I think you should read Barry's posts in the optimal fitness section if you haven't seen them already. Wise words about the balance between actually making radical changes and the stress it can cause. Some of his opinions seem quite arrogant (apologies Barry) but they do seem to stimulate a feeling of, 'I'm getting there too.... ' and with a sense of just making use of the natural world around us, so perhaps just part of his training method.

I was already holding back a chronic health problem with a good diet (including most of the right fats) when I first started reading here
but simply being aware of the importance of circadian rythms, light and darkness, seasons, temperature, meal timing, grounding, artificial technology, the sea etc has made me adapt in many subtle ways and with a few years hindsight I can see a huge improvement and reversal of some of the damage I had done. My personal journal involves looking back to the previous year every Christmas and summer holiday, and each year surprises me a little more. Sometimes I test things that are really difficult to change in everyday life when I am on holiday and if they are really effective I find a way to carry on.

I really recommend reading more here, including some of the references for verification, before you add other sources because there is such a vast wealth of scientific interpretation and Dr Kruse helps us all, paid membership or not. I understand 'reset' as something that might be difficult or uncomfortable for a while but which allows me to start again, albeit in a better educated way and with continued vigilance for my own specific weakness. And it is very much easier when I have a sound scientific explanation for the changes.

When I realised that I could (and had to) help myself I stopped saying anything about what I was doing to any of my medical advisors for a long time after being subjected to some extremely condescending and dismissive remarks or looking at their watches. It's really interesting when some of those 'experts' start asking how you manage to look so well or what you are doing -- and each time you can give them a good scientific reason things change a little. You will find those reasons here. My heart lifted when the young doctor let me explain the basics of what I was doing and said it sounded right to him and he was going to do some reading. Then it crashed when I thought about the political problems this would probably lead him into. I think the greatest opportunity for change comes from more and more people outside the system knowing the science behind what they are asking for and having a rational explanation. Dr Kruse is giving us that knowledge.

You are so lucky to live a Mediterranean lifestyle. I'm sure the relaxed nature and emphasis on family and good quality food compensates for many late nights (just don't ask me which epigenetic modification that might be!). I don't want to overwhelm you with even more specific advice but adding seaweed to your soups and sauces is very easy and it contains many valuable nutrients that are expensive as supplements.

All the best,

Linz

Linz...what a lovely, balanced, supportive post. I agree with you 100 percent.

### Lahelada — 2013-07-17T15:49:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-90275>

Good morning Caroline,

thank you for the kind post. You are right ,when I started reading Dr K I did not understand anything. I had to work back from the responses as to what the direction of the advice actually was. Because of that I jokingly said that this forum is my biology/ chemistry teachers revenge. So I went reading elsewhere, from the fitness magazine to the endocrinology congress in Mannheim Turns out that I did know about cortisol, if you had only called it hydrocortisone and things got a little easier understanding not because of the terminology difference, but that reading in English is not as productive as reading in Spanish and German. So I have built up long forgotten background knowledge again to the tune that I understand what is being said and can evaluate how it applies to me. But yes I did eat myself some EMF in the process but omelette/eggs. It won't be a forever thing. Additionally it is almost like a new hobby to find a headslap moment "why did I never relate the two and think.... instead" The latter hobby part will need to be curbed. However I have a tendency that once I started I want to understand it ALL and NOW.

The forum is difficult to read for the abbreviations that are not always the standard ones. AA is the automobile association or alcoholics anonymous but not ascorbic acid.(an invented example to illustrate) If you are in the know already about adrenal fatigue and know that the supplement is used to help recovery then you make the connection.So yes you are absolutely right that the familiarity of the users with the terrain compensates for that . But it happens everywhere in specialised fields and one gets to know them one by one.

At this stage I am not planning to see any doctor here. I shall see if what I have planned for myself and am developping gets me back to optimum or near optimum. I kind of had an epiphany last night of something that seemed to work for me.

I guess I threw my toys out of the pram yesterday as the whole day was stress for no tangible reason but it led me to the discovery that leptin resistance may not be my primary issue ( i came here with weightloss in mind remember) but adrenal fatigue with high stress levels for things that don't even merit the lift of an eyebrow. Thanks for pointing me in the direction motivatedhealthseeker. So I apologize for kicking up a fuss. Things got the better of me and I was throwing out things willy nilly.

Caroline, I really appreciate you input into my journal and hope you will continue to do so.

### Lahelada — 2013-07-17T16:16:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-90276>

Good morning Linz,

thank you so much for stopping by and writing me this supportive post. You have hit the nail on the head with my preference for a more "natural" approach. I am much more happy with the idea of Maca than any pill I could take when it comes to adrenal fatigue. I am not saying that I will take it in the end, but I will certainly research it as it is so available to me.Taking a nondescript pill is more stressfull to me but I have already included some in my regime. Herbs have always fascinated me and I strongly believe that more things exist between heaven and earth that we currently know of.
I will check out Barry. Thanks for pointing out the thread. It is in a section I would have thought to read last.

You are also correct in "diagnosing" me with stress due to the changes I am making. That was probably why I threw my toys out of the pram as yesterday was a horrible generally stressy day and sitting beside myself for a moment there was no reason at all to stress yet I felt myself reduced for example to reacting to a daily situation of having to sign a contract or moreover the renewal of a contract as if it was completely new and a mistake could cost me dearly. I knew it was not the case but my nervousity was off the charts while I was sitting mentally next to myself asking myself whatever was I getting het up about. Useless as it was not a rational response.

I have been thinking about the seaweed and had another thought that kind of stopped me in my tracks. I have no idea about the seaweeds,types and what is available here so I went onthe importers website . All the stuff comes from either Japan or China, the chinese one from a part that is more known for ecological problems than anything else and the Japanese one well ,letÂ´s just say recent ecological history haunts me.I will try to find out if the recent accident has had lasting effects on the marine life.

Thanks again for speaking up and encouraging me. You were spot on in what ails me!

### Lahelada — 2013-07-17T16:39:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-90277>

Slightly different bedtime routine last night and today I feel well.
I went to bed at 11pm and took a small dose of Melatonin. I was going to kick it but after the day of stress I really wanted to sleep and not wake up with that "panic" reaction . I also experimented with some information I was given re a snack before bedtime Well one of the two worked .I slept from 11.30 to 1.30 and then again until 7.30 . I felt calm on waking but just to make sure I recounted all my stresses to myself,reminded myself of things I needed to do and no adrenaline response .Much better . I don't want the melatonin to become a habit but for a couple of days it will be with me. Then I will try the snack only. In my travels yesterday I found an article that hidrolised casein is used for the purpose of relieving the stresses of adrenal fatigue now. Lactium is the tradename for it . No data no idea about it, but thought I throw it out there .

An article caught my eye today about the importance of sunlight being recognised and a town going to an extreme to get more of it. Hope you enjoy.

[http://www.dailymail.co.uk/news/article-2366073/Town-Norwegian-valley-set-winter-sunlight-time-history-using-giant-MIRRORS.html](http://www.dailymail.co.uk/news/article-2366073/Town-Norwegian-valley-set-winter-sunlight-time-history-using-giant-MIRRORS.html)
