### Jack Kruse — 2013-07-18T18:44:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-90330>

you do not need to see a doctor Lahelada. You need to re connect with the doctor in your head that whispers often to you. You need to relearn how to listen to those words. These words are the instincts and intuition you were born with but socialized to believe were not quite good enough. Instinct gains strength as you retain health and energy. Instinct is about the sharpness of one's spirit. The ability to sense an opportunity and cease the moment when you feel the time is right. INSTINCTS are behaviors that we don't have to learn, they came built in with our software so we could rely on them. They are our natural intuitive powers. Man is the product of his thoughts. What ever you let your mind feed on will be given back to you ten folds by your own subconscious mind. If you want to have good instincts feed your mind with positive thoughts. If you desire bad instincts do the opposite by feeding your mind with negative thoughts. If you think you are beaten, you are. If you think you dare not, you don't, If you like to win, but you think you can't. It is almost certain you won't. Life's battles don't always go to the stronger or faster person, But sooner or later they go to the person who follows their instincts well in the environment they face. This is how people succeed in life's battles.

### Lahelada — 2013-07-18T18:51:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-90334>

Well,well,well the things one learns.I am still hunting for DHEA and came across a product that combines DHEA con Procaina. Before knowing what procaina is I resigned myself to taking this product as it seems the only one on the market.However, Procaina is the same as Novocaine and no way will I take that on a daily basis. The dog would need to take me for a walk instead of the other way round. Well, it is actually the other way round but that has to do with the dog ,not me.
Back to that product though. In the few minutes when I was convinced that I would take it I checked it for interactions with my birthcontrol pill based on drospirenone and the google results I found lead me to another thing. Drospirenone lowers DHEA dramatically. Maybe my natural levels of DHEA are not so bad after all .Ditching the BPC is the way to go and see. I might still need the DHEA but I want to see first how much I still got. Edt : Thinking about it I reacted to the novocaine based on old information . I will check on whatelse it does before a yay or nay. The DHEA product is entirely affordable so will not dismiss it offhand.

I also have aloe vera plants growing outside my house. Apparently they are a great detox agent taken with whisky or any other high percentage alcohol. Liking it already jeje. Still,it is under research as the germanium it contains is a doublesided sword. As already Paracelsus famously said the same substance can be either healthy or poison,the dosis is what makes the difference.

### Lahelada — 2013-07-18T19:14:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-90336>

Dear Dr Kruse,

many thanks for coming into my diary and encouraging me. It is just that idea that I do not recognise myself in "mental" terms anymore that led me to this forum. I have gone from the person who attacks a problem/challenge like a dog that got hold of the postmanÂ´s leg to someone that jumps at an unexpected loud sound. I am coming back from that slowly,the brain fog having most days lifted allowing me to focus again. Originally I came here to hang with people who do not think CT weird and in the process realised that I have way more to learn and repair. A couple of days ago when I wrote I was not giving up , just changing direction and thought erroneously that this would not find a niche here.

Times in Argentina are difficult again and outsiders often do not understand how what is obvious in other countries does not work here (mailorder for example) Mormons cannot even get their traditional undergarments ! Another import ban story from here that amused the country for a while. However, it does not mean nothing can be done. It just needs to approached in a different roundabout way.

So thank you Dr Kruse for coming in here. I appreciate it, especially as you are on your vacation in the Gulf already I believe.

Good morning all,

thank you Caroline. :hug:

I will make it brief for today as I am off out to the market wich is a bit of a trek. I found patagonian Alga porphyra colombina so I am set now for replenishing the iodine.Iodide,too. ### Lahelada — 2013-07-20T15:30:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-90432>

**Chinese whispers**

why the title? It has got to do with the things I turn up seemingly on a whim recently at the Chinese quarter.

Starting with the peashoots I have strange attractions to foods I have not tried before. The other thing was black sesame. I saw it and wanted it. Checking out the nutritionals I came across a Chinese medicine site that uses them for balancing shen energy, the energy that is steadily declining and is responsible for adrenal fatigue.

Yesterday I spent a lot of time to find foods that are high in silica,my vanity nutrient , as I am not prepared to spend mega bucks on a fancy supplement that is essentially what the cat pees on in litter trays. It is actually a bit different,we could not digest it properly and therefore not get at the silica.
I came across a naturopath site though that quoted a study that not horsetail ( herb that is a doublesided sword) but bamboo that is the plant highest in silica. Furthermore it stated that silica is only well absorbed if stomach acids are strong. Guess what I have been looking at every time I go to the Chinese store? Fresh fermented sour bamboo strips ...That they probably have value as a probiotic is another point in their favour. Getting them today. I also learned that silica is not just my vanity herb to preempt saggy skin, but it just happens to be involved in bone formation and the prevention of hardened arteries. Someone is whispering to me..

Today I am off out to get sauerkraut. It is a trek out of the city to the outskirts so I will use it to do some CT at the same time .
Weight steady at 86.5 kilos.

### Lahelada — 2013-07-21T14:58:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-90502>

Good morning everybody. A good Sunday . ( hours sleep starting at 11 and no melatonin. 11 is my ideal time for sleeping at the moment. I feel relaxed waking up . The way I fall asleep has changed ,too. I used to fall asleep hard when the normal lights were on and I sat in the lounge. Now I drift off as I should. Melatonin no more. It said it could cause vivid reams and it sure did. I am thinking of contacting Deam Koontz to see if he wants a new book plot .

Ct was great yesterday. I went to get my sauerkraut in jeans and T shirt with temps around 8 degrees C. It was nice. There seem to be more of us.I don't know if people intentionally CT here ,but I got silent acknowledgements form people as lightly dressed as me. Well there were about 4. The rest gave me incomprehending horrified looks. In the afternoon same get up we went to the boat and the wind was stronger by the river. I sunbathed on deck with bare midriff. The return was funny. My partner feels cold really cold so while I am prancing about injeans and t shirt he wears a leather jacket and a polar fleece. poor thing gets really aggressive looks from the men who consider him less of a gentleman for not giving me his polar shirt at least. I have nothing to do with it. I need a T shirtsaying :"This is therapy- the doc said so"

Taking Vit c now in ascorbic acid form. If google is to be believed I took between 2000 and 3000 mg. I thought it was more like 1000. But whatever it was it did not have the throughput effect a too large dosis is supposed to have so all is good.
I did get the Sauerkraut and also German liverwurst and bratwurst with no fillers. I had not expected that but a chat quickly confirmed that they are true to their prices and actually deliver a quality product.

Thanks for the tip on Sauerkraut,Caroline. I will look them up. I had a good portion raw yesterday and it was excellent. When sauerkraut comes from the butcher it tastes different. I heard that they use the leftover broth from making sausage to cook up the sauerkraut but have not confirmed that.

Fitness@home I did not get to the chinese quarter yesterday. THe kids have their vacation and anywhere is packed. Most parents have run out of entertainment budget and are looking for cheap alternatives . "Oh look we can go visit the Chinese today" I wonder whether the kids really enjoy it. It is only stores . I will post a pic of the bamboo shoots when I get them. I am thining of rushing out there in the morning before the world is out and about.

Today more CT with the dog.Yesterday at night ( 4C) she asked to go home. She hates the cold.

Thanks for the tip on the podcast, Caroline . I will look for it . Yes, I have always loved the cold.When I was a kid I played with the snow until my hands burned.Keeping hat and gloves on me was a fulltime job for Gran. As an adult I swam for hour sd in cold waters when available.

**Sleep** :Well ,went to bed last night at 10.30 and only just got up at 9 . I had some interruptions of being hot and waking up but that was because of too many covers. So great!

**CT** .: Yesterday was much colder so I did a 25 minute session with the dog in the am and a booster session of 10 mins in the afternoon . Maybe that is what made me sleep. Repeating the swame pattern today but slightly longer. We shall see .
Weight: 86.7 kilos

**Food:** I did an extended nutritional listing of my core foods as near as can be and I am pretty pleased. My core foods are ground grassfed beef,freerange chicken,freerange organic eggs,grassfed butter cream and cheese,biological hard salami ( as near as a pig gets to organic due to laws etc)liverwurst not bio but without fillers,black and yellow sesame,algae from Patagonia, poppyseed,cauliflower, peashoots,sauerkraut,green and red cabbage and Brussels sprouts. ### fitness@home — 2013-07-22T19:51:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-90574>

> [Lahelada said:](/goto/post?id=90560)
>
> Hehe nice to be able to put light and fatty in the same expression.

Yes it is! I deviated from my normal weekly batch of cocoa crack and made another recipe called "fat bombs".

1/2 C almond butter (ground it up myself)
1/2 C coconut oil
1/2 C powdered baking chocolate (I have some good organic stuff)
liquid stevia about 10-12 drops
1/2 tsp vanilla

Mix well and pour into lined dish or silicone candy mold for something fancier. Me, I just poured into a lined pie plate and when it was solid cut it into squares. Keep in fridge or freezer.

### Lahelada — 2013-07-23T16:11:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-90633>

Good morning everyone ,

Sleep : 6+2 probably because I had dinner too close
Weight: 87.8 (beginning to worry me slightly as TOM is over)
CT: 1 hr in 42 degrees with windchill of 31.6 ( 3C/-0.3C) in a cotton dress and a more wrapped up outing in jeans and light jacket for 2 hours. I felt cold all day so I ddi not repeat the intense one at night.

I bought bamboo shoots yesterday and tried one today a bit fried up. Hmm,the dog left it too. I will have to try it in a soup . For now it is not a saint of my devotion.
I took pics .Will post them once I reduced them.

Caroline the cheeesecake in a glass goes something like this.I don't measure so my measures are approximate

100 g cream cheese
1 tbsp butter
3 groaund brazil nuts
1 egg
vanilla essence
opt cocoa powder

In a saucepan heat the butter. Pour the ground nuts into a glass that is heat resistant or any other container.
Pour the melted butter on top and transfer the glass to the freezer so that the butter hardens
In the same saucepan add the cream cheese and the egg and cook the mixture through. Add vanilla essence .Depending on the consistency of the cheese
you might need to add a little water. l
Let cool and spoon on top of the nut butter mixture and leave in fridge to set .

If you use chocolate I prefer to cook up the cocoa in a little water asit develops the flavour more makes it more chocolatey and then add the cheesecake.

### Lahelada — 2013-07-26T14:55:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-90907>

Good morning everybody.

Sleep: great,from 11-7

Diet : Oyoyoy, Grazing day. Hugely hungry, barely managed to stick to eating times and that with three meals. Does Leptin sensitivity go backwards ? Remember I started off with no problems with having just two meals straight away,comfortably. Now with CT not so much .We shall see if it calms down now as the really cold weather has gone.
Weight: 86.5 kilos

So yesterday's experiments: I had some **e**ggshells, pulverised them and included them in my seed bread. Not a success, the shells were no bother but the seed bread is just not bread. Won't make again. Will try to include the eggshells in my cheesecake.

**C**innamon: On a whim I bought some cinnamon sticks recently. Yesterday I remembered that a Brazilian once told me that they use cinnamon water to improve the water quality. I had made it before long time ago. This time I thought to google the words " heavy metal fluoride detox" It turns out that it has a very high boron content,is effective against candida and might help with fluoride detox because of the boron. So this time I made the cinnamon water with citric acid to break down the cinnamon structure more than just by boiling alone.
A tasty detox I must say. LetÂ´s see what it does if I can even tell.

Made **P**ectin water. Also tasty, not quite sure if it will do anything for me, but I notice that I am dehydrated even when drinking so letÂ´s see if my body will hold more water this way. The pectin is the sugarfree variety of course.

Apart from that I deep cleaned the house, springclean style. Back in April we were flooded and since then it never "felt" really clean even though it probably was. I had oodles of energy yesterday.

I also found out why I did not sleep well the night before. One really should not take vitamin C before bedtime..

Edit : Boron content of food: parsley flakes (26.878 mg/kg),
ground cinnamon (10.370 mg/kg);
dried onion flakes (6.573 mg/kg),

### Lahelada — 2013-07-27T15:55:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-91002>

Good morning,

a brief message as we are going out sailing. Fits in with my recent read on EMF. We will try to find a cellular dead spot.Normally you get coverage even in the middle of the river be it from Arg or Uruguay.
Weight today 86.4 mildly down. Grrr. Great CT yesterday. I iced my body all over and interestingly just now thinking about it I reached places on my back that I did not reach before so that must mean that I am more agile. Then I did a cold shower with water at 9C or 48 F for 30 minutes. That is a record and I felt great after except that when I had fully warmed up again my shoulder hurt. That is the shoulder that hurts when carrying anything heavy or sometimes without anything. Recent reads lead me to believe it might be an omega 3 mismatch. So from Tues day when I get my shopping ( organic is not available every day) I will change my food list to include more omega3 ,less omega 6 and we will see. The other thing is of course cortisol but it did not feel like it yesterday. We shall see . It is the easier one of the ways forward to find out what is causing this.
On another note it might be wishful thinking but on inspection yesterday I have the idea that my cream cheese like fat has clumped together and feels more solid my skin having more tone.
Gotta run ,we have not been able to go sailing for a month because of weather/low tide and or other commitments and today everything works together .

### Jack Kruse — 2013-07-27T17:49:53+0300

you need to stop weighing yourself...........it will not help you lower cortisol. Focus on changing the field youre in.

### Lahelada — 2013-07-28T16:06:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-91035>

Good morning Dr Kruse . Many thnaks for stopping by and reading a bit here. I really appreciate it .

I am not aware that my daily weighing is a problem with regards to cortisol. The feeling it provokes is more like grrr but hey ho . I have been at this a long time so one takes it with a wry smile . Nevertheless I will give it a try and see if it makes a difference. Thank you .

With regards to changing field I am afraid that is not on my to do list. I will explain. I spent nigh on 20 years as a battery hen in offices under artificial light fighting windmills. What I do now has me either at home or out in the open air all day long away from the city. If we work at night we work in as close to darkness as possible. My new work is my passion, as stress free as can be as far as jobs are concerned. For starters I am my own boss. The one drawback is the occasional late night but for the other benefits I will live with that.

As I am describing my current life I will also give you a view of my physical surroundings.
In the way of EMF we have not got much gear. We are not technophiles. A 10 year old TV, fridge and air conditioning , wifi we share and the router is in the other house . There is only two other wifis around us. We are surrounded and literally overgrown by green.

As far as paleo diet is concerned everything takes longer here. However I do think my choices are more natural than in a lot of places. Pork products I get from the province directly, dairy and egg are a half hour bus ride away but can only be got on two days of the week. Fish is best from the Chinese quarter but only Tues and Friday.
Beef is from the supermarket, only one guarantees their meat as grass fed still. Some people say grassfed is still so much the norm here that they do not even advertise it as Argentines expect it, but with the high demand abroad I am not so sure. The supplement market is very limited. I tried to find a better magnesium and waded through 50 products only to find that mag citrate is the best to be got here. People prefer all in ones here and it is difficult to get mag or other stuff in isolation. I am still reading myself through the vademecum though to see what we have.

BAB today was 4 eggs. one knackwurst and 4 slices salami. 3 cups of tea with gf butter and semi gf cream.
CT yesterday was sailing in 18C /64F temps for 3 hours in muscleshirt and bermudas. I was not sure if it would qualify as CT but when returning to port I was frozen and had to put on more clothes so hopefully it has done me some good. I definitely got a good dose of vit D.

Sleep: 8.5 hours straight (11.30 to 8 )

Supplements: Currently only magnesium and Vit C. Looking for better mag and Coen Q10 of good quality. Kanikama as it is imported costs 70 dollars for 90 capsules and so far it is the only ubiquinol I have seen. Ouch. I see a lot of beef liver in my future.

### caroline — 2013-07-29T06:56:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-91069>

I am not sure that we get enough Vit D from the sun in the winter. Someone here had charts on when it is optimal. The sun has to be more direct and hotter.

I am going to test my Vit D soon. It was really high all summer and this winter I have only taken it occasionally to see if it remains in a good range or not.

When you were out on the water - were you cold? I guess there is a lot of physical activity when sailing? I have never been....

### Lahelada — 2013-07-29T14:52:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-91082>

Good morning.

Sleep : 7 hours straight
Food : lunch 3 ozs cheese,3 ozs salami, 8 ozs mate
dinner3 eggs,cauliflower,sprinkles of cheese
pectin water 20 ozs,2 herbal teas
CT 3 hours at 55F sailing

Good morning Caroline.Yes one does feel cold when on the boat. Apart from a few activities it is pretty much a sedentary affair. LetÂ´s say out of the 3 hours we were out, we are only physically active for 20 minutes. I do feel cold,first pleasantly so then after three hours I have to put on another T shirt to be able to function well when we dock. To give you an idea my skin was still cold to the touch on my upper arms and legs when we arrived at 8 at night so that is 2 hours after leaving the boat. I do not cover up much though, just jeans and a longsleeved cotton T shirt when out and about.

About the Vit D I am not sure either . Will try to find some data on it. I ran out of vit D3 and still looking for a better replacement . It only comes in combos here and the last one had magnesium oxide and calcium. As combos are the way to go here I am looking for a more eclect effective mix as I want to know that the magnesium is absorbed as much as possible and the absorption rate is not very good for the oxide one. The best mag I could find so far is the citrate one

### Lahelada — 2013-07-29T16:03:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-91087>

**UK :** November to March no vit D from Sun
**US:** north of Atlanta no vit D from sun ( Further south (34 degrees N and 18 degrees N), sunlight effectively photoconverted 7-dehydrocholesterol to previtamin D3 in the middle of winter. )
Tasmania / new Zealand no vit D production in winter.
**Arg** : it seems Buenos Aires is still in a vit D producing latitude in winter. Case in point ,I actually got a light sunburn yesterday on my shoulder .
However interestingly enough we can store Vit D to get us through the winter. The estimations vary between one and three month reserves. In summer we are out in the open air for nigh on 8 hours. Whether I have a store or not because of that would need to be tested . There is a thesis that Northern European people are better able to store Vit D than lets say people of mediterranean descent. I supplement haphazardly with D3 at the moment.

I do wonder though quite unscientifically if we have less Vit D in winter because of our habits too as we cover up and sit in our houses. I am thinking about the feel good effect a postcard of snowladen fir trees in bright sunshine produces. A recognition that this would be a day to feel good if only we went outside? It seems a "bad" evolutionary move to leave us 3 months without something we need. Or were we nomadic for that purpose?

interesting oldfashioned advice from Europe.: In months with an R you need a daily spoon of codliver oil. A rule from Germany originally but could be applied to English as well.

### Lahelada — 2013-07-29T16:54:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-91094>

Well, we went out sailing on the river /Rio de la Plata estuary every day this weekend . We went east as the delta is populated by powerboaters on the weekends and too stressful for that reason.

We sailed around noon on Saturday a glorious day, not a cloud in sight. The river was not as populated as on other days so having uninterrupted sailing space waters was not all that difficult. Winter is the pejerrey season here and the fishermen were out in droves, well wrapped up in bad weather gear to deal with the cold. As you said Caroline, people wrap up beyond what is necessary. The pejerey swim quite close to the surface so the seagulls and cormorants were well happy. You could hear their squawking on the wind. The water also seems clearer in winter time. Maybe that is what makes the fishing so easy for the birds.
It is immensely relaxing to hear the water rushing past the hull and the wind catching the sails. We are not speed sailors, we like a good pace but not to the extent that we need to maneouver a lot. The idea is to spend the day contemplating the water, the changing light and of course to see what other boats are out there .

On Sunday we sailed around the same time. It was a day with more clouds and as we were to find later with fog coming in. Still gorgeous though. More boats on the water so that gave us time to boat watch, the nautical equivalent of people watching. We spotted 4 classic wooden sailboats. The wind was not as constant as the day before, failing at points so around three we decided to turn back as a longer calm would let the fog descend and with that many boats about not such a relaxing experience. We got back to the dock at 4 had a picnic of cheese and salami and awaited sundown. Fog was descending and this morning the headline on the news was " You can't see anything "

### Lahelada — 2013-07-31T15:18:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-91255>

Good morning all . Sleep today 7 hours starting from 11pm .
BAB: 4 eggs 6 ozs sausage earl grey with gf butter

A good day yesterday. Did all my paleo shopping,found some more things like gf cuartirolo cheese and smoke fermented raw sausage (teewurst) I need to know still though whether they make it the traditional way. Frankly I canÂ´t see any other way of them making it but I will still ask .
I finally got the web site from the coop I get my eggs from and yes they do eat grains but only as a supplement and ten only linseed.The rest is what they can find during pasture. I would think that does something for the omega 3 content of the eggs and possibly the chicken itself. I will investigate a bit . The only disappointment was the chocolate. On a previous visit they had assured me that it was 100 %, but alas no. Not all is lost yet though. I might have found somewhere where they sell the cocoa paste. If it is unsweetened success. I will make my own chocolate. If not cocoa bark will have to do.

Caroline ,the attitudes to body and sex are strange here. There is a whiff of Catholicism all over the place and the country seems reticent. When you go into the parks though a lot of girls sun themselves in g string bikinis that leave nothing to the imagination. Every part of town has special "fully equipped" high tech modern hotels you rent by the hour that unlike in other parts of the world are not frequented by professional ladies, but by just about everyone. So laissez faire with a very conservative public official morale . Quite funny really. Having said that in spring summer there could be occasions when one could go sailing naked ,also in my garden I could sun myself without being overlooked as well so yes there is possibilities to air myself 100 %.

I also went to a pharmacy yesterday to see if I could get a chelated magnesium supplement. The only chelated minerals are imported and part of a multivitamin and of course do not contain more than the RDA . So I am thinking of using Epsom salts and redirect my saving to another supplement being Coen Q10 or DHEA. I asked a question recently about the combination of DHEA and procaine which was a wonder combination in the sixties and supposedly a favourite of the Kennedys. They have revived the mix here (in the US it was banned for being unapproved,not due to actual health issues ) However the labs that make them are the kind that sell you stuff on late night TV. The other DHEA availablefrom companies that have more than a hotmail address is in conjunction with estradiol. I will need to check out the latter one. I am doubtful about both.

My stress levels are down. Yesterday I found myself in a melÃ©e of hooting cars, mums with kids shoving everyone out of the way,homicidal sack cart operators with inbuilt priority and street vendors hawking their wares from tablecloths spread out on the pavement. Normally I would have got out of there as fast as I can with gritted teeth but yesterday I even stopped to try on shoes . Horrible fashion at the moment. Hope spring brings nicer stuff.
Yesterday I could almost have sworn that my pain in my shoulder has lessened. The sunburn itch I had recently was actually no sunburn at all so I had kind of hoped that it was healing. But no, today it is back more in the middle than yesterday.

I wrote enough. Time to do stuff.

.

strange night

It would be great if you came to Argentina. I think you would like it. The city is disorganised chaos while I imagine New York is organised chaos. I have never been. My preferences always were with the other half of the continent and Mexico. I did go to California, LA and SF but must admit I really only enjoyed stopping in the little places and Sequoia Natl Park.

I also checked on the **vitamin D** and how to find out whether you can make it in winter or generally when to make it. I don't know what I was looking at the lasttime I checked, but it was not the altitude. So thanks for bringing this to my attention Caroline.

**Vitamin C** continuing with high doses and so far no diarrhetic effect . I am up to a teaspoon. Wow. I also bought a multivit only to find it contains guaranÃ¡ . Hmm not so good. I found another online here but in the places they are supposed to sell it they donÂ´t . That one has an excellent spec .
**
Pectin water**: It seems to be doing something for me. When I do the turgor test on my hand it actually springs back now and I have not increased my water intake by a lot maybe 20 ozs a day. I actually wake up with skin turgor when I was previously dehydrated.

I also did something that I should not have done and that is read here during the hours I could not sleep. The surprising thing is though that a lot of things went click that I had read previously. ### Lahelada — 2013-08-02T16:14:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-91368>

**Sleep** : 11.15 - 8.30 9 hrs yay

**Food** : B 4 eggs,liverwurst,salami
L ground beef with andean potatoes
D ossobuco with 1 oz carrot and more andean potatoes

_Ullucos_ are 85 % water,15 protein 14% carb, neutraceutic according to folklore . It is cultivated at 2800 m altitude. Folklore has it that it is kills tumors,
makes acne scar appearance better and is the natural viagra. Sounds good to me.If correct I wonder if it will do something for stretchmarks after weightloss. If it does or if it does not it tastes like a watery potato but is locarb. That alone works for me ..
A newspaper article from Bolivia for a pic
[http://www.opinion.com.bo/opinion/articulos/2012/0307/noticias.php?id=46955](http://www.opinion.com.bo/opinion/articulos/2012/0307/noticias.php?id=46955)
A quick word about taste. I used to be a pathological chilihead,the hotter the better.I tried some in my eggs the other day and nothing like th usual aaah effect ,infact a little bit of owww. Coincidence or has something in my gut changed I wonder.

**CT** : Strange . I spent about half an hour outside in muscle shirt and jeans at temps of 10 degrees C/50F doing my tree position.,and then 1.5 hours in the nude inside lying by the open window at 12C/54F. For a good 2 hours I felt cold shivery after that ,got fed up, had a hot shower and wondered how a cold shower would feel afyer that .Did it and it was strangely enough easier than I had thought.

Caroline, I picked up the idea of pectin water from various sources. Here it was mentioned for possibly structuring water. In other places (German naturopaths mainly ) it is considered beneficial for aiding the healing of a leaky gut. It can cause constipation and diarrhea ,don't ask me how that works. It inhibits the intake of medication so I would assume also the intake of nutrients. I therefore take it separately during the day leaving an interval of three hourse between the water and meds or food . You can also achieve the same effect with agar agar I read but I have not tried that.
Making it is simple . Boil water, one liter and add a teaspoon of pectin. Apple pectin is best. At first the pectin will clump,stir it to break it up and it will go. Boil it until it gels.(maybe 3 mins) The consistency it will have is like the syrup from canned peaches in light syrup. I don't like it that thick so I often decant the cooled mix into another bottle with water or cinnamon water. Keep in fridge.

Off out. 8C/46F today . Making use of that for being outside in bikini bottom and muscle shirt .

### Lahelada — 2013-08-02T16:26:34+0300

Forgot to add,yesterdayÂ´s CT was rudely interrupted by the neighbour's cat who thought that my legs made an ideal rubbing post..
No sooner had i dispatched her then arrived the other.End of focus on being a powerful tree but lots of laughter as the luffy things were adamant in not being deterred. Laughter is good,the furry things deserve a fishy treat.
I am writing this sitting outside ,it has warmed up to 9C/56F. Just looking around. I like this house .It is overgrown with all sorts of greenery.,ivy being the chief one. It is overcast so I can only take a stab at where the sun is but my internal compass is getting pretty good these days.It might have to do with the fact that when sailing in warmer times of the year we always stay out until sundown or sometimes even longer.
The improvement is matched by my increased ability to absorb , retain things I read. She is coming back and she is coming back improved. The annual software update (release 4.9 ) will see a vast improvement.

Well,.I will put this infernal machine away for now and contemplate my contemplatory nature.

### Lahelada — 2013-08-03T02:23:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-91391>

Addendum for Caroline re the tree position.

I went through the position with my other half and asked him to fine tune me. Turns out that the way the movement is shown in the video I sent you is not optimal for medicinal Tai Chi. Your knees need to be bent more, you need to be more crouched. Your weight sits on your heels and the middle of your foot. You should be able to lift the toes and flex up the balls of your feet. DonÂ´t try just imagine it. I almost sat myself on my butt checking it) The yong chun point needs to be in contact with the earth for you to root ( grounding anyone ?) Basically your knee must be form a vertical line with the tips of your feet, not over not further back.

A great teacher from over your way was Erle Montaigue, his son Eli has now taken over his school. This is a free download from his site. On page 54 there is a much better explanation than mine of how to go about this standing position, time to hold it etc.
[http://www.taijiworld.com/taiji-qigong/Free\_To\_Download/Books/Old%20Published%20Articles.pdf](http://www.taijiworld.com/taiji-qigong/Free_To_Download/Books/Old%20Published%20Articles.pdf)

### Lahelada — 2013-08-03T14:50:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-91420>

Good morning,all.

Sleep 7.5 hours straight
Food: breakfast the usual , L 2 debreczin sausages,1 oz cheese, D 2 debreczin sausages with ullucos .3 cups of tea with butter and semi gf cream , Total butter 2 ozs, coconut oil 2 tbsp, tricked out dark chocolate 1oz
CT : outside for 30 minutes in bikini and ne hour in bikini bottom and muscle shirt at 9C /56F

Not much to report . Wrote a lot yesterday so giving it a break today. One highlight of the day yesterday was though that I found the way to trick out my 72% chocolate to a higher cocoa value. The idea came to me reading the Lindt 99 ingredients.Basically it is a lower cacao mass enriched with cocoa butter and cocoa powder . I made this one with coconut oil,have probably underdone the cocoa powder on this batch but next time I know better . As it stands I have to count each piece I made as one carb. I used an ice cube tray for a mold hence the individual pieces.The next one should be really dark with cocoa nibs included for extra interest and cocoa content.
I shall call it Lindo Xocolatl as it will also feature chili .

Another highlight of teh day was that the cold morning weather will persist until end of the month so extended CT will be possible at least until then. After that I will have to invent myself a gadget that gets water out of the shower colder. I have an idea but no trials as of yet.

Caroline, I hope you find the reading interesting. What I meant was that Erle was Australian and if you are interested enough to pursue it you will probably find a teacher trained by him. Reason why we like him is that he is not the silk shirt "I am a Tai Chi master" type of guy. In one of his books he states." No black silk shirts but barefoot ,in jeans and rockn roll . " His teachings however are as traditional as can be,taught with the original purpose of Tai Chi in mind,not for show , one of the few masters actually graduated in China.
BTW, no personal interest in his site, we just like it.

**CT** : 1 hr 13C/55 f in jeans and muscleshirt, 2 hours sailing in jeans and longsleeved T shirt with 13C/55F wind 21km/13mph. My skin ended up pink even in the covered places. Further CT of 1.5 hours in jeans and T shirt at 11 C/52F

### caroline — 2013-08-06T02:40:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-91598>

Thanks - I will look after the Q&A - it should be starting soon.

About the eggs and cream......I don't have anything concrete to base that on really - just for me - I do way better without them. Others found cream not helpful if trying to lose tho.

This is just another observation - but I feel women do better eating "simple" just a few basic things. For me - I want the focus off of food and that has happened now ....I don't want it to be my recreation like alot of my friends and like it used to b for me. \[and my career as a matter of fact!\]

I love a beautiful piece of fish or a steak - don't get me wrong but I want food that is nutrient/electron dense ......

### Lahelada — 2013-08-06T18:36:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-91638>

**Seabass and Schumann for lunch.No not Robert,Winifred..**

Well,I was going to present my new recipe here which I tried yesterday but the concoction did a numebr on me . Basically I had the fleeting thought that the tea leftovers in effect spent green leaves would make an ideal supplement in my eggs. So I prepared a dry mix and then thought that it would go great with the "bolognese" too I had just made . Result 5 hours sleep only .I presume that the "caffeine" ingested reacts differently as when steeped.
I think the idea has merit but maybe not at dinner. So I got up forced down a BAB of eggs and ground beef and laid down again not wanting to start the day . Browsed the archive and found a question about Schumann frequency music. Well, there is no data whether it does anything but I was game to try.
I youtubed it and from the first moment I felt better. Now I feel revitalised. I shall experiment a bit more with this, see what this does for me just before bedtime.I was sceptic but it felt good .Might have been a fluke though.
The seabass was good too,but expensive so I will stick to my white "salmon"

I might have solved all my supplement supply problems, even for DHEA .Tomorrow I shall go check it out. If they sell everything and not just a few select items directly to the public it will be really cheap.

Going to do some CT now and face the sun which I have not done today yet.

### caroline — 2013-08-07T02:28:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-91690>

That video was breathtaking - thankyou for sharing it...

Someone here - I think maybe nonchalant - talked of getting rid of the caffeine in green tea. I think they dunk the bag in and out for 4 or 5 seconds and then throw that water away and then make the tea. Maybe I will remember where I read that.... have a great day and an awesome sleep!

### Lahelada — 2013-08-07T13:06:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-91734>

**fishy moments no more**

Sleep : 10 - 6 am Woot !
Diet : 3 meals spaced 6 or 7 hours apart . Woot !
CT : Something has to be a no- woot occasion...

Caroline thanks for the tip re green tea. I solved my two tea mysteries yesterday, cause for the fishy title. My green tea is from the mountains of Yunan. It seems that teas from there have a fishy taste which was the reason I clung on to cream. After the eating tea episode that gave me caffeine overload I actually looked up what people do with Matcha the Japanese eating tea and also why my tea tastes fishy. I had always liked green tea and thought I was remembering the taste incorrectly as I had always used sugar. Well,apparently Yunan tea tastes fishy,no reason given. However, they do state that the Chinese brew their first tea in the morning and throw away the first cup . They then re brew and keep re brewing the same leaves for the rest of the day. Hey, now even the expensive green tea I never buy is affordable if I only need 2 tsps a day and on top of that can eat it when they are spent .

I have a long roundtrip ahead of me today. It is shopping day and I have two new places to check out. The chocolatier that once served as the supplier to the Royal Spanish court, my usual organic purchases, the drugstore that sells more supplements than you can shake a stick at and finally buy some fish, too. Naturally they are all in different parts of town. However I feel refreshed after 8 hours sleep so it shall be interesting to explore. This is the way of Argentina. If it is not mainstream it is usually hidden away,has however existed since 1901 like the chocolatier and no one remembers them as they have quietly become mainly wholesalers. My other half does not even know the name. I shall surprise him with some white chocolate.

The idea for myself is to make the highest cacao percentage chocolate that I can. They advertise their various cocoa masses and cocoas and I think some will fit the bill if they do them in retail quantities.

I like the "frequential music" I discovered yesterday. I know all music is frequency music. What I mean though is music that is based around the notes of solfege. (ut,re,mi fa,sol ,la). Gregorian chant for example is based on it.

### caroline — 2013-08-08T03:13:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-91781>

Good luck on your shopping excursion - I would love that so much ..... what fun - poking around and finding treasures!

I keep thinking I must have been a puppy in a former life........

Have you seen the boron thread?

### Lahelada — 2013-08-08T12:46:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-91814>

Good morning folks,

Sleep : 6 hours ,not so great today .
Diet : great Bab at 6 then nothing for 9 hours,lunch was cheese and salami and dinner a hamburgers with cauli and brussles at 7.30pm .Bedtime was 11pm
CT:20 mnutes body icing for 5 minutes and then 15 minutes cold shower

So I did my shopping yesterday. The drugstore does not sell to the public. They are manufacturers too and just getting into the place is more difficult than getting into Fort Knox .Well ,as I knew I was close to the organic store I decided to walk instead of the bus. Two miles later I just about orientated myself again to ..take a bus . Bwahaha

Chocolate was a similar experience. I probably met the rudest woman in all of Buenos Aires who was only interested in going to lunch . However she did let me know through gritted teeth that I would need to buy 7 kilos of raw materials as their minimum. Now that is a years supply. I am not sure I want that much chocolate all at once. I did buy some amazing non alkalinised cocoa though and made some tricked out "90%" chocolate with it. I bought a normal 70% chocolate 1.75ozs ,melted it down,added 2 tbsps of coconut oil and 6 tbsps of cocoa powder in a water bath of course so as not to burn the chocolate. Heated it until a glossy mix and filled it into my ice cube container for individual 10 g bars . To explain the 90% comment, it has the bitterness of 90% but due to the CO it is actually a 78%. However as I need to include CO in my diet as well it is a happy marriage . I will get cocoa butter today or next week so the next one will be a proper 90% one .
I did find that it fills me up. I ate 3 pieces after dinner and the effect was as if I had eaten a normal bar at least . I had been worried about portion control but it is just fine.

Caroline, I have read the boron thread . It was something I had been researching some days back when I came up with cinnamon water. I have not found the pure borax yet here only the one used in welding so I was a bit cautious.Well, I have not found it. I only heard it exists via google. So I googled boron content in foods . Dried parsley flakes and cinnamon came up as being the foods with the highest boron content . I make cinnamon water as water here is heavily fluoridated and am hoping that the cinnamon limits the fluoride uptake.

[http://www.epa.gov/safewater/ccl/pdfs/reg\_determine2/healthadvisory\_ccl2-reg2\_boron.pdf](http://www.epa.gov/safewater/ccl/pdfs/reg_determine2/healthadvisory_ccl2-reg2_boron.pdf)

I ditched the pectin water for the moment . No other reason than convenience. I seem to be rehydrated so I feel it is not as urgent as before and want to dedicate time to do other stuff.

### caroline — 2013-08-09T03:21:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-91883>

How do you know that you are properly hydrated? I think I still struggle with hydration....

so - you are making cocoa crack ....you can add GF butter and pistachios and coconut too. There must be a recipe in Jack's cookbook too - I need to make some again and I want to add sea salt and chilli...

### Lahelada — 2013-08-09T14:02:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-91922>

Good morning everybody.

sleep : 5 hours
Diet : ok,3 meals
CT : no

Well times have got challenging. No money coming in for a while and a lot going out. Trying to think positive. I have an opportunity to turn this around some time next week . We shall see how it goes.
So I am rethinking as to how to make this cheaper and still keep it highly nutritious.
I was kind of regretting buying the chocolate yesterday but it is a very functional food so I shall plan it as such.

Caroline,thanks for the tips on what to put into the chocolate. I actually bought cocoa butter yesterday so it willbe "real" chocolate . I had forgotten about coconut and guess who has coconut in the house already.
You asked how I know if I am rehydrated. Well, I don't know. In the normal way of verifying ,the skin turgor test on the back of the hand,I am doing better than ever. I was never very good at keeping myself hydrated so this is the best so far. Rather than explain what it is here is a 20 sec video from a dermatologist determining the test. I am sure that it cannot be all, but it is way better than before,a baseline to work from so to speak.
[http://youtu.be/Ztn\_NmNR6jc](http://youtu.be/Ztn_NmNR6jc)

Well I shall take advantage of the cold weather and do some cold air CT. I prefer the air as I can do it for way longer. Additionally our water is heavily fluored, some nice person cares about my teeth  so I restrict the water CT to days when it is not sufficently cold.

### Lahelada — 2013-08-10T15:21:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-92019>

Good morning all

Sleep : 7 hrs
Diet : ok , probably too much chocolate though 2 ozs but got in some fish -hake.
CT: very little maybe 30 minutes of just cold air
Weight : 85.2 down from 87.4 kilos 2.2 kilos lost

Well,things are chugging along . We have the money to pay the rent until end of September and enough for eating just . Next week I will have an opportunity to make a difference. Wish me luck .

The weight loss was a nice surprise. 2.2 kilos down . I could do with an ongoing success story right about now.

Laughed a lot last night as I looked up " Always look on the bright side of life" Eric Idle sang it to the Prince Charles and changed the lyrics like this.
""When you're 60 years of age. And your mom won't leave the stage. Remember that you're still prince of Wales." Another classic of his and with a lot of wisdom "life has but a simple plot,first you are there and then you are not" Sounds depressing at first sight, but it is just another way of saying "carpe diem" [http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=g2jb9U7fXH0](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=g2jb9U7fXH0) if ya wanna see. A very British affair for sure.

### caroline — 2013-08-11T01:57:31+0300

Good morning! Great positive news on the weight loss for sure .... have you taken measurements? I have said \[ad nauseum\] that my shape changed way before my weight went down ...is this happening to you?

Good luck next week with your opportunity ..... I love your style and how you think about things ........

Having money makes things so much easier .... I know it isn't everything....but not having enough gets old pretty fast.

### Lahelada — 2013-08-11T15:41:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-92095>

Good morning,

great sleep of 8.5 hours and almost at the correct time . CT : Afraid not unless you count running around in T shirt with 14 degrees.
Food : good if you don't count the bargain meat beef . I decided to go to the wholesalers area for some good and cheap beef .Well I must have picked the wrong one because the meat was disgusting. Never mind grassfed or not grassfed ,it smelled ok when fresh but when cooked had a very strange odour and colour . It did not taste off, just strange . I had picked the oldfashioned looking butcher shop. I will try one more time in a more modern from this century butcher shop . Failing that we go back to our grassfed beef supplier and just halve the size of our portions. I should feel privileged though. The cow must have seen a few historic events like the fall of the Bastille for example. tee hee ya gotta laugh.

Suffered from constipation yesterday. Other half helped me with some Chinese medicine . I was in fits of laughter as he explained to me beforehand the procedure..Apparently the correct term for the accupressure point that deals with this is "Mouth of the Tiger". Bearing in mind the nature of the problem I thought that was hilarious. I did not laugh though when he applied pressure on the point which lies between the thumb and the index finger. It was painful. He told me that it only hurts when something is off in that region. Of course being the sceptic type once the problem had been resolved ( it was ) I asked him to do the pressure point again and sure enough it did not hurt,well a little as any hard pressure.

Well gotta run.

Edit: Forgot to answer your question Caroline. I did not take measurements as such however I am wearing jeans that fit before starting and without a belt on them I think I would not make it down the street . Nice anecdote ,went into a store recently to buy some T shirts and asked for L or Xl. The lady kindly asked if they were for me.I said yes to which seh replied : Oh no,you wnat an M ... I bought M and she was a bit kind as the Tee is snug but not snug in a "lincolnshire sausage" kind of way.

That is a huge problem for me - sizing. I always bought XL. I, likewise, couldn't beleive it when an assistant told me I needed a small shirt. Sometimes a large was too small. I just bought an XS T-shirt last week .... never in my wildest dreams.....

I still don't really understand what shape/size I am...... even looking in the mirror naked - I don't get it...

That "procedure" sounds interesting ......

### Lahelada — 2013-08-14T18:39:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-92517>

Well, today is the day. My interview for something new. We could really do with the money but I am not as pressured to get this job as I thought I would be. I mean no desperation about it. We will work things out one way or the other. (Do remind me of this when I fall apart in the future please;) I have develloped a bold strategy for the interview that is structured as " come and see if you like what we have achieved and want to be part of it" It is a new venture but old concept. It has a flaw that I became aware of this morning, so I will lead the interview to that as it is easily remedied and will effectively be the icing on the cake. I am not dressing demurely as an employee "gis a job", but am making a marked entrance,by not complying with the blue or black suit rule but am feeling myself into the ambiente the place will have.Sit up and take notice in other words If I read the situation right it is the correct approach to take. Wish me luck please.

It sounds like bread today sausage tomorrow. I am considering it though as it is a way in .
Other than that the last couple of days were a bit off in terms of CT and sleeping. I slept well the night before almost 8 hours but last night 4 and then 2 . ### Lahelada — 2013-08-16T15:59:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-92771>

Good morning everybody .

Thank you my friend for your kind words. It looks like hotel people promise more than they can keep as is the example of Inger too. I think I will take it for a while if it is offered to me. If they are as nice as the pretend to be it should not be such a bad gig. No late nights they tell me so that should be good . At the latest I should be out by 8pm.
Well to business : **CT** was haphazard all week. Usually I crave cold but this week not at all. I used our stone tiled terrace for full body exposure this week when temps were 38 degrees but the rest was pretty much underdressed for teh weather type CT an hour a day. I shall get back to the icing today.
I took a photo of my arms a month back (yes the hated bingo wings) and they have tightened up a little with CT which gives me enormous pleasure and hope.
**Diet** is ok. We went back to gf beef . I bought a very cheap cut with bone and made a puchero last night basically a stew with beef potatoes carrots and squash. The potatoes were not for me of course. I allowed for the carb creep though and made myself a portion of cauliflower on the side to not overeat within my 25 carbs limit . I would actually like to push my limit now a little bit and see at what point I am still in ketosis . I have a feeling that this is also a measure of how the healing process is going. Still thinking though.
**Sleep** : After yesterday's bad sleep of 4 + 3 hours I actually managed to have a siesta around three only half an hour, but from what I read that is a sign of normalcy returning as well. Today I slept 8.5 hours. Well, I did wake up, but it was for a couple of minutes. The good news is that it was without magnesium before bedtime . That reminds me I will need to ask a question re magnesium and calcium combined. I have a packet of that mix before I knew any better and the question is while I do not plan to buy it again whether I could use the mix for now. I am already getting 160mg from a multivit I am taking which does not have calcium.

### Lahelada — 2013-08-17T02:49:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-92879>

Wow, I hope those sausages were great. Here a kilo of organic sausages comes out at 14 dollars as well. Meat cuts are reasonably cheap but anything like sausages or cold cuts have gone through the roof. We have a starnge phenomenon at the moment where cuts of beef are cheaper than ground beef. itÂ´s part of the Argentine paradox. It is often not easy to tell whether beef is grassfed or not. One supermarket categorically states that their sis and I did perceive a quality difference even before I knew .
No,I don't know the book you mentioned.I am oit of touch with bookshops as the book shops here carry mainly national titles due top the import taxes.I will have a look around what I can find on net . I am already pleased with the way my arms are shaping up. I have not done hard CT and there is a difference so I am hoping with time they will bounce back ,which is not to say that no exercise is needed.

Just finished dinner shark (Patagonian smooth hound)and stick fish with mixed veg on the side with a spicy sauce base. Not impressed with either of them to be honest but all other fish looked like yesterdayÂ´s news.

Oh last but not least it looks like I got the job.  Negotiations next week. It could be a lot of fun or just an underpaid job dressed up in ritzy drag . We shall see.

### caroline — 2013-08-17T03:49:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-92892>

I would never have guessed that the kettle bell swings would make such a difference to my midsection. I don't even do them every day and only do about 25 swings - I will up that .....but the difference is significant. I am an apple shape and now absolutely have a waist! - especially at the back - don't know what you call that part.
Even when I used to starve myself down to a decent weight - I always had a fat roll around the middle ........now - when I sit down - there is no roll there ....I keep feeling myself there in amazement! I hope no one is watching me!

Good luck with your negotiations ... take no prisoners...and go after what you want and need!
You know your worth - so make sure they do too....

### Lahelada — 2013-08-18T19:39:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-93045>

" I found out during some radionuclide Iodine studies I ordered on some patients that the isotopes of iodine seemed to bunch up in places in the gut that corresponded to the gut lining and paracellular pathway in enterocytes. This implied to me, that maybe the _leaky gut was the designed to increase our ability to absorb iodine_ tremendously to fuel alterations to the primate brain and the mammalian body plan to give us some of the unique morphology that humans have. Iodine is needed to form T4 and T3..." (From braingut 5)

Maybe I am reading this wrong but I understand it as the leaky gut being the body's defense mechanism to make the all important iodine more available instead of being a symptom of wear and tear . That would mean though that I can use this as a way to get more iodine in my body while it exists? (within limits I know) In other words a leaky gut cure should include iodine treatment . Now I know if I go to the leaky gut RX I will probably find that this has always been the case but there is a certain magic here . Things I have read all over suddenly apply to me when before it was a lesson in quantum physics chemistry and biology.

Anyhow I am testing out something . I have applied iodine transdermally before and it disappeared . Reading up on it it is not conclusive as a test as iodine degrades on the the skin to iodide which is colourless . That would however mean that this will always happen no matter how much iodine you have applied as it is evaporation and a reaction on the skin that makes the yellow colouring disappear according to the doubting sources of Mr Google .

Well I have been applying iodine solution since yesterday . The first one disappeared in three hours, the next I did not watch , the one today has been with me for the last 5 hours . I just applied one more drop on my other arm . LetÂ´s see if it disappears just as quick as the first one or if it stays.

TOM sneaked up on me. It did it so quietly that I almost missed it. No first day cramps which I was expecting . If you remember I ditched drospirenone as it reduces DHEA and I had expected to get my usual cramps. Well no. Something is going right which please me enormously as for the foreseeable future I cannot throw money at anything to accelerate my progress.

Have a nice Sunday all.

### caroline — 2013-08-19T01:13:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-93066>

You are doing great - I am so proud of you for hanging in! lots don't .....they want a fast fix- someone to tell them what to do and then move on. This is our amazing journey of a lifetime ...and I, for one, am having a ball......

All this learning stuff hurts my head and I sure wish I could be more articulate - but - hey ...who knows what the future might hold! One of these days I may be uttering scientific principles and quoting Einstein - and really driving everyone crazy!!

Even my really dear friend is now asking me " what would Dr. kruse say about that" everyday really is amazing.....
lots of folks thought this was all woo woo - I am pretty sure that we have left them at the starting gate.....

### Lahelada — 2013-08-24T01:23:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-93559>

She is back on her epic epi paleo journey. I got the job . It is no great thing yet but it has promise as it is a start up so we grow together. It may have great benefits on the side or not . I will stick it for a little while.So I am back on a reduced spend diet. However we are managing to keep the gf beef in smaller portions and the fish for me so that is good news . Eggs will stay on the menu for a cheap fill up and the rest we knit pearl and drop.

I just had my Argentine sandperch with cauliflower an egg and chilisauce. A strangemix but it works for me . Fish was super fresh but the expensive kind.Hake is much cheaper ,$3 for 1 lb, but looked as if the dogs had already argued over it.So I got my favourite which is the sand perch which comes out at $5 for a pound.

I have not ben cting and also slept badly . Strange as it may sound,I hear my heart when I wear my ear plugs for sleeping quite strongly and it has kept me awake the last two nights.

Other than that nothing new to report.

Good morning ,
excellent day except for I still don't sleep .The heart noise has abated but now I am feeling hot again and wake up at 6 . I need to sleep until 7 I think. I will do some more changes today and reincorporate magnesium . I let that one slide due to the expense involved but I think it is needed.
Diet wise going ok . I had fish yesterday and realised that it leaves me hungry even when paired with a couple of eggs on the side . Hmm.Need to find quick BABs too as I am starting on Monday and an easy routine would be nice.
CT was half heartedly reattempted yesterday, fresh air CT for 30 minutes at temps of 45F .
Weight is down to 83.8 or 184.5 lbs.
Maybe going sailing today .Yay.

### Lahelada — 2013-09-25T03:21:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-96014>

Well,still here, sort of. I had to cut way back on everything in this first month of my new working life. I hung on to my essentials but I had to give up all my supplements and most of all my homemade 95% chocolate. I did cling onto my ketogenic diet and grounding. CT fell by the wayside until now except for going out underdressed in 50F degrees. Funny sidenote ,the guy at our corner store commented today "ooh a pullover, must be cold out today all said with a wink naturally". It was not actually but I did feel cold.Strange.However, I now have access to a bath tub so I should think that I could get a proper ice bath about once a week at least.

And today I got an unexpected present. A new water dispenser was installed at work and as the new boss likes gizmos it is top of the range. Can you guess what it has ? An RO filter .... He is probably still bemused by why that strange lady got so excited about something like a watercooler....
So the world is still in one place, I am ,we have survived the virtual invasion and are ready to go.
Crazy lunch today. RO water and Schumann frequency on the headphones..
Yeah I am still hanging in there.

### Lahelada — 2013-09-26T02:21:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-96138>

Evening all. I was just reading the question about dreaming as recently I am dreaming almost every night and was going to put a question in about it. Someone beat me to it in the " Ask Jack " section. Apparently I must be doing something right as Dr J says it is a sign of good current flow on brain surface. I must have indavertently been doing something right then. The dreams are entirely meaningless , about going shopping opening the door and standing in a field , stuff like that ,no emotions attached to them.I always used to be a person who syas that they never dream.

Jaja, Caroline ,the bossi making longterm plans and askedme if I was prepared to stay years. I can't comment as we are the newkids on the block so we are keeping everything under wraps until inauguration. Later .. I can tell you though that my job is a very hands on PR role.

Today was a good day. I manage to eat two meals a day now. BAB and dinner . I am wondering about trying to see what a shift to more fat lee protein would do for me as asked and answered in the Big BAB thread of recent. I think I am ready and if I am not I can always go back to BAB and dinner. It would certainly suit my new lifestyle more.

Weight is steadily shifting and shifting around too. I have not lost much in pounds but one of my new bosses commented that she can see a difference in shape sice I came. Must be all those stairs too. Currently I weigh in at 182 lbs a new low. Still not CTing ,actualy I am feeling cold when other people feel cold recently.

Enough for today. Gonna do some reading now.

### caroline — 2013-09-26T02:55:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-96143>

I am so glad that all is well LAH.. I can't wait to hear about your job! Can your boss afford to keep you? you will be drinking all his R/O water

The naked swim thing.....after I talked to you - I opened an email from a friend who has a pool ....and she swims naked quite often - so problem solved! She told me one time she went out to the pool with just a towel on .....her neighbour's huge dog jumped over her fence........she screamed and dropped her towel and ran away starkers!

don't make the same mistake with buying clothes as I did .... my shape is still changing. The very odd thing is - I weigh 10 pounds less than I ever have but I still could go down another 5 pounds. My body comp is so different now.
No wonder all those years I felt like a cow.....there really was a small body under the fat ....and I instinctively knew that - but couldn't fix it.

But the clothes thing ...I bought a pair of shorts about six weeks ago that were tight - and now they are loose. I really can't afford this new body

### Lahelada — 2013-10-16T01:26:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-97919>

Well, I seem to be more absent than present so it might be good to reviw what I have been doing and not doing .

**BAB**: still doing that,working almost normal some days I am not hungry at lunch other times I am and I follow my hunger accordingly.
**CT** : Have not been doing any. Only yesterday I started again with cold showers. Reason for starting was backache.More separately. I had an aversion to cold which is strange as in winter time I craved it .
**Carbs diet general** : Eating more carbs to the point that I am currently not in ketosis. Need to eat more fish.With the change of weather to warm I am craving them,nothing in particular just carbs in general. Doubtful, might be slippery slope . Still eating too close to bedtime though. Think it is the lesser of two evils. YET..
**Weight**: on 25/9 182 lbs yesterday 176.5 the most I ever lost
**Supplements**: dropped all for cost reason but found recently that magnesium chloride is dead cheap if only I can get to the store that sells it .SO I will have mag again which I think is missing.
**Concentration**: still better than before but sliding back
**Sleep**: great 8 hours a night almost at the right time 10-11 pm . Disturbed in the last two days though ,only slept 4 hours each night due to strong backache
**Aches**: The shoulder ache I complained about is gone. I have gone from not being able to carry 4 lbs in a shoulder shopping bag to being able to carry 15 lbs with no ill effect. My ankle pain is back though ( that could be improper footwear at work while being on my feet all day) Bears watching. Two days of unexplained backache however.
**Vision:** eyesight as bad as ever. I have started the Bates method this weekend which goes nicely with grounding and sungazing routines .LetÂ´s see if I can improve my vision still at my age (49) My prescription is high -6 so I am only hoping to be able to go out without glasses and recognise the number on the bus.
EMF: changed TV for less wattage one. No other changes possible at the moment except for staying of netbook which is automatically happening due to work. Reduced online hours from 7 hours to 2 . Now where is that redfaced smiley...
Light cycles: Going to bed when I should,greeting the sun and setting my body clock at midday and sunset. Wearing sunglasses at night and have only 25 w electric light after dark.

### Lahelada — 2013-10-16T02:50:09+0300

Hi C, missed you too. I have been lurking though. Yes the job is going good . It is too much work for too little pay but it will improve hopefully as soon as next month. Enjoying it and all the stair running is giving me a nice derriÃ©re as I am reliably informed

I don't know about the glasses what they block. They were the best sunglasses available from the optician at the time still made from glass and darker than any plastic lens. I have been looking at blueblockers but as I have a myopia prescription of - 6 they re expensive. As I have only few hours in the evening to do the things I want to do I prefer not to be blind and in the dark for now at least.. As soon as I have some sopare cash though I will try some plain yellow safety goggles and see how life is with that.

The ice water might happen as soon as tomorrow as we have a bath tub in the new place so I just need to sneak in for my lunch period.

Did you see my "daft " question in the beginners section about vit D and showering ? I am gathering info .If there is truth to it then that might be a reason why so many people are deficient even if they get a good dose of sun,soap being the culprit. I will research and report back.

Other than that I amabsolutely enchanted with Buenos Aires at the moment. The Paraiso trees are in bloom and the aroma reminds me faintly of lilacs which I adore and which do not exist here. I walk around with a smile on my face every time I am outside.

I am still looking for the EMF free spot at work .There are some almost free ones due to the thick walls but zero I have not encountered yet. I wonder if that has something to do with my sudden weight loss.Or is it just the stairs ? Who knows,better not question it too much.

### Lahelada — 2013-10-16T03:23:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-97946>

I will have a look what we have here in terms of yellow glasses . I donpt think hunting is big here as strange as that may sound. Cute guys ? No shortage. Additionally cat calling is still a tradition here but you need to speak Spanish to know if you allow yourself a smile or an icy stare depending on the blue element of the comment. Some need an arctic response ,others are sweet and poetic. They are not the same as chat up lines but encouraging comments the nice ones anyway . Women here are used to it ,miss them when they move to other countries but foreigners often find them hard to take. Not the best example but it moight go like this : Quick change to the shadowy side of the street. With this kind of sun a sweet thing like you will melt. Or " I would like to be the sea and you a rock because at high tide I could climb up and kiss you on the mouth " Usually it is a passing comment ,with both parties going their separate ways smiling. There might be a great age mismatch but it is not about "pairing " It is about the poetry of the moment, the speaker proud of his poetry and the recipient flattered.

### Lahelada — 2014-01-20T20:31:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-106307>

**Just because you can does not mean you should**

The title says it all I think. Since Xmas I have been taking ever more frequent forays into normalland and hey it was great. On top of it I did not gain weight, felt ok, well yes brain fog but who would not in the heat but hey...
However in the past week or so I began to notice that I was not feeling right again, that basic concepts were difficult to repeat, basically that the cotton wool in my brain in whose agility I once took so much pride in was back. I hummed and hahed and thought well it is time but..
The kick in the pants came in the form of a post on this forum talking about weight gain (yes, in this week I gained back 4 lbs of the almost 50 I lost ) and dehydration at cellular level leading to brain fog. There should really be a facepalm smiley on this forum...

So what is the plan? Planning for today only: Defrost my old fridge that will give me enough ice for some CT. I also made some tricked out meatballs with hidden algae. I never liked the taste of algae and with curried meatballs it all but disappears. It bugs me though that the algae I can easily find has the lowest iodine content((Nori). I made some paleo icecream so that is tonightÂ´s treat and who knows it might lead to another treat...Waiting for sunset and I will throw myself into the grass for a while reading a book .

It is very hot here and that has not helped matters. My job is quite physical and the place is just to big to be airconditioned so dehydration is easily explained even without the other factors.

So where am I ? I am still ahead of teh game I think .One year of living fairly strict ketosis has helped somewhat to repair my metabolism as evidenced by being able to eat like normal folks with gaining weight for quite some time until recently. However I don't think I should continue down that route. It feels the wrong road to take.

So what are my current symptoms?
1\. Brain fog
2 weight gain
3 finger joint pain
4 numbness of hands on waking

I have lost the shoulder pain that did not allow me to carry any shoulder bag and am back to being able to hump a heavy shopping bag slung over my shoulder. The ankle pain is not gone but less acute and sporadic. I manged to keep most of my weight off that I lost .I went from 224 llbs to 175.5 but am now at 179.5. Probably water weight but symptomatic nevertheless of things beginning to go wrong again. I am still sleeping reasonably well but also on the decline.

EMF is still my sore point. Lack of funds prevent me from doing too much in that respect.

Starting afresh, not ketogenic for this week at least but I might change my mind on that.

Thanks Shijin for the post re dehydration and thanks Caroline for pointing me towards Dave AspreyÂ´s paleo ice cream.

### Lahelada — 2014-01-22T14:46:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-106619>

Good news for today ,lost some weight ,water weight of course from recent carb indulges but on the right track. Down to 176.2 from 178.8 yesterday.
So I listened to the free webinar yesterday. My takeway points are as follows

\- get back into CT although I am still confused about whether doing it in fluoridated water negates the good effects.
\- look into aerating my drinking water. I looked up that wine aerator thing on the web. Not sure if I can get it here but on the same page they showed
a wine whisk,the same as you would use for whisking an omelette only smaller .Now if it aerates wine it should aerate water if you whisk vigorously
enough.I am also wondering if making pectin water again is a good idea.

\- resistant starches I had already incorprated in my diet the lowcarb ones that is. Sesame,coconut flour,linseed and of course cocoa. I made some
"bread"yesterday using coconut flour,sesame and egg basically. Pretty good. It will turn into a pizza base tomorrow . I will attach a pic

What worries me most at the moment is that I seem to be losing my grip,literally. My hands feel always "musclebound" and it is difficult to undo knots. I tried icing them with great results on the same day and the next day they were worse . My joints hurt when I make a fist. I had put it down to repetive movement strain from work but I am now on day 5 of my holiday and no improvement. I will continue with the icing and see what happens. I also supplement with anything anti inflammatory like curcumin,garlic,parsley anything that the kitchen holds.

### sooperb — 2014-01-22T19:30:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-106633>

I have a machine, German made, that oscillates and claims to banish arthritis and the like. They sometimes have them on ebay.de I have used it and I can feel a big difference between flexing fingers before I use it and after I have. It's called a Massator. Here's a link to the Australian site: [http://www.advancedtherapy.com.au/massator.html](http://www.advancedtherapy.com.au/massator.html) In the UK they are sold via shows and exhibitions and are very over-priced but I think from Germany they are a lot, lot cheaper. It may help.

Another thing I have a raging headache and am wondering if that is due to me having to enter a building today that is full of mould.The smell literally stayed on my clothes. Or maybe I am just seeing ghosts.
Good news today my weight is back down to 79.3 which is close to where I left off.
