### Lahelada — 2014-06-15T23:38:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-130941>

Looks like pati is my fish with 9.41% n3 with a relation of n6 to n3 of 0.58 ,.I cant resolve how much of it is DHA but for a standby fish it will do.
Now the reason for the search. For good fish I need to go to teh Chinese quarter which is nice but at the wrong time it can cost 2 hours of teh day which i do not always have. Frozen fish in the supermarket is usually imported and expensive iwth the exception of our riverfish . I tried sabalo today which does not comeout bad in terms of n3 at 6.74 so it is a good second ,with a n6 to n3 relation of 0.89 .
I am also interested to see that the fish is naturally yellow fleshed . Could be good for something.
[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Luciopimelodus\_pati](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Luciopimelodus_pati)
[http://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Prochilodus\_lineatus](http://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Prochilodus_lineatus)
Looks like sabalo is overfished so wont give a helping hand to that which leaves pati.
long explanation probably not interesting to anyone but for me as a quickreference whne in doubt as I am still without notebook.

other tales
Doing well with sleep, food took aa nosedive for a couple of weeks for pecuniary reasons but back in teh saddle . I did find another obsession though kumquats which you eat with the peel ,rich in quercetin,hesperidin whatever that may be good for and pectin so in total it might be a good food to eat for trying to repopulate the gut. they are in season here,the neighbour has a tree full of them although i do buy mine from the greengrocer .quite addictive the flavour, very perfumed like bergamot.
other than that not much to tell . Doing my being outside bit in the coldest part of teh day with as little clothes as possible again which makes me feel great.

I found a n interesting summary of EMF sources how to check for them with inexpensive methods for anyone who wants to dabble. The conclusions the author draws are very traditional and possibly wrong,his dietary recommendations for sure are but he describes quite accurately sources,mitigators and things that serve as an antenna. For example my earthquake proof house in Mexico I was o proud of as it was ecologicaally cool becasue it was built from basalt rocks with steel reinforced columns probably surrounded me with EMF . When Doc K says details matter it is always good to know more details .

Ah, I also did a little test. I was talking earlier about salicylate and alcohol sensitivity and got it into my head that my sulphuration pathway was off and that molybdenum would help. Well,only source i could lay my hands on that was cheap was split peas and it did help . no headaches from cinnamon and no red cheeks. now split peas are great for a quick and dirty experiment but not really for a ketogenic winter diet . So now I eat a lot moreliver which seems o do teh trick too and has more advantages besides,one being price hehe.

Bored you enough . need to check out reasonable Pc liek things now as reading never mind typing on my i phone drives me potty,probably quite literally.

### Lahelada — 2014-06-21T16:46:35+0300

Back to basics. My gut is still displeased at my sudden intake of pectins. Did the albeit not very accurate bicarb test for HCL presence and nada even after an hour. Adding to that that even as a kid I was a vinegar addict and while normal gummi bears leave me cold the super acid ones ( covered in ascorbic acid powder) I want to eat by the boatload. Maybe my pathological chile addiction features into this too. I must have made inroads as I find some chiles I previously adored too hot now. Still,leaky gut seems to be an issue still so back to that RX with OSF series in mind as well of course.

### Lahelada — 2014-06-21T17:34:05+0300

Comparing the list of so called acid producing foods with foods high in salicylates there is a lot of common ground and all my cannot put down my spoon when eating foods are on both lists.

Another interesting factoid I came across yesterday. I would have sworn that my brain fog is of recent years but a documentary about Stonehenge reminded me of a trip there that was marked by the same excess fatigue symptoms.That was in my thirties. Interestingly it was also a time when I was fascinated by socalled power places and in particular Glastonbury. The search for a more optimal geomanetic field perhaps as it coincides with my highest exposure to EMF professionally speaking.Makes me wonder what attracted me to Amsterdam. As far as I know there are few people who go to a different country for rollerskating.
Enough of the memory lane stuff. Back to basics and out to buy fish.

### Gracelind — 2014-06-22T00:21:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-131682>

Re: your comment on Stonehenge, I have been there in 1995 with horrid jet lag. Then again in 2004 on a day with dramatic clouds and fresh winds--so invigorating on that day. Lots of natural ozone on that day, maybe?

In Bath the rain poured down on us so hard we just stood under our umbrellas and laughed. Later at Glastonbury, the late afternoon sun shone low through the rain clouds--maybe we lucked out with fresh, cleansing weather?

I do know that horrid fatigue and brain fog you describe, more common than not in my, life, too. May we all find solutions.

### Lahelada — 2014-06-26T18:50:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-132344>

Well, I am here. I could not find another place I could go in just a day. I should have clarified that this is my third and last day off. But that is ok. Plenty to do and possibly better as I am still nursing a cold .
Right off the bat, is getting a cold a sign of lowered redox? I am wondering as I am spending my time in max temps of 13 degrees or 55F. Last yr I was a bit more cautious and avoided the winter llergy. Dont misunderstand x.I dont mean that cold exposure means catching cold.
I wonder what made me less resistant. Actually I just gave mysepf the answer. My three weeks of carbs did that to me. Mystery solved.
SLEEP: apart from one night I have been sleeping well but in a different timezone. Sleep lasts from 11 or 12 to 6 am minimum or even 10 am,sometimes with a break at 6 am,if I am not working that is. I see a partial

### Lahelada — 2014-06-26T19:03:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-132346>

recovery of circadian rythm in the sense that my body knows it is winter and demands more sleep but not synched to night and day. Come to think of it it seems to be demanding those extra two hours that I go to bed too late tagged on to nighttime hours which currently end with sunrise at 7.50. Yes I know what I have to do. I just dont want to.
Taking a break now to watch the football. I am an opportunist fan with the virus having a 4year incubation period. more to telp later about tensegrity 2. Have you all discovered it yet?

### Inger — 2014-06-26T22:45:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-132358>

> [Lahelada said:](/goto/post?id=132346)
>
> Yes I know what I have to do. I just dont want to.

Why you do not want to....... Bad decisions..?

I think I am almost drunk - from 1 glass of wine...haha.. and way too late. It is 22:35 PM here. I had the last glass of wine from that expensive bottle, and bye bye wine. I thought it would help my stress.. and it does just the opposite... haha I should have known that? I still can lie to myself, and say... oh man girl you have such a hard time, have some fun! Do some "bad stuff". It will help. Loosen up. But don't you think I regret it every single time. Because the fun... became ugly. Ugly nights... ugly sleep..... no waking up in oxytocin rush...... harder to sense the beauty, harder to do good decisions.

Oh man. If we only could be so wise as to know, what do we need. Or... I actually know. But to do? That is the thing. The only game changer.

I have to say, I could be so much better. It is not about self pity or anything like that. I just see clearly, when I make bad decisions, it is no good - it is not what I need
sorry for rambling in your journal Lahelada. I just wanted to talk to you - and I am proud you are still here - I am proud of every single one that is still on this forum. Seriously.

### Lahelada — 2014-06-26T23:05:41+0300

Hi Inger,
You very eloquently expressed what my throwaway line of "but I dont want to" meant. I got here ,resisted,finally did almost all the necessary and have got better because of it . I am "fine" but not reliably so as when I start to be better I ease back in my pursuit of optimal. Guess that means I am not fine,just better. It is tempting to stay up late just because I can now whereas before I fell asleep woke up at 1am spending the rest of the night trying t fall asleep again.
So today will see me in bed by 11pm and by Sunday 10. Ask me about it.

Oh and never worry about musing in my diary, you are always welcome t d so.It breathes life into it.

### Lahelada — 2014-07-01T16:43:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-132876>

So giving the dog that much dha is not a good idea. She spent all day yesterday giving it back via the same route.  Poor doggie.

I was just reading in the thread maxing autophagy. The side topic being weightloss surgery and cutting the vagus neve in the process. Caroline was surprised that doctors would lend themselves to that. I think it has to do with religion,not the overt churhgoing kind butnthe values our society holds as a result of puritan/protestant ethics that are mainstream. If you eat too much it must be gluttony and if you cannot master your baser instincts then any measure that will is good enough considering the scenario. I dont think it is conscious but serves as a justjfication for such a major intervention in bodily function. Do no harm is offset by the supposed benefits you gain in being societally acceptable.
An anecdote aside but somewhat related. I once saw a doc with a very haughty attitude who could only prescribe me the pill just this once but that I needed to be aware of calories. Well I looked him over with the same disdain and in all innocence asked him whether he would prefer me to eat X or why bread combined with x or why spread rattling off the calories and what carb fat ptot ratio is actually preferable bearing in mind that I would need to get sufficient minerals and vits ? All finished off with a trusting look in the good doctors eyes. Response: Well you gotta eat less. My response: Honestly doc ? I really did not know. Obviously I was only doing all this reading for fun...
We never saw each other again .
Now that doc meant well but could not allow the idea in his mind that maybe his wisdom did not actually lead to results. To add insult to injury he was not exactly svelte.
Anyway just a meandering musing where dogma can lead.

Cold and foggy here for a week running now. I have not been doing trad CT but cold air almost 10 hours a day ranging from temps of 4 degrees centigrade for half an hour to 45 mins to around 6 hours in temps of 12 degrees.

### Lahelada — 2014-07-01T16:55:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-132878>

Well just realised that my math was off in the previous post . 6.5 hours in real cold only. Still it is something as I wear T shirt and summer weight jeans only. To be honest though I cannot face water Ct after this. Being cold all day long to some extent I do crave a hot shower before going out in the cold. If anyone has an opinion as to whether what I am doing is beneficial feel welcome to comment. Reason I do air Ct is th at our water is fluoridated.
Changed my BPC too. I usually get ground coffee but a quick read told me that freeze dried has much less caffeine which I think has been bothering my sleep.
Enough! The sun has come out so I need to be out too. Not working today so I can increase the grade of undress.

### nicld — 2014-07-01T17:00:56+0300

She was standing by the back of the van when I was loading stuff up just waiting to get in and looked so sad. I could not take her with as is was a warmer day and she would have had to sit in the van while I dropped the DD off at respite camp.

Lets just say that she has not touched her food in the past 2 days.

### Lahelada — 2014-07-02T02:58:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-132933>

So I spent all day in the cold either outside or in the nude by the open window. Temps from 4 to 9 degrees. Naked CT was 9 degrees for one hour . That should be like 53 F.

Ate 4 ozs salmon,no pork no oysters.other than that a football day. Both Germany and xargentjna have qualified for the quarterfinals. I joked with the cornerstore owner that if Germany meets Argentina I will not know who to give my loyalty. Wise answer "the winning team" It is all good. We are not really fans but it is interesting viewing. Growing up in Germany in a football family I remember ethics questions being raised when a team committed foul after foul and ther were few teams with that rep. The home team had tobe holier than thou to pass muster. The fouls I saw today were more reminiscent of rugby than football. Me thinks it is allpart of the slippery slope we are on.
BTw the US team was great today. It is obvious that they lack experience but great promise. Other than that I liked the Ghana team . They put on a great dance routine with their goal. Thinking about Caroline and another threadnI would have to say that the shorts fashion is no good. Too long...

Other than that not much new.They are selling shark at our local supermarket so this pms CT will be used to walk up there and get some. No reason for buying it other than never having tried it.

### Lahelada — 2014-07-13T03:05:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-134126>

Lol Caroline you rock.
But no, I do what is requested of me,think about OSF7 and generally mind my own business these days. Sometimes I fall back into my old ways but I pull myself back. So not worth it.
I am concerned about the boss though. He has an op coming up of which he only remembers that it starts with lymph and that it is nothing. As far as I can see no op starting with that has a good reason. He is constantly cold too which he never used to be. Sincerely wishing him that it all amounts to nothing.

But how about mey life? Well treading water at the moment but there are things a tad further down the road. A very EMF reduced weekend retreat,a possibility to do my own thing finally albeit part time to start.
CT is slightly reduced as it js warming up a bit but I still manage an hour in around 5 0 degrees. Absolutely no seafood this week until payday next week.lots of pork ,beef and respective fat though.
Grounding and sunning still happening although less when at work.

### Lahelada — 2014-07-14T03:26:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-134234>

Tired...
Too much sun,wine and laughter.
Sailed,CTed and enjoyed the clear aair thanks to the rain and the world cup.
Encountered a bird onthe way which we had never seen before. Hugged a coniferous tree that turned out to be not. It was an Australian pine,casuarina cantrememberwhat. Quite shocked though to find more celltowers than in the city. Still I spent at least 2 hours out of reach of Wifjs.
Oh yeah Germany did win the world cup. I missed the game except for the overtime. I think as the Argentjnes have lost I will pretend to be Swiss for a while. Just call me Heidi.

Oh yeah and remind me to tel, you about the photon /eating light /crocodiles/bill Haley see you later alligator natural conversation progression... tomorrow.....falling asleep where I sit.

### Hope — 2014-07-27T03:21:36+0300

I wanted Arg to win for all the individuals I know who were absolutely excited. Collectively though it is pyobably better that there is no euphoria that papers over the cracks. My personal favourite was Costa Rics as they did really well,had never got that far and had an amazing goalie. I did feel a bit of nostalgia too though thinking about past worldcups when most of my family was still alive. So who dif I

LOL. I cannot tell you how much I miss germany already after just a few days of being back....more than I can express in words. I know it's far from perfect there, like everywhere, and yet deep in my heart I long to be there. If for nothing else, the AIR there.....it is cleaner, not so humid....anyway. OH - and the stores and streets, when Germany was playing - was the same too....everything we leer gefegt. People were watching everywhere it seems...in little and big pubs, restaurants, outside, on the beach, in parks. So cool. The Brazil game we were at the beach and we could hear people cheering from across the street from our hotel room. it is a great sport and I'm so glad there is soccer...I have never gotten much into baseball and football. I'm hoping the US will catch soccer's fire with their German coach's help. Who knows?!

Oh - and I read that at least the German team had these cooling contraptions to wear in between games or half times etc, to keep from overheating as it was so hot there. Forget what they are called now but apparently they were quite helpful. Made me wish I could reach them and teach them all about CT etc.

I sure went crazy too about those Germans winning. And yes they were lucky, in a few games...in ways there were unlucky before, often....and I'm happy they won. But definitely - ARG could have taken it for sure and I sure felt bad for them...like also for Brazil though their fouling in some of the games - not nice and it's so sad what pressure those players are under too. Yes Costa Rica was great and I thought CH played well too...I am certain if Germany played Holland they would have lost. THough I thought the same about ARG - I never thought they'd make it. But I also think it would be nice for them all to win. They all work so hard etc....and I also am like, why do I care so much for Germany. Or others for another country. At the end of the day, we are people, who cares what country we're from. I mean in the beginning we didn't have all these countries etc.

Anyway. I love your idea with the dress. You describe it so perfectly!!!!!! I recently learned the story of Coco/Gabrielle Chanel...she was an orphan and had a tough life and somehow she created an empire. Some of her choices weren't the best either but it is amazing, what she made for herself. I can't wait for you to put up a pic of the model you will make!!

### Lahelada — 2014-07-27T12:34:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-135703>

Good morning @[caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/).
Too true about that red dress. I had been wondering how to make good o n that design. Money is short,the ffer of good fabric is limited. If it is not Modal that stretches three ways till Sunday it is hard to find. I had an idea though.I had a voluminous pair of red Indian trousers which I have already turned into a floor length skirt. It should have enough fabric for a dress. I will investigate in my next days off. Made me think of the wartime ladies who painted their legs with tea and drew a line up the back of their legs to simulate nylons. definitely crazy with our modern outlook. How can you think of such frivolous things when everything is crashing around you. I think we have lost something on the way.
Gotta run,out of time. @Hope:Will get back to you later.
I have more Feynman videos to watch while making breakfast for the hotel guests. A good giggle, a true showman,a great physicist.

### Lahelada — 2014-08-06T23:45:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-137007>

So this morning I made a bow and arrow,very crude not for any purpose except to give the dog some retrieval work. Why ? Because the recently pruned tree wood was there and reminded me of making them when I was a kid.
Other than that I made a start on the red dress. The once maxi skirt iznow a midi with tendency to mini skirt. It feels real flirty around the legs so the hemline might just move up a bit more. Ihave enough fabric left for the top either with sleeves or as a halterneck. However what I have in mind right now is see how it looks with a Carmen style neckline. It might be too voluminous but it is the quickest mock up . After that cuts will get more intricate.

### Lahelada — 2014-08-08T23:51:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-137319>

Hello Inger, nope the dress is not yet finished,partly because I cannot visualise the top part with an "thats it" certainty and the other part being that there is lots on my mind at the moment. I am branching out into language teaching and translation work so that takes a lot of leg work. I have resolved though that the dress will be ready by 9th Sept which is
Vit D day, the first day when the sun is high enough to produce vit D .@ Caroline, if you are reading it is also your vit d day!

I will say something about the general economic situation. While things were bad before a lot of people are now being pushed towards the edge. Gas (the cooking type) has gone up by 300% today and inflation is running at 30% and rising.
Things will be difficult in the months to come.
Anyhoo,moving swiftly on,biohacking eyes today. For today I have not encountered a change in vision due to being back jn the EMF soup. Lets see how the week goes on. I have taken some easy measurements as to how far away I can comfortably see. Let's see if that changes over the next few months.

### caroline — 2014-08-09T02:35:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-137336>

Good morning Lah..... interesting bio hacks!

the economy sounds dreadful ......xo

People must be in so much distress....

### Lahelada — 2014-08-09T02:48:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-137337>

Good morning,Caroline.
It was Neil B s post that got me started on this. I am blind as a bat or so I thought. Well,to give you a perspective if you got on the bus and I sat in the last row I would not recognize you. After 32 years of glasses I am ready for a change.

Re the economy the mood has definitely changed. But what can you do,the only way is through. Lol,bad rhyme.

So any plsns for Vit D day? Will the red shorts get an outing?

### caroline — 2014-08-09T02:53:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-137338>

I wear them all the time! they are a little short - so just on my balcony!

I have made it my mission to wear shorts and no underwear and sandals all winter!

so I am always walking around cool .....

One good thing ...when I meet friends at the beach for coffee ...they always give me the spot in the sun! win/win
they all have long pants and turtleneck sweaters on ... and jackets!

### Lahelada — 2014-08-10T02:39:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-137435>

Interesting observation for the week. I spent an hour every day this week in a small place in close proximity to a wifi router,a large smart tv an old laptop and a couple of cellphones . Results were the same every day ,really hot dacial flushes and wild hair duento satic. Refinitelynan unhealthy room . I might just get forgetful and unplug some things..
Visual observation:
Some muscle mvements are really hard which lends credence to myneye muscles being slack. I will have to pass on sarcasm as I cannot roll my eyes very well.
I also read somewhere that longterm glasses wearers have a tendency to view every detail at the same time while non lenswearera.s flick back and forth between details to make up the picture. As objects are smaller to a myopic person with glasses it is convenient to take it all in . I tried the theory and it seems to be correct as without glasses my eyeball moves a lot more.
Random observations: With glasses in sunny places the sun dazzles me and I need to avert my eyes,without I can look reasonably comfortably into the sun. Blue light must have the same effect. How many hours did I professionally spend in front of a VDU screen? Lol showing my age.
Time without glasses today: 3 hours

### Lahelada — 2014-08-10T02:52:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-137438>

Just a little calculation: 6 hours of Pc work over 17 years and 35 years of 3 hours daily Tv. Well thats just 65000 hours of blue light. That cant be that bad,can it ? ( I can sarcastically roll my eyes virually )

### Lahelada — 2014-08-10T02:58:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-137439>

So we just did an eye rolling contest and I just cannot complete the circle . OH was watching me and the movement to the left just stops.Freaky . I am obviously not a sarcastic person or I would have noticed sooner.
I also notice that when looking out of the corner of my left eye I have less peripheral vision than on the right.

Vision experiments:
It has been so long that I went without glasses at night that I forgot: All the lights from cars traffic lights and street lights turn into stars. Really purdy .
Found out I can roll my eyes. Not sure if I had an off day the other day or if the exercises have already born fruit.
No detectable improvement in vision.

I found a forum that solely deals with natural vision improvement. I have no jntention of spending that much time on it but I did find a good addtl. exercise.

### Lahelada — 2014-08-14T12:44:34+0300

Good morning all.
A cold morning and I can say from the heart that I am now officially fed up with cold especially cold feet. Do remind me of that when I complain about the heat next week. I hope though it means that it was a winter well lived and that it served its intended purpose.
The dress designed itself
yesterday . It will be V back and front. As you may recall the fabric came from
voluminous harem trousers. My idea had been to keep as many of the seams intact so as not to have to handsew too much. That in turn is giving me mechanical problems with the skirt riding up,the cleavage being too flappy etc. Seams are a bit like dogma ,they need to be undone to allow free movement.
LOL you heard jt here first the Dogma model from the House of Philosophy. Well it could work, true religion sold well.
Kidding,maybe..who knows. Should I know? I don't.
You guessed correctly. It is 6.45 am and there is no coffee in the house...lol.
Signing off to start the day !

### Inger — 2014-08-14T15:55:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-137826>

No coffee in the house? I still love my 1 cup/day of cold cardamom coffee that i sip on for many hours.....  I think it is a mental thing?

Do not forget to post a pic from the dress, ok

I ate a flower of my bush... tasted very good! They are one sort of Malven too, the Hibiskus... so you guessed right from the beginning

It has been about 16-17 degree C inside lately, I love that. Outside around 18 C at its warmest. Like that temp too! My feet get a little cold too as the floors are stone but not really much, in the night my feet always want to be outside the blanket lately, go figure! It is like they want to breath? My feet do not easily get cold anymore... i have walked so much barefoot lately. That said, it has not been cold here yet!
Do you have stone floors too, Lah? Are they grounded too, 1st floor? I still cannot believe how lucky i am to have grounded stone floors... this winter I am going to get so skinny!!! going to be barefoot on the stone floor all the time = grounded - i actually cannot wait for the winter to be honest

If you are in a high EMF area the hot weather might not be too fun..... at least it was not for me...

### Lahelada — 2014-08-15T15:01:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-137917>

Good morning Arrrgentina!
Good morning @[Inger](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1057/). Glad it was a hibiscus. They fascinated me from childhood. We have 2 in the garden. I should try them. In Mexico they dry the red ones and make a tea infusion which is very refreshing drunk with ice. It is called Jamaica.

Our floors here are ceramic tile over concrete in the kitchen and the rest is floating parquet floor over concrete. We also have a ceramic tile patio and a bit of grass. I do spend a lot of hours at work ungrounded though.
It will be interesting to see what the heat does to me. I hav noticed that when I work in my bosses apartment I flush as If xi have a sunburn ,same when out on the patio. The bosses place is full of gizmos,router,smart tv where they sleep...And the boss is suffering from arthritis and the bossesse from backache and insomnia. Lol,mood swings too but that is my perception only...
Anyhoo,I will have to do a lot more grounding especially as two more wifis are now looking in at home. Bedroom still only has 2 though.
I was intrigued by what the doc said,that he thinks that the AC to DC rectification has a lot to answer for. It may explain why this house feels good. I don't think we have anything that has a Dc converter incorporated. Having worked with cutting edge technology for so long they lost all appeal to me. Th most up to date thing we have is a" flat screen" TV from the 90s.

### Lahelada — 2014-08-16T15:37:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-138011>

Good morning. Lovely day here. Spent the first hour of the morning letting out pork fat.
There is a question that keeps buzzing through my mind that someone asked me. Bearing in mind the restrictions here would I consider moving again. I said no . Today I read in Ingers journal that she sees her life as becoming
more nomadic to avoid more EMF encroaching on her. I see it as a battle of staY.
y and fight that is mitigate but am also wondering if that is my comfort zone talking. I am no stranger to big moves,have immensely enjoyed them but now I would really like to stay put

### Inger — 2014-08-16T16:02:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-138012>

Good morning Lah
I cannot help to think about what might come... not only what is now... and that i need a clear brain and mind to have the wisdom to help my beloved ones the best.
I cannot see me coming anywhere close to optimal bathing in man made EMF 24/7. Of course it depends how much. But we still spend time at the computer.... or I am going to, even if as safely as it gets but it is still never good. I need to balance that out. A camper seems so optimal to me.... i can travel and visit family and friends... i can just go wherever i want... or stay, and it not costing a fortune. I actually found a very nice camper for 2500 €... oh man that was a real beauty! I am a bit obsessed with this thought now. The ex called and i told him about my dream... he does not seemed very enthusiastic about it yet... he is that kind of a man that would never sleep in a tent, but always choose a nice hotel... but i just need to get him here and work on him a bit... we will see
does not matter if it takes a while, i am happy here for now. I am just knowing it is going to get worse here too..... i for sure hope not. But this is the trend I see everywhere

If I was a rich girl, i would buy property somewhere remote, like Jack. That is ideal.... i can tell as a woman i am very tied to my home and my home-surroundings.. and it hurts so bad to leave what was your home. But i have to be strong and i need to just rise over my emotions in this regard... it is about survival. But it is also about, how can i help others when they need me. How can i combine those two, because alone isolated in the woods is nothing for me. what good would that do for the world. And i am highly social too.. But i am also a woman.. and suffer more from the crazyness in our world than a man would do... we need to take special care...

### Lahelada — 2014-08-16T16:10:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-138013>

We shall see. Italked to OH and he says in general yes to a warmer place where there is work and a different mjndset. Hmm,difficult...
The dress is still a brainchild waiting tbe born. It is sort of stuck in the birthcanal. I could not sleep last night and got to reading here and thinking. The reason I do not want to finish it is that the fabric is just enough for one attempt. If I get the cut wrong I will have to make do with a skirt. I am saying this here as I recognise how ridiculous a notion it is. Do you want a dress or not,girl? It does tell me though that my dopamine levels ain't all that which given that I am surrounded by lack of dopa is not surprising. So more threelegged stool basics for me.

I did get some sleep though although disjointed ,some 6 hours or so. While not sleeping I did read here,the jetlag blog which does not really concern me as I am not planning to go anywhere ...Wrong,lots of info in there that transcends the original topic. Good practical advice as to how to increase autophagy. Recommended for everyone. Last but not least the grand Maunder mininum we are maybe facing. I had come across it in my meteo forum where people are also wondering about it, professional meteorologists and hobby ones.
I am going to look into this more deeply as it may just affect a current plan of ours either affirming or nixing it,not sure yet which.
The most immediate thing is though finding another source of income fast. We have 40% inflation here and salaries do not keep up for now anyways. Discretionary spending has dried up which is what our business models were based on. There is a lifeline ahead but only starting December. There is a Mexican mariachi song that seems apt. Translated it means.
"You dont have to be the first,you just have to know how to get there. "
Another thing that hit me last night. This smartphone affects me a lot during the night and in the morning with my typing finger going numb. That does not happen in the afternoon. I will move my usage time to later as I am obviously unzipped.
Reason for my intermittent sleep? A foolish indulgence in too much coffee. I know how much I can take but as I had not had any in a week. I overindulged. Probably also to do with the dopamine thing.
Gotta run things to do.

### Sue-UK — 2014-08-18T15:46:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-138161>

OK here goes .....

When I am an old woman I shall wear a thick purple jumper in summer
With a red hat that doesn't go, and doesn't suit me,
And I shall spend my pension on expensive supplements, crap food
And case loads of fruit juice,
and say I’ve no money for butter.
I shall sit down on the pavement when I am tired, and you will drop everything to come and get me
And gobble up food that isn’t mine, and set off alarm bells in the minds of my family, whilst they are powerless to do anything about it but worry……
And run my stick along the public railings just to be annoying,
And make sure the sobriety of my old age becomes the dampener of any festive occasion
I shall go out in my slippers in the rain, and then moan about having cold wet feet
And pick the flowers in other people's gardens,
And learn to spit, and admire my being so clever at pushing people away ……
I can wear terrible shirts and grow more fat,
And eat three pounds of sausages at a go,
Or only bread and pickle for a week, and then moan about the medical profession when they can’t fix me………….
And hoard pens and pencils and beer mats and things in boxes, so that when I am no longer around my family can clean up my squalor….
But now I must have clothes that keep me dry,
And pay my rent and not swear in the street,
And set a good example for the children.
I will have friends to dinner (whilst I still have any) and read the papers.
But maybe I ought to practise haemorrhaging electrons a little now?
So people who know me are not too shocked and surprised,
When suddenly I am old and start to wear purple!

within 30 min of arriving.

### Lahelada — 2014-08-22T01:42:09+0300

repeat this sequence three times. Rest eyes by cupping them giving them as much blackness as you can

### Lahelada — 2014-10-24T21:20:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-144971>

Time for a spring clean and dust off some cobwebs in here.
Life has been pretty funny here,not in the haha sense though. On the 30 th of July I posted a Gardel tango from the 30s that details life as it is again. In short my income did not keep up withh inflation and I had to go back to a higher carb diet. I tried to keep damage to a minimum by choosing as wisely as I could. Fish went out of the window but I hung on to pork fat,liver . I did eat potatoes carrots and peas. We did allow ourselves the occasional $1 bottle of wine.
After the initial despair of thinking that this would set me back right to the beginning my will to fight returned.
I grounded with a vengeance whenever possible,CT too. Water I could not keep up due to cost. Circadian rythm apart from out of their light cycle carbs I minded strictly and I minded the mental environment I was in. At the first sign of negativity I withdrew. A song for every day,the cheesier the better but upbeat.
My results:
\- the first few weeks were pretty surprising. No brain fog or lack of concentration. Sleep was normal at least 6 hourswith dreaming.
Things turned in the last week of September when all the old aches and pains came back ....and more. While i had never had sciatic nerve trouble before and in that week my busiest I was unable to lift my fright foot to go upstairs for example. Thanks to some auriculotherapy and THE squat I got mobility back with the exception of my ankle and the occasional feeling of stepping without impact which scared me more than the pain. The left ankle had its usual pain. No auriculotherapy could alleviate it. But it was manageable.
Then magic,well magic I made,two new income streams and I am back on xepi Paleo.
What did I learn? My redox potential seems to be better than it once was but not good. I deduce that as with one week of being back on plan ,although by choice no
ketogenic , all my pains have gone again , I have my energy back that in the end was sadly lacking. Interesting thing my vision kept improving even when eating wrong.

So where am I? Cold shower cT,sunbathing nude with epsom salt spritz,more ct at night. Epi Paleo as much as possible,fish is still rather expensive but I am getting some. Eggs are cheap so I am betting on them big time..
Pate and salami are lifesavers too. I cannot help wondering that things may have been. Better if I had not let slide the. Grounding being outside in nature Sept/Oct.
Well,confession over. Don't expect too many updates though as I am avoiding this thing so as not to eeduce the eecharge to minute trickle charging.
Oh one more thing, the blogs. I never understood them better.It is almost as if someone had taken my telescope away and given me a microscope instead. Tensegrity 7 was the easiest read so far. Kudos Dr K,your writing style is very accessible these days.

### Lahelada — 2014-11-08T00:36:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-146496>

So things are better but to get well again I had to get worse first. After changing diet I felt immediately better onlynto find one day that there was not a drop of juice left in the battery. I was so stressed out,the stress came in waves,the sensation was so physical that I literally wanted to spit it all out. No stressor on the day just a double work week without a break and on the first day off this happened. Scared the living daylights out of me. So I got busy,grounding,water,DHA,sun the lot. It has taken me until yesterday to get back to normal and maybe even
better. Our busy period is coming up and I had been worried about not being able to keep pace. But the last two days proved that I can shift into higher gear.
My food template is paleo qith some epi  meaning vegetables of all kinds welcome. Come to think of it I have been hitting sulphurous vegetables recently. Specific fruits have also called my attention, watermelon specifically also peaches,neither a great favourite in the case of watermelon an actual dislike before. Strange.

I was thinking of Inger today as I found berries growing in the middle of the city.Everyone had told me that they were growing in the park so I duly looked for bushes. Wrong! here they are berries of the tree kind,the size of a cherry tree. Yummy....

### Lahelada — 2014-11-10T00:56:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-146680>

Thirty degrees today! tThe heat does not bother m which is something I was worried about after enjoying cold so much.
Another interesting observation : This week I ran out of energy twice. I sat myself on the redclay tiled patio for 10 minutes.
and the stiff joints
disappeared and some
energy returned.
Reason this is interesting is that sitting inside did not have the same effect and that my workplace is EMF hell with about 30 wifi looking in. Sunlight plus IR heat at least in my case seems to trump Emf somewhat. I will try this more often.

### caroline — 2014-11-10T05:18:12+0200

### Lahelada — 2014-11-10T14:11:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-146748>

Glad you liked
I got some more today.
While looking up the links @[HoneyChild](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/5501/) posted ( hat tip) I came across the name of the berry I have tried this week.
Well,without beating around the mulberry bush....it is one...
Of course I googled what this fruit may contain.it does have resveratrol,in fruit but mainly in its bark. Well as I am not going to commit an act of vandalism on a public tree I started reading about the leaves. What doyou know they also come with goodness! Oxyjirinomycin no less.! Now free and beneficial is always a winning combo. So what does it do? It lowers postprandial glucose. Now that may or may not be a goal for me personally as my diet is low carb but it is a strong antiinflammatory...

nuts to boot

### Lahelada — 2014-11-30T17:43:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-148843>

my bright idea for the day. I bought a calamar,as they were dirt cheap, 30 cents for a 12 ounce ..well ..thing...My thought was if I dont eat it the dog gets it. Well youtube is your friend and I have not stopped laughing since. My comfort zone is still ginormous but my intuition about the dog was spot on...
Here a video to see what I am talking about....I will let you know which way it went..

### Lahelada — 2014-11-30T19:11:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-148846>

Lol,never have I had so much fun with 30 cents spent at the supermarket.
I cleaned the things ,there were 3,looks worse than it is. The dog was attentive since opening the packet. When I dangled a tentacle in the air above her I swear she grinned. She jumped to get it, total enjoyment. Me, I ventured to eat one of the little suckers raw ( I am not swearing I mean the podlike things on the tentacle ) but decided that maybe I should read up on eating it raw before doing so. So I cooked a couple of tentacles Galician style with garlic...Retch factor 10 out of 10. I managed to get some down the middle. The raw sucker had not been a problem. Hmm,decided to investigate. Apparently raw is quite common in Asian communities. And then I found this . My partner said he will not kiss me for a good while. My protests that the thing was dead and frozen solid were to no avail....All said in joke of course interspersed with lots of laughter.
Oh yeah I forgot yo share the reason for all the mirth. Have a read.....
[http://mobile.news.com.au/technolog...-eating-calamari/story-fn5fsgyc-1226397391618](http://mobile.news.com.au/technology/science/woman-63-becomes-pregnant-in-the-mouth-with-baby-squid-after-eating-calamari/story-fn5fsgyc-1226397391618)

### Lahelada — 2014-12-03T01:49:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-149063>

Caroline,
I would check things in this order.

1\. clear cache,cookies browsing history.
2\. scan your new downloads and the whole system for malware.
3\. download spybot or a new antjvirus and scab again if your previous result for viruses was negative.
4\. download a lesser known browser I suggest Opera and see. Youtube opens.

5\. nothing worked? Oen a 5 $ bottle for yourself

> I found this:
>
> "d oined: 01 Apr 2011, 21:18 Posts: 3 Salicylate intolerance (SI) is a condition in which affected people develop a change in the way they metabolise certain fatty chemicals which are normally part of the membranes of the bodies cells. **When aspirin/salicylates/ non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs such as ibuprofen are taken, they block the normal metabolism of the membrane lipids including arachidonic acid which is normally converted to a group of chemicals called prostaglandins.** These are involved both in some normal physiological control systems and also in the generation of inflammation (which is why aspirin and NSAIDs reduce inflammation).
>
> In SI when metabolism of arachidonic acid is blocked by salicylates, the arachidonic acid gets converted to a group of chemicals called leukotrienes which are even more inflammatory than prostaglandins. Leukotrienes cause symoms such as asthma, urticarial nettle rash and intestinal colics and diarrhoea. In 2008 I and my colleagues published a report in the British Journal of Dermatology in which we showed that a group of patients with severe SI were cleared of their symptoms by taking high doses of fish oils rich in omega-3 fatty acids.
>
> The abstract is below:
> Abstract
> We report three patients with disabling salicylate-induced intolerance who experienced abrogation of symptoms following dietary supplementation with omega-3 polyunsaturated fatty acids (PUFAs). All three patients experienced severe urticaria, asthma requiring systemic steroid therapy and anaphylactic reactions. After dietary supplementation with 10 g daily of fish oils rich in omega-3 PUFAs for 6-8 weeks all three experienced complete or virtually complete resolution of symptoms allowing discontinuation of systemic corticosteroid therapy. Symptoms relapsed after dose reduction. Fish oil appears a safe and effective treatment for this difficult and often serious condition. The rationale for the success of omega=3 fish oils is that the omega-3 fatty acids substitute for arachidonic acid and allow the formation of much less inflammatory types of leukotrienes. This effect takes at least a month, requires up to 10 capsules (10 grams) of omega-3oils, and continues to increase over 3-6 months. Such high levels of fish oils are generally safe but occasionally they can lead to nose bleeds and poor clotting of blood. I hope this is of some use in educating the correspondents to this page. Peter S Friedmann MD FRCP FMedSci, Emeritus Professor of Dermatology, University o

I've spent the better part of the past day reading through your Journal.
I had to quote this and comment on it because it is so interesting, and because I want to remember to look more into this myself when I get a spare moment.

I have glaucoma, and my specialist was very happy at the positive way my IOP decreased when he had me try prostaglandin eye drops.

I take also two advil every morning for arthritis in my big toe and chronic inflammation in my right hip. Advil is ibuprofen. Your information above essentially says that Ibuprofen blocks the ability to naturally produce prostaglandins. I find it very interesting then that I developed glaucoma (which responds positively to prostaglandin therapy) AFTER being on daily ibuprofen for about 3-4-5 years or so now (which causes my body to stop making prostaglandins properly on it's own apparently.)

Very interesting indeed that giving them omega-3 fish oil helps... I'm allergic to shellfish and have never included seafood in my diet since the day I was born in any way, shape, or form. I began taking 2 fish oil pills per day a few days ago. I hope that my eyesight improves.

Very interesting indeed! Thank you for posting this!

I see this problem as a spine surgeon because of the acceleration of disc degeneration in the spine. Orthopedic surgeons see it in the major joints. We are taught to replace those joints instead of fixing the real problem…….the altered gut. I got this message about 7 years ago and began to change the way I managed the diseases of the spine and brain. The ethanol and acetaldehyde will also help hasten the brain by allowing more inflammation into through the blood brain barrier and into the brain. When this occurs we see depression, the loss of DHA (fish oil fat), phospholipids, ceremides and sphingolipids in the brain and function slowly diminishes overtime. Protein folding becomes abnormal and things like Alzheimer’s, and Parkinson’s, and cerebral strokes become very common. When we lose DHA we tend to replace it with other PUFA’s that don’t have the. .. "

And I believe this is the post you were talking about when you posted in my journal yesterday about my resistance to alcohol. Very interesting, thank you for pointing me this way! I have a lot to consider here. I was a complete non-drinker until after my mother passed away in October 2003. I began drinking several evenings per week and continued with this habit through 2008, by which time I had become so ill in every way you can imagine, and had been referred to so many specialists for so many things without any resolution, that I decide to quit drinking completely and see if that had any impact (my nerves were behaving oddly - they tested me for MS, ALS, so many things.. now reading your quote above it all makes so much sense and is so simple...). It has had very little impact on most things but that is probably because the damage has been done. Part of why I stopped drinking in 2008 is because that was when my husband got ill with colitis that the doctors couldn't get him well from, and eventually found that our well water was infected with so much bacteria and e coli. Now I think perhaps my gut has been bad since then. Thank you very much for taking the time to read my journal and then point me in this direction!

### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-06T14:55:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-151812>

> [Gracelind said:](/goto/post?id=129631)
>
> Hi Lahelada,
>
> From the SNPs level, 23 and Me tests Alcohol Flush reaction.
>
> _Genes vs. Environment
>
> Sensitivity to alcohol— the alcohol flush reaction—depends almost entirely on a person's [genotype](https://www.23andme.com/you/journal/alcoholflush/overview/#Genotype) at two genes, [ALDH2](https://www.23andme.com/you/journal/alcoholflush/overview/#ALDH2) and [ADH1B](https://www.23andme.com/you/journal/alcoholflush/overview/#ADH1B). 23andMe currently reports your genotype at a [SNP](https://www.23andme.com/you/journal/alcoholflush/overview/#SNP) in ALDH2. It is possible that those with the AG or GG genotypes at the SNP are more sensitive to alcohol due to their genotype at ADH1B (which 23andMe does not report).
>
> The [ALDH2](https://www.23andme.com/you/journal/alcoholflush/overview/#ALDH2) [gene](https://www.23andme.com/you/journal/alcoholflush/overview/#Gene) rs671 encodes a [protein](https://www.23andme.com/you/journal/alcoholflush/overview/#Protein) called aldehyde dehydrogenase. This protein is an [enzyme](https://www.23andme.com/you/journal/alcoholflush/overview/#Enzyme) responsible for the second step of [ethanol](https://www.23andme.com/you/journal/alcoholflush/overview/#Ethanol) processing: the conversion of the highly toxic [acetaldehyde](https://www.23andme.com/you/journal/alcoholflush/overview/#Acetaldehyde) to the harmless acetic acid (vinegar). The A version of the [SNP](https://www.23andme.com/you/journal/alcoholflush/overview/#SNP) causes the gene to [encode](https://www.23andme.com/you/journal/alcoholflush/overview/#Encode) an enzyme that is completely inactive and unable to convert acetaldehyde at all. Having two copies of this version results in an extremely unpleasant experience when drinking, as the ethanol is converted into acetaldehyde and is only removed from the body very slowly.
>
> This process makes a person so sensitive to alcohol that it can be used to treat alcoholics. Alcoholics are often given a drug called disulfiram (trade name Antabuse). Disulfiram acts by blocking aldehyde dehydrogenase enzymes and causing a buildup of acetaldehyde, mimicking the naturally occurring slow version of ALDH2. Similarly, people taking Antabuse have unpleasant effects after drinking even small amounts of alcohol.
>
> An ancient Chinese herbal remedy for alcohol abuse contains a compound called daidzin. Although it acts on aldehyde dehydrogenase, it appears to do so in a way that does not cause a buildup of acetaldehyde. Instead it may affect brain chemistry and the rewarding or pleasurable effect of drinking alcohol._
>
> Can you tolerate iodine in natural form in seafood and sea veggies?

Does anyone know whether 23andme still gives this sort of info? Their website has a disclaimer on top that says they do DNA profiling for genealogy/ancestry purposes, no longer doing health-related genetic reports. I think this would fall into the category of "health-related", right? So is there someplace else to get this info that everyone refers to so often, if 23andme no longer does it? Thanks!

### fitness@home — 2015-01-06T15:07:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-151814>

The important pasrt of the 23andme results is the **raw data**. You can take your raw data file and run it through a variety of programs that are already out there and get a lot of SNP related info.

### Lahelada — 2015-03-30T21:09:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-159860>

So summer is over and with that the carb experiment or almost "normal" eating. I will try and update in the different aspects of this summer but will do it in stages as I still have not got a netbook. lol ,that was the first update.

general wellbeing: I would give that 8 out of 10. I had more energy this summer,less brain fog and a generally well balanced positive spirit. That turned in the last 2 months or so to a more up and down rythm with unexplained aches coming back , a bout of stinky sweating that lasted about three weeks. Interesting thing though at the beginning of summer I still suffered from sunburn wheras after the sweaty period I now tan where before I went from white to red to white. Iodine reloading of skin ? The sweaty period was also marked by high cortisol.

Food: Quite strange. I did eat bread over an extended Xmas period but the main carb source was fruit.Interesting that i was never much of a fruit person but as i went through the season I switched my alliance from peaches to cherries to grapes and finally to plums and pears. The pears were interesting as they are the fruit I find most boring as we had a peartree in the garden . Yet when I saw the first ones in the store they almost had a luminescence around them .
Weight is up. I dont yet know how much as the scale was banned. My clothes fit but they are tight so I would guess around 5 -7 lbs .

EMF : 4G raised its ugly head and tegh city has gone nuts with cell towers and repeaters. Every 3 blocks you get at least a repeater station on someone's roof. On my way to work I counted 25 rooftop aerials and 3 towers. Yikes. Home still feels clean and relaxing but not sure how long that will last. I had been wondering if the aches and pains are related and still do. We shall see what happens when the carbs go out of teh window again. Right now I am tapering off with vegetables,no fruit at all.

Money: Still tight ,but got a lot better.

Hair : Wow, it has not been this long and ample since I was a teenager. I even have the hint of a complete eyebrow again. Downside is needing to shave legs again after years of not having to.  I am growing my hair to see if I can grow it to my waist ,just for fun.

Job : I finally found wisdom and try not to antagonise the bosses. If it is midday and I am being told it is night then it is night. I get paid whichever way and this way my stress level is way down. I am making moves out of this system though,self employment. Teaching is already working, but for now it does not even feed the dog. I got another idea I am researching at the moment .

CT : Almost none except fro some spot icing

Fish : .....well yeah ,they don't see me as a predator really.... Hardly any fish. Autumn /Winter shall be different.

So ,been tanning in the nude ,grounding all summer but I must have overdone the fruit as my joints are achy. I have been working a lot as well so that doesn not help.
I thought I should explain what I meant by eating like normal people. I do not mean diving into the frozen TV dinner section but eating carbs without restriction other than my wanting them. I think I am starting to want fruit more now than I should so it went out of the window with the autumn solstice. I do eat strange stuff still though, the peel from fruits like prickly pear,the pith of oranges, aloe vera leaves and young loqat leaves. Oh yeah, I also turned 50 recently. Quietly slipped into that one.
Been reading a lot about the microbiome lately. Fascinating new research and some quite disturbing. Apparently there are plans to enhance your microbiome resulting in your excrement being a luminous neon colour. I had no idea that was on my wish list or anyone's for that matter. ### Lahelada — 2015-04-12T23:52:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-161022>

I promised to reveal today my raw fish delivery method. First of all thanks tothe people and places that inspired this thought process.
@[Hope2Learn](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/12220/) for reminding me that cold does not carry smell or taste that well.
Feria de las Nieves Tulyehualco for the oyster ice cream.
El Bulli for molecular cooking and making me think differently.
And finally La Mar for the Ceviche Fb photos that are pretty as a picture.

Now the problem:
Raw fish tastes,smells and look like fish.

The solution:
Encase the fish in something tasty and freeze it in swallowable chunks.

Example:
lemon juice.
Cream.
Melted butter.
Rose water.
Orange juice.
Malbec.

Use your imagination.
\*\*\*idea copyrighted excluding commercial use

Truth bombs today: i am extremely comfortable being naked outside. I am extremely uncomfortable with the idea of being discovered. So I am prolonging the experience and the worry has faded. Their problem. I made myself a private space,if they invade it it is their lookout. I had to lol. My boss just texted me and I was naked. Lol at the the sudden self consciusness. Typing this naked in the yard too.
To top off the achievement list I just ate stinky canned mackerel. How? I connected three dots,my partners comment that the little fish I eat I eat wjth the same distaste others have for the taste of prescription meds. Second thought,the thought of eating tons of fish puts me off eating any and third what if fish does not taste bad but just offends my nose?

So picture this if you will: there I am. Standjng naked in the kjtchen eating mackerel out of a tin even the undescipherable bits of its anatomy for the first time in half a century....How? WHY SIMPLE ! With a big fat clothes peg on my nose,how else...
I kid you not.

### Lahelada — 2015-05-11T02:21:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-163801>

So today I downloaded all the necessary apps to get this tablet maximised. Amongst them I found an EMF detector apps . Well it was free so I downloaded it . This sent me into a measuring frenzy.
Some results :
bed 0.2 - with spikes to 7
rest of bedroom 10 microteslas
living 10-15
kitchen : 10-15
fridge: 35
tv ; spike to 45 microts then settles at 35
powersockets: 15-30 microts
tablet keyboard : 10 -80 depending on part of the screen

really strange reading : windowsill 480 Microt but only in one square foot

and then I went outside.....

street : 0.2- 18
street with antennas on roof : 10-26
under a tree : rarely above 12
supermarket 1 : generally 5-15 exceptions near price checker (45)tills (18-24)and electronic section (40)
second supermarket : 5-20
railway crossing:25-40
metal traffic light post : 40
transformer in street : 25
outside meter area : 15-40
overhead wire junction directly underneath: 20-25

Could not think of anything else.
Apps used :EMF Analyser

### Lahelada — 2015-05-11T12:40:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-163822>

So this morning I will join the misguided souls and be glued to my mobile for the duration of my bus journey. Hold it,not what you think. I would like to see if and when the EMF load changes. And that kitchen at work...

Other than that my social quantum entangled period has started. It is cold for Argentine standards but I can still go out in skirt, T shirt and bare legs . Cold is 11 degrees centigrade,enough for polar fleeces,wintercoats and scarves here. Social because people stop me in the street to ask whether I am not cold. I tell them the reason, they hear my accent and say oh but you are from Germany. Of course this is not cold for you. As it happens my Gran used to kit me out for an Arctic trip when I went to school.Needless to say everything came off as soon as I as out of sight..
Gotta run. I will report my numbers tonight.

### Lahelada — 2015-05-14T15:30:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-164234>

Good morning folks,

the promised update on EMF in my workplace. Quite surprising really. I knew that it would be high. But
General areas: between 15 and 30,near the wifi router mainly
Wifi router : 45-50 microts
Balcony facing dozens of apartments: 20-30 microts must measure metal railing though
Kitchen stuff: industrial fridge 40-80 (the higher value when compressor active)
small electrodomestics not turned on 20-40
juicer when working 100-120 measured near motor
The price goes to the transformer though. 140 when only plugged in.
Metal food prep area:80 !
Back to the transformer..I removed it an measured the place where it
usually sits = 140 microts which is where it dawned on me. I am not
mechanically minded so forgot that the thing has magnets in it. Now
I understand why my cell freezes and needs reinitialising when I put
it on that surface. I tried putting it on the shelf above and works just
fine
Object of the exercise : Knowing what to avoid where to spend downtime. I also found it interesting that people are magically drawn to the back patio. It is theplace of the least EMF,there are plants and it looks more natural.

Enough EMF talk.

### Lahelada — 2015-05-14T15:56:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-164239>

More news. I am working on an escape plan from the above place which is why updates are more sporadic these days. The object of the exercise is to replicate my fixed income and then of course expand it. Once I get to this level I jump ship.
The new venture means working from home but having to go out regularly.People who have read here before may remember that I tried various things but lack of seed money usually meant little success. This one is going to be different.

Change of season : We are in autumn now. My sleep in summer was fair to middling. With the onset of longer nights I sleep my standard 8 hours again and am not waking up at the magical hour of 2.40 am. I am still wondering how to lower my EMF environment though as far as the bedroom is concerned. I did not want to get into shielding but may experiment that I at least have a crude way of measuring.
Cold resistance : It is still there. Yay . Sleeping with windows open at 11 C and go out in summer T shirt and skirt. People think I am three stops from Dagenham.
Lol,just wanted to make this expression international. English slang : the third stop before Dagenham is Barking. Barking mad ...geddit? Shortened to just Dagnham too.
Leaving you with on this useful note

### Lahelada — 2015-05-14T17:57:07+0300

sunning,weat sulpur burning,iodine stink,tanning,vit d mag sulfate

ignore this just future keywords for somethingb else I rhymed together this summer

The other is a display at the greengrocers. Instead of grouping veg with veg and fruit with fruit this one builds "landscapes"
I have to pass with a good camera.

### Lahelada — 2015-05-16T23:05:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-164465>

I just came back from a trip to the outer 'burbs. Man ,they have some some cell towers there. I always feel exhausted when I travel there even though it is just another bus ride,just in a different direction..
Measurements are lower than expected though. Meter not suited to pick up those freqs?
One thing I did notice though- silence ,silent enough to make me go Ahhhh. I always thought home was silent but I guess it is silent IN COMPARISON to where I work which is downtown.
Succumbed to a bowl of Bolivian Chairo ,full of wheat grains but also real bone broth. Call it a no harm done and at least the cortisol comes out unscathed,
I had time though to consider the body composition of my fellow comensales . Almost everyone over 40 is overweight,the flip side of coming down from great altitude to almost sea level? Or more to do with light conditions? Actually come to think about it one consideration does not necessarily exclude the other.
I do like the atmosphere of the place though. For some reason I think it is on the same level as New orleans is for the doc. Laissez faire is pretty much order of the day. I sat in two restaurants today for 20 minutes each in absolutely no danger of being served...Time stood still for the waiter at least.

### fitness@home — 2015-05-16T23:45:11+0300

I love the way you write! Could picture myself there

### Lahelada — 2015-05-17T15:00:01+0300

### Hope — 2015-05-17T19:01:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-164525>

> [Lahelada said:](/goto/post?id=164514)
>
> You read my mind ! I should have put the magically in quotation marks.
> I am working onsomething though that I have only realised intuitively so far. EMF and plants whta is the protection for example trees can afford . I am looking for studies but so far I have only come up with pop science stuff ,no hard science . I will keep digging. Whatcha think ?There must be some studies in german bearing in mind the whole forest /fresh air culture (wandern,Kneipp etc) .

Yes. I think Kneipp is a big one......not sure about studies as a whole, but certainly Kneipp is a great case study himself. I am so amazed at how many little southern towns put kneipp baths right in them, or along hiking paths - for legs/feet AND the arms. So amazing. It truly is such a different culture in some ways even if they pick up a lot of US garbage too. And even the food, the quality is often so much better, I really don't like to go out here anymore - except in places with the right stuff. Plus you can easily get mackerel and all kinds of fish in rolls, in dishes in any German restaurant etc. it's just so cool. Like a matjes filet with a side of veggies...yummy.

looking forward to your research results!

### Lahelada — 2015-05-21T15:47:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-164923>

More navel pondering. I figured I should leave Inger's journal in peace and muse on ad nauseam here.
We just laughed though here at my house . Sometimes you speak the truth without realising. My partner just said in a popular Zen way that every day is a new day and I replied without thinking not in my workplace.
The minute I had said that it dawned on me that this was in a nutshell exactly what it was. No matter at what time you enter or leave you live the same day .
The reason why the job is foremost on my mind today is that yesterday the longawaited clash happened and I am happy to say that it was not me at the centre of it although my colleague tried his utmost to put me in the centre of things to make up for his faux pas,whch was basically throwing his gloves at his boss.
It shook them up pretty good,so much so that they let their guard down and let things slip that should remain behind the scenes in their private life. Remember my ...itch bossesse ? Poor cow is all I can say . In truth she is the most put upon as she comes off the above mentioned ..itch when Him indoors (owner boss) pulls the strings and yanks everyone's chain. I can't and won't sort out their lives but I will not let them stress me as they have done in the past . If I lose this job before I am ready then so be it. Reason for this is that I woke up this morning at 5 and my first thought was of "THe place" the second was "stupid cow you are not paid to think for them" but after years of analysing customer behaviour it is so second nature that it is hard . Easiest way to shut off the thought machine is input does not equal output. Effort does not equal pay.
Prune your life like a rosebush, only the most viable flowers. To see what they are you need to tend the whole bush for a while. My Hallmark saying for the day.

### Inger — 2015-05-21T19:04:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-164945>

Hi Lah
My Vet bosses are constantly overworked and stressed... and seem to be a bit unsatisfied with their life...
I have realized many are... so I am concentrating on enjoying my life and not get trapped into that road
I am worth it to enjoy every single second of this beautiful heaven    I guess so?
Guess what? The boyfriend seemed to think it was wrong how I enjoy it so much.. like beeing in the woods etc... like you are not supposed to do that
but just work and work.. but yeah I work too.. but I really like my both jobs tho... and when I come home I am outside as much as I can like now I am laying on the terrace in bikini in the evening sun
I am so lucky  I work 7 days a week now but in the week days I finish around 1 PM so I have all the afternoon to tan if the sun is out
That is why I choose that job  and weekends I work all day but it is on the oyster beach and there is so fun to work too so it does not matter one bit!

### Lahelada — 2015-06-04T12:13:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-166530>

**"All I want for Chrismass iss my two front teess ..."**

Well, actually I am not so sure about that. To explain: I have two false teeth attached to a gum plate.The teeth in turn attach with a type of braces metal wire to the solid teeth next to them,a neat contraption that looks real with the wire hidden when worn. So last week it broke and I had to go without after more than 4 years of wearing them and I felt so relieved. I first of all thought that it was the lack of pressure in the mouth but wellbeing continued plus I have gone from bad plaque to no plaque...So it looks like my DC circuit has been boosted by the lack of metal in my mouth. Now the question is what to replace them with.
As I started with song lyrics I will finish with them,albeit modified.
**
"Just a li'l bit of metal let me down.."**

Austral hugs to you.

### Lahelada — 2015-06-11T14:34:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-167591>

**Writing under the influence ....
**
of a 2mg nicotine gum . First impresssions: I am currently with a head cold,very congested nose all that,have been for the past week and woke up with this again this morning. The nicotine gum cleared the congestion almost immediately. I am alllowing for this to be the effect of the high mint content for the moment. If it persists I will advise. It has been 10 minutes now and congestion has only returned slightly. It is difficult not to treat this like a chewing gum and keep on chewing it. Although just now it feels like licking an ash tray. I have never been a smoker but been around heavy smokers all my life. I did smoke clove cigarettes though when sailing and that was a sublime experience but in all my life mayb 6 packs thereof to give an idea of quantity.
Not much more to be said for now.

### Lahelada — 2015-06-11T15:29:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-167599>

So 40 minutes ago I started chewing this nicotine gum - 2 mg - and I don't want to give it up . I want another now...I won't though. The congestion has somewhat returned but it is sniffling level instead of must blow your nose right now girl . i also feel a pleasant minty cool cold on my exposed skin arms and legs .
For reference I will report anything that strikes my attention.It may just be that I am looking and would have noticed the same if I had not taken the gum.
Another thing I notice is the broad thought spectrum. I am reading the links @[Shijin13](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/969/) provided and am the same time thinking about the post by @[Paul S](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/39/) re flow experienes . I have had one that I let evaporate in the sand but ever so often I still feel the after effects ,the ruminations,reflecting back trying to recreate the passion level of that moment . I cannot get over the sensation of this having been a private event so am not ready to share it yet but am wondering whether I should. Thanks Paul for bringing it to 0my attention. I dived into your question as I did not understand it and came away with the term alotelic personality. A selfish thought too. If my experience is worth anything it should not appear in the book of someone else . It should appear in my book the one I think I should be writing but have not even started as the idea has just a binding but no pages . I am learning that selfish is not such a bad thing. Selfish gets you a lot more respect ion this societry than selfless and done right selfishness is a selfless act . Now there is a rambling thought with a grain or harvest of truth.
I realised recently that someone has adopted my way of doing things with good effect but has not given credit.
Giving credit turns a selfish act of adopting into a selfless act of sharing the love,giving back. Selfishness+act of giving back the acquired to the originator=thanks / (originator ≠owner of idea )= generosity of spirit.
That was probably in the back of my mind when I selected my signature.

End of ramble but I wante to document my mind's going ons while chewing just for a larf..

### Lahelada — 2015-06-11T15:38:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-167603>

Footnote to alotelic personality : I remembered a conversation with my older cousin who experienced me as a baby when he was around 15 .Unclear what age I was but definitely before talking stage,come to think of it I don't know when I started talking.he remarked that he thought ,him being 15,that I only watched very interested in everything but did not communicate in form of crying,babbling. fast forward to my thirties when a heyoka told me that I was a bird. Prompted he elaborated that I was like a bird on a wire constantly moving my head watching so nothing escapes my attention. Asked the question whether I had "the power" ( my NEw Age days ) he responded yes but you do not know what to do with it ,which is exactly the response I had at the end of my peak experience "Now what the heck do I do with that ? " a question I only answered partially to this day .
Now I really need to get off here...

I realised recently that someone has adopted my way of doing things with good effect but has not given credit.
Giving credit turns a selfish act of adopting into a selfless act of sharing the love,giving back. Selfishness+act of giving back the acquired to the originator=thanks / (originator nequal to owner of idea = selflessness

### Lahelada — 2015-06-13T02:16:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ever-decreasing-circles.7493/post-167879>

The good news a lb of chernia is winging its way to my stomach. I would not say I am a fish convert but a chaser of red wine ever so often (read really often) does the trick. tastewise blech one of those substances that as you chew them grow larger in your mouth. Thank goodness wine is cheap. Before anyone says cheap wine is no good my wine is 3$ but sells in the US at 20. There are benefits of being a wine producing county. The wine, Malbec ,grows at an altitude of 700 m . When I can find it I get a different one, Quara Tannat variety which grows at 1800 metres . It is more expensive though , a whole three dollars fifty...I can feel my sirtuins dancing....
Footnote : Tannat is an interesting underdeveloped variety,very small grape thicker skinned than Malbec all good things for Resveratrol content. It is not from Mendoza but from Salta province ,which is known for a very emblematic white wine ,the Torrontes, a very drinkable summer wine, complex like a Gewuerztraminer.

[http://www.anuvawines.com/tasting-a...tina-finca-quara-2010-single-vineyard-tannat/](http://www.anuvawines.com/tasting-argentina/the-best-wines-of-argentina-finca-quara-2010-single-vineyard-tannat/http://www.anuvawines.com/tasting-argentina/the-best-wines-of-argentina-finca-quara-2010-single-vineyard-tannat/)
