### Inger — 2013-06-29T08:11:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-epi-paleo-win.7509/post-89183>

Cool. I Norway we have the saying; Spis fisk sÃ¥ blir du frisk. It means; Eat fish and you will stay/get healthy. How true! There is an old Scandinavian tradition to feed children with codliver oil since forever... me too got to take it as a kid. As we were so poor and lived on almost only carbs like oat porridge and potatoes, I remember how I craved the codliver oil. I could have easily drunk a whole bottle. But we only got one tablespoonful each...lol

### marcus — 2013-06-29T08:27:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-epi-paleo-win.7509/post-89185>

I don't like studies which are concentrated on just one single aspect. But in this case... amazing!

I made sure that my wife ate at least two times a week mackerel while being pregnant and after birth while breast feeding our children. My oldest child loves doing puzzles all day which are recommend for much older children (at least two years plus, I think). Coincidence? Or just the characteristic exaggerative sense of a father?  Maybe... but I think my daughter isn't at the lower (compared to other children) end of her cognitive development.

By the way... after work i play for at least half an hour with my daughter in the garden... barefoot!

To sum up: Just trying to support my daughter as best as I can by some Jack Kruse techniques!

"Besides the development of eye cancer, cell phone radiation can also be responsible for the formation of cataracts. Burning of the eyes, pain behind the eyes, â€˜floatersâ€™ (actually little bits of gel that detach themselves from the inside of the eye and float around), and a general deterioration of the vision are all hallmarks of damage caused by cell phone radiation."

Slowly but surely the EMF story is getting out....perhaps too late for everyone.

Just think of all the kids using smart phones today and what that will portend in the next 30 years.

M-Water has undergone a subtle transformation through duplicating the forces of nature under ideal circumstances and the main difference between activated M-Water and any other type of water lies in the change of clusters of water molecules to a smaller seize, thus facilitating the transfer of water via so-called "aquaporin" channels into any cell and facilitating cellular hydration. (The discovery of aquaporin channels in cell membranes was awarded with a Nobel Prize in 2003.)

M-Water is sometimes referred to as “Primordial Water”. It is originally sourced from artesian springs in the Cascade Mountain range of Oregon. It is then subjected to a proprietary method of concussive and low-level electromagnetic forces resulting in a restructuring of the water molecules into smaller and more stable “clusters”. This “activated” or “energized” water forms crystals that show greater clarity when visualized at freezing temperatures using high precision microscopy. A study has shown M-Water to be readily available to cells and highly efficient at facilitating hydration and detoxification.

Independent clinical studies prove that drinking sufficient amounts of M-Activated Water™ created by adding small amounts of M-WaterÂ® to clean drinking water significantly increases cellular hydration within a few days and then stabilizes at higher levels over extended periods of time. In addition to increased energy and more available oxygen, drinking plenty of M-Activated Water™ results in: better absorption and utilization of nutrients, decreased inflammation, more coherent brain chemistry, a better functioning immune system, more efficient digestion and elimination, clearer thinking, more balanced emotions, improved athletics and sports and over all improved cellular chemistry.

M-WaterÂ® is a micro-structured water that contains high levels of stored energy and more available oxygen. Our patented M-Power Aqua TechnologyÂ® is utilized to transform ultra purified water using the revolutionary ElixÂ® system from MilliporeÂ® with the addition of small amounts of minerals from ancient clay sources. NMR O17 spectroscopic analysis proves that M-WaterÂ® contains much smaller H2O clusters than the same water that has not been transformed with Health Breakthroughs patent pending technology. Based on water memory transfer, ordinary clean drinking water is transformed with the addition of small amounts of M-WaterÂ®, with the stability of both M-WaterÂ® and the water it is added to – remaining stable with smaller H2O clusters for several months and even years.

These "ideal", smaller clusters of water molecules in M-Water also form a power grid of stored energy, which can be measured in (milli-)watts, a measure of stored energy. It also represents the donation of electrons and oxygen.

With the so-called Fenestra Analysing method (USA), using various parameters to test water in vitro and in vivo, it was found that the wattage of M-Water is hundreds of times higher than any other water ever tested; a direct reflection of the stored energy available to the body!

In 2004 a study was done, using the Fenestra Analysing method, with the conclusion that >90% of all people tested showed cellular dehydration, and that after drinking M-Water daily for a month, everyone who partook in the study, showed an increase in cellular hydration, the average being 22.3%.

In 2005 a follow-up study was done, this time over a six months period and with Core Water added. Again everyone who partook showed an increase in cellular hydration, the average being 35%, this time. M-Water is the only product to optimise cellular hydration, which is validated by clinical studies.

With the proven ability of M-Water to move freely into and out of cells this has to imply that M-Water plays a role in balancing the positive and negative charges of cell membranes.

The state of hydration also plays a major role with pH balance. Whereas the body fluids, like saliva, may show no sign of acidity, this is by no means a reflection on intra-cellular pH. Cells may be very acid, especially as a result of poor detoxification and subsequent accumulation of acid waste products.

Core Water essentially has the same properties as M-Water, but is 100 times more vibrationally potent. When the vibration of water is totally biocompatible, as in M- and Core Water, it has great organising power on cellular metabolism and hence will promote health and longevity. Increasing the strength of biocompatible vibration in Core Water has shown to dramatically increase cellular hydration (with all its metabolic benefits), when taken in conjunction with M-Water.

# Thread: The Best Bio-Hacking Gear?

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-06-30T19:35:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-best-bio-hacking-gear.7519/post-89246>

Hey everyone! Hope everyone's been doing well lately. I was just wondering if anyone knew of any interesting bio-hacker gear/tech that would be a wise purchase? I'm talking about anything that can help quantify various markers, somehow increase performance/well-being, etc. Personally one I would love to utilize is 23andMe, but I'll do that eventually.

I found two things that I'd love to purchase in the future, and maybe some people here would be interested in these devices:
[http://www.akersbiosciences.com/products/us/metron/index.php](http://www.akersbiosciences.com/products/us/metron/index.php) - this measures ketones released in your breath. Much more accurate than ketone test strips, although I believe that maybe if you're really keto-adapted you might not show a ton of ketones through your breath, but maybe I'm wrong. I'd love to get this to experiment with some ideas I have in my head

[http://www.akersbiosciences.com/products/us/vivo.php](http://www.akersbiosciences.com/products/us/vivo.php) - this is from the same company and measures oxidative stress through your breath. I think that'd be another great device to have. I haven't really gotten into tracking various bio-markers and what not yet, but even if I had/end up having the funds, I think I'd rather have something like this to quantify oxidative stress or something else to quantify fatigue rather than getting some cholesterol readings.

Does anybody else own anything or know of anything that would be neat for some lil bio-hacks? I already know a lot of people own/recommend magnetico sleep pads, that's why I didn't post that. Personally, before I'd even think about getting one of those, I'd want a better mattress. I was also thinking:
1\. GreenWave EMF filters
2\. Grounded Laptop
3\. Switch all bulbs for amber light bulbs
4\. RestWise: A piece of tech to 'quantify fatigue', but I think that can be used as a proxy for stress/sleep/overall health

Anybody else have any others!?

### Dali Dula — 2013-07-01T04:51:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-best-bio-hacking-gear.7519/post-89274>

Blood glucose meter. Cheap critical info. Do you or don't you have glucose metabolism issues? Take readings over a week's time, morning fasting, post prandial after different types of meals, post workout. Testing annually at check up is not good enough. I know this first hand.

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-07-01T05:32:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-best-bio-hacking-gear.7519/post-89275>

I don't have any issues with blood glucose that I know of, by that I mean, all my appointments with pediatrics and my current health care practitioner state otherwise (from simple blood and urine analysis), but obviously it isn't the most accurate testing and I've never done any 'fasted' tests. I'm just going off that, the fact that I'm young, have no family history of diabetes, and so on. I feel as though measuring blood glucose personally would offer some good information as to how my body is processing the fuel I'm putting in there, so that'd be nice. It would also be cool to experiment with how I can change things up and quantify the effects, that's why I originally wanted a blood glucose and ketone monitoring system to keep track of that. I'm assuming you own a blood glucose monitor, can you recommend one?

Blackout Shades

red head lamp for taking care of tasks after dark w/out blue lights
battery operated alarm clock
100 gallon stock tank for CTing.
RO water
Spending as much time outside as possible regardless of the season

Well in April - I had additional testing on my allergies - and in talking w/the allergist after all the testing; he told me I'm not allergic to trees, grasses, molds, pollen etc. The only allergies I have are to dust mites, cockroaches, and cats. Basically, I only respond to these 3 things at the molecular level, and my IL-4 is below 14, so I AM NOT ALLERGIC TO EVERYTHING GREEN.

I've decided to take on a thought experiment. I'm going to do some Mindlight meditation and focus on the fact that I AM NOT ALLERGIC TO EVERYTHING GREEN. I'm going to build a Mindlight ball with that thought, and push it through each cell in my body to flip the switch.

I'm also going to do the same thing for my dehydration issues, and my inflammation issues as well in addition to all the JK Protocols.

A thought can change your DNA; then that same thought can eliminate an incorrect thought on an illness that doesn't actually exist

# Thread: Stroke has a new indicator!  The tongue!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stroke-has-a-new-indicator-the-tongue.7528/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dextery — 2013-07-01T19:57:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stroke-has-a-new-indicator-the-tongue.7528/post-89306>

I have known about these indicators for years...but now a crooked tongue is another indicator.

Stroke has a new indicator! They say if you forward this to ten people, you stand a chance of saving one life. Will you send this along? Blood Clots/Stroke - They Now Have a Fourth Indicator, the Tongue:

During a BBQ, a woman stumbled and took a little fall - she assured everyone that she was fine (they offered to call paramedics) ...she said she had just tripped over a brick because of her new shoes.

They got her cleaned up and got her a new plate of food. While she appeared a bit shaken up, she went about enjoying herself the rest of the evening.

Her husband called later telling everyone that his wife had been taken to the hospital - (at 6:00 PM she passed away.) She had suffered a stroke at the BBQ. Had they known how to identify the signs of a stroke, perhaps she would be with us today. Some don't die. They end up in a helpless, hopeless condition instead.

It only takes a minute to read this.

A neurologist says that if he can get to a stroke victim within 3 hours he can totally reverse the effects of a stroke...totally. He said the trick was getting a stroke recognized, diagnosed, and then getting the patient medically cared for within 3 hours, which is tough.

\>>RECOGNIZING A STROKE<<

Thank God for the sense to remember the '3' steps, STR. Read and Learn!

Sometimes symptoms of a stroke are difficult to identify. Unfortunately, the lack of awareness spells disaster. The stroke victim may suffer severe brain damage when people nearby fail to recognize the symptoms of a stroke.

Now doctors say a bystander can recognize a stroke by asking three simple questions:

S \*Ask the individual to SMILE.

T \*Ask the person to TALK and SPEAK A SIMPLE SENTENCE (Coherently)
(i.e. Chicken Soup)

R \*Ask him or her to RAISE BOTH ARMS.

If he or she has trouble with ANY ONE of these tasks, call emergency number immediately and describe the symptoms to the dispatcher.

New Sign of a Stroke -------- Stick out Your Tongue

NOTE: Another 'sign' of a stroke is this: Ask the person to 'stick' out his tongue. If the tongue is

'crooked', if it goes to one side or the other that is also an indication of a stroke.

A cardiologist says if everyone who gets this e-mail sends it to 10 people; you can bet that at least one life will be saved.

### Hope — 2013-07-02T01:17:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stroke-has-a-new-indicator-the-tongue.7528/post-89327>

Fascinating. My MD has told me a crooked tongue may also be a sign of lyme issues.....

### colt — 2013-07-02T03:18:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stroke-has-a-new-indicator-the-tongue.7528/post-89338>

Doc correct me if this is wrong. I think neurology is huge here. The cortex fires down to the brainstem on the same side. Then from there the cranial nerves go out. The right cortex fires to the right brain stem then down the glossopharyngeal nerve to the tongue muscle. The tongue muscle is one of the only muscles that can actually push. So if the right side is not getting as much stimulation (as in a category I right in Chiro and Neuro terms) then it will not push as hard. This will cause the left to push more and the tongue will deviate to the right.
The palate is similar. It rises up. So the right side in this case would have less ability to rise. Looking at it statically we could see it lower if it has paresis or higher if it is atrophied. We look at the tongue all the time. It can tell what side of the brain is dominant. Thoughts?

# Thread: Not sure what to make of this:
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-sure-what-to-make-of-this.7530/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2013-07-01T20:46:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-sure-what-to-make-of-this.7530/post-89308>

FDA NEWS RELEASE

For Immediate Release: June 28, 2013
Media Inquiries: Andrea Fischer, 301-796-0393, [andrea.fischer@fda.hhs.gov](mailto:andrea.fischer@fda.hhs.gov)
Consumer Inquiries: 888-INFO-FDA
En EspaÃ±ol

FDA approves the first non-hormonal treatment for hot flashes associated with menopause

The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today approved Brisdelle (paroxetine)to treat moderate to severe hot flashes (vasomotor symptoms) associated with menopause. Brisdelle, which contains the selective serotonin reuptake inhibitor paroxetine mesylate, is currently the only non-hormonal treatment for hot flashes approved by the FDA.

There are a variety of FDA-approved treatments for hot flashes, but all contain either estrogen alone or estrogen plus a progestin.

Hot flashes associated with menopause occur in up to 75 percent of women and can persist for up to five years, or even longer in some women. Hot flashes are not life-threatening, but the symptoms can be very bothersome, causing discomfort, embarrassment and disruption of sleep.

â€œThere are a significant number of women who suffer from hot flashes associated with menopause and who cannot or do not want to use hormonal treatments,â€￾ said Hylton V. Joffe, M.D., M.M.Sc., director of the Division of Bone, Reproductive and Urologic Products in the FDAâ€™s Center for Drug Evaluation and Research. â€œTodayâ€™s approval provides women with the first FDA-approved, non-hormonal therapeutic option to help ease the hot flashes that are so common in menopause.â€￾

The safety and effectiveness of Brisdelle were established in two randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled studies in a total of 1,175 postmenopausal women with moderate to severe hot flashes (a minimum of seven to eight per day or 50-60 per week). The treatment period lasted 12 weeks in one study and 24 weeks in the other study. The results showed that Brisdelle reduced hot flashes compared to placebo. The mechanism by which Brisdelle reduces hot flashes is unknown.

The most common side effects in patients treated with Brisdelle were headache, fatigue, and nausea/vomiting.

Brisdelle contains 7.5 mg of paroxetine and is dosed once daily at bedtime. Other medications such as Paxil and Pexeva contain higher doses of paroxetine and are approved for treating conditions such as major depressive disorder, obsessive-compulsive disorder, panic disorder and generalized anxiety disorder. All medications that are approved for treating depression, including Paxil and Pexeva, have a Boxed Warning about an increased risk of suicide in children and young adults. Because Brisdelle contains the same active ingredient as Paxil and Pexeva, a Boxed Warning about suicidality is included in the Brisdelle label.

Additional labeled warnings include a possible reduction in the effectiveness of tamoxifen if both medications are used together, an increased risk of bleeding, and a risk of developing serotonin syndrome (signs and symptoms can include confusion, rapid heart rate, and high blood pressure). Brisdelle will be dispensed with a Medication Guide that informs patients of the most important information about the medication. The Medication Guide will be distributed to patients each time the prescription is refilled.

Consumers and health care professionals are encouraged to report adverse reactions from the use of Brisdelle to the FDA MedWatch Adverse Event Reporting program atwww.fda.gov/MedWatch or by calling 1-800-FDA-1088.

Brisdelle and Pexeva are marketed by Noven Therapeutics, LLC., based in Miami, Fla. Paxil is marketed by GlaxoSmithKline, based in Philadelphia, Pa.

# Thread: Bloating, Fatigue, + other symptoms. New Theory.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bloating-fatigue-other-symptoms-new-theory.7531/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### seanb4 — 2013-07-01T22:35:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bloating-fatigue-other-symptoms-new-theory.7531/post-89314>

For some unknown reason whenever something enters my digestive system (probably stomach/small intestines) my body produces an inflammatory response. I get really bloated afterwards (depending on food/drink volume) (even drinking plain old water), my heart rate increases and I feel more fatigued. I have tried many things but the bloating etc happens regardless (even when water is the only thing I consume).

This inflammation/stress response after eating must cause a rise in cortisol, which blocks my thyroid, and the inflammation probably causes leaky gut. This leads to the fatigue, constipation, bloating, etc.
So I need to accept that the response is going to happen every time I eat and find a way to counter the inflammation response / reduce the effects.

I understand that I will need to get some labs at some point but for the foreseeable future I cannot afford this.

So my question is, what is the best way to treat this chronic inflammation response? I know I cannot treat the underlying cause without knowing what it is so instead I will treat the symptoms. The mental and physical fatigue is killing me.

I have recently started taking pregnenolone and noticed improvements in mental fatigue, skin and hair. Any suggestions guys?

Thanks, Sean.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-07-02T04:55:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bloating-fatigue-other-symptoms-new-theory.7531/post-89347>

# Thread: More reason to be a beach bum!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-reason-to-be-a-beach-bum.7532/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Martin — 2013-07-02T00:02:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-reason-to-be-a-beach-bum.7532/post-89320>

[http://www.dailymail.co.uk/sciencet...s-good-day-beach-theres-science-prove-it.html](http://www.dailymail.co.uk/sciencetech/article-2352159/Everyone-feels-good-day-beach-theres-science-prove-it.html)

### Hope — 2013-07-02T01:16:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-reason-to-be-a-beach-bum.7532/post-89326>

Thanks for sharing and I find it kind of funny....why? In Germany, they have been offering healing visits to the ocean for decades, where you spend several weeks at the ocean, being exposed to the air and water if possible and just being there is healing, though other modalities may be added like massage, other physical therapies like hydrotherapy based on Kneipp, a 19th century monk who healed himself by bathing and wading in ice cold water (an old method of CT I guess), as well as eating healthy foods like local fish and veggies etc. Though Americans of course consider Germany a socialist country, especially when it comes to healthcare. But many average people with maybe not great means have benefited from these at least three week long therapy sessions by the north shore. Even other European countries like at least Poland have offered this too. In fact, health insurance companies, which over there are not meant to be focused on profit, help pay for these visits if an MD considers it necessary, and people can qualify - last I heard - for these visits about every four years, and patients pay an affordable copay, like $40 a day. But even if not, folks just having 5-6 weeks yearly vacation where they can go up there themselves also helps...in pretty reasonable condos or private homes with rooms for rent, compared to here.

In fact, moms often accompany their sick kids, especially kids with respiratory and/or skin issues....I know a few that have done this even recently. It's a whole different way of looking to help heal people other than drugs and surgery...though of course it's different than living near the beach too which is the ideal. The downside of course is taxes are much higher and people make less money to begin with compared to the US. But they get to live in stone houses with less mold issues generally...i actually met a personal patient of Dr Dietrich Klinghardt today (who has mold issues and that gene issue Jack talks about in his lyme/mold blog from some time ago, and actually told me she sleeps not in her house but an empty church basement - with her husband who is also sick), the German MD in Seattle who talks a lot about EMF and mold and even he says people there seem healthier generally, though of course they have EMFs and eat a bunch of bad foods etc. but apparently less GMOs than here. Fascinating stuff.

But I digress. Just meant to thank you for this article. Always appreciate it

# Thread: Frequent urination and waking up too early. How to properly adjust sleep cycle?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frequent-urination-and-waking-up-too-early-how-to-properly-adjust-sleep-cycle.7534/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 24
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### future_man — 2013-07-02T03:19:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frequent-urination-and-waking-up-too-early-how-to-properly-adjust-sleep-cycle.7534/post-89339>

I've recently made some lifestyle, dietary, and supplement changes in an effort to address what appears to be longterm Chronic Fatigue Syndrome. Overall, I feel like these recent changes have been moving me in a better direction as I'm taking less supplements and have eliminated some of the stressors and things that were aggravating the condition.

However, one area which hasn't improved as much as I'd hoped is sleep. I'm currently in my mid-40s and had sleep issues as long as I can remember. I've dealt with it over the years mostly through taking various sleep related supplements and Chinese herbs. Earlier this year, during a bad allergy season, I ended up taking 25-50mg of Benadryl a night on a regular basis which both helped significantly with the allergies as well as my sleep. About a month ago I made the decision to stop taking it along with a bunch of other supplements that may or may have not been necessary and helping.

While I'm generally able to fall asleep better now (most likely due to reduced caffeine intake), I'm still not sleeping through the sleep and waking up before I'm fully rested. Part of the issue is having to get up multiple times through the evening to urinate. I'm usually in bed by 10:30pm, asleep by 11pm or a little after, but then waking up several times after that. If I wake up in the 4-5am range, there's a good chance that I won't go back to sleep without taking some kind of sleep aid (which I am trying to limit). This amounts to about 5-6 hours of broken sleep leaving me unrested and adding to the next day's exhaustion.

The frequent urination issue seems to be happening during the day as well as the night as my body appears to be flushing a lot of fluids out. It doesn't seem like an issue with my prostate and I'm wondering if my body is doing a lot of detoxing or something. I know that I have some cortisol/adrenal issues including low sodium/chloride, low iron, and low morning cortisol.

I've also come to the conclusion that I have elevated nighttime cortisol, so I've recently started experimenting with Seriphos supplementation. I've been taking two capsules before dinner and then again two more before bed. \*I think\* it may be helping to reduce the nighttime cortisol levels as I feel more even during the night now, but I'm still waking up to urinate and my brain/body thinks its time to get up around 4-5am every morning.

Due to family and other obligations, my bedtime is pretty much set around 11pm, which means that I would ideally need to sleep to at least 6-7am to get a good night's rest. The way it feels to me is that I've been losing at least two hours of sleep for months now.

I'm thinking about shifting the Seriphos to before bed and upon waking in the middle of the night to see if that helps with the 4-5am wake up. I've also tried eating some protein or good carbs in the middle of the night when I wake up and that generally seems to help me go back to sleep. However I'd like to avoid that if possible and figure out what is causing all of this in the first place.

I have some recent lab numbers and other data and could provide it if anyone would be interested in taking a look.

Any help is appreciated.

Thanks,
John

### Jack Kruse — 2013-07-02T04:51:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frequent-urination-and-waking-up-too-early-how-to-properly-adjust-sleep-cycle.7534/post-89345>

Look up Dr. Willliam Rae's clinic in Dallas.

### caroline — 2013-07-02T04:55:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frequent-urination-and-waking-up-too-early-how-to-properly-adjust-sleep-cycle.7534/post-89346>

Hi John! Did you do a diurnal saliva ASI test so you know where your cortisol is? It really isn't very efficient to be guessing and adding in supps without knowing your levels - can be expensive and not addressing the issues correctly. Have you had your thyroid checked? free T3 and freeT4 and rT3 and antibodies? Have you done a hsCRP to see what your level of inflammation is?

Are you eating epi-paleo? are you doing C/T? Have you done the leptin reset? \[that should have been my first question\]
Dr. Kruse believes we should be getting our nutrients from food as much as possible.

What about EMF's?.... and probably the most important .... following light cycles/ circadian rhythm .... and are you drinking spring water? all of these things are hugely important.

Peeing alot is a sign of dehydration.....coffee can do that too. Are you using artificial sweeteners? They really do a number on my bladder.

Going back to the leptin reset ...... You need to eat a BAB within 30 minutes of waking after getting sun on your retina.
Leave at least 4 hours between meals and eat at least 4 hours prior to going to bed .... and you already recognise that you are going to bed too late. I know you said it is because of family commitments - but your health absolutely needs to come first.

You also need to be using blueblocker glasses in the evening - with no artificial lights on.

Sorry ..... this is a lot to take in! If you read the easy start guide it will be easier I think!

### future_man — 2013-07-02T05:55:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frequent-urination-and-waking-up-too-early-how-to-properly-adjust-sleep-cycle.7534/post-89350>

Hi Caroline,

Thank you for the reply. My comments follow.

\>Did you do a diurnal saliva ASI test so you know where your cortisol is? It really isn't very efficient to be guessing and adding in supps without knowing your levels - can be expensive and not addressing the issues correctly. Have you had your thyroid checked? free T3 and freeT4 and rT3 and antibodies? Have you done a hsCRP to see what your level of inflammation is?

Yes, here are my recent numbers (those available):

06/03/2013 Cortisol Saliva ASI

Morning 3.1 ng/ml (range given: 3.7-9.5)
Noon 2.2 ng/ml (range given: 1.2-3.0)
Evening 1 ng/ml (range given: 0.6-1.9)
Night 0.8 ng/ml (range given: 0.4-1.0)

05/06/13 Thyroid Panel

TSH 2.93 (0.450-4.500)
T4, Free (Direct) 1.31 (0.82-1.77)
T3, Free, Serum 2.4 (range: 2.0-4.4)

Didn't get RT3 at the time. Haven't done an hsCRP. Willing to do more testing if I can narrow it down to the most important ones as budget is an issue.

\>Are you eating epi-paleo?

I've been eating a fairly strict Paleo diet for about four years now. As a result, I'm much better off now than I was before, but I'm still dealing with fatigue and sleep issues.

\>are you doing C/T?

Sorry, I'm not sure what this is referring to.

\>Have you done the leptin reset?

From what I know about the Leptin reset, I believe that I went through something like this during my first year of transitioning to a Paleo diet and going low carb. I'll had to read up on it some more though, to see if there are any significant differences with whist I've been doing.

\>Dr. Kruse believes we should be getting our nutrients from food as much as possible.

I agree with this as well, and I'm constantly working on doing a better job of that.

\>What about EMF's?.... and probably the most important .... following light cycles/ circadian rhythm ....

I've made quite a few changes in regards to my sleep practices/hygiene over the last couple of years. Dark room, no ambient lighting, cool temps, good airflow, air filter for cleaner air and to drown out outside noise.

\>and are you drinking spring water?

I'm drinking filtered water mostly.

\>Peeing alot is a sign of dehydration.....coffee can do that too. Are you using artificial sweeteners? They really do a number on my bladder.

Definitely no artificial sweeteners or chemical additives. I was eating a very low carb diet for a while, but felt that it might be too much for me at the present time considering the state of my adrenals and thyroid. So, I've been gradually adding back in healthy starches (mostly sweet potatoes and yams) in small amounts to make sure that I have sufficient liver glycogen stores.

\>Going back to the leptin reset ...... You need to eat a BAB within 30 minutes of waking after getting sun on your retina.

BAB? Is that breakfast? I do eat a protein/fat breakfast every morning within about an hour or so of waking.

\>Leave at least 4 hours between meals and eat at least 4 hours prior to going to bed ....

Hmmm… I tend to eat small amounts throughout the day as it seems to keep my blood sugar more level than if I let myself get really hungry. Is that an issue?

\>and you already recognise that you are going to bed too late.

Yes, still working on that.

\>You also need to be using blueblocker glasses in the evening - with no artificial lights on.

I have a couple pairs but do not wear them regularly. I do use ambient lighting in the house as much as I can after dusk.

My problem these days tends to be not so much falling asleep as it is waking through the night. However, it sounds as if you're saying that all of this stuff could be the cause of that?

\>Sorry ..... this is a lot to take in! If you read the easy start guide it will be easier I think!

No, its good. Thank you. I'll find the easy start guide and read it.

John

### caroline — 2013-07-02T06:22:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frequent-urination-and-waking-up-too-early-how-to-properly-adjust-sleep-cycle.7534/post-89352>

C/T ..... cold thermogenesis. John there is an easy start guide for this too and it is extremely important.
It all ties together in a huge way ......limiting EMF is the most important - how are you doing with that?

If you are a gold member you have access to all the webinars. Dr. Kruse is extremely easy to listen too. Some of the blogs hurt your head a lot! I sometimes think that Dr. K. likes to hurt our head as often as possible. I have a zilch science background so I always struggle - do you have any science at all?

Actually - one more thing ..... It is very worthwhile to purchase Jack' book - it sort of is an overview and is great for referring back to. I think for someone new to this forum it might be the easiest way to start to make sense of all of this - altho I do think you have quite a good grasp already!

There are a few people here that are great at reading labs and when they have a spare moment they will give you some feedback ....

BAB is a big ass Breakfast - and Dr. K. does mean big! at least 50grams of protein - heavy in fat. I had to eat at least that and sometimes alot more - upwards of 80gr. and I am not a very big woman.
You literally have to stuff yourself and then that knocks out the cravings later in the day - It absolutely works. I used to have terrible cravings around 4:00pm. The BAB knocked all that out and I went down to 2 meals a day pretty quickly.
I also did better when I added a lot more fat - I think most here will say the same thing.
Eating a lot of little meals isn't good - but if you eat a big enough BAB hunger won't be an issue. I also did much better when I stopped all fruit.

If you are a member you can see the EMF series - actually - I think they are going to be replaying that - I think there was just an email. Jack and Misty hired an EMF expert - Michael Neuhart\] and he was excellent.

Just keep reading and asking questions......you seem very committed. This is quite an amazing journey.....

### future_man — 2013-07-04T15:51:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frequent-urination-and-waking-up-too-early-how-to-properly-adjust-sleep-cycle.7534/post-89500>

Hi Caroline,

Thanks for checking in. I was up again at 5am this morning after about 6 hours of so-so sleep. Could be worse, but I feel like I haven't had a full 7 or 8 hours of good sleep in months. I met with a new doctor yesterday to get some additional lab work done and rule out some possibilities.

The goods news is that I seem to be making some progress with my sleep patterns. I'm now generally going to bed easier with less supplementation and not waking up and having to pee quite as much. Here are the things that I think are making a difference:

1\. No caffeine after 9am
2\. 200mg Magnesium Glycinate before bedtime
3\. 2 capsules of Seriphos before bedtime
4\. A glass of water containing a 1/4 to 1/2 teaspoon of sea salt before bedtime
5\. Light protein/fat snack before bedtime if hungry

I'm getting in bed around 10-10:30pm and usually falling asleep around 11pm. I'll usually wake up to urinate around 1am or so, and take two more capsules of Seriphos and another 200mg of Magnesium. I may wake up again to pee again, and if I do, I seem to be going back to sleep easier than before.

However, my brain/body seems to think that its time to wake up every morning around 4:30-5am regardless. As best I can tell, that is when my cortisol levels begin to naturally rise for the next day. Ideally, I'd be able to sleep until at least 6-7am considering how much quality sleep I'm actually getting through the night.

So my current plan is to continue trying to delay the cortisol release as much as possible through the night and push it closer to 7am (hoping that it will reset over time). I'm also trying to go to bed earlier, but that continues to be a challenge with our family and work schedules.

A couple of side notes:

In the mornings I've been experimenting with a new-ish ritual. The first thing I do upon waking is to drink a large room temperature glass of water with a 1/4 to 1/2 teaspoon of sea salt, half of a fresh lemon/lime, and 1 tbs. of Apple Cider Vinegar (followed by a second glass of water to rehydrate and flush). I'm doing this primarily for detox reasons. I then take my dog out for a short walk around the block both for her, and to get some natural sunlight. I've also been experimenting with doing it barefoot for grounding and the sensory input.

I'm attempting to get a better anchor with the natural rhythms of light and dark throughout the day. In this same spirit, I've stopped wearing my sunglasses during the day (per Cindy's suggestion above) to let more light in and emphasize the difference between the light of the day and dark of the night.

Lastly, I purchased Dr. Kruse's book and have been reading it.

### Grizz — 2013-07-04T17:48:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frequent-urination-and-waking-up-too-early-how-to-properly-adjust-sleep-cycle.7534/post-89503>

future\_man,

I suggest that you use "Natural Salt" instead of "Sea Salt." If your sea salt is pure white in color, then all of the minerals including iodine have been stripped out. Here are examples of Natural Salt:
\* Natural Celtic Salt,
\* Himalayan Pink Salt,
\* Redmonds Salt
\* Hawaiin black salt
All are available at Amazon.Com

All forms of real salt have color, and include all the natural minerals to help in optimal health,
Grizz

### future_man — 2013-07-04T18:15:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frequent-urination-and-waking-up-too-early-how-to-properly-adjust-sleep-cycle.7534/post-89504>

Thanks Grizz! Will pick some up today.

### caroline — 2013-07-05T12:24:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frequent-urination-and-waking-up-too-early-how-to-properly-adjust-sleep-cycle.7534/post-89519>

John - you are really getting this all dialed in ....good for you! I hope Jack's book is shedding a little more light on this crazy journey!

I just had a thought about light as I was reading your post .....when you get up for the bathroom at nite time - you don't turn any lights on do you? There has been studies about putting a pen light behind someone's knee and that was enough to disrupt the light cycle.

Have you thought of wearing an eye mask?

How is the BAB going? are you still finding that you are hungry thru the day?

Some people find they sleep really well if they C/T in the evening ......

Also - I have found that regular coffee really bothers me .... I am sure it is the mould. I know that you really don't need more to read but......Check out Dave Asprey's site - The bulletproof executive. Did any of us mention BPC to you? that is bullet proof coffee with coconut oil and butter and if you like - cocoa and vanilla......... have fun reading.

Hope I am not driving you crazy!

Another thing I have noticed .... if I spend much time on my ipad I am screwed and I think a kindle isn't alot better ...... high rates of EMF. Someone here measured their ipad and there was very significantly high readings front and back. I can get away with a little in the morning..... but that's it.

I have also started adding celtic sea salt to my sparkling mineral water and really loving it lot!

### caroline — 2013-07-05T12:27:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frequent-urination-and-waking-up-too-early-how-to-properly-adjust-sleep-cycle.7534/post-89520>

One more thing - there are grounding sandals for when you are out walking the dog - if you can't be barefooted.......for obvious reasons...

### future_man — 2013-07-05T15:35:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frequent-urination-and-waking-up-too-early-how-to-properly-adjust-sleep-cycle.7534/post-89524>

\>when you get up for the bathroom at nite time - you don't turn any lights on do you? There has been studies about putting a pen light behind someone's knee and that was enough to disrupt the light cycle.

No, definitely not. I've read and observed similar things.

\>Have you thought of wearing an eye mask?

Since my bedroom is so dark, I'm not sure it would be worth the trouble as I generally do not like sleeping with something on my face unless necessary.

\>How is the BAB going? are you still finding that you are hungry thru the day?

Pretty good. I've been experimenting ways of getting a good variety of foods in large enough amounts at breakfast without eating the same eggs and sausage every day.

\>Also - I have found that regular coffee really bothers me .... I am sure it is the mould. I know that you really don't need more to read but......Check out Dave Asprey's site - The bulletproof executive. Did any of us mention BPC to you? that is bullet proof coffee with coconut oil and butter and if you like - cocoa and vanilla......... have fun reading.

I am familiar with BPC and have tried it. I ordered a bag earlier in the year and honestly wasn't that impressed with it. I can appreciate what he's trying to do with the purity of the coffee processing - but it seems overpriced to me for what you get and I'm not a fan of the particular roast. I also drink a low caffeine blend, and that's tough to do without spending a lot of money on BPC (I know they have decaf now, but its even more expensive).

\>Another thing I have noticed .... if I spend much time on my ipad I am screwed and I think a kindle isn't alot better ...... high rates of EMF. Someone here measured their ipad and there was very significantly high readings front and back. I can get away with a little in the morning..... but that's it.

I hear you. I have an iPad and while I can appreciate the convenience of it, the degree to which these devices have become so intertwined with our lives really concerns me.

\>I have also started adding celtic sea salt to my sparkling mineral water and really loving it lot!

Cool. I'm enjoying it as well.

\>One more thing - there are grounding sandals for when you are out walking the dog - if you can't be barefooted.......for obvious reasons...

I've been looking at some of those online. Does anyone have any recommendations for the better ones?

Thanks!

### caroline — 2013-07-06T06:04:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frequent-urination-and-waking-up-too-early-how-to-properly-adjust-sleep-cycle.7534/post-89580>

re - the grounding sandals .... if you pm Shiijin ....I know she has some but I can't remember the name.

I just got some Bulletproof coffee the other day for the first time and I don't think I am impressed either - for the price.
I have a nespresso machine that uses capsules and I have zero problems with their coffee - in fact I love it and all the different flavours \[they have a new chocolate one that I love\] I can even have one later in the day and have no problem sleeping ..... it is just pretty expensive to keep using the capsules.

Having said that .....Jack told me - probably a year ago now - if you have adrenal issues you shouldn't be drink!ing coffee!

One other thing......are you limiting TV? I do better with no TV at all....

I also forgot to ask you - have you gotten all EMFs out of the bedroom? clock radio etc? I was sleeping with mine next to my head! Are you able to turn the breaker to your bedroom off at nite?

### Pamela — 2013-07-02T21:09:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lunch-ideas.7537/post-89396>

I'm good on Breakfast and Dinner but have a hard time figuring out what to eat for lunch. What do you all have for lunch?

### endless — 2013-07-02T21:11:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lunch-ideas.7537/post-89397>

A can of sardines if I am out on the road....a can of sardines with some salad if I'm at home. Homemade tuna salad in an avocado.

### Dextery — 2013-07-02T22:08:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lunch-ideas.7537/post-89402>

Many of us have no desire for lunch after we eat BAB...50 gm of protein within 30 min of awakening in the morning. If you need lunch, eat more breakfast.

### Pamela — 2013-07-02T22:09:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lunch-ideas.7537/post-89403>

Ok, I had a can of sardines in the cupboard so decided to give them a try. Not too bad, thanks for the suggestion!

Yea, I do need to eat more protein for breakfast so will try that too.

### GlenPDQ — 2013-07-03T01:29:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lunch-ideas.7537/post-89410>

This recipe uses toasted bread, but maybe you could substitute a sliced vegetable or something baked with nut flour or a hollowed out tomato.
The rigas zelts (Latvian sardines) are canned in sunflower oil so maybe use a brand canned in virgin olive oil.

[http://kitchenmouse.rozentali.com/2010/03/sprats-and-avocado-sandwich/](http://kitchenmouse.rozentali.com/2010/03/sprats-and-avocado-sandwich/)

### caroline — 2013-07-03T05:24:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lunch-ideas.7537/post-89419>

yep ..... big enough BAB means I don't ever need lunch ...... but I have dinner around 4:30 and thats it! and not hungry ever later.....

### fitness@home — 2013-07-06T04:07:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lunch-ideas.7537/post-89567>

> [Pamela said:](/goto/post?id=89396)
>
> I'm good on Breakfast and Dinner but have a hard time figuring out what to eat for lunch. What do you all have for lunch?

Pamela,

About twice a week, I stop by the grocery store and visit the seafood counter. The have a steamer. I just pick out the fillet and have it seasoned lightly. 10-15 minutes later and I have a fantastic lunch. Usually pick up an avocado or some olives from the olive bar to go with it and a kombucha drink.

This is my favorite lunch...

### beach bunny — 2013-07-06T05:02:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lunch-ideas.7537/post-89574>

> [Pamela said:](/goto/post?id=89396)
>
> I'm good on Breakfast and Dinner but have a hard time figuring out what to eat for lunch. What do you all have for lunch?

# Thread: Correct me if I'm wrong: Leptin
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/correct-me-if-im-wrong-leptin.7539/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-07-02T21:47:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/correct-me-if-im-wrong-leptin.7539/post-89400>

Hey everyone! I just wanted to make a quick post rehashing the things I've learned thus far, and hopefully the members here and/or Dr. Kruse can explain if I'm grasping the concepts. I have recently just finished every blog in the leptin series except for the one on Lyme disease and Osteoporosis, but I will do that tonight. I'm putting all my annotations into a binder so I can frequently refresh, and before I start the next series..... I'm looking through every single citation/video throughout the blogs and comments. So, I have a lot more learning to do on Leptin. I'm also planning on ordering Mastering Leptin (3rd Edition) in the coming weeks. Well, let's see if I at least have a brief understanding of leptin. \*not looking at notes, lol\*

As most of you know, leptin is the 'master hormone'. Resistance to leptin occurs before Insulin resistance, sometimes years before hand (I remember 5-7yrs being the example Dr. Kruse gave). Leptin is made from our white fat cells (but I've read it is also made to a certain degree by our muscles - Lyle McDonald's book on Bromocrpytine) and is essentially the connection from our fat stores to our brain, more specifically our hypothalamus. The hypothalamus controls our endocrine system, hence the important connection between leptin, our fat, and the connection that is sent to the hypothalamus. We receive leptin via our mothers colostrum (don't remember/understand how we receive it without breastfeeding, anyone care to elaborate?), and not being exposed to breastfeeding can literally change the methylation of our DNA. What is done prior to pregnancy, during pregnancy, and for the first six years of a child's life has drastic effects. This is getting off topic, though.... back to Leptin. Very thing, as well as very overweight people can and normally are Leptin resistant. You can check this with tests as well such as HS CRP, rT3, Vitamin D, o3/o6 ratio, and even your Leptin level (over 10 would be resistant). Leptin needs a minimum of four hours to bind to leptin receptors in the brain and other areas, hence why there is no snacking in the reset, and no eating after dinner/before bed. I know that Byron Richards of Mastering Leptin recommends 5-6hrs between meals - maybe that isn't necessary, maybe it is, I don't know. I've been doing just breakfast and dinner so no worries really, occasionally a snack of chocolate or an avocado and some cooked greens with butter after 4hrs from breakfast.

Leptin needs a minimum of four hours prior to bed to bind to its receptors in the hypothalamus. The blogs delving into this (Why The Leptin Reset Works blog) are new to me, and I haven't re-read them much yet. I know leptin has something to do with dopamine receptors (D2 I believe) and melatonin. This controls for pulsatile growth hormone secretion which can aids in fat burning/apoptosis, lean muscle gain, as well as autophagy. We need this surge in GH for our well-being. This got me thinking that one of the major reasons IFing may work, aside from refeeds, is that you're not eating at night thus giving your liver/pancreas/digestive system a break while allowing leptin to bind to its receptor and receiving proper GH secretions. Since leptin controls for all our energy in the body, it makes sense that there would be a blog on Osteoporosis because our exoskeleton requires massive amounts of energy to turnover. Leptin also controls fertility, and if one is LR, they won't get pregnant because the body will perceive a state of inadequate energy to sustain a life.

I'm still a bit rusty on how protein/fat loading in the morning essentially aids in regaining leptin sensitivity/signalling, but I know it has to do with the orexins, especially HCrT1 & 2. I know Ghrelin and these orexins, as well as other hormones, account for the food that we are eating and can essentially tell apart protein, fat, and carbs. We are also stimulating the vagus nerve which sends signals, stimulating these incretin gut hormones, priming us for digestion and assimilation. This is why eating breakfast every morning, along with no snacking, aids in leptin sensitivity and more energy and an easily sustainable appetite, because the ultradian rhythms of these gut hormones, namely ghrelin, all start working in unison and at similar times of the day. Hence why Dr. Kruse says it's surgery without a blade. We are using our vagus nerve and gut hormones to account for the food were eating, rather than light cycles and what not, we are using timing of food and macronutrients.

I'm beginning to understand how leptin plays an enormous role in the so called PPP, especially for athletics. Proper leptin functioning, as well as liver and thyroid, allow us to more readily and efficiently burn fat at the muscle level (peripheral leptin sensitivity) i.e. fatty acids and/or ketones, oh, and not to mention muscle is largely made up of intra-muscular triglycerides (IMTGs), hence more efficient use of fuel during exercise/competition. This also leads me to believe that fat would aid in muscle and strength increases even more than normal - Paul Jaminet in his book speaks about using sat/monounsaturated fats for muscle building, but this isn't even taking into account leptin sensitivity and more efficient use of fat by the muscles themselves. The proper orchestrated unison of the liver, thyroid, and leptin signalling also allows UCP1 and 3 to work better, so not only can we use fat as energy, use fat at the muscle, but we can also burn off calories as free heat rather than creating ATP from it. This is another reason why calories begin to lose significance. You partition them better with leptin and insulin sensitivity (glycogen and muscle vs new fat cells), use them better (as fuel), and we can even burn them as free heat!

There is a lot more, I know. I really want to memorize how exactly leptin works with the gut incretin hormones and allows the reset to work properly. Before I dig into the citations, I plan on re-reading Hormones 101, How/Why The Leptin Reset Works, and possibly the Leptin FAQS. I want all this knowledge ingrained in my brain so I can explain it to people, as well more easily understand how to utilize various protocols to my benefit. I remember a bit about pregnenolone steal syndrome - essentially our body is not properly using LDL's for sex steroid production and it is shunted towards cortisol production because our body will never sacrifice cortisol (which makes sense evolutionarily speaking), hence we get hypercortisolism (or is it hypo? lol) which leads to it's own problems such as increased truncal adipose mass, anxiety, etc.

So.... how am I doing so far? Looking at the citation list I compiled, I still have a LOT more to learn, lol.

### Lahelada — 2013-07-04T15:37:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-foot-bathhot-shower-contraproductive.7551/post-89499>

Good morning.I seem to be fairly well cold adapted even though I only started something like 2 weeks ago. My question is this: I like cold, but I also like hot showers.I mean taking the two quite separated during the day.For the purpose of cleanliness hot feels better, for epsom foot baths I wonder if it gets absorbed better transdermally from hot wateras the exposure area is relatively small. Any experiences or guidelines?

### caroline — 2013-07-05T12:33:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-foot-bathhot-shower-contraproductive.7551/post-89521>

Apparently epsom salts do dissolve okay in cold water but I tend to dissolve them first in a little warm water.

Are you only doing cold showers? you don't put ice in your bathtub and submerge? Most people will say \[I am one\] that cold showers are sooooo hard......

### Lahelada — 2013-07-05T16:38:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-foot-bathhot-shower-contraproductive.7551/post-89531>

I am afraid it is the only option available at the moment. I only have a shower, so unfortunately immersion is not an option. I enjoy immersion more as well. Happy memories of swimming for "hours" in icy spring water. But I do use ice on my skin to cool myself down before hand so it is much more bearable to have the running cold water. I am okay with it on my front but directing the spray on my back costs me . Ice I can put on directly. Strange huh.
Thank you for answering my question about the epsom salts. I followed epsom salt
protocol of yore and panicked a bit afterwards.The next one I will do cold.

### MeghanK — 2013-07-05T17:11:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-foot-bathhot-shower-contraproductive.7551/post-89536>

@Lahelada I have been doing a hot cold contrast shower... 20" cold, 10" hot.... cycling through for 5 minutes....
@caroline.... is doing a cold shower not as effective as submersion?
@ JACK...Do you have to shiver for CT to be effective?

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-07-06T02:34:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-foot-bathhot-shower-contraproductive.7551/post-89561>

@MeghanK: I haven't been doing CT to the degree that many at these boards have been doing (will step it up soon), I just take cold showers and expose myself to the temperature it is outside no matter what in minimal clothing. I would argue from my experience and minimal reading into CT (yet) that total submersions would be a lot more effective than doing a cold shower, but you definitely still get benefits from taking cold showers - no doubt. Go look up the CT threads at MDA and one individual posts lots of studies to back up CT (because many were skeptical) and how even minimal exposure (contrast shower for a few minutes) increases your resting metabolism, burns fat, and activates BAT. That is also the reason shivering isn't _necessary_ , though it may have its own set of benefits, such as burning glycogen which also leads to beneficial hormonal effects.

### MeghanK — 2013-07-06T03:02:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-foot-bathhot-shower-contraproductive.7551/post-89564>

Thank you BigPapa... how long do you do your showers? Do you do hot/cold contrast? Have you noticed a big difference.... I don't have weight to lose so I don't know how to determine if it is effective.....

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-07-06T04:11:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-foot-bathhot-shower-contraproductive.7551/post-89568>

It really depends. I like to shower before work so I feel clean and what not and I normally only take a couple minutes in the shower in that case so I'm not running late. But any other time, I try and time myself and get a minimum of ten minutes, sometimes I've done them for a little longer. Sometimes I do contrast showers, for some reason there are some times that I feel in the mood for one, even though I really enjoy cold showers. These are purely my thoughts, but I've seen a lot of evidence for contrast showers/baths being equally as effective for fat burning, and I've heard of 'hot baths/thermogenesis' through Ben Greenfield for fat loss, and I know that our body adapts to regular exercise and we get less benefits, therefor a change in the regiment is needed.... Maybe the same thing happens with daily cold showers..? Maybe after you start accumulating more BAT your burning less fat simply because of the amount of BAT you have, and it would send your system a good shock by introducing a minute or two of hot water followed by cold again. Haven't looked into it enough to really know if that is effective or not.

In terms of weight/fat, I haven't noticed a difference, mainly because I'm already lean AND I'm trying to gain weight, and I don't have any calipers or anything to estimate my bodyfat, that would help because I would know if cold showers alone were helping me keep fat down while gaining muscle. I do notice benefits from cold showers alone, though, such as: (1) Increased alertness and 'mental energy' during and post-cold shower; (2) Only done the experiment once, but one time I took a ten minute cold shower right before lifting weights and all my lifts went up slightly and I was less fatigued (3) helps my back/neck pain and stiffness (4) Trying not to be explicit..... but it seems to get me in an erotic type mood shortly after, and it's not like I have some hormonal problems that would keep me from having a strong libido. So I see it as almost an aphrodisiac. I would really enjoy, umm, releasing some oxytocin with my fiance while taking a cold shower together, lol.

You should just track some things, go on quantified-mind.com or do brain aerobics and see how you perform after taking a cold shower, or even exercise directly after. I'd personally recommend cold showers to contrast showers, but again, maybe as I theorized up above an occasional contrast shower can burn more fat.

### caroline — 2013-07-06T04:40:58+0300

submersion is definitely better - I am going to listen to the C/T webinar again....I need a refresher!

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-07-06T04:51:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-foot-bathhot-shower-contraproductive.7551/post-89571>

@Caroline: I would also like to know how/where to get cheap epsom salts/magnesium flakes so I can start utilizing them. I'm going to look on this MDA thread, I believe I saw someone post something about where they get them for cheap, but I may be thinking about something else besides epsom salts. I'll post back when I find that thread.

In terms of CT/Contrast showers/"hot thermogenesis, everyone should look at this:
"Ben: Yeah, yeah. So your little icy cumberbun that you could wear to prom but they also, I found this this clothing company called Spartzy and its spartzy.com and what they do is they make this special fabric that’s a heated fabric and the idea behind this heated fabric they called it the T-X fabric material is it warms your muscles and by warming your muscles, it increases blood flow to the muscle and can step up metabolic rate in an area of localized blood flow and research has indeed shown that local heating that increases skeletal-muscle blood flow can also amplify fat oxidation, it can amplify carbohydrate oxidation so basically overall metabolism in whatever area is being heated and there’s a few different studies that have actually looked into what’s called the hemodynamic response to heat stress in in leg muscles and arm muscles and found that there there is indeed higher muscle metabolism when you heat up an area in that way. Its different when in response to cold so when you’re doing something like cold thermogenesis, you’re getting activation of like brown adipose tissue to generate heat to keep you warm and when you warm a muscle you get that increase in blood flow that steps up fat oxidation and carbohydrate oxidation just because of an increase in localized metabolism and this is probably why something like the protocol that we talked about at the Become Superhuman Conference a few months ago that we did where a guy named Ray Cronise was there, a NASA materials engineer and he was talking about how you get better fat loss results if you’re gonna use temperature for fat loss, **you get better results alternating hot and cold water** when you’re like taking a shower you know, 20 seconds cold to 10 seconds hot 10 times through, for example for a 5 minute shower. You get better fat loss results from doing something like that than if you were to do just a cold shower ‘cause you’re giving yourself the best of both worlds right. You increase blood flow, and then also the activation of the brown adipose tissue so ultimately, you know, this whole like hot bath thing especially if you were to follow it with like a cold shower, you could do that. You could also do something like wear one of these you know, cool fat-burner vests at work and then you know, maybe put on some of this this Spartzy fabric and you know, warm the muscle back up so, a few different ways you could play around temperature in that sense but you know, it’s kinda funny just because like you know, some people would do all this stuff and be like you know, whatever eat a hamburger for lunch and skip their workout for the day and it’s like we’re talking about the extra like 5% here. You know, granted in the case of Ray Cronise, he did show some data that indicated that even in the absence of exercise, doing this hot-cold contrast showers twice a day for 5-10 minutes, he was actually inducing 20-30 pound weight loss in some of the folks he was working with but again.."

Found that from this [http://www.bengreenfieldfitness.com/2013/06/episode-244-full-transcript/](http://www.bengreenfieldfitness.com/2013/06/episode-244-full-transcript/)

I'm thinking of just doing contrast showers now, with occasional full-blown cold showers before working out, then maybe occasionally throw in cold bath (can't afford ice baths, can throw in some pre-made ice, though) once or so a week for the 'cold adaptation' and activation of BAT and to deplete glycogen, enhance ketosis, etc.

### Lahelada — 2013-07-06T15:42:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-foot-bathhot-shower-contraproductive.7551/post-89596>

Glad my moment of panic sparked a lively conversation. I think I prefer to keep the hot and cold separate. I don't know why, it just feels right. If I had to find a reason then I would say that the effort involved to getting back to normal body temperature is what pulls fat out of the brown fat cells if you have them. Hot/ cold showers seem to short a space of time to make much difference. I mean the body does not feel sufficiently stressed to do something drastic like make BAT. In other words it is not the temperature introduced externally per se,but the duration of the "stress"and the work you give your body to get back to its preferred "normal" that instigates the BAT activation.
Being ergonomic by design in its use of energy, body can deal with lesser stress simply by shivering thermogenesis, which according to some studies I have read is not conducive to reactivating BAT, unlike non shivering thermogenesis. Just my few cents.

### caroline — 2013-07-07T11:11:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-foot-bathhot-shower-contraproductive.7551/post-89639>

I like your 2 cents ..... and I like your Einstein quote.........

# Thread: Getting back to a normal weight from the other end of the scale
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 427
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hemming — 2013-07-04T22:10:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-89507>

As opposed to many other who come here I have the problem that I need to gain weight - not lose it. I've already told some of my story in this thread [http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?7308-How-to-recover-from-anorexia](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?7308-How-to-recover-from-anorexia) (I really appreciate all the replies I got) but figured I wanted to make it a bit more formal and with ongoing updates. I really hope that a lot of the people here will take part in my journey with comments, questions etc.

I'm a 26y old male, 5'9''. My story is that I developed anorexia athletica after I was suddenly done studying, had the perfect job but didn't know what to do with my life outside of that as I had no clear goal(s) which I was use to (as in get and education and get a job). Following that I developed an eating disorder which took me to a weight of 108lbs at the lowest, I'm currently around 113.5-114.5lbs. I've been feeling miserable both physically and mentally for around 1y now but after I discovered Jack's blog and resets my mood has definitely increased. For the last year I've followed a paleo diet and for the last eight months, more or less a ketogenic diet. I've been through all of the physical and mental symptoms of anorexia which I can elaborate on if anyone is interested.

I wanted to start this journal as I will go on medical leave from my job tomorrow to get time to relax and get myself back in shape. It's amazing how much the reset protocols have helped me already but there is still a long way to go, despite my weight being where it is I still find it very difficult to convince myself that I should gain although I don't want to lose weight either.
The thing that has surprised me the most is how effective CT has been in improving my mood. I take a cold shower in the morning before breakfast and another one before leaving work or going to bed. Additionally, I take a swim in the ocean 2-3 times per week for 15-20min - that is my way of getting high.

My plan for the next couple of months is to 'get back to mother nature' by continuing with the protocols and especially ocean swims since summer is on and I live close to the ocean. I'm also seeing a specialist clinic with a doctor, psychologist, dietitian etc., but honestly, I don't understand their methods and especially don't believe in their dietary guidelines.

### caroline — 2013-07-05T13:10:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-89523>

Honestly - I think you should be swimming in the ocean every single day and walking on the rocks and lots and lots of sun.
Dr. Kruse has said somewhere here .....if you live near the ocean and you are not in it every single day ....you are a damn fool!! This is the perfect opportunity for you to spend everyday at the ocean with as little clothing on as possible.

What about fishing? what about camping? you need to enjoy every single second of this spectacular opportunity you have.
Wake up early - get the sun on you - have your BAB...... and head to the ocean.

Do you know anything about Movnat? google it and have a read. Erwan is an amazing young man who is tuned into nature 100%.

Cold showers are only okay if you can't do full immersion - a bathtub full of ice would be so great for you. Do you have a hot tub outside? Dr. K. uses his hot tub as a cold tub ....just add ice and stir .... no clothing allowed! \[you don't want tan lines! and you need the sun on every single inch of your body\]

All of your health problems didn't just happen - they were along time in the making .... and so it is going to take awhile to get better ....just baby steps and enjoy the ride

Are you reading all JK's blogs? You will discover that anorexia and obesity are on the same spectrum - just at opposite ends. Have you listened to the webinars? and don't forget when you read a blog - you really need to read the comments as well - they are often just as important and sometimes more important.

Good luck .... have a beautiful day at the ocean....notice the children - they are majic ....notice the sky and the tress and the flowers etc. and engage in conversations with strangers - they, very often, have a wonderful story to tell.

Dr. Kruse has said .....if your hands are open - you will receive gifts......

You are so young with an amazing future ahead of you - just relax and enjoy your journey to optimal health.

### Hemming — 2013-07-05T16:50:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-89532>

I'll be doing more swimming in the coming months. Don't worry about the clothing - I'm as close to naked as possible without being accused of anything

I've been thinking of those things too. I'll be moving back to my parents next week and as they live in a rural area with forests etc. close (in addition to the beach) I'll be using that too. I'm also considering setting up a tent in their garden and sleeping there. Besides that I have a stack of books that I want to read. I do know about MovNat and that is exactly one of the things I've been thinking about doing.

I don't have a bath tub at my apartment. My parents do have a bath tub inside. I would prefer the swims anyway, especially now that I can feel how I've adapted to them.

I've printed out all the blog posts and put them in different folders so I'm slowly reading them from the beginning. Right now I'm at CT 7 and I can assure you I'm reading the comments and commenting too. I really like that Jack specifically mentions anorexia too, all too many get caught with losing weight. It would be great if Jack did a blog post on anorexia because I completely agree with that anorexia and obesity is on the same spectrum - I've been telling a lot of people that already. The thing is that I need people to tell me it's okay to eat, I really like food, especially fats at the moment (whereas three months ago it was protein) yet I still like to restrict my eating which is also why I haven't gained weight. The BAB I really like but I still find myself heading to work too often thinking I could have eaten a little more. Just like some people can't stop I find it too easy to stop eating. Even though I really coconut and butter for instance I still get scared about eating too much of it even though it's probably better for me to eat more. The fun thing is that the foods I like the most are exactly those on the Epi Paleo diet but I'm still scared of eating too much of them.

Today I did 15min (450m of swimming) in 18 degrees water, it was great!

Thank you so much for the support! I'm hoping that this is only beginning of a journey towards optimal. I'll probably be asking a lot more questions along the way as I need constant reassurance that I'm doing the right thing, this disease completely ruins your brain.

### caroline — 2013-07-06T05:42:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-89576>

Please, please, please ask away..... you are only making this trip once - lets make it outrageous and wonderful and dream big........why the heck not.....

My one big wish for you.......you will get back your health.....and then pay it forward and help others ......actually, I think you already see enough results to start paying it forward. I would guess that this disease is harder for a man?

When you are staying with your parents and sleeping in a tent and looking at the stars and dreaming big dreams ....anything is possible!

You will be able to create a movnat adventure for yourself every day in the forest ..... how lucky are you ..... beach and forest!! Are you able to do a movnat with Erwan? He has the whole mind/ body/ brain thing going on it. It would be worth every dime.

Have you listened to the June webinar? and the Q&A with Erwan? Think of all this as the optimal investment in your health and future.

Please be supremely positive about your future......allow no maybes.......everyone needs support from time to time but you are going to get there if you are 100% committed.....the alternate isn't an option.
Even JK says he learns stuff from us sometime...... he might have just been being nice tho......

Have you thought of having a BPC in the morning with your BAB? \[bulletproof coffee .... and using MCT oil and GS butter in it plus cocoa and vanilla\] Don't stress - you will get there.....you have years of wrong thinking to overcome but you will get there. If you fall down \[and we all do\] just get back up and start again. That is the mark of a committed, courageous person who is 100% all in.

I would love it if you would post everyday and tell us the good, the bad and the ugly. Make a commitment to talk to strangers everyday and listen to their stories. Take yourself out of the equation for awhile ....and smile at a child and see what happens - they lite up the world!

Have a great weekend!

### caroline — 2013-07-06T05:46:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-89577>
