@SeaHorse, thanks a lot for sharing your experiences with adrenal fatigue and cortisol issues. As you mention, my energy varies from day to day. Today I did get in the water but didn't want to push and swam around for 1-2min. I didn't feel like staying in longer. To me these last two degrees from 8C to 6C feels pretty hefty.
I'm concerned about the stress part. If the cold in general stresses my body even further then I'm thinking its not the time to push CT.

I haven't listened to the webinar - I didn't realise there was one

Have you overcome all of your adrenal issues or is there still some left?

I'm very strict on getting enough sleep. I've also found that when I eat better I sleep better. My bedroom is below 14C now so on the days I don't eat enough I get cold in the night.

### Inger — 2013-11-28T17:03:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-101815>

> [Hemming said:](/goto/post?id=101723)
>
> Inger and Caroline, I cut some raw beef that I keep on a plate in the fridge. I had some for breakfast this morning, it tasted really good and 'felt good'. I'm just thinking Inger, how do you avoid your tallow going rancid without rendering it? Do you freeze it and only take out what you need for a couple of days? I'm also thinking about making high meat, didn't you write instructions at some point?

I have tried to freeze raw beef fat but it felt weird when thawed... I had the feeling it went bad fast. I now just leave it in my fridge, and for weeks. I just cut off slices and eat them, I feel great! It turns a little drier and harder and maybe a little rancid too but somehow it does not really bother me. But the fresh thawed one did. I have no clue why. I just never freeze beef fat anymore after that one experiment. If you buy a weekly supply it will be no issue at all, just keep it cold in your fridge.

High meat is super easy. Take raw (never frozen!) meat or heart. Cut away all fat. Cut it in mouth big pieces. Put in a glass jar big enough to leave at least 1/2 of the jar empty. Close it well and put in your fridge. Air it for a minute or so every other day. You can air it every day too if you like, and I sometimes forgot and there will be 2-3 days in between. But too many days un aired is not good, it needs fresh air to not grow dangerous botulism bacterias. I shake the jar to get the bottom pieces aired too.

If you want to quick start it, let it be in room temp for a few days until it starts to get high (you will smell it...), air every day. Then put it in the fridge and age it further. I did this last time and it was great! Otherwise you will have to wait a month or so before it starts to get high. It should smell... pretty bad..lol Like a very old cheese or eggs. I always air it on my balcony or the whole house will stink! Also be careful to have a well closed jar so no smell comes out into your fridge too.

It will start to get moister and softer.. and taste like a old Roquefort or ammonia like. I love it! I have a jar going on now and I want to eat the whole thing... I need to be disciplined and eat only a few pieces a day, huh! GREAT for mood.. a real mood booster!

No outer time limit how long you can age it. I know people who age it for a year! Mine never last that long. it is way too delish! My oldest was maybe 3 months.

### Inger — 2013-11-28T17:14:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-101816>

Hemming the water in the river is 0,8 degree C now! Pretty awesome! I stay in only for maybe 2 minutes. Sometimes even shorter! But man it makes me feel great..... my skin gets red and it feels like burning a while after.
In my bedroom it is very cold now. Just a few degrees or close to zero, depending on how cold outside. I have two very thin duvets, I start with both and then I push the other away when I feel warm. I am amazed how well my body makes heat itself. You know, hubs sleeps in an separate bedroom as he lives here pretty much now, he makes fire in the fire place to heat his rooms (a separate apartment in this house) and I bet his bedroom has about 20 degrees at least when he goes to sleep, windows closed and very thick duvet...lol.
One evening I felt like cuddling and I walked over to his bedroom and crawled in his bed and after just a few minutes I felt way too hot huh! I had to escape to my iglo...haha No way can I sleep in those temps anymore

### Hemming — 2013-12-03T10:53:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-102158>

I'm a few centimeters taller than my dad and we have pretty much the same body type, I think he weighs 10kg more than me. Dad doesn't have a specific diet as such but he doesn't eat processed/crap/junk/fast food. He also raised me with the idea that the meat we eat should be from the calf we have been padding and the vegetables/fruits that we eat we have grown and harvested ourself. He's pretty active with doing a lot of different work (not least for my brother and I), volunteering and running. His activity level pretty much follows the idea of moving around a lot and going all out once in a while.

Dad has been such a great support in all of this. He has done so much for me and been so aware of my situation (actually more than mom). I can assure that I've been the one wanting to hug both mom and dad and he realised at some point how much I wanted that so now he has even started hugging me  I can't wait for going back home to their place to celebrate Christmas with the rest of the family

### Hemming — 2013-12-12T12:21:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-103202>

> [sooperb said:](/goto/post?id=103152)

I have watched it , my Danish is non-existent but I gather the slot was about your trying to achieve weight gain and the other fellow trying to lose weight, was your diet compared to his at all? I wish I could have understood it. That water looked unbelievably cold , well done to you for getting so far with CT. I tried face dunking yesterday but I think the water temperature must have dropped, I could hardly bear it, my forehead hurt quite a lot, to the point where I gave up. I have ordered one of these: HDE Temperature Gun Infrared Thermometer w/ Laser Sight from Amazon so that I can do this a little more accurately. I'm not sure how far I'll get. You're definitely an inspiration Hemming.

There was no mentioning of the diet. She did ask for pictures of me while eating but I declined because people would lose sight of the overall message if I started talking about what I eat. I think at some point she asks what I eat and I reply with 'you wouldn't believe me anyway if I told you'. The show was not about diet but more eating disorders in general. I have a photo reporter following me day and night at the moment. She has taken a picture of me eating liver which will most likely end up in the article although it wont really be mentioned that its liver.
I think the water was 9-10C, it wasn't that bad. I think it looked colder than it really was and it also helps that it was shot late October and sent early December

I'm still working on getting my head around a lot of things. My main problem is still eating enough - which is so stupid. I can clearly feel how I feel better/worse if I eat more/less yet I continue to want to restrict my intake.

### sooperb — 2013-12-12T14:03:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-103209>

"My main problem is still eating enough - which is so stupid."

I don't see that as stupid at all, it is I think, very typical. I think psychology plays a huge part in brain programming and that food manufacturers, governments, doctors and health gurus are well aware of this and use many techniques to plug their diet/product/advice. I have read so much that completely debunks saturated fat as the dietary devil, calorie counting as the only way to diet, exercise being, well, a pointless exercise in weightloss, I believe it, I really do. Yet ........... I still think about how many calories might be in what I am eating, I still think about taking up running because it would burn off so many pounds per mile. When you have learned this over many years (and I have a big start on you young man lol) it is very difficult to shake off. By the same token I regard sugar as a poison, I've been convinced by books written that are backed up by research but ...... I still struggle not to ingest it at least once a day in some form.

I guess what I'm saying is that once you have committed to a pathway and followed it for years, it's can be very difficult to change, even knowing it's the wrong one for you, even knowing that it can be harmful, even by looking at what you've achieved by not following it. It's programming and almost hard wired into your psyche. I applaud anyone who can completely sever the wiring, the after effects are sometimes not pleasant. Think addiction and you will understand what I mean. When your previous path is constantly reinforced by said health professionals, manufacturers, big pharma et al and your lifestyle choices mocked by family and friends, it can be a struggle. You have come a long way, there's still time to undo the more stubborn bits of wiring but it may take time, you against the rest, it's a hard battle but sometimes one you need to win and you will.

### Hemming — 2013-12-12T14:57:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-103213>

Those are some really good points. I think in the same way as you about a lot of things (although I never eat sugar and haven't done for quite some time).

I put all effort into whatever I set in mind. That's both good and bad because I'm just as determined about the 'bad' things.

Its very difficult for anyone to change the mindset - it takes some serious concentrated effort.

### caroline — 2014-01-08T23:55:04+0200

### Hemming — 2014-01-30T22:05:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-107482>

Its been a while since I've written something because things have been going well but then something weird happened Sunday.

I was on the train on my way when I suddenly felt sick. Very quickly I passed out and went in to a cramp seizure. The first thing I remember is having a very blurred vision, not being able to feel my body and desperately trying to breathe. When I regained consciousness and could feel my body I had a guy holding me down saying 'breathe' right in my face. I had to focus to not hyperventilate and my legs and hands were sore after the cramps. I was taken to they hospital where I spent the night for observation. I have never experienced anything like it.
The interesting thing is that they measured my blood sugar which was 6.4mmol/l (115mg/dl) around 1.5-2h after finishing dinner. I've had some of the other symptoms of hyperglycemia such as being thirsty, dry mouth, frequent urination and I've had some small cramps/tingling in my lower legs lately. I measured my breath acetone during those days and it was equivalent of BHB of >2mmol/l.
My question is really if my low carb diet is resulting in too little insulin and that my blood sugar is too high? I think I've seen something similar with other people but I would very much like to hear if anyone has some good references where I investigate it further. If I remember correctly I think Inger also had a somewhat blood sugar and Jack gave a response to that but now I obviously can't find that post.

### caroline — 2014-01-30T22:16:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-107484>

What a scary experience Hemming .... I hope someone has some thoughts for you...

You are okay now? What did the Doctor think?

### Hemming — 2014-01-30T22:49:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-107489>

All my tests came back normal. But then today I got the same feeling of being sick again when I was back at work. Thank god it didn't go further than that. I've felt pretty sluggish the last week and had several bowel movements each day - I'm thinking that has something to do with it too. I'm still trying to eat enough but I haven't felt that hungry and I can see that I've lost some weight.

### yewwei.tan — 2014-01-30T22:58:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-107492>

I've had similar but less serious symptoms like that before, but unfortunately such symptoms are pretty non-specific.

There were I few instance where any amount of physical exertion (ie: increased oxygen demand) produced heavy breathing, etc ...

**1.** When I started taking additional iodine as a supplement. That's when I asked this question -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?8609-Clinical-Symptoms-of-rising-T3](http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?8609-Clinical-Symptoms-of-rising-T3)

This literally happened any time that I took too much than I could handle, with 2 drops of Lugol's being fine, and 3 drops causing my heart rate to jump from 50-60/min to 70+. Rate of breathing also went up. Every time this happened, my blood glucose was higher than normal, but not by much -- normal for me being 70-80mg/dL, and higher meaning 80-90mg/dL .

I was eating a moderate protein ketogenic diet throughout the entire time.

Also interesting to note, was that I felt these symptoms from 2pm to 4pm. Having measured by blood ketones for a couple months now, I know I always know that I wake up around 0.6-0.9mM, and then by 1pm I'm around 1.1-1.8mM, and then by 5pm, I'm upwards of 2mM (and +3mM if I exercised in that window). I don't know if that is related to the symptoms, or just a coincidence.

I'm now up to 12 vertical drops of Lugol's a day without any of the elevated heart rate or palpitation symptoms. Ketone measurements remain exactly as I described in the last paragraph.

Likely explanations include the fact that those are possible detox symptoms when I started off, or that cellular demand for oxygen is increasing dramatically due to ketosis (I don't know if this is true).

**2.** When I get exposed to large amounts of certain foods, which I suspect to be a mold toxin issue, because those foods are almost universally prone to mold. Examples include cocoa powder, coffee, and certain kinds of veggies.

I've also got both risk-alleles that Dr Tim Jackson described in this post: [http://www.ameerrosic.com/can-mold-make-you-sick-the-secret-barrier-to-optimal-digestive-health/](http://www.ameerrosic.com/can-mold-make-you-sick-the-secret-barrier-to-optimal-digestive-health/)

\----

Every time I've had these issues, the quick fix seemed to be a really hot shower, interspersed with 30-seconds bursts of cold water.

My guess as to why that works would be the effect of radiant energy on your body's water, with the cold contrast helping out circulation (and hydration) even more. I would love to try this experiment with an infrared sauna and a ice bath if I could, but who can afford to conveniently feel sick at a spa

\---

"Toxin sequestering" supplements prior to potentially risky meals also helped: Activated Charcoal, Apple Pectin, and Gluthathione being the top 3 that really gave me an effect.

\---

Finally, I was having persistent nighttime leg cramping for the last 2 months. I thought it could be an electrolyte issue, which is common during keto diets, but supplementing with all sorts of electrolytes didn't help.

Everything fixed itself in the last 2 weeks, when I started incorporating a daily serving of red meat into my diet. I have no idea what is in the meat that helps, but I suspect it's the combination of all nutrients, as well as a boost in iron (my transferrin saturation is 0%).

The confounding factor here is also that I started incorporating nuts into my diet starting early January, which did wonders for my gut health, but nothing immediately for the leg cramps.

Other than that, no change in type or frequency of exercise, caloric content, sleep times, daily routines, etc ...

\---

Hopefully the above sparks some ideas. Stay healthy!

### Hemming — 2014-02-16T21:33:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-110656>

I got all the results from my blood sample the day I had the seizure and I could really need some input from people. I've just listed the ones which are not within the normal range and which I think are important.

Thrombocytes 136 10E9/l
Hemoglobin 7.5 mmol/l
Bilirubin 4 µmol/l
Albumin 36 g/l
Potassium 3.3 mmol/l
CRP <0.3 mg/l
Zinc 10 µmol/l

My first issue is that both my white and red blood cells are too low. My thrombocytes have been low for a long time and my doctor thought I had leukemia because of that 1½ year ago. They have improved since then but are now low again coupled with low red blood cells which I'm wondered is because of anemia. I'm not sure if the low bilirubin number is related to this - any thoughts?
My albumin is low and I've had edema in my legs and stomach. I have an idea that the swelling and low albumin could be due to a low protein intake. I've noticed that my urine has smelled of ammonia more than usual.
I read the Redox Rx and noticed that potassium is a huge thing. That is why I really don't like that my potassium is too low. It might also be the reason why I had the seizure.
I'm just glad to see that my CRP is below 0.3.
My zinc continues to be low even though I've tried to eat zinc rich foods.

The symptoms I'm having at the moment is fatigue 24/7, nausea, constipation, cold hands and feet and dizziness. The worst thing though is a strange of unrest in my legs which makes it almost hurt when I have to walk around and if I have to due any hard exercise I feel very bad and almost get 'attacks' where I panic and want to try. Wearing compression socks helps so I'm suspecting it might have something to do with the circulation in my legs and that's why my feet are always cold and feel warmer when I wear the compression socks. My appetite has increased which really annoys me because I think so much about food already. I've tried doing some exercise, mainly anaerobic, besides my commute and I think that it's simply too much for me currently. Swinging kettlebells, doing push-ups etc. I just feel destroyed the day after.
The good thing is that my sleep is good and I wake up naturally when the rises (Jack mentioned in the Leptin webinar that it is an important sign). I've gained weight to 60kg now and I would say most of it is muscle. My waist hasn't really changed where I can see that my arms, torso and legs look better.

I've tried to give as much info as possible but if you need more please ask! Is this a case for a Leptin reset? I'm doing cold showers and that is the most CT I can do at the moment given how I feel. I'm obviously following the Epi-paleo diet with fish, seaweed and offal all from good sources. Any input on what to do from here is greatly appreciated!

### caroline — 2014-02-17T03:19:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-110703>

### sooperb — 2014-02-17T09:53:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-110751>

Above all I would ease off the exercise, it's not something you need to be doing so intensively, it sounds as though you are using it both as a diversion and a solution. Have a massage instead if you can, try to relax more.

### sooperb — 2014-02-18T12:24:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-110973>

Gagnrad was musing about taking up Tai Chi again, it's gentle but effective. Exercise don't have to be about drilling yourself into the ground  maybe HIIT might work for you, short bursts instead of prolonged bouts. How about finding a climbing wall, lots of muscular effort and concentration instead of running along empty headed with music going round your brain (I don't know if that's what you do lol, just generalising).

I have given this some thought in respect to my failed attempts to do much of anything physical beyond walk the dog. I've turned that into more than just walking along day dreaming, I throw his ball rather than use the sling shot, I jump over tussocks, duck under bushes that I would have walked round, yesterday I spent ages pushing mud around with my boots. It's early days but here's hoping I can start adding in a short burst of speed or trying to pull myself up on a tree branch. More like Erwan and less like a heptathlete lol.

### Hemming — 2014-02-18T17:49:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-111016>

Haha, I've never ran with music in my ears! Besides that, I don't run anymore. I was out doing an orienteering race this Sunday, not very strenuous but I still had to jump some ditches, branches/trees etc. Today I was out in the forest too walking 'off-road'.
I've tried HIIT but right now I simply can't handle it. The day after I feel like I've been run over by a train, not just 'normal' DOMS.

### sooperb — 2014-02-18T18:06:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-111023>

I think the one thing that has come up quite a lot from the reading I've done in the last few months is that exercise, whilst necessary to a degree, is not something that needs to be done intensively or for prolonged periods. Walking is great, swimming is pretty good, but marathon running and endless reps in the gym don't achieve much at all. Maybe your body is talking to you, less might be more for a while. Sometimes stopping to smell the coffee is a good option  Are you stressing about anything? I've found that stress can manifest itself in many physical symptoms, some you wouldn't necessarily connect to it.

I also wonder, given your history, if you aren't fighting your old mindset whereby you controlled yourself by controlling your body? Is there something else going on in your life that's upsetting you? The mind is a powerful tool but it can work against us sometimes. Have you tried hypnotism?

### Hemming — 2014-02-25T13:54:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-112090>

> [sooperb said:](/goto/post?id=111023)
>
> I think the one thing that has come up quite a lot from the reading I've done in the last few months is that exercise, whilst necessary to a degree, is not something that needs to be done intensively or for prolonged periods. Walking is great, swimming is pretty good, but marathon running and endless reps in the gym don't achieve much at all. Maybe your body is talking to you, less might be more for a while. Sometimes stopping to smell the coffee is a good option  Are you stressing about anything? I've found that stress can manifest itself in many physical symptoms, some you wouldn't necessarily connect to it.
>
> I also wonder, given your history, if you aren't fighting your old mindset whereby you controlled yourself by controlling your body? Is there something else going on in your life that's upsetting you? The mind is a powerful tool but it can work against us sometimes. Have you tried hypnotism?

I've felt much better the last week where I was on vacation and could relax and focus on myself. I did less exercise than normal but I look much better. I agree that stress can manifest itself in your body, I've tried that too.

I have a lot of things mentally I'm working on improving. Its a very long story about how I ended up in this situation - the good thing is that I now have a very good understanding of why and how I got here so that I can work on getting better. That said, its not easy and wont happen in a week. I still need to realise that I have physical limits, even eating I have to aware of to get enough nutrients. The week I was on vacation I felt better and better while also looking better and better. It was like an omen saying that when I nourish myself I feel better and look better.
I have not tried hypnotism but I have thought about. Have you tried it?

### sooperb — 2014-02-25T15:38:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-112098>

I'm so pleased that you are in better touch/control of your inner self Hemming, it's not easy fighting demons with their whispering voices that work against you. Stick with it young man, it sounds like you're on the right track. You have all the time in the world to make this happen so don't be in a rush.

I only tried hypnotism once and I chose to use someone whom I'd met a few times and who had recently qualified. I had two sessions with her, I wasn't impressed and I didn't see any positive outcomes from the sessions. That said I think the best alternative practitioners tend to be intuitive as well as trained. If I were to try again I would seek a personal recommendation or someone with a lot of positive reviews.

### Hemming — 2014-02-25T16:09:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-112107>

I don't know what hit me. Within a few days I felt so fatigued and I was so bloated. Then again within a couple of days it all reversed and I started looking pretty good (if I have to say so myself  )

I've more or less made the same considerations as you. I've taken note of a specific guy who has a long list of recommendation. I haven't looked into pricing yet but I might consider it.
I've also started seeing my old psychiatrist again which has already been a good experience.

### Hemming — 2014-02-27T12:46:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-112431>

Just a minor update.

I've started eating three times per day now. I actually feel better doing that compared to two times. I guess my redox potential is not optimal yet so I'll stick with this plan for now.

### Hemming — 2014-03-01T20:04:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-112864>

Yesterday I had an EEG taken and today an MR scan following my seizure. I don't expect them to show anything unusual but the doctor wanted to be sure and ordered the MR scan after the EEG. Maybe I'll also have a tilt table test. I'll get back with the results.

On a general note, I still struggle with eating enough. It's a lot better than previously but I simply find it difficult to eat so much that I have the energy I need the following day. I'm not surprised to hear how so many people lose weight following this lifestyle/diet...

### Inger — 2014-03-01T20:13:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-112867>

I often eat tree times too Hemming. Why you have issues to eat enough, could you tell what happends? Do you get full so fast or what is the thing. I can eat so much... I have so hard a time understand how someone cannot. I would love to, tho

### sooperb — 2014-03-02T00:12:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-112918>

Maybe decide what to eat and then just add a little bit more. If you do this at every meal maybe you can slowly build up the quantity rather than trying to eat too much extra and putting yourself off.

### caroline — 2014-03-02T00:23:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-112922>

Hi Hemming. Have you been following Ricardo's posts on lipids and Ketones?

I do so much better on a lot more fat and less protein..... I just can't eat that much either....

Do you eat nuts? I can't remember...

### Hemming — 2014-03-02T11:49:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-113032>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=112867)
>
> I often eat tree times too Hemming. Why you have issues to eat enough, could you tell what happends? Do you get full so fast or what is the thing. I can eat so much... I have so hard a time understand how someone cannot. I would love to, tho

Eating is still not that easy to me. I feel like I'm full, some of that feeling might be psychological though. Food is scary to me, I don't think of it as nourishment but as something 'evil' that I have to do and then I'm trying to minimise the pain by not eating so much or perhaps convincing myself that I'm full.

> [sooperb said:](/goto/post?id=112918)
>
> Maybe decide what to eat and then just add a little bit more. If you do this at every meal maybe you can slowly build up the quantity rather than trying to eat too much extra and putting yourself off.

I'm kind of trying that. Its easier for me to finish a plate where I know I have to eat everything than having several servings. It also simplifies things for me so I can worry a little less about the meal.

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=112922)
>
> Hi Hemming. Have you been following Ricardo's posts on lipids and Ketones?
>
> I do so much better on a lot more fat and less protein..... I just can't eat that much either....
>
> Do you eat nuts? I can't remember...

I've started including a few more nuts lately but its mainly three brazil nuts. Sometimes I do have a few hazelnuts, walnuts or almonds but its not that many. Lately I've had a strong craving for raw coconuts though which I've eaten a lot of

I was in a Danish paper regarding my eating disorder. Its a great article but obviously in Danish - at least you can look at the pictures
[http://www.information.dk/488791](http://www.information.dk/488791)

### caroline — 2014-03-02T23:36:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-113106>

nice pics Hemming - can you summarise for us?
PLease have a look at Ricardo's posts about lipids and cortisol - I think you would find it interesting what he has for his breakfast.....
Is your weight holding steady?
Also have a look at Krista's journal today .....Endless
she talks about what she is doing with resistant starch

### Hemming — 2014-03-03T12:21:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-113156>

It basically tells my story of how I spiralled into this starting when I was 10 years old and home alone with dad when he suddenly collapsed. Since then I've had some sort of PTSD which means that I don't like where I'm not in complete control. My eating disorder started when I was done studying and had the perfect job because than I didn't know what to do from there. My running went out of control because I felt had to do something, when I couldn't run anymore because I was so wasted I started obsessing about what I ate.
Its an amazing article which captures so much of my story. Many people have been shocked reading it because they didn't know how deep an eating disorder goes and how it has and continues to affect my every day life.

My weight has gone down a little lately, I'm at 57kg now. That's not my main issue though. I'm more worried that I'm starting to feel that same fatigue/weakness/tiredness/coldness all over my body as I did when I was in really bad shape. Additionally, it affects my mind too. I have this strange feeling at the moment where I'm hungry but I can't really find something I want to eat. I'm trying to look for new things to eat or figure out what it is I actually want.

I have experimented with resistant starch too. As such I didn't feel much of a difference. One thing I would like to say though is that it is very underrated eating it straight out of the bag - it gets a very interesting mouthfeel/texture after some seconds in the mouth

Thanks for directing me at Ricardo's post. I've looked at what he eats and I get the idea of mixing in a lot of fat. I also saw that you mentioned somewhere what you have for breakfast but without quantities. Can you tell me how butter you use for your BPC for instance?

### caroline — 2014-03-05T05:34:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-113511>

Thanks Hemming for the summary. I really had never known about the control thing until I started talking to you.
Thanks for educating me.
Did you take the RS straight? did you see the separate thread on RS?

Sorry - I don't really measure......I think I shoot for about 1-2 TBSP and one TBSP of MCT oil or 1-2 TBSP of CO

Since I have been having so much more fat and in Ketosis - I have so much more energy and feel so great.

Could you eat out for awhile? or go to your Mom's......might it take the pressure off?

Can you go for long walks with your Dad?... he is such a support for you .......unlimited love and kindness.
and don't forget - as much hugging as possible. Camping in your Dad's backyard with your brothers?

Maybe another trip with the girl you went to Paris with? Remember what a great time you had and the wonderful meals - go back in your journal and have a read......

I need you well so you can do a movnat!

### Hemming — 2014-03-05T11:32:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-113540>

Yeah, I just ate it straight out of the jar. I have read a lot about it and I'm not dismissing it all and I'll probably start eating it again at some point.
The ironic thing is that I feel so much better when I eat more but mentally I find it difficult because I feel like a glutton even though I'm just eating what I need to fuel my body.
Eating out doesn't help me because then there are other people who I think look at me and I eat even less. Besides, its easier to get exactly what I want when I'm at home or with my family.
I can't be with my family in my every day life but I do visit them pretty often. This weekend I'm helping my brother with preparing his birthday and then the entire family is coming to celebrate.
Whenever I'm with my family we always go for walks and cook together which really sooths my mind. I'm going on vacation with all of them soon.
I can't camp in the garden right now but when its summer again and I'm at their place I'm sure I will!
I would love to go to Paris again and she would too. The only problem is that she is studying in Singapore at the moment so until she is done with that there will be no travelling together - sadly.
Hehe, I'm trying to get there!

### caroline — 2014-03-05T11:52:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-113541>

Why don't you go to Singapore?????? That would be awesome!

Have you tried the chicken liver pate recipe? it is half butter, easy and very delicious. Maybe make it for your brother's birthday?

Are you having fun? are you being peculiar? are you being unique?

### caroline — 2014-03-05T11:57:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-113542>

Hemming - You just gave me an idea ....it will sound crazy at first ... but...

Have you ever tried making bread? it is very, very therapeutic and the kneading is calming and soothing, and I assume that your family eats some bread?

I can help you with that if you like. I made tons of bread almost everyday for 35 years!

### sooperb — 2014-03-05T13:48:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-113564>

Singapore, spent 3 days there and loved it. Mainly for shopping first, food second and cleanliness third. The traffic actually stop to let you cross the road instead of seeing how much they can scare you before they stop. I think their very rigid traffic laws have something to do with it  I'd go again like a shot.

### Hemming — 2014-03-05T14:02:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-113567>

> [sooperb said:](/goto/post?id=113564)
>
> Singapore, spent 3 days there and loved it. Mainly for shopping first, food second and cleanliness third. The traffic actually stop to let you cross the road instead of seeing how much they can scare you before they stop. I think their very rigid traffic laws have something to do with it  I'd go again like a shot.

I was there visiting my cousin - I didn't like it  Why go to Singapore for the food?! Its just food courts all over with fried rice and then they have some imported foreign concept restaurants...

### sooperb — 2014-03-05T14:15:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-113570>

Pay attention Hemming , the food was second to the shopping. A girl who shops gotta have food on the hoof! I liked the choice, so many different cultures on offer. We had a great curry in a restaurant by the river. Sitting there outside in the balmy warmth, you can't do that in England even in the Summer lol. I was especially startled by the pigs intestine soup, you pick the bit of intestine and then it's added to broth and they throw some chilli paste in. My husband was a funny shade of red by the time he'd eaten it. I stuck to noodles with stuff lol, safer all round although I expect their hospitals are fine.

### Hemming — 2014-03-05T16:19:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-113585>

Hehe, touché  I did have a good fish head curry.

I also looked for the pig soup but I never found a place they made with some interesting cuts.

I have to say that I think that the city needs some soul, it all seems too polished to me. I would much rather go to Paris

### sooperb — 2014-03-05T16:41:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-113587>

Yes, but I still don't like the place that much. That said, I was there when I was in a bad shape so I could see things differently if I went there now. But I'm travelling around the world before I feel better than I do now.

I've baked bread countless times. Back in the days I did it all the time trying out a lot of different recipes. I had my own sourdough for quite some time

### caroline — 2014-03-06T09:40:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-113732>

some homemade sourdough bread with the pate would be yummy..... and homemade pickles...

you are going to travel around the world???

### Hemming — 2014-03-09T17:16:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-114327>

How does bread go with the epi-paleo diet?

Ah, I forgot a 'not' in there

### caroline — 2014-03-10T10:08:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-114492>

Hemming - don't sweat the small stuff! a small piece would not kill you and your family eats bread - no?

Dr. Jackson talks about people who can not have wheat etc. in the U.S. - but can eat it when they go to Europe.

I had some very yummy really large shrimp and calamari dipped in breadcrumbs and deep fried! .... at a party.

Did I die .... nope! it didn't bother me one bit! would I do it everyday? nope. Maybe I won't again - but maybe next time there is a party with such awesome treats - I will!

MSG does me in way more than anything else I think. I even had some yogurt last week.....

Dr. K. says when our battery is charged - we can handle way more stuff.....I personally am going with that and going to have a life without being worried about every single thing.

I did what I needed to do the last three years ....now I want to lighten up - just a little and only once in awhile if circumstances dictate it

### Hemming — 2014-03-11T21:31:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-114839>

My biggest problem right now is actually that I'm sweating everything. I'm trying to convince myself that I'll not die from having something which I've for so long restricted. The rest of the family does eat bread, I think mom and dad would almost start crying if I had mom's famous buttermilk horns, layer cake or fastelavnsboller

Trust me, I'm the person in the world who the most would like to not feel this way about food. Some times I wish I could take the blue pill and just live in ignorance of all this.

Thanks a lot for pointing out that a few indulgences will not kill you in the big picture!

### caroline — 2014-03-11T23:09:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-114855>

I also forgot to say Hemming..and you alluded to this.....

When I lighten up a bit - but still in control...... I don't know how to say what I mean.......
My friends worry less about me...

I certainly don't want to be fat and sick like they are ..... but I also need to lighten up and have a life...

so do you! easy? ...... nope!

no blue pill for any of us - you aren't alone you know.....

Give your Mom and Dad the biggest hug ever and tell them how much you love and care and appreciate them. It will do all of you a world of good!

BTW .... I took a strip off someone here last night because of what they said about anorexia ..... that was for you my young friend!

### Lahelada — 2014-03-11T23:49:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-114861>

Fastelavnsboller... I googled it and I was right... Fastnachtskrapfen.... None here so cannot even contemplate straying...sigh.

In all seriousness though I hope you find a "live and let live relationship" with yourself and your food. I know from acquaintances that it is hell to know and want to be different but almost have an outside outside force from within directing your move. I myself suffered from OCD due to a high stress situation for a while and almost burst into tears everytime I wanted to eat an apple and had to wash it and wash it. My mind knew ,wanted to stop but could not.My Out came in an unguarded moment where I sat on the Nr 25 bus devouring the Jose Carreras autobiography and accidentally wiped my mouth with an unwashed hand!!!Panic ...and then I thought something seemingly incongruous "but if leukemia patients can survive having their immune system put on hold you young lady will not die from a bit of communal dirt on teh bus". It took another two months for the symptoms to disappear completely but that was my "out" almost 20 years ago now . I hope you find your "OUT".

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-115152>

I have told them already  They're also now understanding how much this is about feelings/emotions and not about the food itself. They don't mind that I don't eat some things on a regular basis - they just want me to be happy.

> [Lahelada said:](/goto/post?id=114861)
>
> Fastelavnsboller... I googled it and I was right... Fastnachtskrapfen.... None here so cannot even contemplate straying...sigh.

I know from acquaintances that it is hell to know and want to be different but almost have an outside outside force from within directing your move. I myself suffered from OCD due to a high stress situation for a while and almost burst into tears everytime I wanted to eat an apple and had to wash it and wash it. My mind knew ,wanted to stop but could not.My Out came in an unguarded moment where I sat on the Nr 25 bus devouring the Jose Carreras autobiography and accidentally wiped my mouth with an unwashed hand!!!Panic ...and then I thought something seemingly incongruous "but if leukemia patients can survive having their immune system put on hold you young lady will not die from a bit of communal dirt on teh bus". It took another two months for the symptoms to disappear completely but that was my "out" almost 20 years ago now . I hope you find your "OUT".

Thanks for sharing the story! I had a similar experience around a year ago when I was at my psychiatrist and broke down completely. When I came I looked at myself in the mirror weighing around 50kg and realised I looked like shit - that was a real turning point for me.
My 'problem' now is that I feel so much better than six months ago that its so much more difficult for me to have that eureka moment again even though I still 'need' another one because I'm not over this yet.

### Hemming — 2014-03-21T10:10:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-116907>

Following my seizure I had an EEG and MR scan done with both turned out normal. Next up is more blood tests and an echocardiogram.
I'm not surprised to see that everything is normal, I think it was just a one time thing.

### caroline — 2014-03-21T11:32:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-116912>

I am so pleased for you Hemming... how are you feeling?
Spring is coming there .... have you been doing C/T?

### Hemming — 2014-03-21T19:49:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-116976>

I'm feeling fine as such but after spending four days on a business trip I'm pretty wasted. Its still a problem for me to eat enough which is still the biggest problem I'm struggling with. I've lost some weight again and I'm feeling hungry more and more often. It annoys the hell out of me that I'm doing this to myself. I'm so tired of not having energy, libido etc. yet the urge to truly get better is overwhelming by guilt from eating.

I dropped a big and heavy knife on my thumb when I was about to cut an entire calf liver. I had to go to the ER and get it stitched together, its better now but no swimming for me at the moment

### caroline — 2014-03-22T00:35:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-117030>

I wish I had that magic blue pill that you speak of.......

I just keep thinking of how much you enjoyed eating and food in Paris .... why was that? was it the food? was it your friend? was it the beautiful city? was it the weather? was it being out of your work environment?
do you love your job? maybe time for a change? what about your idea for raising cattle?

maybe you need a new start? Take a trip to Texas and put a cowboy hat and cowboy boots on and have some fun......

### sooperb — 2014-03-22T19:16:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-117198>

I think Audrey has it, you need a diversion of some sort but one that lasts longer than a few days. I could make suggestions but I don't know you or what would attract you, there must be something you'd like to do but haven't ever tried? Something that requires research and lots of concentration, that isn't over in five minutes, that engages your brain, isn't easy but would give you huge satisfaction to do it?

### Hemming — 2014-03-23T16:51:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-117374>

Both of you are completely spot on. I need that thing to make me realise what I want - not what my eating disorder wants. I need to what Thomas would do - not what my anorexia would do.

sooperb, even though you say you don't know me you describe that kind of thing I'm looking for perfectly  I've given a lot of thought to my work but I can't figure out if its my overall situation of my job that frustrates me. Paris was a great experience because it all came together, it was like when my friend was visiting some times during the year and we spent time with the family. I just felt a relaxation I hadn't done for a long time, for some reason whenever she's around I feel better.
I think my problem that I really want something to happen but I don't know what or how. I feel stuck here.

Wednesday I'm going on vacation with the family and I somehow feel that will be a good time to talk to everyone about life in general. My brother and his wife is having a baby around my birthday which I'm really looking forward and I'm thinking that could help me learn to focus on the bigger things in life.

Earlier this week I spoke to a couple of American people about places to go in the US. I'm considering if I should go alone to really get me out of my comfort zone.

### nicld — 2014-03-23T18:27:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-117391>

Where are you considering to go to? The US is so vast and so much to see.

Listening to your body and feeding it is such a mind game when one has an eating disorder. Learning to let go of controlling your body with food and trusting it is freeking scary. I failed at getting skinny with my anorexia but just got all of the mental crap with it. I still struggle almost daily with it.

Just take it one day at a time sometimes one hour at a time but know that you are worth it.

### Hemming — 2014-03-23T20:48:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-117425>

I'm considering flying to Vegas and from there Wyoming or Idaho. The other idea is going to Texas to see a big ranch and maybe stay there a few days. It could be many different things, I just want to experience the authentic US and not go to New York (that's for another trip).

As I've told some people - you can have an eating disorder at any weight. That's also why I try to explain people that even though I have gained some weight back I'm still struggling with a lot of things, they don't go away as the weight goes up.

### nicld — 2014-03-23T21:01:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-117430>

If you go to Vegas make sure to take a tour to the Grand Canyon but with the tour that goes to the Indian reservation. It is just beautiful and not all commercialized. I heard that Wyoming and Idaho is gorgeous too. My favorite place is Colorado. There is a reason why it is called God's country.

Yep the mental issues of an eating disorder may never go away. The goal is to not let the voices in your head take over.

### Hemming — 2014-03-23T21:07:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-117434>

Thanks a lot for the tips! They are all duly noted. It seems like the trip is getting longer and longer

### prAna303 — 2014-03-23T21:12:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-117438>

> [Hemming said:](/goto/post?id=117425)
>
> I'm considering flying to Vegas and from there Wyoming or Idaho. The other idea is going to Texas to see a big ranch and maybe stay there a few days. It could be many different things, I just want to experience the authentic US and not go to New York (that's for another trip).
>
> As I've told some people - you can have an eating disorder at any weight. That's also why I try to explain people that even though I have gained some weight back I'm still struggling with a lot of things, they don't go away as the weight goes up.

I never had problems with eating but have seen some of it in others...

I feel, from my own life that today most of us is out on sync.

We have two sides today, on one we have the modern life of money and other stuff around it. On top of this we try to find an inner peace, often we attend to everything we can to enlighten us.

For me this will always be a problem, i have always felt "off" compared to my friends around me. I had the feeling of making big money, had the feeling of seeing things that others did not... For many years i lived out of sync with the human (i).

My way to deal with everything is, you must take time to sync your body and mind without any distractions, i did and what a relief.

Put yourself into a little mindgame, reverse time slowly by thinking of everything you have done, backwards. Think about every detail, accept it, go backwards until you find where you lost yourself. I did this eight years ago and i reset myself to age 15 again, this i s where i started to look at my career instead of my passion for art, dance, music and girls...

### sooperb — 2014-03-24T00:44:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-117492>

Audrey don't you think that Erwan is just exercising a different form of control to Hemming? Doing movnat would, for me, be about challenging this tired, fat old body, it would be fun and taxing and many other things, at the end of it, however it turned out, I would be able to settle back into my usual life. Hemming, I think, is missing something from his life, when he moves on, it shouldn't be temporary, he doesn't want to go back to his old self he wants to be like Peter, find the right place that makes him happy and relaxed. It may be under his nose or he may have to move to find it.

### prAna303 — 2014-03-24T00:52:29+0200

Ok, to make it clear... You can not go back and look back only. I think a couple of steps back is ten steps ahead if you understand what is missing. Like you said, everyone MUST find their own passion. What i do, i would not force anyone into...

Movnat, is a mindset for me... When someone is making a business of something it gets a little bit to much for me. But in a world of today what he is doing it is most natural... What i would do IF you would like to move and explore the details of live i turn my nose to, Frank Forencich and his [http://www.exuberantanimal.com/index.php#.Uy9ln61dV8c](http://www.exuberantanimal.com/index.php#.Uy9ln61dV8c)

### caroline — 2014-03-24T00:54:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-117494>

No - I don't agree Sue .....it is about caring for your brain and body and pushing yourself physically and challenging yourself mentally and physically. ..... but it is done outdoors in nature ....redox potential! It is all done on an individual basis - for each of us to challenge ourself to become a better human .... or to become the human that we want to be. of course we may fall back into our old ways ..... but that isn't what this journey is about ...just my opinion of course!

Thomas has to find it for himself - of course ....as we all do.
I just know, for me, when I have been really challenged physically - my mental state soars.....

The three legged stool!

### Hemming — 2014-03-25T17:29:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-117872>

@[prAna303](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1363/) I know what you mean about going back in life and realising where I got sidetracked. I did that with a therapist last summer and that opened my eyes to so many things. As I've previously mentioned I think a big part of my eating disorder is the manifestation of PTSD from being home alone with my dad when his heart 'stopped' because of an irregular sinus nerve. Since then I've avoided situations where I'm not in completely control (which is a lot of situations). I can now see how that episode had such a huge impact on my life afterwards.

Contrary to you, I still need to have that eureka moment where I figure out what I really want.

### prAna303 — 2014-03-25T21:42:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-117895>

Since then I've avoided situations where I'm not in completely control (which is a lot of situations). I can now see how that episode had such a huge impact on my life afterwards.

Just keep asking questions and you will find answers.

### Hemming — 2014-04-03T16:44:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-119612>

> [prAna303 said:](/goto/post?id=117895)
>
> Just keep asking questions and you will find answers.

Yep, I've been working a lot on that by talking to my family and psychiatrist about how things got so sidetracked (and still are). I'm also thinking a lot about why I behave as I do in specific settings and that has also made me realise some things.

I just got back from vacation with the family and that was also a good opportunity for everyone to connect more and to some extent explain how I feel.

### Hemming — 2014-04-11T10:53:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-120952>

I haven't updated for a while because I haven't been that good.

Lately I've felt a general lack of energy and confused in some way. On top of that, I've lost some weight again and I feel like I've relapsed to a lot of the thinking I had 1.5y ago.
Right now I'm trying to get my head around all these things and figure how I go from here.

### caroline — 2014-04-11T12:11:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-120959>

Hi Thomas ..... have you read Jeremy's story?

I am so glad that you popped in.... thinking of you....

How is your family?

### Hemming — 2014-04-11T15:30:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-120972>

Yeah, its very fascinating and interesting. Obviously, I can't really say I'm trying to do the same but I'm trying to be deliberate of all of my actions and rethink everything I do in a way to optimise my health. It has just surprised how similar to when it started getting really bad I feel now. A couple of months ago I was happy with eating whereas I now want to deprive myself and restrict both things and amount. It sounds strange to some but I honestly feel like I don't deserve to eat because I'm just a gluttonous pig. Its a very introvert feeling because I would any day tell everyone else to eat exactly as they like.
I realise that all of this basically has nothing to do with food - its the manifestation of it. As my dad said 'a lot of people who go through the same as you start doing less and eating more but you do more and eat less'.

The family is doing great. My brother's wife is having a child late summer which I'm really looking forward to along with them.

### caroline — 2014-04-11T23:27:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-121044>

Thomas .... Join Jeremy's FB. .... He is a lovely young man....

I wish I could say something to help ....

Martin has an interesting story - for the past 10 days or so he has been helping his brother relocate and doing a lot of heavy lifting ....he feels great and didn't crash like he thought he might.

### sooperb — 2014-04-12T21:58:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-121164>

I think your most positive statement is "It sounds strange to some but I honestly feel like I don't deserve to eat because I'm just a gluttonous pig." That is a good level of self-awareness, it pins down exactly the location of your problem, you have an image problem that is mixed up with guilt.

You have somehow tied together a stressful event which has affected you deeply (I suspect your father's illness) with a responsibility that wasn't yours to take, fear and guilt are unsafe bedfellows. Your guilt (which is misplaced because you have done nothing wrong) has you beating your healthy self in a subconscious attempt to make yourself suffer as you perceive your father did.

I think you know this, you need to find a way forward to repair your self image and let go of the guilt. I think you said you were having professional help, I hope they find a way to let the real Thomas out of the box  He's a fine young man who needs to move on.

### Hemming — 2014-04-13T15:59:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-121261>

> [sooperb said:](/goto/post?id=121164)
>
> I think your most positive statement is "It sounds strange to some but I honestly feel like I don't deserve to eat because I'm just a gluttonous pig." That is a good level of self-awareness, it pins down exactly the location of your problem, you have an image problem that is mixed up with guilt.
>
> You have somehow tied together a stressful event which has affected you deeply (I suspect your father's illness) with a responsibility that wasn't yours to take, fear and guilt are unsafe bedfellows. Your guilt (which is misplaced because you have done nothing wrong) has you beating your healthy self in a subconscious attempt to make yourself suffer as you perceive your father did.
>
> I think you know this, you need to find a way forward to repair your self image and let go of the guilt. I think you said you were having professional help, I hope they find a way to let the real Thomas out of the box  He's a fine young man who needs to move on.

You've described it really well! I haven't thought so much about the guilt before but it makes sense to me. I've more regarded it as always feeling inadequate and therefore wanting to do a lot of things to make my parents happy instead of myself. First it was school/education then job/money then running. When I had all that I tried to do them even more because I didn't know what else to do - I just took the easy way out. When I crashed completely I thought that starving myself would show some form of willpower that they would appreciate. Obviously they would never think like that.
Another therapist I spoke to last year also mentioned the guilt and we did some exercise to 'exorcise the demons' where she was saying different lines inter alia 'realise it is not your fault, that you could have done nothing more and nobody expected anything else from you'.

I'm still seeing a psychiatrist who is really good (we have even talked sous vide equipment and recipes ). I do need to let go of that self image. Ironically, I couldn't care less about how people look or what they wear, because that's not what's important to me yet I still believe that everyone else is noticing every little detail on my body.

Thanks for chiming in, I really appreciate the thoughts.

### Hemming — 2014-04-16T13:52:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-121810>

The last few days have been up and down. This weekend I had one of my 'anxiety attacks' which I haven't had for a long time. The worst part is that it was while talking to dad on the phone and something just ticked me and I started yelling at him. I had to call him Monday to apologise even though he knows the situation.

Now I'm just really looking forward to spending time with the family and relaxing during easter.

### Hemming — 2014-04-24T14:49:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-123305>

It was great being off for Easter! The weather was great and I spent several days at my uncle's farm helping out with different things like cutting grass for the cattle and horses and laying potatoes. I also helped my brother and his wife with their new garden.
Its incredible how much just being outside and digging in the earth can make you feel better. Its just what Ameer and Jack talked about in the latest podcast.

### sooperb — 2014-04-25T08:49:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-123390>

Hi Thomas, I'm pleased that you have had a good time. It was the opportunity to divert yourself and work outside and be with people who don't judge you as hard as you judge yourself. Maybe it was a pointer in how to help yourself heal?

### Inger — 2014-04-25T10:50:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-123407>

maybe you should move to your uncle and work on his farm Hemming

### Hemming — 2014-04-25T15:44:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-123441>

> [sooperb said:](/goto/post?id=123390)
>
> Hi Thomas, I'm pleased that you have had a good time. It was the opportunity to divert yourself and work outside and be with people who don't judge you as hard as you judge yourself. Maybe it was a pointer in how to help yourself heal?

I thought about that too. I have again started to consider if I'm using my life in the way I really want to. Right now I feel so stuck in my mold, drained of mental energy for interacting with other people and generally just depressed. But those days were somehow different, it was like there was a purpose with being again.

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=123407)
>
> maybe you should move to your uncle and work on his farm Hemming

Like I haven't thought of that  That said, it couldn't work out in practice. He also said that I should get my own farm and raise cattle. The idea has been with me for a while and I wont discard it yet.

### Hemming — 2014-11-19T22:06:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-147849>

I felt like writing a short update since it's been so long.

I'm doing a lot better although things are not perfect. The good news is that I feel and look better than I've done for the past two years. I've started powerlifting which has been a fun experience because I've met a whole new set of people with a mindset I've not met before. They don't care about aesthetics, they care about being as strong as possible. That said, I really like seeing that my Buddha belly is becoming smaller and smaller (almost gone) and my sixpack is returning. Ideally I didn't give a shit about having a sixpack but I'm not just there yet.
More importantly I feel much better mentally and get a lot more things done. I still often feel guilty for eating which is my biggest problem right now. I'm trying to identify less and less with it and focus on fueling my body for optimal performance. That strength training can really destroy me if I don't eat and sleep properly.

Me deadlifting 70kg. I know 70kg is laughable but for me its an achievement and everyone in the gym have been so supportive even though my lifts are nowhere near what they're lifting.

### Hemming — 2014-11-20T15:23:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-147961>

It has been an amazing in every sense of the word. Its definitely not over yet though.

Its just an awesome feeling being able to use my body again.

### kovita — 2014-11-20T15:43:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-147964>

>
> It has been an amazing in every sense of the word. Its definitely not over yet though.
>
> Its just an awesome feeling being able to use my body again.

loosing faith and support in your own body is one of the most disappointing things in a man's life. I have experienced it. There is much more into being able to use ypur body again, at least as i sense it, you discover a whole new bunch of levels that you had no idea about before. It is like moving from being "a potato" to being an "onion". The shape might look similar but there is so many levels between the core and the superficie. Well, not sure I explain myself well, most likely not really, but maybe you'll understand.

### nonchalant — 2014-11-20T16:12:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-147972>

Good to hear from you again, Hemming.
Do you feel guilty when you eat oysters? I know when I swallow a dozen of them whole for a meal, they feel like nothing. My tummy doesn't seem to feel them, they just disappear. Like I didn't eat anything, but I'm no longer hungry. It's definitely a different eating experience.

### Hemming — 2014-11-20T16:39:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-147974>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=147972)
>
> Good to hear from you again, Hemming.
> Do you feel guilty when you eat oysters? I know when I swallow a dozen of them whole for a meal, they feel like nothing. My tummy doesn't seem to feel them, they just disappear. Like I didn't eat anything, but I'm no longer hungry. It's definitely a different eating experience.

The guilt goes for any food. The worst thing is that I then eat very low calorie foods like cabbage, kale, broccoli just to feel physically full without being satiated as I'm not getting enough energy. After big amounts of cabbage I feel extremely full physically and then the guilt is even worse as I'm still hungry but feeling like I'm about to vomit. Following that I'll have no energy mentally or physically.
Its getting better with eating more fat simply because I feel so much when I do. I do like oysters a lot and I can eat a ton of them but even with them I would feel guilty.

### Hemming — 2014-11-25T19:25:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-148447>

### Hemming — 2014-11-25T20:59:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-148464>

It sounds silly but it does help. For me the body image is not about attracting women, its about living up to an ideal and doing everything right thinking that if I don't have the body of Apollo I'm a failure.

### nicld — 2014-11-25T22:29:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-148474>

I have been working on the metal issues of my ED since March 2013 when I saw someone for it (video is on my first page of my thread). For the past 20+ years it was all about getting skinny. If I was skinny life would be great and everyone would like me. Granted I have been with my husband for over 26 years so I really was not trying to attract a mate. It has been a struggle but when I started to realize that my friends and coworkers like me for me and not what size I am I was able to start getting rid of the negative body image voices.

The thoughts are still there but I am able to squash them a little better now. That is not to say that I would not like to lose more weight, I do and will, but it is no longer all consuming and I am no longer hurting myself to attain something that I might never be able to attain.

### Hemming — 2014-11-25T23:05:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-148480>

I know exactly what you mean. I also no longer want to hurt myself and I want to feel better but the little voice is still there. Its getting better and better. Actually I've received so much positive feedback on how I look now compared to when I wasn't even at my lowest.

Keep it up Nicole! For me it also helped to talk about it and be honest about it. Thanks again for sharing your thoughts, its really helpful - maybe even for you too to talk about it?

### notsoperfick — 2014-11-26T15:47:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-148544>

It's good to know that you are still around Hemming. I'm curious about why people class themselves as having an ED just because they're fat, is that the way they classify themselves or what they suppose others think? I'm not happy being fat, it impacts on many things, I don't have what I would class an ED, I have an inability to be strict with myself which is where I see the difference between me and say, Hemming. I don't think you have an ED either Hemming, your issue manifests itself as the manner in which you use control to deal with whatever it is that bugs you. I don't think you have an issue with food, it's an issue with your psyche and how you deal/dealt with it. I'm really pleased you have made so much progress though, I hope the weight lifting goes well, I always enjoyed the challenge and though it never changed my weight, it changed my shape.

### Hemming — 2014-11-26T16:47:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-148552>

I completely agree. I've always said its not about food, that's just the physical manifestation of my control mania. Now I wouldn't say that I have an eating disorder but rather a self worth problem.

### notsoperfick — 2014-11-26T18:18:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-148563>

Every one of us has worth and value. There are so many ways that humans give to other humans without even realising it. Never doubt your worth, to your friends, your family, anyone you smile at, the dog you stroke, the people you buy from, all benefit from your being around. Even if you dug a hole in the ground and hid in it, you would have used a spade made and sold by someone and by digging you have oxygenated the soil for all the little creatures that live in it and probably moved some long dormant seeds nearer to the sun which will help them grow.

One of the things I practice is gratitude. At night, in bed, if I'm feeling low, angry or unloved I literally count my blessings and all the things I'm grateful for. It's astonishing just how much there is  You just need to switch your focus from introspective to extrovert, make the world a bigger place and imagine where you could add some special Hemming value, life is not just all about you, it's about every living thing you come into contact with and can make a difference to.

### notsoperfick — 2014-11-27T13:52:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-148634>

### nonchalant — 2015-03-18T19:05:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-158883>

I'd sure like to hear how you have been doing, Hemming.

### Hemming — 2015-03-18T22:11:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-158891>

I guess I was going to do an update in any case

Today has been a day where I've just felt good. My mental and physical energy I felt was back to normal. It reminded me how good I can feel if I allow myself.

The inflection point is related to that. I've now reached a weight which is very close to my all time high (65kg) and I'm so confused on where to go from here. On the one hand I want to get stronger and bigger, both because I want to try something new and its fun getting stronger (besides that, I have 20 guys in the gym constantly encouraging me ). On the other hand, I steel feel guilty for eating and wanting to get bigger. Every time I feel good I fall into the trap of not eating etc. because I'm thinking that all is fine now and I should maybe lose a few pounds.
I'm trying to be very conscious of how everything makes me feel. I've had a bad habit of eating a ton of vegetables to avoid calories, which just left me feeling sick and I was still hungry. Its also about allowing myself to relax and enjoy life not alwas thinking that I should be doing something.

Everyone is telling me how they can tell my mood has changed and that I look much better yet that nagging feeling of wanting to relapse and ruin my life again keeps being there. I feel like this is the time more than ever to fight it for good and bury it.

### shah78 — 2015-03-18T23:01:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale.7553/post-158894>

Keep hanging out with those 20 guys at the gym.(This is coming from a guy who joyously spends just twenty seven minutes a month in the gym, not a minute longer.) For you the alternative doesn't seem wise. Sleep there if you need to!
