> [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/allergic-to-seafood-or-iodine-dont-believe-it.11831/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/allergic-to-seafood-or-iodine-dont-believe-it.11831/)

Thank you for posting, Dr. Kruse. I just yesterday advised my compounding pharmacist to check out your website, since many things he was telling me reminded me of things I've read here, but I felt that too many things were left out or incorrect that he said to me, compared to what you've written. I'm thankful that I found your website and book before my consultation with him so that I knew where to be skeptical/critical of what I was hearing.

That aside.. I feel bad that I still don't understand the relevance of this link, and I've read it all three times people have posted it for me.. I \*know\* I'm not allergic to iodine. I've had it on my skin before in some red solution, I've had it in my veins before in the form of CT contrast... In fact I was advised to use it for my Hashi's by the compounding pharmacist I consulted with yesterday who sounded very much in tune with what is taught here in many ways, with the glaring exception of saying that I should "forget about DHA". (I disagree, but didn't feel the need to argue.)

I also know that my throat closes from certain seafood - including clams and scallops - and my serum IgE is still high from the most recent time this happened just a week or so ago now.

Unfortunately, my blood work was improperly processed for airborn allergens such as pollen and mold instead of foods though so I still don't have specifics. I am in the process of finding out EXACTLY which things I am and am not allergic to, and I plan to eat whatever seafood I can as soon as I know that it's alright for me. Perhaps in time this will change but I feel it is safe to say that right now, today, this week, and probably this month, I am not going to be eating seafood. However, I would like to get onto a much more focused diet sooner than later, so I was just hoping for alternate menu advice.

### Penny — 2015-01-26T06:57:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-eat-epi-paleo-if-allergic-to-seafood.7588/post-153861>

Hi Hope2Learn - here is something crazy to look into - not really crazy but I think it could help you - the trick is to find someone with a clue who knows what they're doing with it - that is DMSO -

I think you could use it on your lymph tumor - or whatever it was and cause it to change - look at this reference:
[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/9680092](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/9680092)

**Dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO) causes a reversible inhibition of telomerase activity in a Burkitt lymphoma cell line.**
[Sharma S](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Sharma%20S%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=9680092)1, [Raymond E](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Raymond%20E%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=9680092), [Soda H](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Soda%20H%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=9680092), [Izbicka E](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Izbicka%20E%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=9680092), [Davidson K](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Davidson%20K%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=9680092), [Lawrence R](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Lawrence%20R%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=9680092), [Von Hoff DD](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Von%20Hoff%20DD%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=9680092).
**[Author information](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/9680092#)**

**Abstract**
**INTRODUCTION:**
Telomerase is an enzyme that is required for maintenance of telomeres. This enzyme has been shown to be present in germline tissues and majority of tumors and tumor cell lines. The regulation of telomerase is an area of active investigation in different models because, potentially, inhibition of this enzyme could be important in cancer therapy. To study the regulation of this enzyme in lymphoma cell lines, we used DMSO to produce a reversible G0/G1 arrest in Raji cell line, as shown earlier \[Sawai M, Takase K, Teraoka H, Tsukada K. Reversible G1 arrest in the cell cycle of human lymphoid cell lines by dimethyl sulphoxide. Exp Cell Res 1990;187:4-10\].

**METHODS:**
In this study, we use a highly quantifiable conventional (non-amplified) assay to study the effect of DMSO on telomerase. In addition, we studied cellular proliferation and cell cycle profiles of the cells treated and, subsequently, released from DMSO induced blockage.

**RESULTS:**
In this model, DMSO reversibly inhibited telomerase activity that could be restored after release from the blockage. The inhibition of telomerase seems to parallel cellular proliferation and it appears that telomerase is regulated upon entry into the cell cycle. This view is consistent with other previously published views on relationship of telomerase with exit from cell cycle.

**CONCLUSION:**

Our observations demonstrate a novel effect of DMSO on cellular mechanisms in Raji cell line. It may provide an attractive model to further study regulation of telomerase in this cell line.

Check out this article:
[http://www.lef.org/Protocols/Cancer/Lymphoma/Page-01](http://www.lef.org/Protocols/Cancer/Lymphoma/Page-01)

Hospitals regularly use it in strokes to dissolve the blood clot - even in the brain -

I get mine from the feed store and use it with arnica for muscular pain - I looked at the contents of Penetrex - great stuff BTW -

Might not be such a hot idea for glaucoma:
[http://www.ucl.ac.uk/glaucoma-retinal-neurodegen/current-work/mechanisms](http://www.ucl.ac.uk/glaucoma-retinal-neurodegen/current-work/mechanisms)
But this guy - who you mentioned before uses it:
[http://www.healingtheeye.com/Articles/Cataracts.html](http://www.healingtheeye.com/Articles/Cataracts.html)

take care and do your own \*very diligent\* research

> [Hope2Learn said:](/goto/post?id=153092)

> Yes, but what about the above, please?
>
> I have an IgE-Mediated seafood allergy which causes anaphylaxis to the extent that even just the air around cooking scallops causes wheezing. The anaphlactic episode that netted me the allergy diagnosiswas set off by clams, so I suspect it is mollusks I am allergic to but I don't want to chance eating anything else until my RAST results are in. At least back then I lived five minutes from a hospital - now it's a full thirty minutes by highway. I can't take the risk.
>
> Right now all I know is the IgE was flagged as high at 143, but the initial items tested were not ones that I reacted to. They initially didn't order the food RAST, which frustrates me still. Hopefully soon here we'll know exactly what all caused this spike.
>
> Back in 2007 my IgE was only 32, so I know that I am not just a person with high IgE as a norm.
>
> Either way, I will not be eating oysters and many other things so I was hoping for some more guidance as how to modify the menu accordingly.

I believe Jack is trying to tell you epigenetics trumps DNA. Just because your body is CURRENTLY expressing a specific gene doesn't mean you have and/or are permanently stuck with it

DNA is just a large blueprint of potential outcomes, which are then molded and determined by environmental signals - DNA performs first 2 folds of protein, environmental signals result in second 2 folds of protein. By correcting environmental signals, you can mold your gene expression into whatever you'd like, given enough time and effort... (or some other outside sourcing of energy to correct the signals at a more rapid pace... ya know, depending on what you believe the rules of this game are).

After this I have to complete your the Jimmy Moore podcasts and I'll have completed them all, and it is/will put a lot of your (Dr. Kruse) information into perspective. Dr. Kruse, direct question on EMF avoidance and quantum nature of reality. As you may know, it is financially impossible for me to get away from the EMFs I'm bombarded with currently - I've been trying to minimize exposure by grounding every day, getting sunlight every day, and unplugging ALL the electronics in my room at night, I'm even going to take most of them out besides my laptop. Now, the question. Can intense daily meditation offset the effects of EMF? I know that sounds outrageous, but in this podcast (about 35mins in as of now), you more or less speak about homeostatic energy, and your elephant example above kind of gives merit to what I'm thinking.... essentially, meditation has been shown to have a lot of benefits, even as little as 10 minutes a day for 30 days. Meditation increases heart rate variability, changes the structure of the brain (amygdala especially), it basically relaxes and slows an individual down. Getting into quantum field theory, which I believe coincides with many spiritual teachings, I believe meditation has quantum effects. Couldn't meditation essentially put us in a more slowed down state of being (i.e. reduced state) and help us attain coherence and energy homeostasis, and if not completely, at least partially?

I've been wanting to start meditating again, and I will, but if I had any slight evidence that it could slow me down (quantum scaling) and essentially make me 'leak' less electrons to my environment, I would do it intensely every single day. I haven't begun the CT series yet as I'm slowly digging through google scholar articles on all the physicians/researchers you cite in the Leptin Prescription, but would CT protect me as well? I've been only slightly engaging myself in CT through cold showers and contrast showers since it is summer and I was thinking it was maybe a mismatch, but now with my current thought on meditation I'm thinking I may be able to do this to offset EMFs:

(1) Take my daily cold showers, if I can't take a bath, aim for 2 cold showers; spot ice; purchase some reusable ice cubes and take ice baths when possible; literally do CT every single day, as much as possible
(2) Begin hyper-intensive meditation; Begin the day with 5 mins of deep nasal breathing, eat breakfast, take a cold shower and do belly breathing/meditation, when I go outside and do my Foundation Training and/or go for a walk (which is always barefoot and with minimal clothing), perform a walking meditation or more deep nasal belly breathing, and lastly, before bed complete a 5-10 min. session of meditation as shown in a book you recommended for helping people fall asleep at night

>
> I have been swimming in the ocean every day for month now and it can be classified as deep cold thermogensis as the water has not gone any higher than fifty degrees according to the cooking thermometer I picked up at the beginning of the month. During the first week of exposure I ended up with a bit of white skin a few times, which is probably indicative of mild hypothermia, but I was able to sprint back home and warm up in the shower with a cup of coffee. Even though I am aware of the stupidity and inherent danger of what happened on those days, I have to admit, the following days ended up being quite possibly some of the best days of my entire life (not hyperbole, I swear)! I experienced virtually no hunger and was "possessed" with boundless energy. Moreover, my psychological well being and my thought patterns were characterized by an unrelenting calm and a self-assuredness I have not experienced since I was a little kid!
>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/july-9-2013-action-alert.7592/post-89781>

Protect your right to personalized medicine: Voice your opposition to proposed legislation

This month, the Senate will vote on a bill that could restrict compounding pharmacies across the nation. The proposed legislation would allow the federal government control over pharmacies and healthcare practices that prescribe and prepare compounded hormone replacement therapy (HRT). If the bill is approved, the Food and Drug Administrations (FDA) would negatively impact how doctors run their practices by taking unprecedented control over compounding pharmacies and prescriber-patient relationships. Hereâ€™s what you can do right now:

1 2 3
Take action today.

To get informed about the issue and send an email to legislators, go to [www.protectmycompounds.com](http://www.protectmycompounds.com). It's easy - there is language you can customize to tell your personal story.

Spread the word.

Forward this email to all those you feel appropriate. Encourage family and friends to participate as well. If you are a prescriber, click here for a handout you can give your patients directing them to take actionâ€”you can include it with prescription bags and treatment plans.

Keep it going.

Use the social media elements we've created for you to share on Twitter, Facebook and LinkedIn from [www.protectmycompounds.com/social](http://www.protectmycompounds.com/social) and watch for more information, tools and resources from PCCA.

Please leverage social media to get this message out. Thanks JK

# Thread: When is weight gain or no weight loss more than just a leptin issue?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-is-weight-gain-or-no-weight-loss-more-than-just-a-leptin-issue.7594/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nicld — 2013-07-10T04:16:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-is-weight-gain-or-no-weight-loss-more-than-just-a-leptin-issue.7594/post-89794>

I have been on the leptin reset for about 5 weeks now and I know that I have put on weight (no scales for me). I am making sure to eat my big arse breakfast usually within 30 minutes but sometimes it is a bit longer if I do not wake up earlier to take my thyroid meds. Keeping is low carb but how much, not sure since I am not tracking (eating disorder issue). I have not really started to workout yet, just some mowing and light walking and biking (trying to recover from an exercise addiction too).

I did just get 15 tubes of blood drawn for various test to see where a lot of the hormones are at. Thankfully I took my list of what blood tests I would like based on reading a bunch of Dr. Kruseâ€™s blogs and my doctor agreed to all of them so we will see where they are at and hopefully I got most of them. I did not do the saliva cortisol one yet. I can send out for that one but even if the cortisol is messed up, the basic leptin reset should fix that right? I will post my results when I get them and see where I need to do., if anything.

I am also trying the cold therogenisis right now. It is getting hot here and I do NOT do well in heat. I am hoping that the cold baths at night help me sleep better since we really do not like to use our window AC much.

I am going to continue this since I am pretty sure that I really really messed up my hormones due to years of chronic undereater and over exercising and how can it hurt.

Thanks

Nicole

### caroline — 2013-07-10T04:50:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-is-weight-gain-or-no-weight-loss-more-than-just-a-leptin-issue.7594/post-89797>

Welcome Nicole! Have you read the Leptin reset? it sounds like you are making a good start. You shouldn't be working out while on the reset - light walking is fine.
What is your EMF exposure? are you following the light cycles? are you drinking spring water?
Dr. Kruse's book is a great introduction to all of this - it makes it much easier to get started in my opinion. It is available in paper and kindle. The webinars are great too if you are able to join.

It sounds like you have a good Doc who is willing to be a partner with you and support you.
It is a good idea to get a diurnal cortisol ASI test done so you know your levels for hormones and cortisol and DHEA.
Limiting EMF's is huge as are grounding and paying attention to light cycles.

Keep reading and asking questions ... we are happy to help!

I just went back to check the leptin reset .....so none to very few carbs and 50 to 75 gr protein or more if needed.

### nicld — 2013-07-11T04:54:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-is-weight-gain-or-no-weight-loss-more-than-just-a-leptin-issue.7594/post-89860>

I am reading it right now and working on the EMF exposure. I do drink "ground" well water at home but not at work, I will start bringing my own water in. Not really doing any workouts per say, just mowing the lawn once a week. Summers seem so short here and I love doing it.

I am really trying hard to not stress about the weight gain and focus on the end goal of getting the hormones fixed.

Thanks

# Thread: Recycling of Mg in cells

### marcus — 2013-07-10T08:15:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recycling-of-mg-in-cells.7598/post-89805>

Hi everyone!

I am not a chemistry geek, so maybe the answer to my question is quite simple. I am thinking about this one: Mg (magnesium) is essential in the metabolism of the cell, especially for ATP. Where does it end up in the whole process of energy production? Can't it be recycled? Will it finally leave the cell / body? Bound to which endproduct?

Thx

### Jack Kruse — 2013-07-10T15:37:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recycling-of-mg-in-cells.7598/post-89822>

it can be recycled but it is coupled to Calcium homeostasis.......when calcium is forced to do something unusual like it is by and EFL EMF........Magnesium cant not work......it loses it dance partner and it loses water........and Maggie is hydrophillic........it can not work in a dehydrated landscape.

I can attest to this... in the past 2 months my Mag RBC has gone from 5.3 to 6.3 and with that increase has been a extraordinary need for water. And once we switched to RO water, that need became even greater. Especially when stressed, I've been doing more Mag foot soaks - and the need for water increases.

### cinnamon — 2013-07-10T19:57:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recycling-of-mg-in-cells.7598/post-89838>

I get lower leg muscle cramping that is instantly resolved by drinking a small quantity of water. I never have the muscle cramping except when it's nearly time to get up in the morning. It wakes me.

Perhaps water is used in some way every time magnesium is? And, especially at night, when there might be a lot of processes going that use magnesium, and there is no steady intake of water, my body is running out of water and getting dehydrated.

I am now taking around 600 mg elemental magnesium orally each day (400 in malate, 200 in bisglycinate). I am also applying mag chloride generously twice per day. My daily water intake is about 5-6 litres of good water (ice cold, non-fluoridated spring water).

Now I'm wondering if I reduce the mag just a little, maybe my body hydration would last through the night? It might mean that there is a little less mag available for processes, but I might get another half hour or so of sound sleep...

Does this make sense? Or am I completely confused here.

Personally, I found the more magnesium the better regarding leg cramps up to 1200 mg Magnesium/day. I thought that it was amazing that my bowel tolerance was that high. My curcumstance maybe fairly unique - currently consume A2 milk kiffer daily, long history of high carb and relatively low fat, years of chelated calcium suppliments and 10 yrs of hypothyroidism. # Thread: Tell me about your diet!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tell-me-about-your-diet.7599/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 23
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### marcus — 2013-07-10T08:22:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tell-me-about-your-diet.7599/post-89806>

Hey guys,

just to get an impression of your diet - two simple questions.

1) Are you eating raw or cooked?

2) Coffee - yes or no?

Speaking for me: raw is totally preferred! And coffee is a no go!

I love to hear some words of you why you are going your way... but please stick to the topic.

Cheers
marcus

### caroline — 2013-07-10T08:45:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tell-me-about-your-diet.7599/post-89808>

I am eating alot raw - seafood and GFbeef. I do have a BPC - but I probably shouldn't be drinking it!
I love raw beef - I find it much easier to digest and need much less of it raw.

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-07-10T08:56:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tell-me-about-your-diet.7599/post-89810>

^What kinds of fish, and what cuts of beef do you eat raw? I'm curious because I'd love to start incorporating at least _some_ raw food into my diet, and cook when I'm with family/friends/fiance, but alone or making food for myself eat food raw. Until I get my next paycheck in a few weeks, I have no way of getting grass-fed beef besides ground beef, and I've heard that you shouldn't eat that raw since there is increased surface area for bacteria. I also have heard to not eat fish raw unless your getting 'sushi grade' or really fresh fish from a market or caught yourself. I can get 2lbs of lean frozen wild caught salmon rather easily, I was thinking about at least making ceviche with it, and it probably wouldn't be raw in the truest sense, but at least far less denatured. If anyone thinks I can consume grass-fed ground beef raw, I may just start throwing it in the oven as a meat loaf for like 10 minutes to warm up and eat it as so...

### caroline — 2013-07-10T10:08:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tell-me-about-your-diet.7599/post-89812>

What I do ......I buy GF rump steak with as much fat as possible. I put it on a wooden board and stick it in the fridge - I sometimes sprinkle with red pepper flakes and black pepper etc. I like it best after at least a week - it starts to dry.

When I say raw fish -I mean sushi. I use lemon or lime juice or sometimes Balsamic vinegar and fresh herbs etc.

Have you tried beef carpaccio? Check out Inger's recipes with pictures. What about Jerky?

Inger does something with frozen fish and she has pics for that too. I think she called it pate.

MHS anyone??

### beach bunny — 2013-07-10T17:24:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tell-me-about-your-diet.7599/post-89832>

Now that summer is here I am eating a lot more veggies...and they are almost 100% raw (except kale/cruciferous veg- so that they don't suppress my thyroid further).

I try to eat raw Oysters at least once a week..they are so delicious! Usually I just squeeze lemon juice and a tiny bit of cocktail sauce on them.

I also tried beef carpaccio for the first time and was so amazed at how much I loved it!

I have not drank coffee in years (although I tend to crave it when I smell it).

### MeghanK — 2013-07-10T17:51:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tell-me-about-your-diet.7599/post-89837>

I don't eat a lot of anything raw... I like my veggies steamed or cooked in my omelette in the morning or stir fried in coconut oil with my fish for dinner... is that bad? Is steaming OK? And for dinner cooking my fish with the veggies in a frying pan only (200 degrees F) with spices mixed in...
No, coffee... just TONS OF tea  LOVE loose leaf black and green

### marcus — 2013-07-10T20:22:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tell-me-about-your-diet.7599/post-89841>

It's an endless discussion about 'raw or cooked'. I prefer raw. There are several reasons for doing so. Just to mention some from a scientific perspective: ever thought about AGEs and ALEs? Jack mentions them in his quilt as well. Animal products are highly ranked in comparison to other foodstuff in therms of AGEs/ALEs when they are cooked. And what about the loss of nutrients?

@Jack Kruse: how do you eat your seafood and meat and animal fats? Raw or cooked?

### Jack Kruse — 2013-07-10T21:09:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tell-me-about-your-diet.7599/post-89842>

1\. both
2\. Now I am.....but when I began I shunned coffee.

### Shijin13 — 2013-07-10T21:42:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tell-me-about-your-diet.7599/post-89845>

I do a balance of raw and cooked. seafood is the majority of my protein - with the occasional grassfed beef/pork. I usually have seafood at least once a day, but more often than not its twice/day.

Of recent I've moved into a phase, where I can't eat the BAB, and I'm lucky to eat one egg, or a small amount of meat. I always have my Bulletproof coffee every morning.

I eat raw when my intuition tells me I need to... otherwise... I just roll with what ever we're eating...

### Chrysanthemama — 2013-07-11T01:04:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tell-me-about-your-diet.7599/post-89849>

Balance of both raw and cooked. I live far from the sea, so my seafood tends to be smoked. Although we eat sashimi when we can.

I drink two shots of espresso in the morning. I love my coffee!

### SeaHorse — 2013-07-11T01:28:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tell-me-about-your-diet.7599/post-89852>

Mostly cooked, but some raw (oysters and organ meats) every week. No coffee...tried the ultra pure bulletproof decaf and it still was a big no.

### endless — 2013-07-11T08:07:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tell-me-about-your-diet.7599/post-89865>

Raw GF beef, elk, venison...I won't buy ground but I have a small manual meat grinder and will grind certain cuts myself. Otherwise just partially thaw and slice very thin or dice and add seasonings. Raw fish as sushi, or raw salmon as ceviche. Raw oysters when I can afford them. I like raw beef or lamb heart too.

I eat cooked meat and seafood as well....just had a dozen and a half big steamed mussels for dinner.

No coffee for me, caffeine is not good for me at all.

### marcus — 2013-07-13T07:48:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tell-me-about-your-diet.7599/post-90020>

To those who eat cooked and raw meat / seafoods: how is your digestion? I my experience you can't live in both 'worlds': either you are cooking or you are eating raw. Doing both ends up in bad digestion.

I eat both with no problems at all. I usually have raw beef for breakfast with a BPC and then throw a leg of lamb in the crockpot etc. etc. and seafood at nite.... I will often have raw beef and cooked shrimp in the morning as well - what ever is in the fridge really.....

### Inger — 2013-07-14T21:15:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tell-me-about-your-diet.7599/post-90119>

I eat almost everything raw, except of cooked mussels once in a while and cooked fish/broth once in a while. I am not religious about raw at all, but I just love the taste and the feel. Kind of like an addiction..lol

Here what I had yesterday for lunch, raw salad from the garden only with lots of good EVOO and balsamic vinegar and salt, the carrots were dirty too..lol Raw wild edible smoothie with mostly nettles, oysters au naturel. Wild berries for dessert.
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

For dinner then raw pollock, this time with raw fresh ginger, a little raw honey, raw lemon with peel, Seaweed (Dulse), raw onion, salt, lots of EVOO.
Yum, it was very good!

I seldom cheat witn HWC... on lingonberries or so when with others.. or some dark chocolate. But very seldom. I really try to avoid dairy. I do have some raw French Gorgonzola once in a while!
I have no issues mixing raw and cooked anymore, like fish broth after raw dinner. Like today I had the weirdest lunch! About 200 grams of raw organic dried Persian figs.. sooo many.. and then after I had 7 oysters! And one glass Organic rose wine. It digested just perfectly!

I do coffee but try to limit it as I feel my body does better without. Now I have slipped into drinking it again.. just so stressing here now. When it gets stressy I have hard time stay away from it. Stupid, I know.

### Anna — 2013-07-15T12:05:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tell-me-about-your-diet.7599/post-90132>

Our hunter gatherers were intuitive - they created fire and knew instinctively which foods to cook and which could be eaten raw - following your instinct I feel is spot on as Shijin13 suggests. Once leptin is active, our tastebuds change and inturn our body encourages us to fuel with the correct foods.

### salamander — 2013-07-15T16:06:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tell-me-about-your-diet.7599/post-90144>

Like a real simple thing is lemon juice, salt, olive oil, and maybe some spices like cumin, tumeric, rosemary. You get the picture.

### marcus — 2013-07-15T22:12:52+0300

2\. where to live (where best to live)

# Thread: Are you a victim of your genes?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-you-a-victim-of-your-genes.7605/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dextery — 2013-07-11T13:06:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-you-a-victim-of-your-genes.7605/post-89869>

When I first met Dr Kruse on line, I think it was at PaleoHacks, he said that your thoughts change your DNA. That idea was derided and laughed at by the know it alls over there. Of course, everyone knows that you are born with your genes of your parents and there is nothing you can do about them. How many times have you heard, "cancer runs in my family"...or "heart disease runs in my family?"

I immediately got the idea in my head...but it was difficult to accept. Except when I started thinking about something that I firmly believed about my life.. that is...... I am totally responsible for my own condition if life. Happiness, money, status, relationships....but I never extended that concept to apply to my own health.

And of course, I never extended it to think that my thoughts could change my DNA. At first, I didn't get that my thoughts can change how I express my DNA. But I just kept on thinking about this thought thing and finally it started to sink in.

Epigenetics. You can change how you express your genes. Your genes are not laid in concrete.

Paige Adams, a health practitioner in Nashville that Dr Kruse has worked with, posted the following link on Facebook about Epigenetics.
[http://medicinereport.com/article/br...as-epigenetics](http://medicinereport.com/article/br...as-epigenetics).

I had never heard of Bruce Lipton, cellular biologist, before tonight. He talks about the following:

".......... Bruce Lipton continued research which led him to believe in Epigenetics and the idea that your beliefs (as well as the beliefs of those around you) can actually control the genes within the cells. As an example, Bruce mentions the placebo effect, which has been proven to work just on the basis of your belief alone."

There is a video on this site from Mercola about Epigenetics which pretty much lays out Dr Kruse's and Bruce Lipton's point of view that...No we are not victims of our genes.

It seems that the idea of Epigenetics has been around for a while, but it took Dr Kruse's courage and determination to present the concept to the laypeople. I think Mercola actually has lifted the idea of epigenetics from Dr Kruse.

[http://medicinereport.com/article/bruce-lipton-and-his-ideas-epigenetics](http://medicinereport.com/article/bruce-lipton-and-his-ideas-epigenetics).

### Jack Kruse — 2013-07-11T13:31:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-you-a-victim-of-your-genes.7605/post-89874>

We are not victims of our genes.........we are however, victims of our thoughts.

### GlenPDQ — 2013-07-11T15:17:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-you-a-victim-of-your-genes.7605/post-89879>

And hopefully we don't have to worry about extensive repair of any negative epigenetics we were dealt because of what the grandparents and parents did/ate.

### HoneyChild — 2013-07-11T17:15:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-you-a-victim-of-your-genes.7605/post-89885>

There was an interesting documentary last night called The Truth About Personality which I think you'd all find interesting. I'm not sure if those outside of the UK will be able to watch it but it might be possible through hide-my-ass.com

[http://www.bbc.co.uk/iplayer/episode/b036ypxw/Horizon\_20122013\_The\_Truth\_About\_Personality/](http://www.bbc.co.uk/iplayer/episode/b036ypxw/Horizon_20122013_The_Truth_About_Personality/)

"Michael Mosley explores the latest science about how our personalities are created - and whether they can be changed.

Despite appearances, Mosley is a pessimist who constantly frets about the future. He wants to worry less and become more of an optimist.

He tries out two techniques to change this aspect of his personality - with surprising results.

And he travels to the frontiers of genetics and neuroscience to find out about the forces that shape all our personalities."

# Thread: PROGESTERONE TO ESTROGEN RATION 22 to 1
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/progesterone-to-estrogen-ration-22-to-1.7609/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### motivatedhealthseeker — 2013-07-11T18:18:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/progesterone-to-estrogen-ration-22-to-1.7609/post-89891>

Hello
My Progesterone to estrogen ratio is 22 to 1. Estrogen is ok but Progesterone is very low. Estrogen was 179.6 (lab value at 33-200) and Progesterone was 4.7 (lab value 1-22) The test was done on day 21. I am a 39 yo female. I am using 20 mg of progesterall by Dr Lee transdermally on day 12-26. Is this enough to bring up my levels into the upper 1/3 of the range as Dr Kruse recommends? Anyone else have similar issues? I also struggled with adrenal fatigue as well.
Thank you!

### MsYo — 2013-07-12T02:40:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/progesterone-to-estrogen-ration-22-to-1.7609/post-89914>

> [motivatedhealthseeker said:](/goto/post?id=89891)
>
> Hello
> My Progesterone to estrogen ratio is 22 to 1. Estrogen is ok but Progesterone is very low. Estrogen was 179.6 (lab value at 33-200) and Progesterone was 4.7 (lab value 1-22) The test was done on day 21. I am a 39 yo female. I am using 20 mg of progesterall by Dr Lee transdermally on day 12-26. Is this enough to bring up my levels into the upper 1/3 of the range as Dr Kruse recommends? Anyone else have similar issues? I also struggled with adrenal fatigue as well.
> Thank you!

It would depend on your absorption rate using Progesterall or any other BHRT cream. I am working through similar issues with initially low progesterone (10) I started almost a year ago and my most recent labs the value was (19). I use 25mg of biodentical hormones (from yams) on day 12-26. I also suffer from adrenal issues as well as thyroid issues. You should also review other lab details to guage your individual treatment plans. Also, be patient and persistant. I started with BHRT to help with reoccurring fibroids. Read the hormone 101/102 blogs for good reference info. I recently (this week) increased my Pregnenolone dose and I am noticing an improvement already.

fibroids------------> iodine

...........

### JanSz — 2013-07-12T04:50:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/progesterone-to-estrogen-ration-22-to-1.7609/post-89922>

> [motivatedhealthseeker said:](/goto/post?id=89891)
>
> Hello
> My Progesterone to estrogen ratio is 22 to 1. Estrogen is ok but Progesterone is very low. Estrogen was 179.6 (lab value at 33-200) and Progesterone was 4.7 (lab value 1-22) The test was done on day 21. I am a 39 yo female. I am using 20 mg of progesterall by Dr Lee transdermally on day 12-26. Is this enough to bring up my levels into the upper 1/3 of the range as Dr Kruse recommends? Anyone else have similar issues? I also struggled with adrenal fatigue as well.
> Thank you!

I am not a doctor.

Check this post and couple posts below, may interest you.
[http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6427-PG-E2-ratio&p=81508&viewfull=1#post81508](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6427-PG-E2-ratio&p=81508&viewfull=1#post81508)

I look three ways (and more) when evaluating progesterone/estrogen.

1\. I am talking about blood levels of this hormones, preferably tested at LabCorp

2\. I would like levels to be as close as possible to dark blue line representing population average levels of progesterone and estradiol at given day in menstrual cycle.
For estradiol this are good levels.
For progesterone they are the minimum desirable.

3\. I would like to get P/E ratio about 200 as recommended by dr Lam (requires higher progesterone level than minimum)

4\. I would like to get P/E ratio about 500 as recommended by dr Kruse (requires higher progesterone than dr Lam's), those levels of progesterone are above top range (ie. much higher than the upper 1/3 that you have mentioned). They are rather comparable with levels of pregnant women in last trimester.

\---------

This table at post above gives P/E2 ratios calculated as molar ratio using indicated calculator.
[http://imageshack.us/a/img255/7585/image303w.jpg](http://imageshack.us/a/img255/7585/image303w.jpg)

\---------

> Estrogen was 179.6 (lab value at 33-200) and
> Progesterone was 4.7 (lab value 1-22)
> The test was done on day 21.

Assume that it was a blood test, so we have

E2=179.6pg/mL blue line on chart is at 130pg/mL E2 is little high
progesterone=4.7ng/mL blue line is at 13 ng/mL, you are low comparing to minimum standard

\--------
To get P/E2~500
when E2=179.6
requires
progesterone=103ng/mL
\------
To get P/E2~500
when E2=130
requires
progesterone=75ng/mL
\--------
So far it was about P/E2 ratio
But usually dr Lam and dr Kruse are describing P/E, where E would stand for Total Estrogens, where estradiol is only part of the Total.
So, above requirements for progesterone may only go higher.
\--------

You have two jobs and not one.

You need to lower your Estradiol and check other estrogens.
Eating cruciferous vegetables may help.
High estrogens tend to raise SHBG and that will ruin your testosterone.

The other job is to raise progesterone.
When all works well,
we produce a lots of pregnenolone from LDL cholesterol, facilitated by
good levels of vit A (retinol from meat) not Beta-carotene (from vegetables)
good vit D (100 loks good)
good FreeT3 (and low RT3)---->>iodine & selenium & natural thyroid hormone & about 40 micronutrients as indicated by Spectracell test results

get above into good shape

That likely may not be enough. If we stop making enough pregnenolone it is hard to re-start production.

I do not like creams. Not only they stain clothes and restrain access to water.
worst
Assuming that they work, they do not show on the blood tests. It is hard to work without benefit of testing.

For my self I have found
pregnenolone Micronized Lipid Matrix 150mg tabs from Nutricology
I am a man 73yo.
This pegnenolone saves my quality of life.
It ends up raising my progesterone to almost 5ng/mL (respectable levels even for women),
higher than what you have now.
It ends up raising my morning cortisol to about 20, which is rather ideal top range number.
I suggest that you give it a try.

If you would like to try this, it would be a good idea to optimize Steroid Hormone Panel in more complete way rather than just P & E2.
Lets cover at least your fatigue.

Look at that post (link above) for tests at LabCorp that are useful.

...

### Jack Kruse — 2013-07-12T05:30:26+0300

### Shijin13 — 2013-07-12T15:40:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/progesterone-to-estrogen-ration-22-to-1.7609/post-89950>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=89922)
>
> I am not a doctor.
>
> Check this post and couple posts below, may interest you.
> [http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6427-PG-E2-ratio&p=81508&viewfull=1#post81508](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6427-PG-E2-ratio&p=81508&viewfull=1#post81508)
>
> I look three ways (and more) when evaluating progesterone/estrogen.
>
> 1\. I am talking about blood levels of this hormones, preferably tested at LabCorp
>
> 2\. I would like levels to be as close as possible to dark blue line representing population average levels of progesterone and estradiol at given day in menstrual cycle.
> For estradiol this are good levels.
> For progesterone they are the minimum desirable.
>
> 3\. I would like to get P/E ratio about 200 as recommended by dr Lam (requires higher progesterone level than minimum)
>
> 4\. I would like to get P/E ratio about 500 as recommended by dr Kruse (requires higher progesterone than dr Lam's), those levels of progesterone are above top range (ie. much higher than the upper 1/3 that you have mentioned). They are rather comparable with levels of pregnant women in last trimester.
>
> \---------
>
> This table at post above gives P/E2 ratios calculated as molar ratio using indicated calculator.
> [http://imageshack.us/a/img255/7585/image303w.jpg](http://imageshack.us/a/img255/7585/image303w.jpg)
>
> \---------
>
>
> Assume that it was a blood test, so we have
>
> E2=179.6pg/mL blue line on chart is at 130pg/mL E2 is little high
> progesterone=4.7ng/mL blue line is at 13 ng/mL, you are low comparing to minimum standard
>
> \--------
> To get P/E2~500
> when E2=179.6
> requires
> progesterone=103ng/mL
> \------
> To get P/E2~500
> when E2=130
> requires
> progesterone=75ng/mL
> \--------
> So far it was about P/E2 ratio
> But usually dr Lam and dr Kruse are describing P/E, where E would stand for Total Estrogens, where estradiol is only part of the Total.
> So, above requirements for progesterone may only go higher.
> \--------
>
> You have two jobs and not one.
>
> You need to lower your Estradiol and check other estrogens.
> Eating cruciferous vegetables may help.
> High estrogens tend to raise SHBG and that will ruin your testosterone.
>
> The other job is to raise progesterone.
> When all works well,
> we produce a lots of pregnenolone from LDL cholesterol, facilitated by
> good levels of vit A (retinol from meat) not Beta-carotene (from vegetables)
> good vit D (100 loks good)
> good FreeT3 (and low RT3)---->>iodine & selenium & natural thyroid hormone & about 40 micronutrients as indicated by Spectracell test results
>
> get above into good shape
>
> That likely may not be enough. If we stop making enough pregnenolone it is hard to re-start production.
>
> I do not like creams. Not only they stain clothes and restrain access to water.
> worst
> Assuming that they work, they do not show on the blood tests. It is hard to work without benefit of testing.
>
> For my self I have found
> pregnenolone Micronized Lipid Matrix 150mg tabs from Nutricology
> I am a man 73yo.
> This pegnenolone saves my quality of life.
> It ends up raising my progesterone to almost 5ng/mL (respectable levels even for women),
> higher than what you have now.
> It ends up raising my morning cortisol to about 20, which is rather ideal top range number.
> I suggest that you give it a try.
>
> If you would like to try this, it would be a good idea to optimize Steroid Hormone Panel in more complete way rather than just P & E2.
> Lets cover at least your fatigue.
>
> Look at that post (link above) for tests at LabCorp that are useful.
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
> ...

Great break down Jan...

WOW! Thank you soooo much for such a detailed answer! I have a ton more questions so please bear with me. I can not find a doctor to address these things to save my life in these last 3 years.. literally! Yes these were blood tests but not sure of which lab my doctor uses.I was trying to be brief with my question but I think having the whole panel results might be more beneficial:

Estradiol E2 is 179.6 pg/ML (33-200)
testosterone total 22 ng/dL (9-55)
testosterone bioavailable 7.0 ng/dL (4.1-25.5)
progesterone 4.7 ng/mL (1-22)
free testosterone 2.7 pg?mL (1.3-9.2)
sex hormone binding globulin 55 nmol/L (30-135) What are optimal levels for SHBG please?
DHEA done on another lab 6 months ago 204 (31-701)

The above tests are form Jan 2013 and I had another hormone test done from labcorp in June of 2012 and in that every hormone seemed to be low. Could it be the difference between 2 different labs or can results be that different in just 6 months. It was suppose to be done on day 21 but I ended up having cycle on day 18 so in reality it was day 3 of follicular cycle

Estradiol 22.3 pg/ML (12.5-166)
progesterone 0.3 ng/ mL (1.7-27)
free testosterone pg/mL < 0.2 pg/mL (0-2.2)
total testosterone 10 ng/mL (8-48)

prolactin 23.8 ng/mL (3-20) My prolactin has always been midly raised and I read that gluten sensitive people can have mildly raised prolactin levels but Ive been gluten free for 3+ years and its still mildy raised?!

I had to push to get these tests done as I was having cycles every 18 days or so twice a month..so one cycle normal and one with 18 days. It had started out with that happening twice a year about 3 yrs ago. So I knew I had to be low in progesterone for this to be happening. Anovulation I guess with pregenelone steal happening with adrenal fatigue etc.
I was thinking of taking DIM plus for decreasing estrogen. I do eat broccoli or cauliflower as rice with lunch and dinner almost every day but its obviously not enough. I have read though that with adrenal fatigue one cant take precursor hormones as they will not be converted properly and that only end hormones should be supplemented. Any thoughts on that? Its hard to find enough thin skin to put 75 mg of progesterone cream on. I am also on 75 mcg of t3 for a reverse t3 issue I had last year..I stay on this for not even though reverse t3 issue is resolved but the reason for developing rt3 is still being addressed which is the low cortisol. I know t3 only is hard on your sex hormones so will try adding in NDT along with t3 in a while.
What is the test I need to request to test all the estrogens? Estrogen fraction test? YOu mentioned transdermal progesterone creams do not show up on blood tests? So how does one see where they stand on a hormonal panel? Will I have to do a salivary test to see where my estrogen and progesterone stand? How long after supplementation should one test?
Again thank you greatly for your time and patience..its very much appreciated!

### motivatedhealthseeker — 2013-07-13T20:55:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/progesterone-to-estrogen-ration-22-to-1.7609/post-90066>

> [MsYo said:](/goto/post?id=89914)
>
> It would depend on your absorption rate using Progesterall or any other BHRT cream. I am working through similar issues with initially low progesterone (10) I started almost a year ago and my most recent labs the value was (19). I use 25mg of biodentical hormones (from yams) on day 12-26. I also suffer from adrenal issues as well as thyroid issues. You should also review other lab details to guage your individual treatment plans. Also, be patient and persistant. I started with BHRT to help with reoccurring fibroids. Read the hormone 101/102 blogs for good reference info. I recently (this week) increased my Pregnenolone dose and I am noticing an improvement already.

Thank you Msyo. Are you doing the progesterone cream transdermally and what have you increased your dose to currently if you dont mind me asking?

So how does one see where they stand on a hormonal panel? Will I have to do a salivary test to see where my estrogen and progesterone stand? How long after supplementation should one test?
> Again thank you greatly for your time and patience..its very much appreciated!

> it was day 3 of follicular cycle
>
> Estradiol 22.3 pg/ML (12.5-166)
> progesterone 0.3 ng/ mL (1.7-27)
> free testosterone pg/mL < 0.2 pg/mL (0-2.2)
> total testosterone 10 ng/mL (8-48)

It happened unintentionally, but **day #3 of cycle is the best information at this time**,
and
it should be easy for you to figure out beginning of menstrual cycle,
so you should have no problem getting again tested at day 2-4.

Estradiol should be slightly higher, but lets be happy with what it is.

progesterone is definitely low.
Please do not use any creams.
Use

> [http://www.thefind.com/search?query=Pregnenolone+150+mg+Micronized+Lipid+Matrix](http://www.thefind.com/search?query=Pregnenolone+150+mg+Micronized+Lipid+Matrix)
>
> Pregnenolone 150 mg Micronized Lipid Matrix 60 Scored Tablets by Allergy Research Group
> [http://www.amazon.com/dp/B003PR3BMO...e=395261&creativeASIN=B003PR3BMO&linkCode=asn](http://www.amazon.com/dp/B003PR3BMO/ref=asc_df_B003PR3BMO2627029?tag=thefind0282486-20&creative=395261&creativeASIN=B003PR3BMO&linkCode=asn)
>
> Important that it must be (Micronized Lipid Matrix)(MLM), they have similar one with out it being MLM
>
> good price when you buy dozen bottles at once:
>
> [http://www.herbspro.com/shop/xq/asp/pid.76456/qx/productDetail.htm](http://www.herbspro.com/shop/xq/asp/pid.76456/qx/productDetail.htm)
> $29.80 per bottle when you buy dozen
>
>
> > Pregnenolone 150 mg Micronized Lipid Matrix 60 Scored Tablets by Allergy Research Group
> > **Eat every day, non stop
> > one tab at wakeup
> > one tab 3-4 hrs latter
> >
> > do not use any progesterone creams,
> > make concise list of the stuff you are taking, dose and when taken**
> >
> > You may want to double above dose on days 12-26
> >
> > > I am a 39 yo female. I am using 20 mg of progesterall by Dr Lee transdermally on day 12-26.

> > DHEA done on another lab 6 months ago 204 (31-701)
>
>
> DHEA 50 mg Micronized Lipid Matrix 60 Scored Tablets by Allergy Research Group
> [http://www.amazon.com/Micronized-Ta...x+60+Scored+Tablets+by+Allergy+Research+Group](http://www.amazon.com/Micronized-Tablets-Allergy-Research-Group/dp/B004BZJCCC/ref=sr_1_1?s=hpc&ie=UTF8&qid=1375142758&sr=1-1&keywords=DHEA+50+mg+Micronized+Lipid+Matrix+60+Scored+Tablets+by+Allergy+Research+Group)
>
> **Eat one tab at wakeup**

> > prolactin 23.8 ng/mL (3-20)
>
>
> Get script:
>
> Cycloset 0.8mg
> 6 tablets before breakfast
>
> For 90 days, 6 refills
> \---
> **But eat only 3 tablets**

> > I am also on 75 mcg of t3
>
>
> Being on 75mcg-T3 may be interesting.
> You are likely having low cortisol.
> When you start on pregnenolone 2-3 hrs latter you may already get additional cortisol,
> and the fun may begin.
>
> If you heart starts pounding, do not take next pregnenolone dose.
> This may not happen, but if it does,
> stop supplementing T3 and pregnenolone for a week
> then
> resume pregnenolone
>
> T3 should not interfere with taking DHEA
>
> Latter, you may resume much smaller T3 dose.
>
> We will figure better dose latter.
>
> Assuming all is as usual, it would be better if you could rather use Natural Thyroid Hormone.
>
> You may get Thyroid-S over internet, it comes in 1 grain tablets.
> I use it.

> After you get two full menstrual cycles while on above doses of pregnenolone, DHEA and Cycloset
> get blood drawn for test on day 2-4 of your third cycle.
>
> Preferably do tests at LabCorp.
> Draw blood 7:30AM, and two more for cortisol, noon & 2:30PM
>
>
> 1 ------- Pregnenolone, Mass Spectrometry--Test Number: 140707 CPT Code: 84140
> 4 ------- Progesterone--Test Number: 004317 CPT Code: 84144
> 6 ------- Cortisol, AM & PM Test Number: 104000 CPT Code: 82533(x2) (7:30AM, 12PM)
> 9 ------- Dehydroepiandrosterone (DHEA)--Test Number: 004100 CPT Code: 82626
> 10 ------- Androstenedione, LCMS--Test Number: 004705 CPT Code: 82157
> 11 ------- Testosterone, Total, Serum Test Number: 004226 CPT Code: 84403
> 12 ------- Testosterone, Free and Weakly Bound--Test Number: 143255 CPT Code: 84402; 84403
> 13 ------- SHBG
> 17 ------- Estradiol, Sensitive Test Number: 140244 CPT Code: 82670
> 35 ------- Prolactin Test Number: 004465 CPT Code: 84146
>
> If you are able to arrange, get also thyroid hormones.
> It is less important at this time.
>
> **Important is that you get Spectracell ASAP**
>
> \------------
>
>
> Take
> 2/day-->Super Selenium Complex, 200 mcg 100 capsules (lef.org)
> 1 dropperfull/day=48mg/day
> Lugol's Iodine Family Pack -- 6 (2 fl. oz.) bottles
> [http://www.amazon.com/Lugols-Iodine-Family-Pack-bottles/dp/B001RRP3FW](http://www.amazon.com/Lugols-Iodine-Family-Pack-bottles/dp/B001RRP3FW)
> recalculate dose if using other iodine/iodide source.

I have read that DHEA or oral Progesterone is not good to take since its metabolized by the liver and is not as effective as transdermal creams? I see a lot of conflicting info on the net and not sure what is appropriate or not. I did see an article today by Dr Merocola where Dr wright talked about how men should be applying DHEA transmucusouly instead of orally as DHEA can turn into estrogen for the men. Can you please share any articles or any info about the oral progesterone?
I have recently started using vitex or chasteberry and that seems to be helping with breast tenderness etc. I am still using the transdermal creams but using more vaginally than on skin. I am already on metformin so feeling a bit squeemish about starting cycloset even though it appears that this medication would cure a lot of my problems..resetting sleep wake cycles, controlling blood sugar issues, decreasing prolactin and increasing dopamine. I have read other peoples stories with it and I;m afraid to add another "medication" on the list. I never even took an aspirin and all of a sudden I am on all kinds of stuff within last year so its been overwhelming. I dont want to be stupid and not do what I need to do either. Chaste berry is natural plus atleast it will help with decreasing prolactin levels and increasing dopamine and increasing Progestesterone levels. What are your thoughts?
Thank you ever so graciously again!

### Jack Kruse — 2016-01-10T06:45:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-increase-dopamine.7610/post-185888>

Time 6 showed you how UV light and aromatic amino acids make dopamine. Serotonin and melatonin are also biologic amines. Did you know this? Serotonin is synthesized from tryptophan, another aromatic amino acid. Tryptophan is transcriptionally activated by UV light as well vitamin D3 sulfate. UVB Sunlight makes Vitamin D3. EPA increases serotonin release from presynaptic neurons by reducing E2 prostaglandins. DHA has huge pi electron clouds that draw light to them photo-electrically. This is one of the more amazing abilities DHA has that few people even know. DHA also has a massive influence on the serotonin receptor action, by increasing cell membrane fluidity and improving tunneling of electrons and protons in postsynaptic neurons in the gut just behind the enterocytes. These cells face the microbiome. The microbiome is made up by prokaryote bacteria that are designed to release 5000 time more light then our cells. In the gut surface the story is still a light story. The light is then changed to chemical, mechanical and optical signals by atoms in our cells. These signals make connections in our gut neurons and head directly to the floor of the fourth ventricle in the brain, at the area postrema to create strong parasympathetic signal. This balances the stress responses from the paraventricular nucleus in the brainstem that is the guardian of the sympathetic response. The central retinal pathways however govern the PVN because of their light relays. Suprachiasmatic efferents to the paraventricular nucleus (PVN) and the supraoptic nucleus (SON) give the eye the power over the gut relays to the area postrema. This is how the brain gut axis is organized for optical signaling. The SCN also sends direct and indirect neural projections to the arcuate nucleus (ARC). This projection consisted of both excitatory and inhibitory components and may contribute to the entrainment of the rhythm in growth hormone secretion to the day-night cycle. Some SCN neurons project to both the SON and the ARC. The SCN in turn receives excitatory and inhibitory inputs from the ARC and the peri-nuclear zone of the SON (peri-SON). The extensive two-way connections between the SCN and its target nuclei contribute to the generation of day-night neuroendocrine rhythms. These complex control system also allows for non-photic entrainment of the SCN, for example, in response to feeding and meal times. The Leptin Rx uses these relays to re-entrain the SCN. Today modern man faces globally low sulfated Vitamin D3 levels because we continue to bury the sun many ways. Dermatology beliefs, clothing, sun screen, and lack of exposure to AM sunlight are important variables to consider. But society has also been depriving themselves of vital EPA & DHA in seafood for 70,000 years since we moved away from shorelines, built cities and homes indoors with alien sun's as humans left Africa.

### Dextery — 2013-07-11T23:31:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-listen-in-color-tedx-talk-by-a-cyborg.7612/post-89909>

[http://www.ted.com/talks/neil\_harbisson\_i\_listen\_to\_color.html](http://www.ted.com/talks/neil_harbisson_i_listen_to_color.html)

This man was born totally colorblind...living in a gray scale environment all his life. Except now he can hear colors.

Facinating that this man actually is using a computer program chip installed in his brain to be able to look at a color and the frequency of that color translate to the name we have given that color.

He can also listen to a concert and see the frequencies of the notes in color. He can also hear colors that are out of range of what the the rest of us cannot see.

And most facinating is that his brain is melding with the computer chip to become one.

Electrons and conductivity across the neurons.

# Thread: Foot swells after eating?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/foot-swells-after-eating.7613/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Eve — 2013-07-12T04:00:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/foot-swells-after-eating.7613/post-89918>

For the past couple of months, I've noticed my right foot mainly swells after eating. It comes down again maybe 1h or so later, but is prone to going back up. I'm on the Leptin Reset for 1.5 year and have Leaky gut (but it is slowly improving).
The only thing I've changed in the past couple of months is went on T3 only (previously I was on T3 and T4). Also, it is getting warmer here but it's not everyday. It's like 25 Celsius...I think that's 77F...and I'm not generally hot.
I drink 1 Gallon of spring water or slightly more each day and have since March? or around there.

What is going on? I'd like it to stop because before this started I bought some nice sandal wedges that I want to wear and can't get my fat feet into them!

Edit: I am also on Celebrex, a NSAID, which is my internal medicine doctor prescribed as my stomach was seriously inflamed (and nearly 8 months of eating nearly nothing but fish did not help at all)...It is helping (I take it with an acid blocker, which mitigates its wearing, long-term effects on the stomach lining), this is why I am not going off it yet. I looked up it's side effects and water retention/edema can be one of them....So maybe that explains it.

### Grizz — 2013-07-12T10:46:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/foot-swells-after-eating.7613/post-89934>

Eve,

I have 2 thoughts for you.
On your leaky gut I suggest you read the book, "Wheat Belly" by Dr. Davis.
[http://www.wheatbellyblog.com/](http://www.wheatbellyblog.com/)
Modern wheat is devastating to human health for many people, as detailed in the Dr. Davis book.

Do the Dr. Brownstein Iodine Protocol for stomach & thyroid.
[http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Handout](http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Handout)
Every cell in our body requires iodine, especially the thyroid, female organs & stomach. The toxins of bromide, fluoride & chlorine displace iodine from our cells causing health problems. Our thyroid cannot function properly on these toxins that have taken over our cell receptors intended for iodine. A [Pandoras Box of disease](http://www.thenutritionalhealingcenter.com/is-iodine-the-fountain-of-youth-part-one/) ([Part 2](http://www.thenutritionalhealingcenter.com/iodine-the-fountain-of-youth-part-two/#.UTGp-eyAuMw.twitter) )result from iodine deficiency. The book, [The Iodine Crises](http://www.amazon.com/The-Iodine-Crisis-About-Wreck/dp/098603200X/ref=sr_1_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1373614618&sr=8-1&keywords=iodine+crises) describes all of this.
Your need for thyroid medication could be reduced or eliminated entirely if you were getting enough iodine. Dr. Brownstein recommends 25 to 50mg a day of iodoral or Lugols with required co-supplements to overcome the toxins that surround us every day that are in our food, water, furniture, bedding. We live in a sea of these toxins.

Grizz

# Thread: Transitioning from spot CT to spot CT with water
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/transitioning-from-spot-ct-to-spot-ct-with-water.7615/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 32
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### fireforlunch — 2013-07-12T06:09:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/transitioning-from-spot-ct-to-spot-ct-with-water.7615/post-89926>

30# of ice on my chest for an hour is fairly easy but when i add water even 78 degrees its miserable and i hate it.

I know its not going to be easy, thats just the eay it is but does anyone have any suggestions on how i can successfully acomplish this for the full 30 minites?

It seems like if i start with 15 minutes and try to add a few minutes each time that i will be going backwards from my 60 minutes of ice on my chest and stomach

### nonchalant — 2013-07-12T06:25:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/transitioning-from-spot-ct-to-spot-ct-with-water.7615/post-89927>

Could you try colder water? If the water isn't cold enough, the cold sensation won't be "turned off".
If you are used to large amounts of ice for an hour, perhaps you need the colder water.

DH was surprised at me when I started to get cold while in a lake a few days ago. I explained to him that the water wasn't cold enough to activate the magic.

### Shijin13 — 2013-07-12T16:00:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/transitioning-from-spot-ct-to-spot-ct-with-water.7615/post-89951>

2nd' Nonchalant's recommendation to go colder... I've found my sweetspot for CT is below 50deg. above that and its harder to deal. But I've worked my way up to that level.

Initially, I found when CTing, I responded when the water was below 60. Now it takes more to get in the zone....

### PrimalPam — 2013-07-12T16:32:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/transitioning-from-spot-ct-to-spot-ct-with-water.7615/post-89952>

When I first started, I tried face dunks, which I found very hard. I then tried spot CT, which I found even harder.

One bored Saturday, I dumped all the ice from my freezer into the bathtub, added water, took my meat thermometer with me, and just....got in. The temp was in the low
60's, and I found it actually easy to stay in for 30 mins. The very next day, I used even more ice, got it to the low 50's, and stayed in for close to an hour, which is what I've done ever since. These days, if I don't do it, I miss it.

My vote: Get in the cold tub sooner. Skip all the spot-icing. Take a thermometer and find your sweet spot.

### fireforlunch — 2013-07-12T23:26:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/transitioning-from-spot-ct-to-spot-ct-with-water.7615/post-89998>

Man that sounds horrific?

I tried that a couple weeks ago but I only got the water down to about 68 degrees and it was terribly hard.

Guess I'll try to make it even colder

>

> 60's, and I found it actually easy to stay in for 30 mins. The very next day, I used even more ice, got it to the low 50's, and stayed in for close to an hour, which is what I've done ever since. These days, if I don't do it, I miss it.
>
> My vote: Get in the cold tub sooner. Skip all the spot-icing. Take a thermometer and find your sweet spot.

### PrimalPam — 2013-07-13T00:31:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/transitioning-from-spot-ct-to-spot-ct-with-water.7615/post-90004>

> [fireforlunch said:](/goto/post?id=89998)
>
> Man that sounds horrific?
>
> I tried that a couple weeks ago but I only got the water down to about 68 degrees and it was terribly hard.
>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/transitioning-from-spot-ct-to-spot-ct-with-water.7615/post-90011>

okay, I'll give that a shot.

If it's up to the top of my shoulders my thighs and knees won't fit in there

### CTforlife — 2013-07-13T06:30:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/transitioning-from-spot-ct-to-spot-ct-with-water.7615/post-90017>

I'm not entirely sure of your background, but are you really LS and low body fat? It can take longer to adapt said JK in one of the CT posts. If you are really LR and horribly inflamed I really don't see why the temp is an issue? Whats your o36 ratio? Like this isn't "hardcore people CT'ing", we can all CT at the optimal 50-55 degrees F temperature to stop inflammation and preserve your charge. We can all tap into the ancient pathway. If you can't fit your whole body I would suggest the places with the most visceral fat, for me that was my torso. Then I got a bigger tub, then starting outdoor CT. I'm pretty damn sure I had some form of raynaud's syndrome in my hands and feet, but I just ate what my brain needed and then CT'ed with full body immersion. Hands in the water, and it was up to my neck, holding my breath for as long as I could at times at my local river mornings sessions. I had a "broken" clock for a while, but CT in harmony with Leptin Rx eating Epi-Paleo is incredible. I don't see why CT isn't as mainstream to "health" organizations, but it is for performance athletes. Also, mind your mitochondria, and CT will pay off even bigger. While CT I always reflected that I was the perfect example of epigenetics. The environment I grew up in was tailored for mediocrity. Why can't kids think clearly nowadays in classrooms? Holy shit 30 damn iphones doing damage faster than we can handle. See we are the ultimate paradox, we have incredibly fast epigenetics, but when an incredibly fast change happens, we adapt only for survival, but unfortunately,( I hate thinking this), we are on the path leading up to an extinction. We are already 7 billion humans deep on this giant rock, and it isn't slowing. CT is your defense against this assault on your biology, and I suggest you use it to your advantage. Sorry I went there, but it needed to be said. Will my kids go to school ? I don't know... I don't want to finish college because of the implications of EMF. The reality is actually pretty damn grim for the masses, but for the individuals who don't accept mediocrity and always seek to be Optimal, CT is your best answer. I hope what I said helps you embrace the cold and what it means.

### PrimalPam — 2013-07-14T18:07:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/transitioning-from-spot-ct-to-spot-ct-with-water.7615/post-90104>

Re: Not fitting your body entirely into your tub. I use my bathtub, which is a standard size, and I can fill it only enough so that when I lay on my back, my entire backside is covered, but my entire front is exposed, out of the water. I read a tip (on here, on this exact topic!) to turn over about halfway through. So now, that's what I do: I turn over and prop on my elbows and finish my CT session getting my frontside all in. Not ideal; I'd love to have a larger tub, but we do what we can. Is that an option for you, somehow turning or moving your body to get the rest exposed during part of the time?

### nonchalant — 2013-07-14T18:32:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/transitioning-from-spot-ct-to-spot-ct-with-water.7615/post-90108>

> [PrimalPam said:](/goto/post?id=90104)
>
> Re: Not fitting your body entirely into your tub. I use my bathtub, which is a standard size, and I can fill it only enough so that when I lay on my back, my entire backside is covered, but my entire front is exposed, out of the water. I read a tip (on here, on this exact topic!) to turn over about halfway through. So now, that's what I do: I turn over and prop on my elbows and finish my CT session getting my frontside all in. Not ideal; I'd love to have a larger tub, but we do what we can. Is that an option for you, somehow turning or moving your body to get the rest exposed during part of the time?

That's what I do. 20 minutes for the legs, 20 minutes for the back & shoulders, 20 minutes for the tummy, chest & neck (with a face dunk or two). I also try to minimize contact with the bottom of the tub (while on my back and tummy), because it feels distinctly warmer than the water.

### PrimalPam — 2013-07-14T23:35:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/transitioning-from-spot-ct-to-spot-ct-with-water.7615/post-90121>

OMG that's almost exactly what I do! Down to minimizing contact with the tub itself, as I notice when I move my body parts away from the tub, they feel colder. The tub itself is most definitely warmer than the water, so I try to keep my body as much up as possible.

### fireforlunch — 2013-07-15T15:22:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/transitioning-from-spot-ct-to-spot-ct-with-water.7615/post-90138>

Stuck my feet in and for the first 5 seconds wasn't actually that bad.

But the longer they were in there the more it felt like needles until I couldn't take it at 10 minutes.

Found out that my thermometer is about 4 or 5 degrees off.

I will try 55 degrees tomorrow and pray that I won't be such a wuss about this

### nonchalant — 2013-07-15T15:54:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/transitioning-from-spot-ct-to-spot-ct-with-water.7615/post-90143>

### fireforlunch — 2013-07-15T17:33:03+0300

### PrimalPam — 2013-07-16T02:37:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/transitioning-from-spot-ct-to-spot-ct-with-water.7615/post-90194>

Here's a tip that might help: Don't put your feet or hands in the water at first. See if you can get your body in, while propping your feet and hands (at least toes and fingers) out. It will minimize the needles feeling. Later, when you're more adapted, go ahead and put them in. It took me months to be able to get my feet and hands in.

### fireforlunch — 2013-07-16T03:30:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/transitioning-from-spot-ct-to-spot-ct-with-water.7615/post-90201>

PrimalPam, I'm just now seeing your suggestion.

Sounds like a great idea.

But I already did my CT but I am really excited to report that at 56 degrees I just went for it and did 15 minutes up to my waste then 15 minutes laying down with my knees up but feet in the water.

Yesterday's feet in the water must have really helped because it was not nearly as bad.

I have to work tomorrow but I'm really excited to see what it's like on Thursday.

**THANK YOU SO MUCH FOR ALL THE INPUT AND ENCOURAGEMENT.**

What seemed nearly impossible has become very possible

### CTforlife — 2013-07-16T06:59:43+0300

# Thread: High Progesterone in a female
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-progesterone-in-a-female.7618/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hope — 2013-07-12T17:30:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-progesterone-in-a-female.7618/post-89956>

I was wondering if anyone here has ever experienced high PG levels in the follicular phase of their cycle? I had a blood and US test yesterday, my day 10 and the PG level came back many times the normal, at 10. Estrogen levels were in the normal, low range for the F phase.

MD thinks I didn't have a real period this last cycle. I am confused.

I had higher than normal PG in F phase last October in a salivary hormone panel for a month....and was told it could be Endometriosis but my MD now doesn't think that.

\*\*I\*\* am wondering if it's because of several months of taking PG in the luteal phase that doesn't clear? I also have been on pregnenolone since March (200mg) and DHEA (50mg) since it was too low and I wonder if it's thats contributing in looking at the hormone cascade. Will stop the pregnenolone for a while and will recheck.....but I still wonder.

Have any of you other women ever experienced this? It seems really hard to find much info on higher PG than the usual E2 dominance. Ugh.

### Shijin13 — 2013-07-12T19:55:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-progesterone-in-a-female.7618/post-89973>

There are some women who aren't estrogen dominant. some of these woman tend to be tall, thing and don't have traditional symptoms. we talked about this in one of the BHRT Threads... I'll have to see if I can dig it up...

What do your other labs look like... you really need to see all the labs together to begin to piece this all together..

### Hope — 2013-07-12T23:05:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-progesterone-in-a-female.7618/post-89994>

Thanks, Gretchen. The other labs - DHEA is over the top at 463 (range up to 350 but MD says it's OK); pregnenolone is 93 now. T3 was 2.6 in late June, T4 .9 and TSH 3.5. It's always fluctuating up and down - I'm unable to get a smooth sailing thyroid dosage. Best T3 i have had was when I was off all thyroid meds for a month. Go figure. I have tried armour, compounded T3, levoxyl, nothing seems to help (and I always end up having to get off due to heart palpitations, like now, when I just tried upping the dose two weeks ago, ugh). RT3 has been fine the last 6 months so that is good. JAck said it's EMF related but I've been working hard at avoiding it, as much as possible in this bath. Love the magnetico.

Vitamin D in most recent labs in June was 91, did not do cholesterol then bc in April all was well, all in a good range, per the optimal ranges we know but will redo later this month. HS-CRP has been consistently below 0.2, that's how they report it for me, for the last 6 months. DHEA has been steadily rising to where it is now. I take DHEA and pregnenolone. Also the iodine protocol etc, epi-paleo etc.

I do take l-theanine or was regularly which I learned from Jack raises the dopamine and PG/E2 ratio, for that purpose. Not sure that contributed too. Such a bummer because with such a high level this early on the MD says I couldn't likely get pregnant bc the uterine lining is affected by all that progesterone even if all the other ducks are in a row. Sigh. More waiting but maybe it's a good thing....I can even out other issues more.

I WAS estrogen dominant last cycle, day 20, but it is getting better (but still E dom) so that's all weird. to me.

Thanks again, Gretchen....so appreciate it. Oh, and while tall, I'm definitely not thin. LOL. Though I am starting to wonder if I'm not losing a bit of fat and gaining a bit of muscle....as my clothes seem a bit looser, my belly looks a bit nicer (smaller LOL) but the scale is still at 150lbs (I'm 5'8'') and usually wear a size 10. But I still have huge thighs and butt. LOL.

# Thread: Question and observation of CT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-and-observation-of-ct.7619/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nicld — 2013-07-12T17:39:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-and-observation-of-ct.7619/post-89959>

I have been trying the CT for about a week now and each day it seems to be getting better, and I am amazed. I am so glad that I am trying this in the summer since I seem to get so hot and the cold water feels really good now. Last night, I did get to the point of having the coldest water filling the tub that I could get and actually thought it was not horrible. I also checked my internal temp one day before and after the CT and it actually went up. My usual temp is about 97.7 and I actually got up to 98.2. I will try to remember to check it every time I do CT and see what is says.

My question is; will this help with the overall body temperature regulation? In the summer I seem to be so sensitive to the heat and I have had a heat stroke and heat exhaustion in the past (summer not really my favorite time of the year because of it). Will it also make me better able to tolerate the cold too? As I sit here now, I am in a short sleeve shirt sitting at work with the AC blasting and trying real hard not to put on a sweatshirt.

Is it also ok to concentrate more on having the cold water on the lower body as that is where most of my excess fat it? Does one have to add the ice part to get the full effect or will some benefits still be seen just by using cold water?

It is going to be hot and humid here this weekend so I am actually looking forward to my cold baths.

Nicole

### nonchalant — 2013-07-12T18:11:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-and-observation-of-ct.7619/post-89968>

Hi Nicole! Glad you are getting into the CT. I used to suffer horribly in summers also. I think it was high inflammation. CT helps me tolerate high temps, as well as cold temps.

It seemed that my temperature tolerance was a very small band of degrees, a few years ago. But now I'll be fine, when others are complaining that it is too hot or too cold.

It's ok to concentrate the cold on the lower part of your body, but for the full effect, you need to get the upper body in for at least a while. Any cold water is good.

### nicld — 2013-07-13T18:46:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-and-observation-of-ct.7619/post-90056>

That is what I am hoping to get out of this is better body temperature regulation. It seems to be helping already. Last night I sat for 40 minutes in as cold as I could get my water and it felt pretty good. I did have my upper body out of the water most of the time then the last 5-10 put the whole body it. I was actually reading Dr. Kruse's book specifically the chapter on cold therapy as I was in the tub.

### nonchalant — 2013-07-13T19:02:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-and-observation-of-ct.7619/post-90059>

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=90056)
>
> I was actually reading Dr. Kruse's book specifically the chapter on cold therapy as I was in the tub.

Hey I think you get extra points for that! ;-)
