### nonchalant — 2013-07-20T19:27:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-air-thermogenesis.7665/post-90449>

Are y'all talking about bitter melon?

### Hemming — 2013-07-20T20:27:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-air-thermogenesis.7665/post-90452>

### nonchalant — 2013-07-20T20:40:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-air-thermogenesis.7665/post-90453>

I used it, early on. Chopped some off of the cucumber-like fruit and added it to my eggs. Tasted good, in a grassy, chlorophyll-like way. Not bitter, but strong-flavored. I used 3-4 of the melons while I was adapting to CT.

### Lahelada — 2013-07-21T15:22:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-air-thermogenesis.7665/post-90503>

Good morning. A windy day yesterday and I spent about 4 hours doing my errands in the cold. Great ! I finally wore a dark cotton T shirt with long sleeves ( winter colours) and loose jeans.
In the afternoon we went to the boat and I did a bit of sunbathing in the cold wind with exposed midriff .
CT is a problem for my partner. He goes out dressed like a polar explorer. When people see us together they give him poisonous looks enquiring why he does not find his gentleman side and give me one of his jackets . So far he is taking it well. The dog is fed up though . I took her to the park after dark with temp around 4 and halfway around she asked to go home. We normally do the full circle but when I told her the magic word "HOME" she bolted straight across the grass in the direction of home. Bwahaha.

No bittermelon here either. Before I knew that it would be good for me they had it as a vegetable but now it has disappeared from the chinese store. There was a study that it aided in BAT production from China and that it helps with not feeling the cold so much . For the latter I don't really need it.

# Thread: DIY Supplements!?

**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 8

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-07-20T06:00:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-supplements.7667/post-90417>

Hey all, just wanted to ask what everyone's opinion was on this matter. I haven't really been reading up on here/bulletproof forums lately as I have absolutely devoured some recent books I purchased for extremely cheap (like $50, all new), everyone should check this site out for books: [http://www.alibris.com/](http://www.alibris.com/) can't wait to see if I can get my college textbooks on there! Okay, okay, back to the topic at hand, DIY supplements. Here are two videos that recently made me think about this: [http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SeU--wadrMY](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SeU--wadrMY) and [http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=76asvMWcEM4](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=76asvMWcEM4). The second one is magnesium for fish tanks mixing mag. sulfite and mag. chloride. I've seen members on these boards claiming to have gotten salmon oil through pet food stores and used those as o3 supplements, why would using things like that for fish tanks be any difference. Here is a site with tons of supplements for fish and the tanks: [http://www.kentmarine.com/](http://www.kentmarine.com/) there is even Lugol's solution, phytoplankton, o3 supplements, etc. Wouldn't taking these be similar to going and purchasing something from NOW/LEF yet for much less? The main reason I'm asking this is because after reading 4HWW in a day (now re-reading it), I really want to optimize everything I purchase, from the time I spend researching the product and pricing, to the actual price and effectiveness. As many may know, I'm on an extremely limited budget, and I'm also trying to drastically alter the health and well-being of both my younger brother and fiance - I want to find or create my own proprietary supplements for myself and those closest to me. I wouldn't have to worry about soy or anything because I would be making it myself, for instance, for liposomal Vit. C I would simply replace soy lecithin with sunflower lecithin, problem solved.

So, does anyone have any knowledge/experience with this? What about the fish/fish tank supplements? I'm extremely interested in 'hacking' the cost of Topical Magnesium/Magnesium Baths to radically boost my cellular level of magnesium. I think Liposomal Vit. C would be great too, I recently just went through a mega-dosing of Soloray Extended Release Vit. C because of my fiance and her family getting **terribly** ill - I went out of my way to kiss my fiance when she was sick just to test the waters of my immune system, I didn't get so much as a scratchy throat.

Well, thanks in advance for anyone who could enlighten me on this topic, I'd love to get into DIY supplements and maybe help some others out by finding crazy deals.... maybe I just found my niche, lol!

### fitness@home — 2013-07-20T15:29:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-supplements.7667/post-90431>

Interesting ideas BigPapa...will be watching this thread.

I was one of those people that ordered wild caught salmon oil from a pet supply vendor in the NW. I now eat seafood at _least_ once a day and don't feel the need to supplement this any longer. At the time, I actually shared it with my cat & dog...they really liked it.

### Lahelada — 2013-07-20T15:48:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-supplements.7667/post-90433>

I was looking at this too and for here I have put it on the backburner.My only reason is that the stuff I was looking at comes in labeled bags or home labelled jars that you can only get via ebay by mail order and no return address. I did go out yesterday though to a bakery supply store and got ascorbic acid much cheaper than a vit c supplement. I am researching citric pectin for its potential for goodness.
Coming back to your situation I would stick with anything that is known not to hurt mammals . You might find that they use something in the production of the stuff for fishies to make it cheaper that may or may not be so good for us and maybe do not even have to label. An example: I read somewhere that you should not take magnesium at the same time as a carbonated drink as the carbon dioxide combines with the magnesium and makes it unuseable for the body.
If you are after magnesium, have you looked into Epsom salt labeled as food grade magnesium sulphate and it is way cheaper than any supplement. Additionally transdermally you can ensure better absorption. In other words where else is Epsom salt used and not called Epsom salt ? That is my point.
.
A supplement I will be making myself is calcium citrate from ground eggshells and citric acid. As the hardening agent for the shells is supposedly silica I might get a good dose of that at the same time.
I have a trustworthy supplier for my eggs though.

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-07-20T20:07:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-supplements.7667/post-90451>

Thanks for all the input thus far, I appreciate it. I suppose I will dig a little deeper and if I find something that seems potentially useful, I will contact the manufacturer and inquire about its safety with humans, maybe phrasing it so I don't sound crazy, like, "Oh, if an owner of a fish tank were to accidentally ingest some of this substance, would they be harmed in any way, or no?" and hope I get an answer such as it is okay for humans but marketed towards fish/fish tanks - things of that sort. The Bulletproof Exec Forums have a nice longgg post on supplements for the Bulletproof Diet and one or two of the members dug really deep and basically found the best prices for those supplements, but again, they were actual supplements rather than food grade compounds or 'supplements' marketed towards dogs or fish or magnesium used to melt ice. What I'm getting at though, is maybe I can just find cheappp bulk powedered supplements and mix in RO water or use that liposomal technique in the above video to create a more bioavailable form of other vitamins. I don't care much for supplementing Vitamin C, I just did it to aid in recovery for any potential bug I may have caught from my fiance/family, and either my immune system is REALLY good as of these last months, or the Vitamin C mega-dosing for 2-3 days just fortified it a ton (or both). I'd be more interested in bulk and cheap magnesium chloride to apply topically or in a bath, or finding out about some of those 'fish supplements' and getting my hands on cheaply sourced iodine and/or phytoplankton.

Thanks again for all the input so far. I'll be looking forward to more comments, and when I find more information I will be sure to post back here.

### HoneyChild — 2013-07-20T21:04:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-supplements.7667/post-90454>

Some more pretty interesting ideas here: [https://sites.google.com/site/lowmoonglowing/minerals](https://sites.google.com/site/lowmoonglowing/minerals)

### Jonathin — 2013-07-21T00:54:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-supplements.7667/post-90474>

Big Papa, I have fooled with DYI food supplements as well as fertilizer materials for years. You want to make sure you can get truly quality ingredients with an analysis. You don't want to get rancid oil or minerals with aluminum or heavy metals. Pharmaceutical grade (USP) is generally good. Food grade is probably OK if the manufacturer is reputable such as Albion Labs. Check out pforlife.com and nutrabio.com for bulk supplements. For other sites, check out what they say about their quality standards. Often not much is said.

Even tho I do take some bulk supplements such as magnesium, DHEA, resveratrol, Vit D, etc., you might find that putting more of your money into quality food is a better use of your time unless you are trying to address something specific and monitoring with testing. ~ Jon

Anyone have a site that helps with combat vets like me (Marine Corps) with constant pains mainly back and sternum and PTSD. My meds in the last 3 months made me gain 35 lbs even though I eat mostly clean. 95% I have a few cheat days like my bday...x-mas and my anniversary. I'm find through the study of n=1 that certain veggies cause bloating but I really want to lose the weight (I'm 190 currently)..and get back in the gym
And paleopot is the best. Just reaching out to his smart friends

### Shijin13 — 2013-07-20T21:36:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/combat-vet-needs-help.7669/post-90455>

Rich

Welcome. You're at the right spot to help deal with pain and PTSD - at least in an un-traditional manner. I highly recommend following Jack's Leptin Prescription, Eating the EpiPaleo Template, and CT.

I'm former Air Force, and also have a dx of PTSD, though not from combat. You've gone through far many different things, that I have, but you're in the right place. Many of Jack's protocols have helped free me from some of the anxiety I would get in certain situations. The road is slow going but Epipaleo and CT have helped me.

Start with his leptin reset - and go from there. Jack is teaching us how to actively manage our health, though complex lessons in biochemistry, physics and quantum biology... dig in. and ask questions!

gretchen

It's taken me 12 yrs to get help and for the va to finally quasi help me. Last three months have been very hard. All of my symptoms hit me all at once. Before it was a little here and a little there. But ad I've gotten older my body decided it has had enough punishment. My short term memory is toast due to concussions syndrome, I have crush feet syndrome. Migraines, and severe back and spine spasms, and my sternum feels like its going to break. The meds put 35 lbs on me. I'm a freaking painful hot mess.

### Dextery — 2013-07-20T22:41:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/combat-vet-needs-help.7669/post-90466>

On a tight budget, sardines in tomato sauce at Wal-Mart in a 15oz tin costs about $1.50. One tin plus 4 eggs panfried in coconut oil is a good breakfast for me and I do not need to eat until dinner. I pour off most of the tomato sauce...but I do like the hot tomato sauce some brands come in. Canned oysters non boiled costs around $1.85 for 8oz. I flavor them up with any kind of herbs I have plus Old Bay seafood seasoning...plus some cheese. Pan fried to melted cheese. Look for bacon ends packages instead of the pretty looking bacon strips. I make bacon bits from cutting up the ends into little pieces and cooking.

### Rich — 2013-07-20T22:54:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/combat-vet-needs-help.7669/post-90467>

### Rich — 2013-07-20T23:19:16+0300

### Rich — 2013-07-20T23:26:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/combat-vet-needs-help.7669/post-90471>

I guess I maybe.confusing people about concussions and concussion syndrome. Concessions usually caused by being hit in the head and possibly knocked out. Concussions syndrome is sorta like adult shaken baby syndrome. I was in field artillery and got to close to the baffles. Doing so rattled my brain around in my head causing my short term memory to be pretty much gone. I'm also 40% deaf. I can't hear soft tones and I have a constant ringing in my ears. Dull buzz. To the occasional painful pinging.

### Shijin13 — 2013-07-21T00:20:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/combat-vet-needs-help.7669/post-90472>

Rich

you've given us some great info to help you. TBI and concussions are a huge challenge b/c there are no visible signs of what the injury has done to you! Dex has given you some great tips for eating EpiPaleo.. if you do a cost comparison - you'll find that buying sardines and oysters in cans can be cheaper than chicken.. and considering what you're recovering from Chicken is high in O6s which will make recovery that much longer. Go for the oysters and small canned fish.

YOU NEED TO READ THIS BLOG FROM JACK ASAP: [http://www.jackkruse.com/concussioncte-prescription/](http://www.jackkruse.com/concussioncte-prescription/)

Also do you have international markets in your area - they usually have great prices on fish & meat. the international mkt near us sells 1lb of mixed frozen seafood (oysters, mussels, shrimp, octopus/squid and cuttle fish) for $3.99/lb. Trader Joes sells something similar for $11.99/lb!

I'd also suggest considering doing things like asking the fish counter if they have any salmon heads, mackerel head or other fish head. you can make a soup out of it. or you can blend it up - like Inger does. I usually break out the mackerel head smoothies when I'm having a stressfull time at work etc.

Nonchalant is right - you want to eat as much coconut oil as possible. get some cacao powder (Chocolate in its raw form ground) and mix that w/the coconut oil. you can also get refined coconut oil - the taste is gone, but the goodness is still there...

Ghee is good too, not as good a coconut oil for your condition but its still good.

you should focus on High fat, moderate protein, low carb (fresh seasonal veggies if you can get them) your brain need TONS OF OMEGA 3's .... Walter Reed is now using High Potency Fish oils to treat TBI, and PTSD. Jack usually doesn't recommend supplementing with Fish oil - but in your case you might want to consider it, as it will help your recovery.

keep asking questions and post your frustrations. there's a lot of people here who are doing this on a fixed income... Your doc may not know where to turn to help you...but you can take him some of Jack's blogs to him.

One of the biggest things you can do to help your self is make sure you're not eating any ARTIFICIAL Sweeteners, the support neurological degeneration. Also, MAke sure any food your eating doesn't have MSG or any of its hidden names "natural flavoring" MSG IS IN EVERYTHING... you've got to make sure its not in the food you eat

here are a few he's done on TBI:
[http://www.jackkruse.com/concussions-diet-and-neurodegenerative-disease-part-1/](http://www.jackkruse.com/concussions-diet-and-neurodegenerative-disease-part-1/)
[http://www.jackkruse.com/where-concussions-diet-and-neurodegeneration-meet/](http://www.jackkruse.com/where-concussions-diet-and-neurodegeneration-meet/)
[http://www.jackkruse.com/msg-your-gut-and-your-brain-post-trauma/](http://www.jackkruse.com/msg-your-gut-and-your-brain-post-trauma/)
[http://www.jackkruse.com/concussioncte-prescription/](http://www.jackkruse.com/concussioncte-prescription/)

Take these to your doc - it will help him help you. Tell him "hey I found this blog - its from a neurosurgeon - he's got some things that might pertain to me, would you consider reading them to see if you think they might be worth a shot? " If he's searching to help you - he'll jump at anything that can help him fix you... right now you have an amazing opportunity to change the way your doc treats patients. you're his guide. help him help you, so he can help others like you!

start a journal on here... its a good way to keep track of what you've got going on...

### Rich — 2013-07-21T00:31:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/combat-vet-needs-help.7669/post-90473>

Thank you a lot of info. I don't have access to a fish market but I do have a cosco. On Aug 1st I go to Dartmouth Hitchcock medical center for brain mapping on my TBI. So in a nut shell eat 2 cups of veggies a day...eat eggs, coconut oil, and tons of fish. Eating this way should help with weight loss?. I'm planning on lifting heavy with compound lifts starting Mon...3x a week if my back will hold up. Starting valium tonight for 10 days so I can finally get 8 hrs. I havnt gotten more than 4 hrs a night due to being woken up for lower lumbar pain. No reason for this pain just started happening 2 weeks ago. Only thing I can think is between the fast weight I may have slept wrong and stretch out a muscle or ligament. Bone x-rays where done today and the bones are good

### Shijin13 — 2013-07-21T01:03:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/combat-vet-needs-help.7669/post-90475>

Food plan sounds great... eat w/in 30min of waking, then eat 4hrs later, and 4hrs after that but at least 3hrs before you want to go to bed. Turn off all lights at night, and use red lights (think your combat flashlight) for reading and doing things after dark. If you eat this way, and follow the light cycles (so if its dark out side its dark in your house except the red light) Also if you have WiFi - turn it off/unplug it except when you NEED To be on the internet, or switch to a hardlined modem/router.

Hold off on the working out and focusing on weight loss, instead get yourself in a COLD BATH, if you can try to do it right after you eat breakfast, and do it again before dinner. If the cold bath isn't an option, get cold packs and put them on the affected area, You've got some serious inflammation going on that's causing a pinched nerve...

When you're trying to fix your brain and you're working out, you're only going to stress it more. go outside sit on the ground and focus on clearing your mind. I use a technique called Mind Light.

find a comfortable spot to sit, preferably outside on the ground indian style, with your arms resting gently on your legs. close your eyes, take several deep breaths. Imagine your holding a baseball/softball. think about how the ball feels in your hands. spin it around, toss it up, catch it. now in your "mind" see the ball - as you see the ball, begin to notice the light radiating out from the ball. as you breath in and out you feel the light energy begin to pulse with your breath. imagine that light can penetrate your fingers, and travel up your body. the ball is now able to get bigger or smaller as if its playdough. play with its size, shape. as you do this, notice the light changes, the energy around the ball travels at different speeds depending on the size of the ball. Smaller/ faster Bigger/Slower. now practice taking the light energy into your hands. once you have all the light energy push it back into the ball. notice something? how do your hands feel? continue practicing this exercise of moving the light energy. move it to where your back hurts, hold it there, then put it back into the ball. how do your back feel? move the light energy to your brain, imagine it expanding, reaching every fold, nook, cranny in your skull. feel the light energy as it crosses your synapses. take a deep breath in, feel the light energy expand, as you breath out, feel the negative that's stuck leave your brain. continue to do deep breathing. with each breath see the light energy get brighter, faster, more powerful. As it gets bigger, you see it filling up the emptiness, When you are fill up with what you can handle, transfer the light back to the ball in your hands. as you breathe, watch the light fade away and see the baseball/softball reappear. toss it up, catch it. do this until you're ready to be done. then open your eyes.

Don't worry about your weight at this moment. worry about feeding your brain, and reducing your inflammation. Ice packs or blocks of ice you make yourself... make sure you wear a compression shirt when using the ice packs or ice blocks...

### Inger — 2013-07-21T20:52:47+0300

Rick, I'll explain to you.

Always check what the animal get for feed, that is what makes the quality of your protein/meat source. If you eat chicken fed with grains, soy, hormones, antibiotics.. who knows what else, they get pumped up to weigh as much as possible as fast as possible (money rules) all that shit is going to be in the meat you are eating, coming into your body too . Same if your pork is fed common feed. Not good at all. I would eat only natural / grass fed pork or boar.

The quality of your food is important. Never look for the cheapest, but for quality instead. Your health is worth it. Or not. Only you know. To me my health is worth more than anything else. I always eat the best I can afford. I also think we do not need lean, we need fat.. When I eat lean protein I always add lots and lots of fat on it!

### Rich — 2013-07-21T22:36:43+0300

Guess I didn't understand the argument against lean meat. Thanks for the input though Inger. Helps with the reminders because I do forget a lot of things.

### Shijin13 — 2013-07-21T23:27:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/combat-vet-needs-help.7669/post-90524>

> [Rich said:](/goto/post?id=90521)
>
> I understand all of that. It's still cheaper than organic beef. I was looking for a reason. Everything I eat, I try to eat organic when I can. Feeding a family of 5 isn't cheap. Guess I didn't understand the argument against lean meat. Thanks for the input though Inger. Helps with the reminders because I do forget a lot of things.

Rich

If you're going to eat meat this should be the priority - as far a nutrient dense meats

1) shellfish - oysters/mussels (Canned or frozen work)
2) crustaceans - shrimp/crab/crawfish
3) small fish - sardines, mackerel, blue fish
4) Large fish or farmed fish - salmon, tuna, catfish
5) grassfed beef/pork or wild game
6) Organ meats
7) cage free eggs
8) conventional beef/port
9) conventional eggs
10) organic or conventional chicken.

Chicken is the least nutritionally available meet on the market - even organic/free range. its pretty much a farm pigeon . my grandfather used to say Chicken is Foul (Fowl) meat. we no longer eat it. you'd be better off buying bulk conventional eggs from costco over buying organic chicken... not only better nutrition, but better bang for you buck. buy grass fed meats when you can it doesn't have to be organic. We've made the decision to buy conventional thick cut bacon from Costco, and buy more seafood, and grass fed roasts/ground beef etc from our local farmer. they're cheaper than steak, some times they'll cut you a deal when you buy in bulk. Around DC - you can get grassfeed meat for $3.99/lb or less if you buy a 1/4 steer or more... whole cattle - is running for less that $3.39/lb. when you do the math - if you have a deep freezer, and the cash to pay up front - you're paying less for the grassfed beef buying straight from the farmer - not everyone can do this - but the farmers who offer this, will probably work w/you if you make a regularly scheduled trip to buy certain cuts, that he has a hard time selling - like heart, liver, ground beef, chuck roasts ect...

here's a link you can use to see if you can find a local farmer you can work with... [http://www.localharvest.org/csa/](http://www.localharvest.org/csa/)

### Inger — 2013-07-24T08:45:47+0300

> [Rich said:](/goto/post?id=90639)
>
> Is organic popcorn...made on the stovetop ok to eat especially those trying to lose weight

No.. lol At least I would not touch it! I would not touch corn... after I saw pictureson how it looked like originally, and what we have manipulated it to look like now.. huh. It cannot be good. As with grains.

Rick, please do not say, I cannot! That is why you will never be able to either.. to change. You know, there is a way you can get organ meat down without tasting a thing! Just close your nose, and swallow. No need to chew either, it will digest just fine. Then, with still closed nose, rinse your mouth, and put something you like in it, or a sip of coffee, etc. Breath again. Easy peasy! I do this with everything I do not like, or find yucky, but I know works wonder for my health. Or make a smoothie out of it and drink with closed nose. Might be even easier.
Everything that you do not like you can get used to Rick. I promise. Just start veeery slow. Go slow but steady, in your own pace, but go forward. Take a tiny little bit. Imagine you are so daring.. you want to be, don't you? Daring is very sexy. To be coward is very unsexy... I always think like that. Maybe it helps you too.

Anyhow Rick, if you eat lots of crustaceans, shrimp, oysters, seafood.. I even think you can skip the organ meats. At least they will not be as important anymore. I think they are rather if you eat a diet mainly consisted of land mammals.

I think to buy canned sardines or oysters etc. is a great idea.  And buy bulk when something is on offer. That way you can save lots of money too.

### Abdrx — 2013-09-01T01:30:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/combat-vet-needs-help.7669/post-94519>

Hi Rich, I am new to posting so I hope it is okay what I'm posting here to you. Your mention of PTSD and concussion syndrome struck a cord with me b/c in my own journey back to health this topic arose when I came across a pod cast on methylation and MTHFR. It is here with Dr. Tim Jackson: [http://www.superhumanradio.com/components/com\_podcast/media/mp3s/SHR\_Show\_923.mp3](http://www.superhumanradio.com/components/com_podcast/media/mp3s/SHR_Show_923.mp3)

A little history on me: Am 62 now, was in excellent health prior to Hurricane Katrina in 2005, age 54. I believe, besides the enormous stress that put me into, that I probably experienced some PTSD. (My home was underwater for a month, and entire area devastated, had just lost my mother one month prior to Parkinson's, my dad had been diagnosed with lung cancer 1 year prior and I'd looked forward to helping him through recovery, after Mom passed away. We also lost 4 investment properties, and a business, and 3 family members had to relocate 8-11 hours away, but we still had to regularly see them, my husband was so ill he couldn't get out of bed, and 3 immediate family members lost all as well. It was overwhelming and it was my husband and myself who always "took care" of everyone with the desire to do so. Not complaining here. Just trying to give you somewhat of a complete picture and how this might help you). And really the stress of it all is has only fully lifted when I retired this past March. So went from eating very healthfully and taking care of my body/mind/spirit to total anxiety, worry, and care giver to several people near and far. Had to live with brother-in-law for a year, started eating poorly, unlike I'd ever done, and simply tried to manage it all. Also, then began having wifi in house. In 2010, having found a RN practioner who got my husband better, but not entirely well, discovered I had Hashimoto's, then tumor on my parathyroid gland. Went from being very healthy to barely being able to get out of bed, pain so terrible in my feet that I could barely put my foot on gas pedal, terrible sleep, depression, and total exhaustion. Still had to work in hospital for health insurance, had moved 65 miles away from work, but finally had to stay at hotel attached to hospital b/c couldn't make it home any longer, but needed health insurance. Had tumor removed in Sept 2011 and thought I'd just bounce back in 3 days b/c of my past excellent health. Didn't and could never manage to continually feel strong enough to work out regularly or have any consistent energy. In Feb 2010 found out I had the MTHFR gene mutation and had been put on Deplin. Didn't work out well for me, finally realizing it made me worse. Then in Spring 2012, happened upon this talk on methylation: [http://www.superhumanradio.com/components/com\_podcast/media/mp3s/SHR\_Show\_923.mp3](http://www.superhumanradio.com/components/com_podcast/media/mp3s/SHR_Show_923.mp3)
It all started making sense to me then, my other family's health issues. In this talk, at end, there is mention of how PTSD and concussion syndrome affects one with the mutation. So almost 1 year ago started the process for me and couple of family members. Has seemed to resolve our issues, but I am the only one who truly does incorporate what Jack preaches, though I get the most computer exposure, others don't eat so well. I have bounced back greatly, but still want to get more optimal, which I think there is room for. At the end of the talk, there is mention of PTSD and it's effects on methylation. You might want to check into that as well and do all Jack prescribes. Jack even gave an interview on this topic on the mthfrsupport.com web site. This might be a very important part of your healing, finding out if you have the MTHFR gene. It is epigenetically affected and you've certainly been through enough to have triggered it, like I was. Hope this is okay to post. BTW, I spent some 4 months researching the topic before being convinced of it all and am very glad we have done so. Found out one family member had unacceptable mold exposure and working on that alone changed their life in about 5 months time.

# Thread: What to do about scoliosis?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-to-do-about-scoliosis.7670/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Wurst23 — 2013-07-21T02:46:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-to-do-about-scoliosis.7670/post-90479>

Hi,

not sure where to post this, but since it bothers me mostly during/after sports, i'll try here!

Well my problem is, although im only 26 ive had a scoliosis and resulting (?) leg length difference of approx 0,7cm (could be more now, not sure) ever since my teen days.

Ive been to the orthopedic doctor of course, he made an x-ray and said there is nothing that can be done about it, told me to stop lifting weights immidiately, and "deal with it". He also sent me to a chiropractic who noticed there is also some rotation involved, meaning that my shoulders are misaligned as well, but again offered neither cause nor solution

So, ive been "dealing with it" for the last couple of years, trying my best to deal with post-workout stiffness, sometimes a little pain and creaky lumbar discs (not sure how to correctly describe this in english) and a feeling of being misaligned/out of balance a bit.

Long story short - now that i've started on the path of optimal health, and learning from Dr. Kruse that everything in the body is connected, i decided to ask here for any opinions on what to do next? If possible id like to avoid any further X-rays or MRI (or surgery, heh) as you can imagine, but im also a bit clueless about my options!

Thank you for any hints, tips and advice!

### fitness@home — 2013-07-21T03:14:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-to-do-about-scoliosis.7670/post-90483>

Wurst,

I have slight scoliosis in the spine area between my shoulder blades. Exercise can "tweak" it for want of a better word. You creaky description sounds familiar. If I roll my shoulders back after exercise or when I first get up in the morning, I can hear creakiness.

My parents made me wear a brace/vest (under my clothing) for awhile when I was in JR high. Very damn uncomfortable!

### nonchalant — 2013-07-21T04:51:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-to-do-about-scoliosis.7670/post-90489>

Wurst, I developed a similar issue in my lower back. Chiro visits helped me gain some flexibility, but I was very dependent on frequent visits. When Dr. K announced the EpiPaleo Rx, and I started eating seafood daily, I made huge gains, and I began to space out visits to the chiro a bit more. Then other things he recommended, like squatting, grounding, lots of water, and the Magnetico all gave me significant improvement.

I am seeing a different chiro now. I wouldn't let him take new xrays, he just had to look at the old ones. And he was very surprised at how flexible I am now, compared to my xrays from early last year. When I was getting better, last Fall, my back did start squeaking/grinding/creaking a lot. But that seems to be slowly going away as I continue to improve. I think hydration is the most important treatment.

### Shijin13 — 2013-07-25T16:11:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-to-do-about-scoliosis.7670/post-90801>

> [fitness@home said:](/goto/post?id=90799)

OMG... I have this same thing going on... 2nd time this month... woke up at the beginning of the month - and I could barely turn my head to the left. Did it again the night before last with the same thing... not sure if its stress or an inflammation response... I could barely move last night ( I did alittle yoga to help loosen up my neck) I was in so much pain - I actually had to sleep with an icepack on my neck and upper back and I had to pop some motrin. I had dh rub some arnica oil on my neck/back too. I wonder what's going on thats causing this. usually when I do something to my neck/back its on my right side... I had this happen back in march/april too... weird...

### SeaHorse — 2013-07-25T18:40:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-to-do-about-scoliosis.7670/post-90821>

Pillows are critical aren't they! I used to get the same kind of spasms during the night that would take a day or two to release (probably it was during the time my neck vertebrae were fusing)....I also have scoliosis. The pillow I love right now is called Indulgence by Isotonic. It has just the right amount of squish and support for side sleeping. It keeps my neck aligned so I don't compress one side or the other. I usually top it off with a chillow...keeps my brain nice and cool during the summer. Since my neck fused (C3 and C4) there is a lot less movement and it has actually helped with my back pain....not exactly how you want to get relief but it all happened without anybody taking any notice of it. CT has been the best for lower back issues and in general just doing in the inflammation that I've lived with forever.

### GlenPDQ — 2013-07-22T20:59:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/primo-water-from-walmart-ok.7671/post-90581>

Does the Primo water dispenser at WalMart remove fluoride? It has a sediment filter, carbon filter, and does Reverse Osmosis on the water. The higher the pressure that water is forced through an RO filter means more contaminants could be forced through.

# Thread: Planing my escape
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/planing-my-escape.7676/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 25
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Meg — 2013-07-21T23:02:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/planing-my-escape.7676/post-90522>

Okay we did it. We sold the house and now we are selling most all of our possessions. We are going to try to find a better place, a different way of doing things, a different way to live. In my mind Iâ€™ve been dreaming about this escape for a while now, even before I learned about EMFâ€™s, before I put two and two together -that I have been hugely affected by the smart meter that was put on my house 2 Â½ years ago. Iâ€™ve craved being free of so much stuff, Iâ€™ve craved extra space in my head and heart to create instead of care for belongings. My husband, he craves financial freedom, heâ€™s excited about not having to watch money drain out of his hands each month to take care of all of our stuff. He wants to have freedom to pursue a different career- a new path, he wants to find out if moving away from a smart meter will help him not wake up in the middle of the night feeling like his shoulders have been electrocuted.
Four weeks ago we sold our house on the first day it was on the market. You would think I would have jumped for joy but I did not. For the first week I was in shock and felt paralyzed, the next two weeks my health went rapidly downhill â€“ one week ago I think I had what would be called a panic attack. This past week I have spent hours in the sun in an ice bath to help right myself. I have upped my game- I have to feed my body and brain so I can finish the task before me. I am doing better, I am thinking clearer, but I can tell I am living in a precarious spot. Thanks to all I have learned from Dr K and this site I believe I have the tools to stay in the game.
This is our plan-we close in two weeks and we will put some things in storage at my mother in laws house and live in someoneâ€™s basement as we â€œplan our escapeâ€. We are looking for flexibility and freedom to move about as we want with nothing tying us down as we experiment with different places. We are about to book tickets for a month in Central America in late June. Weâ€™ve skyped with a guy in Cost Rica (Emerging Terraines) who is developing an Island off the coast of Panama (Playa Mystica) that only uses solar or generators for electricity and have plans to meet with him and stay on the island. We have no idea where we will go after central America, right now we are shooting from the hip.
Why the panic attacks? Fear of the unknown. Fear of not knowing where I am going to lay my head at night or where I am going to cook my food in the day. Fear of leaving my children! I have four, the youngest is 17 and sheâ€™s going with us, however we are leaving our 19 year old son homeless -heâ€™s been going to college and working from home. Another 21 year old son is in college in Atlanta and our 24 year old daughter is working as a nurse. And then there is the stress of dismantling a large house with years of memories.
I was fortunate enough have one of the last consults with Dr Kruse did before he went off grid and we talked about a lot of important things regarding my health but do you know what the main thing I remember about the consult? A simple phrase that he just threw out thereâ€¦â€¦. â€œdo what you should do, not what you want to doâ€. It has become my mantra. I say it over and over again in my head. I say it to my kids, I say it as Iâ€™m selling off my childrenâ€™s musical instruments from their youth, as I sit in ice and eat coconut oil for lunch. I know what I should do â€“ itâ€™s what weâ€™re doing, heading out to look for a better way to live. I know that living where we are living is not cutting it. Weâ€™re slowing wilting away, like the proverbial frog in the boiling pot of water. Is this crazy move what really what I want to do? Yes and NO. I like where I live, I like having my kids and extended family near by, I love MY bed and I really really like(liked) my house. But I know that we have to do something different.
I donâ€™t know what that different is going to be or look like. I know it is going to be hard- change always is, but my husband and I are in agreement that one way or another, weâ€™re planning an escape.

\*\*\*This was originally posted in early June but the thread (and comments) got deleted so I am re-posting in hopes of continuing the escape story\*\*\*

### Jack Kruse — 2013-07-21T23:14:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/planing-my-escape.7676/post-90523>

Keep us posted and what is the update?

### Meg — 2013-07-22T00:35:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/planing-my-escape.7676/post-90527>

I just returned from a month in Central America (spent most of my time in Panama & Nicaragua) and I have so many interesting observations regarding my physical and emotional well being. One of the main goals of our trip was to see how we felt away from all the EMF’s we are normally exposed to here in a suburb of Atlanta. Everywhere we went I was constantly acessing the EMF’s around me. Was there wifi where I was sleeping? Were there smart meters (yes- sadly these countries have smart meters!!!)? Where was the closest cell tower? How much electrical wiring surrounded our sleeping area? We lived low to the ground the whole month and we spent four nights on an island with no wifi and only a generator on for a few hours each day. I had my feet on the earth and my body in the water as often as I could and I must say I am sportin an awesome tan! Interestingly, my diet was not as optimal as it would be normally, I cheated for the first time in a year- not because of cravings but because of lack of choices at times. OVERALL…..Did I feel better physically? YES. Did I feel better emotionally? YES. There are many things to consider, many variables to feeling better, such as, I have no house to clean and no business to tend to (the lack of these two items can be considered both a source of relief and a source of stress, depending on the day and the mood). I slept great- even when sleeping in small beds with extreme heat (that’s the way it felt to me) and pesky nightly noises. I did not have any strange heart palps – something I was struggling with before we left. Interestingly, we have been back in ATL for 4 nights and I have not slept well at all.
My husband has his own story to tell. He did not have one bout of being woken in the night with his shoulders feeling electrocuted. For months he’s struggled with muscle cramping ( especially in his hands and feet) this got better but did not go away. His sleep was greatly improved as well.
I still have a lot to ponder but I feel like there were several things that attributed to us feeling better. First being the total lack of EMF’s. Sometimes our hostel had wifi and electricity but the surrounding twenty homes did not, so we were never swimming in a bath of EMF’s but rather we were exposed a bit here and there. Second, we found ourselves going to bed at 8pm (sun goes down just after 6pm) and getting up with the sun around 5:00 ish. Our bodies adapted to this and it just felt right. We were exposed to very little artificial light at night.

### fitness@home — 2013-07-22T01:33:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/planing-my-escape.7676/post-90533>

Bravo Meg! Love what you are doing.

Please keep updating here...we can all learn much from you.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-07-22T01:44:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/planing-my-escape.7676/post-90534>

good stuff..........

### caroline — 2013-07-22T01:45:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/planing-my-escape.7676/post-90535>

Please tell us more about your amazing adventure to wellness and peace.....

How are all the kids doing?

### SeaHorse — 2013-07-22T03:32:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/planing-my-escape.7676/post-90544>

it's so exciting to hear you take action and experiment with your life at such a big level of change. Hooray for you!

### JanSz — 2013-07-22T03:56:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/planing-my-escape.7676/post-90545>

> [Meg said:](/goto/post?id=90527)
>
> Second, we found ourselves going to bed at 8pm (sun goes down just after 6pm) and getting up with the sun around 5:00 ish. Our bodies adapted to this and it just felt right. We were exposed to very little artificial light at night.

1979-1980 I have spend with my family, one year at the village Pria Brava Brazil.
Next village to Itaona, where I worked.

23Â° 0' 27" S 44Â° 27' 30" O

After about 6 months something was bothering me.
It was getting worst.
Then I realized, what was bothering me were 12hrs days and 12 hrs nights.

I loved having house right on the beach.
Loved dry air and 100+F sun straight down,
but at 5:30PM I was expecting another 4hrs of sun and daylight,
so when it was dark about half hour latter it was shocking to me (every day).

### Shijin13 — 2013-07-22T16:42:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/planing-my-escape.7676/post-90561>

I love visiting my aunts house in Baja. She's totally off the grid. living off Solar power, and generator to run the washer/dryer. she also has to have water trucked in... but she's right off the Pacific. and about 1hr from Cabo - so you've got Costco, Sams, Walmart. Of course you need to have your Pesos ready for the Federales when they stop you.... but hey... there's a price for living off the grid... And medical care down there isn't great...but then... its not that great here either...

### Meg — 2013-07-22T17:46:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/planing-my-escape.7676/post-90564>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=90561)
>
> I love visiting my aunts house in Baja. She's totally off the grid. living off Solar power, and generator to run the washer/dryer. she also has to have water trucked in... but she's right off the Pacific. and about 1hr from Cabo - so you've got Costco, Sams, Walmart. Of course you need to have your Pesos ready for the Federales when they stop you.... but hey... there's a price for living off the grid... And medical care down there isn't great...but then... its not that great here either...

I've been wondering what it would be like to go off grid ever since the EMF series began. I've also been wondering how much "off grid" I can live with realistically. Now that I am back and have had my 5th night of not sleeping good I am really starting to think about this. We met a 35 year old british man who's been in San Juan Del Sur, Nicaragua for 7 years now who told us- "when you leave here is when you realize how great it is". I'm starting to believe him. I will try to post some pictures of the sunsets I was forced to endure there.....

Heathcare.......we experienced it first hand. My 17 year old daughter woke up at midnight one night with the highest fever I've ever felt on one of my kids, followed by diarrhea and vomiting. I, of course thought she might have Denghe fever and got very worried. We were on the island of Ometepe in the middle of Lake Nicaragua. We called a cab at 5 am and took the 7am ferry off the island to the nearest hospital in Rivas. Of course, it was a Sunday - my kids never get sick during normal business hours. The hospital - I don't really know how to describe it and make you believe it. Dirty, HOT, no toilet paper (bring your own), no drinking water (bring your own), people sitting in chairs hooked up to IV's everywhere (dehydration is big there). But, when all was said and done, I was pleased with the detailed care we got. Blood tests revealed no Denghe fever, ( the Dr even commented that she was a little low in iron), but she had a virus w/ a bad case of travelers diarrhea. She was given an IV with fluids, fever & diarrhea meds & electrolytes packets to take with us when we left- and do you want to know what it cost? Nothing. My husband was impressed. I was just so grateful. Once again, something to process and think about as I re enter the American way of doing things

### Meg — 2013-07-22T18:24:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/planing-my-escape.7676/post-90565>

[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/photo-jpg.143/)[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/photo-2-jpg.145/)
Sunset at San Juan Del Sur Nicaragua. Notice the second picture- the poor children don't have the money for cleats and a field to play soccer on so they are forced to play in the dark on the beach. Talk about electrons!

### SeaHorse — 2013-07-22T19:31:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/planing-my-escape.7676/post-90572>

Glad to hear your daughter is ok....as for living off the grid, the other day we had a wild twister-like wind storm, lightening and thunder here in Vermont and it knocked out our power. I was so happy being in a silent house using candles again. I haven't used candles since the winter because it stays light so late....but the silence was really something and I'm going to go ahead and start getting some electrical work in the house so we can kill most of the circuits every night. Despite being in pretty good shape for EMFs it was a noticeable difference when EVERYTHING was off......blissful.

### Meg — 2013-07-22T23:51:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/planing-my-escape.7676/post-90595>

> [SeaHorse said:](/goto/post?id=90572)
>
> Glad to hear your daughter is ok....as for living off the grid, the other day we had a wild twister-like wind storm, lightening and thunder here in Vermont and it knocked out our power. I was so happy being in a silent house using candles again. I haven't used candles since the winter because it stays light so late....but the silence was really something and I'm going to go ahead and start getting some electrical work in the house so we can kill most of the circuits every night. Despite being in pretty good shape for EMFs it was a noticeable difference when EVERYTHING was off......blissful.

That sounds wonderful Seahorse. Kind of like camping out when I was a kid, but now I'm grown up and I'm thinking I instinctively knew something as a kid......that pure silence is good for me!
At home I have always used a fan for white noise to help me sleep, hubby says I'm addicted to it. In central America I did not use/need or miss it. The pure silence was fine. It makes me wonder if maybe I've been trying to block out or over ride something I sense from the environment. I am starting to think this because since being back I feel like I hear a constant low grade hum/ring in my ears. I'm not sure I would've noticed this if I hadn't gone without it for a month. I so don't want to jump to any quick conclusions yet.

### caroline — 2013-07-23T02:11:35+0300

Meg ....please keep posting all your thoughts as you digest all of this.....amazing, amazing, amazing.......

### Meg — 2013-08-01T18:35:13+0300

I have had a non stop headache since the third day being stateside. My sleep has been bad and I feel like my cortisol patterns are shifting- can't get to sleep at night and feel drugged trying to get up in the morning. there is a deep reason no two people get the same effect........the field around a person interacts with their own oscialltions and frequencies to produce the effect. This is why leaky gut never seems to go away for many and why some TG never go down........it is also why some get migraines.......also remember where you live has different EMF and ELF signals.......they are both additive and subtractive to the Schumann in your locale. Moreover, the Schumann oscilalates on Earth from 0-30 Hz.........not all of us live in a place where it is great........and some of live in places where EMF is pure deadly.

I believe you once told me that the schuman resonance was good in Central America, so perhaps that played a role in why I felt better there - the extra dose of Schuman without all the EMF'S & ELF'S to subtract or add from it. ### Meg — 2013-12-31T18:35:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/planing-my-escape.7676/post-104510>

Boy have I missed this site. So many new names, so many new ideas, so many new successes. I am overwhelmed trying to get caught up. I have had tears in my eyes reading some of the personal journals.
Hubby and I have been on the move in Central America seeing if we think we could/should live there. If it weren’t for our kids the decision would be easy for me. My husband says, because of the kids the decision should be easy. He is able to think forward better than me. My heart gets in the way.
What does one do for 3 months in Central America with only a backpack and a husband to keep up with? Things I haven’t done for years. I sat around and talked to people for hours on end, and I spent hours - days, no WEEKS, just thinking. If there was one thing that changed inside of me on this trip it’s the ability to think and see things differently. I read some books on quantum physics and the brain (they talked about the double slit electron test) and did some thinking about my “thinking.” I was able contemplate things about my life and my past and my patterns of thought and I was able to see them from a different perspective, almost like I was an outside observer seeing my thoughts and choices for the first time. But without the emotional baggage. From this perspective I felt like I could make clearer choices about the future and choose which thoughts I want to have and which I want to avoid. I realized places where I have been trapped in both my physical and emotional health. Place where my thinking would not let me move forward. It could not let me move forward because of the thoughts I was choosing to let rule me. has been quite enlightening for me. Very freeing. I don’t feel like I’ve had this much mental creativity or clarity since I was a child when I thrived on my imaginary world.
I can’t help but wonder the science behind this. Is it the extra Vitamin D I received being in the tropics during my normal fall season? Is it the lack of EMF’s? Is it that the magnetic field (Schumann) is so much better in Nicaragua than Georgia? Is it the stress free environment I put myself in? Extra dopamine from somewhere? I honestly am not sure. But, there is no doubt that I feel differently now that I am back in the USA. Especially in my brain and my ability to think better.
We might be heading back soon, even if to just test “me” and see if things change back for the better. I was born in Florida and lived my whole childhood just one block from the beach. There is little doubt in my mind that I feel my best next to the water.
Happy New Years to everyone out there searching for optimal. I feel like we are family.

Are you keeping a journal? can you write a book? Please share all this amazing stuff that is happening to you and your husband. Think of the back lash for your children ..... they must be unbelievably proud of their oldies! How is all this inspiring them to live their life to the fullest?

Why not let your wonderful future find you? ......

# Thread: Why are the feet so difficult
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-are-the-feet-so-difficult.7680/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 22
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### fireforlunch — 2013-07-22T22:19:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-are-the-feet-so-difficult.7680/post-90585>

When I first started in the water last week I only put my feet in and it was pretty difficult so I bailed after 10 minutes.

I thought my whole body would be as painful as the feet but that isn't the case.

I've seen some people say that they didn't put their feet in for 6 months.

Why do they seem to take the cold the worst?

Is it because they are farthest from the heart or is there some other reason?

### nonchalant — 2013-07-22T22:41:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-are-the-feet-so-difficult.7680/post-90587>

Very good question, FFL. Since our feet were made for strolling around in the cold water, feeling for shellfish, you would think they could tolerate the cold best.

I have been CT'ing for over a year and I still keep my toes out of the water, except for the first couple minutes. It might just be a habit, however. When I first started getting the water really cold, just above freezing, I would end up with whitish splotches on the soles of my feet. The calloused areas of my feet would freeze. The arch area was just fine, however. I don't see that freezing effect anymore.

### Shijin13 — 2013-07-22T22:46:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-are-the-feet-so-difficult.7680/post-90588>

Even when I deep CT I still wear 2-3pair of wool socks...

this Blog should give you some insight. [http://www.jackkruse.com/quantum-biology-6-bipedalism/](http://www.jackkruse.com/quantum-biology-6-bipedalism/)

### Inger — 2013-07-24T09:13:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-are-the-feet-so-difficult.7680/post-90716>

Yeah.. it goes very slow! But now I recognize I need way less on my feet to keep them warm than the others in this house. Here is stone floor in my parents kitchen and living room. And it is cold! No floor heating. I still walk barefoot all the time. The others need socks and complain of cold feet, even if it is summer. My feet are always warm, or just fine. And I am not even doing much CT except of a short swim in the river every day years round and some face dunks in the morning! But I have been doing it for 2,5 years now.

### Nathan — 2013-11-15T05:41:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-are-the-feet-so-difficult.7680/post-100752>

My feet adapted pretty quickly to being in the water (I do around 52-55F). My problem is they stay cold for hours afterward. I don't CT my hands but they are bad too. Been driving to work with the windows down in the 40s lately and I'm fine except for my hands which get cold and achy and stay cold for a long time after. Just need to stick with it I guess

I have the same problem. My feet are fine but hands are a pain. For me I simply think its partly due to low blood pressure and heart rate which makes it more difficult for the warm blood to get to my hands. Its quite annoying because I feel bad overall even though its only my hands which are cold.

# Thread: Leptin level in a blood test

**Posts:** 1

### nicld — 2013-07-22T23:25:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-level-in-a-blood-test.7682/post-90594>

How accurate are these?

I had a doctors appointment about 2 weeks ago and asked for this blood test to be done and just got the results back.

It said that my level was 6.3 ng/mL and that normal levels are above 8 in females with a BMI over 25 (which I still have).

This test was done in the morning after fasting (not sure if that would make a difference).

I had been doing the reset for 6 weeks already and just starting doing CT.

Just got back from a mini vacation and I was able to stay with the eating program (for the most part) but did have some treats and the CT was just a quick cold shower (and boy I missed them). I know that I will have to start counting today and day one now due to this set back but that's ok. I did not screw up my leptin over night, I can't fix it over night but I can fix it.

I will be trying to scour the blogs and Dr. Kruse's site and see if I can find some more stuff on this but if anyone has any ideas on this, I would love to hear them.

# Thread: Grounding Revisited...Earthing Mats, Feet On Concrete, Feet on Grass...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-revisited-earthing-mats-feet-on-concrete-feet-on-grass.7684/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MeghanK — 2013-07-23T03:24:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-revisited-earthing-mats-feet-on-concrete-feet-on-grass.7684/post-90606>

Ok, I was trying to read through the grounding post with RayB/Anna discussing Earthing vs. Diet.... but it is a MONSTER post and I'm getting dizzy... so I will just ask....
1) **Earthing Mats-** What is the consensus these days? Dirty electricity or OK? I know NonChalant uses... Dr. Kruse not a fan? Better than nothing especially if living in an apartment in the city?
2) Can you **ground walking barefoot on concrete** or ONLY grass.... I have a park across the way from my apartment that I try to walk around the grass for at least 20 minutes a day but it would be convenient if I could walk on the cement barefoot and get the benefits since there is not much grass on the way to the store etc....
3) **How long** do you need to ground outside per day? meaning what is the minimum you can get away with?
4)What is the consensus on **EMF blocking or reducing bracelets?** Best Brand or Don't bother.....

### SeaHorse — 2013-07-23T17:01:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-revisited-earthing-mats-feet-on-concrete-feet-on-grass.7684/post-90640>

Meghan....here are my answers.

1\. I would not use these plugged into the wall....if you are in an apartment save your dollars and get a magnetico instead.
2\. Concrete does give you access to the earth resonance....grass is better, wet grass is even better and anything away from power lines, buildings, etc. is best.
3\. There is no minimum that you "can get away with" The idea is that we should be spending our whole lives grounded. So ANY time is better than no time. It is entirely individual as far as effects. There is no way to assign a time because of all the variables.
4\. I don't have experience with these, but I'm interested....I like the shungite stone that people were talking about earlier in the year. Still haven't purchased any though.

Just my 2 cents: Do what you can at the very highest level of your ability and experiment with your own responses. You won't get a "prescription" here because we are all different.

### MeghanK — 2013-07-23T18:14:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-revisited-earthing-mats-feet-on-concrete-feet-on-grass.7684/post-90661>

I know this is a lot of N= 1 but would love to hear others thoughts especially those who also live in cities/apartments/condos and or have used smart meters to test....
THANKS Sea Horse... never heard of the shungite stone.. can you tell me more about it..
